MNI BONDS: EGBs-GILTS CASH CLOSE: Early Yield Rises Fade <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakOUI6aJhcRh1HA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJGtptJe2eJOIQ> 

European curves traded mixed Wednesday, with Gilts outperforming EGBs.

*	Yields gapped higher on the reopen, reflecting a jump related to Tuesday's month/quarter-end dynamics, as well as UK fiscal concerns centered on defence spending. Further weakness ensued on Iran once again playing down the idea of final negotiations with the US nearing.
*	The high in yields for the day was set around 2pm London however and would move lower for the rest of the session.
*	The bond rally was triggered in large part by dovishly-received central bank speakers at the ECB's Sintra symposium: BoE Governor Bailey reiterated that the UK economy and labour market are soft, and Fed Chair Warsh and ECB President Lagarde each commented that inflation risks had come down since their respective June meetings.
*	Data also contributed to a more constructive tone into the European close, with US manufacturing survey readings on the soft side of expectations.
*	In data earlier in the session, final European manufacturing PMIs were mixed vs prelims, while June flash Eurozone HICP was softer-than-expected.
*	Thursday's global calendar is highlighted by the US nonfarm payrolls release, while in Europe we get comments by BOE's Mann and ECB's Escriva, and some 2nd tier data including unemployment for Spain/Italy/Eurozone.

Closing Yields / 10-Yr EGB Spreads To Germany

*	Germany: The 2-Yr yield is down 1.6bps at 2.512%, 5-Yr is up 0.6bps at 2.601%, 10-Yr is up 1.8bps at 2.878%, and 30-Yr is up 3bps at 3.451%.
*	UK: The 2-Yr yield is down 2.7bps at 4.126%, 5-Yr is down 0.8bps at 4.294%, 10-Yr is down 0.1bps at 4.756%, and 30-Yr is up 1.4bps at 5.487%.
*	Italian BTP spread up 1.7bps at 78.7bps / Spanish up 1.1bps at 49.5bps

MNI EGB OPTIONS: Call Spreads, Ladders, Condors And Calendars <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakOUI6aJhcRh1HQ~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJGtptJe2eJOIQ> 

Wednesday's Europe rates/bond options flow included:

*	ERQ6 97.62/9775cs bought for half in 5k
*	ERU6 97.56/97.62/97.68c ladder, bought for 2 in 5k
*	ERV6 97.50/97.62/97.68/97.81c condor vs 97.18p, bought the condor for 0.75 in 10k
*	ERZ6 97.6875/97.8125cs, bought for 0.75 in 10k
*	SFIZ6 96.25/95.75/95.25p fly, sold at 13.75 in 4.5k
*	SFIZ6/U6 96.25/96.35 calendar call spread, bought for 1.25 in 5k (buys the Dec)

MNI FOREX: USD Steady Ahead of June Payrolls, EURGBP Extending Lower <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakOUI6aJhcRh1Eg~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJGtptJe2eJOIQ> 

*	The USD index is 0.15% in the green on Wednesday, but has largely spent the session consolidating as global markets await tomorrowвҖҷs US employment report ahead of the July 04 long weekend. Chair Warsh offered little new information at the policy panel in Sintra, acknowledging that inflation expectations have recently improved, but reiterated the committee will deliver price stability in the US.
*	Confirmation of softer-than-expected inflation data in the Eurozone may have moderately weighed on the Euro, prompting EURUSD to slip back below 1.14 and keep a bearish theme of the pair intact. The most recent bounce appears corrective and the downtrend remains intact, highlighted by moving average studies that are in a bear-mode position. 1.1325 remains the bear trigger.
*	Some divergence between the Euro and Sterling is notable on Wednesday, exacerbated by some important EURGBP chart points giving way. 0.54% weakness today has seen the cross slip to the lowest level in a year, extending on a break below key supports of 0.8610 and 0.8597. A close at current levels would mark an important medium-term bearish development.
*	Scope is seen for a more protracted selloff towards 0.8508, the June 27 вҖҷ25 low. This level would closely coincide with the base of a bear channel, which is drawn from the November 2025 high.
*	Elsewhere, AUD remains one of the poorer performers, consolidating around the 0.69 mark on Wednesday. Sights remain on 0.6833, the Mar 30 low and a key support.
*	Topside momentum for USDJPY has stalled, and the pair has edged around 40 pips off the 162.84 cycle highs posted earlier in the session. Market participants remain aware of the risks surrounding more forceful verbal intervention from officials, and initial support is seen at 161.02, the 20-day EMA.
*	Swiss CPI and Eurozone unemployment data precede the key US payrolls release Thursday.

*	RES 1: 7567.75/7648.75 High Jun 30 / 15

*	SUP 2: 7200.00 Round number support
*	SUP 3: 7135.00 Low Apr 23 
*	SUP 4: 7047.75 50.0% retracement of the Mar 31 - Jun 1 bull cycle

The trend theme in S&P E-Minis is bullish and the latest pullback appears corrective. Note that moving average studies remain in a bull-mode position and this continues to highlight a dominant uptrend. MondayвҖҷs rally highlights a bullish engulfing candle - a reversal signal. If correct, it signals the end of the recent correction. Key support lies at 7292.25, the Jun 11 low. A continuation higher would open 7693.75, the Jun 2 high and M/T bull trigger.

MNI COMMODITIES: Crude Falls Further Amid Signs of Near-Term Oversupply <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakOUI6aJhcRh1SA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJGtptJe2eJOIQ> 

*	Crude has remained under pressure on Wednesday amid signs of near-term oversupply as Strait of Hormuz oil flows have recovered with US-Iran indirect talks underway.
*	WTI Aug 26 is down by 1.6% at $68.4/bbl.
*	OPEC+ will likely agree a further hike in their output targets from August when they meet on Sunday, three sources told Reuters on Wednesday.
*	According to Iran, the Wednesday talks are aimed at implementing certain provisions of the MoU and are focused on IranвҖҷs frozen funds and the Strait of Hormuz.
*	From a technical perspective, a short-term bearish theme in WTI futures remains intact, with scope for an extension towards $65.95, the 76.4% retracement of the Dec 16 - May 18 bull leg.
*	Meanwhile, precious metals have risen further today, buoyed by the pullback in the US dollar from earlier session highs after Fed Chair Walsh acknowledged the recent improvement in inflation expectations.
*	Spot gold has risen by 1.4% to $4,064/oz, while silver has rallied by 2% to $59.8/oz.
*	Despite the move, a bearish theme in gold remains intact, with sights on the $3,900.00 handle next. Initial firm resistance is $4,183.0, the 20-day EMA.
*	For silver, the trend set-up also remains bearish and recent gains have been corrective. Key support at $61.007, the Mar 23 low and a bear trigger, has been cleared, opening $52.577, a Fibonacci projection. Initial 20-day EMA resistance is at $64.221.

emails.subject: Data Center Power in Texas: Discussion on ERCOTвҖҷs Batch Zero Process | Jul 7 | 12pm ET
emails.body:
Corporate Access

 



 

  

MORGAN STANLEY

 

Data Center Power in Texas: 

Discussion on ERCOTвҖҷs Batch Zero Process

 

 

Tuesday, July 7th 

 

Time:

12:00pm вҖ“ 1:00pm ET

 

Format:

Virtual Group Meeting

 

Please email your Morgan Stanley representative

if you are interested. Space is limited.

 

 

 

 

Please join MS Analysts Stephen Byrd, David Arcaro and Jordan Grimes, North American Power & Gas Trading for a discussion with Clayton Greer, VP, Energy Division at Cholla Petroleum on ERCOTвҖҷs Batch Zero Process.

 

 

Speakers: 

 

 

Clayton Greer, VP, Energy Division at Cholla Petroleum 

Topics of Discussion:

*	Large load / data center interconnection process in Texas
*	Potential size of large load approvals, timing, and impacts on the ERCOT market
*	Implications of batching process for projects under development by power and crypto companies

 

 

Speaker Bio: Clayton Greer is at the forefront of integrating cryptocurrency mining operations and data center management with electrical power grids as the Vice President of the Energy Division at Cholla Petroleum. With a career spanning over three decades in the Electrical Power Industry, Clayton has been instrumental in the evolution of power distribution and market operations, particularly within ERCOT's zonal and nodal markets.

Beginning his journey at Public Service of Oklahoma, Clayton honed his skills in power system analysis, substation design, and emergency response in Tulsa, OK. His expertise grew through roles at Texas New Mexico Power and significant contributions to ERCOT's stakeholder process, where he played a key role in shaping Texas's electricity market. Clayton's career further expanded into power marketing and trading with notable firms before his strategic move to Cholla in 2022.

An honors graduate in Electrical Engineering from the University of Tulsa, Clayton's professional journey is underpinned by a deep commitment to innovation and efficiency in power distribution. His work not only ensures a reliable and efficient energy supply but also paves the way for sustainable energy practices in the rapidly evolving sectors of cryptocurrency and data centers.

 

 

 

Research

Stephen Byrd

David Arcaro
David.Arcaro@morganstanley.com <mailto:%0bDavid.Arcaro@morganstanley.com> 

 

 

 

Corporate Access Specialist

Timoney Campbell 

Timoney.Campbell@morganstanley.com <mailto:Timoney.Campbell@morganstanley.com>

emails.subject: Mexico Equity Strategy: USMCA Will Not Be Rubber-Stamped; We Are Buyers of Trade-Induced Anxiety (Ramos)
emails.body:
Mexico Equity Strategy



USMCA Will Not Be Rubber-Stamped; We Are Buyers of Trade-Induced Anxiety 

Full Report <https://bradescobbi.bluematrix.com/links2/html/0b531e9c-80ba-4120-a074-c88da8d928b8> 	

In today's virtual USMCA Joint Review, the US declined to renew-or "rubber-stamp"-the agreement, defaulting instead to annual reviews. Given that the third and last round of bilateral talks is scheduled for July 20, it was implicit the review would not be concluded by July 1 as initially expected. We believe this fact, coupled with positive headlines regarding Mexico's cooperation on security, explains the muted response in the FX and equity markets, even in nearshoring proxies. Mexico's Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard framed it as a procedural transition to negotiate structural adjustments, rather than a systemic crisis. It is worth noting that the USMCA remains in force for another 10 years unless a member country exits, which would need to notify the other two members six months in advance.

After the meeting, Ambassador Jamieson Greer (USTR) said that the review process will focus on addressing the US trade deficit with Mexico and tackling regional supply-chain imbalances. The US is pushing for stricter rules of origin, including requiring that 50% of automotive components specifically be made in the US, and raising the overall regional value content threshold for autos from 75% to over 80%. Furthermore, the US is demanding a unified front on export controls and investment limits to block China from using Mexico to dodge tariffs.

Our take: It seems as if the July 1 deadline had little significance in the overall context consisting of just a virtual meeting between the three countries to publicly, and formally, announce the US wouldn't be backing the agreement as it currently stands, which everyone had already anticipated. Instead, we believe the July 20 in-person meeting in Mexico City might provide us with clues of what exactly the US is looking to change and facilitate an agreement between Mexico and the US. A separate negotiation, which would bode well for nearshoring sentiment, is related to Section 232 tariffs. We would be buyers of any USMCA-induced market anxiety. A new deal tipping the scale in favor of the US would also give the Trump administration political ammunition ahead of the US midterm elections. Companies that are sensitive to US trade policies remain cautiously optimistic, including those in the steel and auto sectors. We maintain our positive and out-of-consensus view on the US-Mexico trade negotiations and favor nearshoring proxies Vesta, Fibra Prologis, OMA, and Banorte. We have also recently added Ternium to our LatAm equity strategy (A Second Opportunity in the Second Half <https://bradescobbi.bluematrix.com/links2/link/html/0b531e9c-80ba-4120-a074-c88da8d928b8/d0b38bb4-0480-48d5-9ab4-1409db2fb691> ).

Figure 1 - USMCA Review Timeline and Scenario Analysis	
	
Source: Bradesco BBI	




Rodolfo Ramos, CFA

Juan Ponce

+1 (347) 703-9252

General Disclosures

Such market commentary or trading strategies reflect the different time frames, assumptions, views and analytical methods of the persons who prepared them, and ГҒgora CTVM and its affiliates are under no obligation to ensure that such market commentary or trading strategies are brought to the attention of any investor who to receive this information. READ IN APP <https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ET-_!,w_36,c_scale,f_png,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack.com%2Ficon%2FLucideArrowUpRight%3Fv%3D4%26height%3D36%26fill%3Dnone%26stroke%3D%2523808080%26strokeWidth%3D2> 
 	

By Mark LaPedus 

Intel has broken ground on a new photomask manufacturing facility in Santa Clara, Calif.

The new facility will expand IntelвҖҷs existing photomask manufacturing operations at the Bowers Campus in Santa Clara. For several years, the companyвҖҷs internal mask-making unit--Intel Mask Operations (IMO)--has been producing masks at the Bowers Campus.

IMOвҖҷs new facility at the Bowers Campus is expected to be a 107,000-square-foot operation. At some point in the future, the new facility will make leading-edge photomasks for extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography.

Photomasks, sometimes called masks or reticles, are an important part of the semiconductor manufacturing process. Masks are a critical step in chip production, as they transfer circuit patterns onto silicon wafers <https://substack.com/redirect/b23d436f-d0c8-4878-8165-a27ff8000783?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> , according to Intel.

Nonetheless, besides Santa Clara, IMO also manufactures masks at a facility in Hillsboro, Ore. IMO mainly manufactures photomasks for IntelвҖҷs internal requirements. IMO also makes masks for IntelвҖҷs foundry unit.

IMO builds a wide range of masks spanning the 32nm to 1.4nm nodes. The unit builds EUV masks, including reticles for low- and high-NA EUV.

вҖңSanta Clara has been home to some of IntelвҖҷs most important manufacturing innovations for decades. By expanding the Bowers campus mask operations, weвҖҷre strengthening a critical capability that supports advanced process technology production around the world and reinforces Intel FoundryвҖҷs commitment to advancing U.S. semiconductor manufacturing leadership,вҖқ said Frank Abboud, vice president of Intel Foundry and general manager of IMO.

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/b733c95c-05aa-45c4-8947-c1ece3685c6c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 		

Intel celebrated the expansion of its operations at its Mask Operations facility on Bowers Avenue in Santa Clara. The event brought together IntelвҖҷs executives, employees, suppliers and local officials. Left to right in the photo: Lisa Gillmor (Santa Clara Mayor), Naga Chandrasekaran (EVP Intel Foundry), Lip-Bu Tan (Intel CEO), Frank Abboud (VP Intel Foundry, GM IMO) and Clay Haden (Skanska USA Building President) Source: Intel

emails.subject: SHORT CANON - May Industry Camera Sales -DD% YoY (1st DD decline since Dec 2023), more China-driven on tough comp but watching for downtrading trends, MSR US HW Consumer Tracker shows growing inflation concerns
emails.body:
SALES COMMENTARY ONLY (NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH)

For institutional use onLY

 

I personally continue to like Canon on the short side. I personally think there is still opportunity in the вҖңMemory LosersвҖқ trade, focused on companies where end-demand is not extremely strong (unlike servers) and where companies total memory buying power is low. Cameras continue to screen in this risk category.

 

May Industry Data for Digital Cameras was released <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/3a69de54-7534-11f1-9560-223b52d1e427?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> , with units down -17.6% YoY. This is the first month of double digit YoY decline since Dec 2023. MS Japan Precision analyst Mie Yamazaki points out this was likely driven by China (-39% YoY), which may be a result of a tough comp from last yearвҖҷs electronics subsidies. For the other regions: Japan +10% YoY, Europe -14% YoY, Americas -6.6% YoY, Asia -4.8%.

 

For Canon, worth noting it has less exposure to China than the industry. CanonвҖҷs CY25 Camera RoW Sales Ratio was 33%, vs Industry CY25 China Ratio 28%. Canon has relatively higher exposure to Americas (30% vs Industry 23%) and Europe (29% vs Industry 21%). So beyond the fact IвҖҷm admittedly cherry picking monthly industry data (April <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/8c429cec-5d93-11f1-bb82-5734c7383398?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  was fine), this data point alone may not be a direct reflection of CanonвҖҷs trends. However, China is the single largest camera market in 2025 (28%), and has been the key driver of industry growth in recent years.

 

 

The upside risk IвҖҷm watching out for is companies having price hike power if consumerвҖҷs expectations re-anchor to higher inflation (similar to 2022 inflation pick-up). AAPLвҖҷs <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/e2c2d0a6-7098-11f1-b8bb-85ba1f0ac5ae?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  price hike announcement last week raise that risk, although market initially feared memory-driven price hikes would lead to potential demand destruction. In the May Industry Data, Shipment ASP was +19.7% YoY. Yamazaki san thinks higher selling prices may have weighed on demand. There was also a divergence in Built-In Lens Camera unit growth -0.6% YoY vs Interchangeable Lens Camera units -23% YoY (Mirrorless -21%). That could imply some users in entry class mirrorless cameras are downtrading to built-in lens. If thatвҖҷs a sustained trend, that me be a negative impact to Canon (interchangeable / mirrorless lens cameras are higher margin).

 

 

IвҖҷd also flag recent US Consumer spending tracker <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/5df66cae-70a5-11f1-b8bb-85ba1f0ac5ae?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  from MS US HW analyst Erik Woodring. Consumer Sentiment improved MoM, but next 6mo consumer electronics spending intentions worsened MoM. Usually these are correlated at a 1mo lag, so Erik is watching for the latter to likely improve MoM. However, he highlights that "coping with inflation" as a headwind has been steadily rising since Jan '26 as a top concern for consumers for this year, some of which reflects memory/component inflation in higher consumer electronics. 

 

 

Report: Precision Instruments: Digital Camera Stats (May 2026): Shipment Volume Down 2-digits YoY; Watching For a Continuation Ahead (1 Jul 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/3a69de54-7534-11f1-9560-223b52d1e427?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

 

Happy to discuss more and connect you with Yamazaki san. She is OW on Canon.

 

Amir Amerian   
Morgan Stanley | Institutional Equity Division   
1585 Broadway, 5th Floor | New York, NY  10036   
Office: +1-212-761-7684

Cell: +1-347-491-1938
Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com <mailto:Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>  

Pronouns: He, Him, His

 

 

Digital camera shipment volume trend (red markings are sales addition only and donвҖҷt represent research view)



 

 

MSR US HW: Sixty percent of consumers in our survey flagged that coping with inflation is among their top concerns for this year, and this percentage has been steadily rising since Jan' 26.

(red markings are sales addition only and donвҖҷt represent research view)



 

From: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com> 
Sent: Thursday, June 4, 2026 5:45 PM
To: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>
Subject: SHORT CANON - memory risks growing while cons just in-line w guide & valuation now ABOVE 1Q level, Cameras seeing negative mix shift (less room to pass cost) + guide assumed ME conflict ends May & could use cost controls to mitigate

 

SALES COMMENTARY ONLY (NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH)

For institutional use onLY

 

I personally continue to like Canon on the short side.  Dell <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/512ebaca-5b70-11f1-a062-f8758c538433?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  and HPE <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/ccb78f4c-5dc2-11f1-bb82-5734c7383398?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  results highlighted the risks of being overly cautious on memory cost impacts. But I personally think there is still opportunity in the вҖңMemory LosersвҖқ trade, focused on companies where end-demand is not extremely strong (unlike servers) and where companies total memory buying power is low. Cameras continues to screen in this risk category.

 

MS Japan Precision analyst Mie Yamazaki is OW on Canon, but across her reports post-1Q result she continues to highlight downside risk to CanonвҖҷs guide and consensus.

 

 

The MS Global Semis & Hardware team is out with a big Insight report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/4163b912-4ec1-11f1-b588-961df8ad6aaa?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  highlighting a longer-term shortage of memory. As AI buyers / hyperscalers lock up more of the future memory supply with LTAs, that leaves a smaller, more volatile, and structurally higher priced memory pool left for smaller buyers. Yamazaki san and the coverage team say Cameras screen poorly: medium-low on availability, medium on cost impact, medium (but lagged) on pass-through ability, and medium-high on demand destruction [See Exhibit 39 <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/4163b912-4ec1-11f1-b588-961df8ad6aaa?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> ].

 

 

Based on camera industry data through March, Yamazaki san says unit demand for digital cameras remains resilient, but ASPs are declining due to consumersвҖҷ preference for lower-priced products. This mix shift downward suggests there may be a lack of progress passing on higher semi prices.

 

 

Canon after its 1Q result / downward revision to guide said it has secured memory volumes for the rest of 2026. However, it has NOT secured price. Company said pricing remains subject to revisions (every 3 months), and mgmt noted the possibility of future price fluctuations..

 

 

Sell-side consensus F12/26 OP has moved from 479bn pre-result to 454bn now. But this is JUST IN-LINE with co guide 456bn. The fact that consensus hasnвҖҷt moved well-below guide despite memory pricing risk through the year makes me personally think sell-side consensus continues to underappreciate this risk. F12/27 cons OP is also down just 4% since result. With the stock pretty much back at pre-result levels, this means valuation has actually gone UP slightly since result. Now we get earnings downside risk AND some room for de-rating.

 

 

There is also risk from higher oil price (especially for ink). CanonвҖҷs new guide didnвҖҷt factor in higher costs from ME conflict based on assumption it would resolve by May. Back in 2022, Canon faced a ~20bn cost impact due to oil above $100, of which it was able to offset ~50% via cost control measures.

 

 

The upside risk to this thesis (beside SPE or Medical massively outperforming) is that Canon could find a way to pass on higher prices or further control costs. But I personally think the experience of 2022 was unique. At the time, consumers/corporates globally were faced with a once-in-generation inflation acceleration, which initially created room to raise prices. Especially Cameras experienced a combo of post-Covid reopening +  demand + retro вҖңcoolвҖқ status demand. But I personally think in general and specific to cameras, there is less tolerance from consumer/corporates to fully accept cost inflation (especially if it isnвҖҷt related to live experiences / AI / tokens).

 

 

Reports:

Precision Instruments: JanвҖ“Mar 2026 Results Wrap (26 May 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/165c5be2-580e-11f1-a062-f8758c538433?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

Canon: Japan Summit 2026 Feedback: Indication of Growth Potential in Space Business (21 May 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/44976578-550d-11f1-9b0f-bf78b8368a11?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

Precision Instruments: Digital Camera Stats (Mar 2026): Shipment Volume Solid, but ASPs Trending Downward (1 May 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/df581784-450d-11f1-96be-793908518459?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

Canon: Post-results Company Contact (26 Apr 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/2dac097a-4097-11f1-9722-317872234fd6?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

 

 

Happy to discuss more and connect you with Yamazaki san.

 

Amir Amerian   
Morgan Stanley | Institutional Equity Division   
1585 Broadway, 5th Floor | New York, NY  10036   
Office: +1-212-761-7684

Cell: +1-347-491-1938
Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com <mailto:Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>  

Pronouns: He, Him, His

 

From: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com> 
Sent: Thursday, April 23, 2026 9:38 AM
To: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>
Subject: CANON MISS+CUT - Memory cost raised >600% in new guide due to spot procurement, Below-cons guide still has no Middle East cost impact - SHORT CANON

 

SALES COMMENTARY ONLY (NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH)

For institutional use onLY

 

This was a great callout by MS Japan Precision analyst Mie Yamazaki. Despite being OW on Canon, she previewed <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/b9edcfc2-379b-11f1-890c-084a1480e771?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  a major guidance cut driven by higher-than-expected memory cost impact in the Cameras вҖңImagingвҖқ division. That materialized today. Canon reported 1Q OP of 71.3bn (BBG cons 94.9bn) due to worse mix in the Printers division. More importantly, co cut its OP guide from 479bn to 456bn (cons 475bn), mainly driven by Cameras вҖңImagingвҖқ OP cut from -184bn to 176bn (MSRe 165bn) due worse than expected Memory impact.

 

 

Canon widened the FY Memory Cost impact from -7bn to -50bn. This is because company needed to procure a lot of memory in the spot market (!!). Company says this did not impact 1Q OP, so the drag will come through the rest of the year. Most of the impact was in Consumer Cameras rather than Networking Cameras. Canon says it has secured now 96% of its needs for the year. So one could argue there isnвҖҷt much downside to F12/26 from memory cost directly, but itвҖҷs worth watching to how consensus accounts for memory impact in F12/27 (MSR <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/9219853e-37e8-11f1-890c-084a1480e771?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  view is that absolute prices remain high all through CY27).

^my original comment in red above assumed co secured supply and price for rest of 2026, but that was incorrect, only supply was secured (correction as of June 4th, 2026)

 

Yamazaki san says while sell-side cons wasnвҖҷt modeling enough of this risk, buyside cons was likely closer to 460bn going in (vs new guide 456bn). What makes her worry about more downside even to guidance is Middle East conflict. The company today did NOT factor in higher costs related to Middle East, as shown by Printing OP guide kept basically same at 254bn. The Printing division is potentially impacted by higher raw material and shipping costs, similar to the dynamics in 2022 at start of Russia-Ukraine war. Back then, Canon raised the cost impact from 4Q21 <https://global.canon/en/ir/conference/pdf/conf2021e.pdf>  0bn to 1Q22 <https://global.canon/en/ir/conference/pdf/conf2022q1e.pdf>  -28.9bn to 2Q22 <https://global.canon/en/ir/conference/pdf/conf2022q2e-1.pdf>  -77.5bn. But in 2022 Canon was able to raise prices to make up for these higher costs. ItвҖҷs yet to be seen if Canon can similarly make up for these higher costs given Canon already raised price in 2025.

 

 

I personally continue to like the stock on the short side. The upside risk would be from Semi Cap. Company said it saw strong increase in inquiries and orders in 1Q, particularly driven by Memory customers. Canon raised its FY unit shipments by 14 units to 238 unit, but hasnвҖҷt reflected this strength in its 2H guide. But I personally think strength in its Semi Cap division is likely better understood, and especially as a relative pair vs many other Semi Cap or AI-exposed tech names, I personally think there is less room for the stock to outperform driven by its Semi Cap business.

 

Report: Canon: F12/26 1Q Results: Impact of Rising Memory Costs Greater than Expected, Lingering Concerns on Earnings due to Middle East (23 Apr 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/435fbcda-3ef8-11f1-9722-317872234fd6?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

 

Happy to discuss more and connect you with Yamazaki san.

 

Amir Amerian   
Morgan Stanley | Institutional Equity Division   
1585 Broadway, 5th Floor | New York, NY  10036   
Office: +1-212-761-7684

Cell: +1-347-491-1938
Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com <mailto:Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>  

Pronouns: He, Him, His

 

From: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 4:38 PM
To: Amerian, Amir (IED) <Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>
Subject: SHORT CAMERAS (Canon) - memory risks, MSR OW but lowers Canon 2026e OP below-cons and sees guide cut risk at this result (now 10% below guided Imaging OP)

 

SALES COMMENTARY ONLY (NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH)

For institutional use onLY

 

I personally think Canon looks interesting on the short side. The camera makers to me still look like an interesting hunting ground for memory losers. Although Canon has underperformed our MS Asia Memory Losers Basket (MSAPASML Index) by 6% YTD, Canon has outperformed by ~14% since Oct 1st when market started to really price-in memory supply/margin impacts among OEMs. MS Japan Precision Instruments analyst Mie Yamazaki is OW on Canon but she just cut her F12/26 OP to 465bn, below cons 475bn / guide 479bn, and expects co to cut guide at upcoming result. She sees risks from both memory pressure and Middle East conflict. [REPORT HERE <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/b9edcfc2-379b-11f1-890c-084a1480e771?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> ]

 

 

Quick background on Cameras post-Covid (my personal summary) вҖ“ they were in structural decline since the rise of smartphones. But post-Covid, they have seen renewed consumer interest. In the initial 2021 recovery, the thinking was this was driven by вҖңreopeningвҖқ / live personal event demand. But demand has been stable for longer than many expected. There is a noticeable element of cameras being вҖңcoolвҖқ especially among Gen Z, and there are even small businesses popping up offering digital camera rentals for events like weddings, etc.

 

Because Cameras were in structural decline, the industry (especially high-end) is decently consolidated. This includes Sony, Canon, Fujifilm, Nikon (Olympus sold-off its business years ago). IвҖҷd personally liken it to the HDD supply chain вҖ“ because it was thought to be in structural decline, you donвҖҷt hear of major competitors anymore from Korea/Taiwan/China as theyвҖҷve either stopped investing in the business or never tried to get in in the first place.

 

 

Consolidation + Market Shift toward High-end mean that Cameras are relatively high margin businesses and still provide a decent chunk of earnings even as the relevant companies have diversified their businesses away from legacy tech like this.

 

 

Memory & Cameras (color from Yamazaki san) вҖ“ Memory (DRAM+NAND) makes up roughly 10-15% of BOM for AI-enabled cameras, and less than that for non-AI cameras. Higher-end models have more memory content vs low-end. Other than margin, one needs to consider the ability to procure memory. Non-AI Consumer markets are having a harder time procuring memory likely given they lower priority customers for Memory makers. MS Japan Semis & Consumer Electronics analyst Kazuo Yoshikawa in his Socionext downgrade <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/629d297e-f9b7-11f0-8bb0-3a84fbe785bc?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>  highlighted the risk of digital & action camera customers potentially struggling to procure enough memory in 2026. Yoshikawa san believes companies with greater buying scale across entire company (Sony & Canon) have more bargaining power to secure supply than those at smaller scale (Fujifilm, Nikon). 

 

 

Why Canon as potential short over the others? I think Fujifilm could also still work as short, but stock is also down 14% since Oct 1st (in-line with basket) as it also prices in weakness in its bio-CDMO business. Yamazaki sanвҖҷs is OW and her lowered F3/27 OP is in-line w cons. Sony doesnвҖҷt generate a large portion of its earnings from Cameras and I agree with Yoshikawa san there are more than enough levers to make up for memory pressure in much-more-important Games business. For Nikon, MS Japan Semi Cap analyst Suzune Tamura is UW, but IвҖҷm personally hesitant to pitch it as a short given 1) we are in WFE upcycle; and 2) potentially upside risks from strategic relationship with EL FP.

 

CanonвҖҷs вҖңImagingвҖқ Segment contributed 37% of 2025 OP, with OPM of 16.4%, well-above total co average OPM 9.8%. Yamazaki san estimates that Cameras contributed 59% of Imaging segment sales in 2025. The rest coming from Network Cameras & Others. The biggest source of earnings difference between her and guide is Imaging вҖ“ she expects F12/26 OP of 165bn vs guide 184bn.

 

 

Reports:

Canon: 1Q Results Focus: Risk of Guidance Cut, But Shares Have Already Begun Pulling Back (14 Apr 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/b9edcfc2-379b-11f1-890c-084a1480e771?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

FUJIFILM Holdings: 4Q Results Focus Points: Few Catalysts But Confirmation of Stable Earnings Likely (14 Apr 2026) <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/94a2375a-3794-11f1-890c-084a1480e771?t=1782929968:6055:42924:816774ccf5ea&m=1&ch=Email Safety Catch#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 

 

 

Happy to discuss more and connect you with Yamazaki san.

 

Amir Amerian   
Morgan Stanley | Institutional Equity Division   
1585 Broadway, 5th Floor | New York, NY  10036   
Office: +1-212-761-7684

Cell: +1-347-491-1938
Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com <mailto:Amir.Amerian@morganstanley.com>  

Pronouns: He, Him, His

 

 

 <https://linkback.morganstanley.com/web/sendlink/webapp/f/f5jdaphg-3sbl-g00g-959a-35c9fdc3cb84?store=0&d=UwBCbGFzdG1haWwAYm1fdHJhY2tpbmdfaW1n&user=ib_1_1cnmd033imqh8_YmNkNmYyYTFfZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==&__gda__=1877537973_898f55e8cf749e4098a809a831831a2b>

emails.subject: Vital Knowledge: Is SpaceX going to sell a phone?
emails.body:
<https://vitalknowledge.net/assets/images/resources//section1.png> 	
 <https://vitalknowledge.net/assets/images/resources//vertical_divider.png> INTRADAY COMMENTARY 	
Jul 01, 2026 2:09:46 PM EST 	
Is SpaceX going to sell a phone? 	

*	SPCX (SpaceX) has developed a handset-like device prototype and shown it off to some investors according to a WSJ article <https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/spacex-showed-investors-prototype-of-elon-musks-new-ai-device-b445c57b?mod=newsviewer_click&adobe_mc=MCMID%3D26622675878327304541006841196593856970%7CMCORGID%3DCB68E4BA55144CAA0A4C98A5%2540AdobeOrg%7CTS%3D1782929193&_gl=1*ldlqm4*_gcl_au*NTQ3MzM3MDE0LjE3Nzk1MDUzMTg.*_ga*MTU4ODI3OTQ5Ni4xNzc5NTA1MzE4*_ga_K2H7B9JRSS*czE3ODI5Mjg1ODEkbzk3JGcxJHQxNzgyOTI5MTkxJGo2MCRsMSRoODIxMTc1OTQy>  published at 1:55pmET Wed afternoon. The device, which runs on a proprietary OS and Qualcomm chip, aims to вҖңreshape how humans interact with AIвҖқ. 
*	What does this mean? Telecom stocks have been hit hard lately amid concerns that SpaceX plans to become a larger force in the consumer mobile market, and this WSJ report about a device prototype may only add to those fears. However, SpaceX has a long way to go before successfully manufacturing a consumer device at scale and competing against the leading platforms (incidentally, the same can be said about OpenAI, which is why investors should be very skeptical whenever they read about that companyвҖҷs consumer hardware initiatives). Musk-led companies are given a massive benefit of the doubt when it comes to product promises (which translates into enormous valuation premiums at SPCX and TSLA based on products that are more ideas than reality), but itвҖҷs hard to imagine SpaceX becoming a force in consumer electronics (and on the telecom services front, Starlink is likely to remain more a complimentary service to AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile as opposed to a replacement for the foreseeable future).

HBC stacking DRAM on the compute die increases memory bandwidth and skips CoWoS. The packaging problem does not go away, it shifts to 3D.

Vikram Sekar <https://substack.com/@vikramskr> 
Jul 1
	вҲҷ
	Preview
 <https://substack.com/@vikramskr> 	
 	
 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2065897&post_id=204472898&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjozMDI5NTE0NDIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwNDQ3Mjg5OCwicmVhY3Rpb24iOiLinaQiLCJpYXQiOjE3ODI5MjY5MjcsImV4cCI6MTc4NTUxODkyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNjU4OTciLCJzdWIiOiJyZWFjdGlvbiJ9.4q4P9SHveNJvd2OJEYOL1zA8uCPT-5OoteNqopsz7z8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=50datu> 	
	 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2065897&post_id=204472898&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjozMDI5NTE0NDIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwNDQ3Mjg5OCwiaWF0IjoxNzgyOTI2OTI3LCJleHAiOjE3ODU1MTg5MjcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0yMDY1ODk3Iiwic3ViIjoicG9zdC1yZWFjdGlvbiJ9.Qje237jpMDJ6bmIKALJgXIIOy7rbHpcJt-wGafdjy6c&r=50datu&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> 	
	 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2065897&post_id=204472898&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=50datu&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjozMDI5NTE0NDIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwNDQ3Mjg5OCwiaWF0IjoxNzgyOTI2OTI3LCJleHAiOjE3ODU1MTg5MjcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0yMDY1ODk3Iiwic3ViIjoicG9zdC1yZWFjdGlvbiJ9.Qje237jpMDJ6bmIKALJgXIIOy7rbHpcJt-wGafdjy6c> 	
	 <https://substack..9bzIN1YsX5hBsdSdOweooK_JSpqyPsk6qnIKZspO54k?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> 	
<https://open.substack.com/pub/viksnewsletter/p/qualcomms-high-bandwidth-compute?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> 
READ IN APP <https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ET-_!,w_36,c_scale,f_png,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack.com%2Ficon%2FLucideArrowUpRight%3Fv%3D4%26height%3D36%26fill%3Dnone%26stroke%3D%2523808080%26strokeWidth%3D2> 
 	

At its 2026 Investor Day, Qualcomm introduced High Bandwidth Compute (HBC), which is DRAM stacked on top of a logic die with a dense interface that gives high bandwidth between the two. It lays the foundation for QualcommвҖҷs route into the data center using a memory architecture that does not use the traditional HBM+CoWoS approach.

HBC is a platform technology, the foundation Qualcomm means to run through every business unit and not only the data center, and the whole thing rests on one move in packaging: it does not remove the hard part, it shifts it from the advanced substrate bracket (CoWoS) to a stacked-die approach that brings its own complexities.

Contents:

*	HBC as the Unit of AI Intelligence

*	Memory-on-Logic Packaging as a Key Risk Factor

*	Logic Die Function in HBM versus HBC

*	The Power Budget Under the Stack

*	Who Builds the HBC Stack

*	What to Watch For

Disclaimer: I am an ex-Qualcomm employee but hold no MNPI regarding their line of datacenter products. I worked as an RF engineer dealing with transistor devices вҖ“ far from the sexy world of AI. The facts in this article are from public sources, and include my own inferences. I currently do not hold any QCOM stock. Not financial advice. Do your own research.

________________________________

Also see the full ethics statement <https://substack.com/redirect/56c6e2a6-380b-439d-bc12-90aace234c4e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> .

If you would like to engage boutique research services for your project, reach out to us.

Contact SemiExponent <https://substack.com/redirect/a24f6d96-7b1a-4913-aa04-c319778822fc?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 

________________________________


HBC as the Unit of AI Intelligence


The figure shows a standard packaging substrate with an System-on-Chip (SoC) and an low-power DDR (LPDDR) memory stack sitting on top of an XPU. At first glance it looks like an XPU next to HBM, but it is not that. The DRAM is stacked on the XPU, the die that runs the matrix multiplications, and the SoC beside it is a separate chip whose identity changes with the end market.

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/0bb42fc2-e477-4e7b-b30f-e1f9788a6de9?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 		

That second chip is the part Qualcomm has always built. It has been a modem, an automotive ADAS processor, an IoT SOC, and even a robotics chip. The XPU with the DRAM on top is the new part that provides Qualcomm with an AI accelerator. The main idea behind HBC for Qualcomm is for it to serve as the unit of intelligence, and pairing it with one of QualcommвҖҷs business-unit SoCs is what makes that line AI-enabled. As such, its applicability and importance extends to its entire business.


Memory-on-Logic Packaging as a Key Risk Factor


QualcommвҖҷs claim is that HBC removes the need for advanced packaging, whose supply chains are hard to get hold of right now, and that HBC therefore runs on standard packaging substrates, which are cheaper, more available, and easier to integrate on. HBC does avoid the 2.5D interposer that an XPU-plus-HBM design needs, and the gains it buys are real.

Stacking the memory on the logic die swaps the HBM path, a high-speed interface running along the die edge and out through an interposer, for short vertical links through the die area. That lifts the connection count from about 2,048 (in HBM4) along the edge to the order of 10,000-100,000 through the area, and it brings down the energy needed to move bits by an order of magnitude.

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/2cec4afc-5be4-4779-8bd4-099f4e0fe90f?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 		

Qualcomm however glossed over the fact that packaging difficulty has not gone away. It moves from the advanced substrate (CoWoS) to 3D die-to-die stacking, which is also advanced packaging, and on a compute die, it is the harder version of it. Qualcomm is not removing the packaging step but instead trading a supply-constrained one for a harder and less proven one.

Stacking DRAM on logic is not new in itself. HBM already does it via a core logic die sitting under a stack of DRAM connected through silicon vias. HBC looks similar, but the logic die does a different job, and that difference is the whole risk...

emails.subject: MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Right To Hold Off On Hikes For Now - Keen
emails.body:
<https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakb0I6aJhck8gHg~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJD1ptJe2eJOIQ> 	 <https://emedia.marketnews.com/marketnewsintl/mni-pr-01-25.png?t=1> 	


MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Right To Hold Off On Hikes For Now - Keen

By Pedro Nicolaci da Costa
Jul 1, 2026


WASHINGTON - The Federal Reserve should wait to see how much inflation comes down in the next few months following an easing of Middle East tensions that have brought oil prices sharply lower, Benjamin Keen, a former consultant and visiting scholar at several regional Fed banks, told MNI. 

вҖңA lot of the inflation was being driven by oil prices, and weвҖҷre starting to see oil prices go down. I would expect in the coming months for that to show up in the data,вҖқ he said.  

вҖңThe Fed still wants to get the economy back to its long run 2% inflation target,вҖқ said Keen, adding that whether rate hikes will be needed to achieve that outcome has вҖңyet to be determined.вҖқ

Fed officials are right to be worried about above-target inflation, and Chair WarshвҖҷs focus on price stability is understandable given economic conditions and his priors as a Fed governor during the 2008 financial crisis, said Keen. (See MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Can't Ignore Mounting Prices Pressures-Liang <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakb0I6aJhck8gHw~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJD1ptJe2eJOIQ> ) 

NO RUSH 

Still, the direction of price pressures is not uncertain, and Warsh and the FOMC are probably right to take their time before rushing into any tightening move. 

вҖңBased on what theyвҖҷre seeing in the data, that seems to be a reasonable course of action,вҖқ said Keen, an associate professor at the University of Oklahoma. 

Like Warsh, Keen is optimistic about the prospect that the AI boom could unleash a new wave of productivity that also helps keep a lid on prices. 

вҖңWe could raise the natural rate from productivity build out, but as AI comes along and lowers per unit production cost, those will eventually be passed on to the consumer in terms of lower prices or lower growth in prices, which means what productivity shocks are disinflationary,вҖқ he said.

At the same time, the labor market is showing little hint of the kind of weakness that would require more accommodative policies from the Fed, said Keen.

вҖңThe macro data does not show the labor market needs support.  We do have a segment of the labor market, recent college graduates, that we have a problem with. But overall the macro environment looks good. ThatвҖҷs the FedвҖҷs job, to worry about the overall macro. The Fed doesnвҖҷt need to be in the business of targeting unemployment rates for groups,вҖқ said Keen.

CONSENSUS

Warsh is trying to build consensus not just on the path of interest rates but also on his stated push for reform in areas like communications and the balance sheet, Keen said. 

The chair is right to shun forward guidance in a highly uncertain environment, he added. 

вҖңItвҖҷs the assessment of the chair that forward guidance is tying our hands to some degree,вҖқ he said. вҖңThereвҖҷs a benefit to doing it when we are at the zero lower bound, but right now it comes with a greater cost because if the market is expecting one thing and theyвҖҷre doing something different because new information has come in, then itвҖҷs going to catch the market by surprise.вҖқ

The Fed is still likely to want to avoid major market surprises, but the shift in communications approach could throw some curveballs.  

вҖңWe've gotten used to the current paradigm for a while, and now what he's suggesting is let's change that paradigm, and let's go back to, we walk into every meeting, we have a discussion on the facts on the ground, and the data that we're seeing,вҖқ Keen said. 

For more information, please contact:
sales@marketnews.com <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakb0I6aJhck8jTA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJD1ptJe2eJOIQ>

July 01, 2026

Quick summary, investorsвҖҰ

*	Are watching tomorrowвҖҷs Payrolls release with the same intensity as normal
*	Think the market reaction will be risk-on 

	*	52% risk-on
	*	17% mixed/negligible
	*	31% risk-off

*	ExpectвҖҰ 

	*	Payrolls at 133k (BBG consensus 115k, 113k at time of survey)
	*	AHE in-line at 0.3%
	*	Urate lower at 4.2%, BBG consensus 4.3% 

*	Are split on whether stronger data is risk-on or risk-off

PAYROLLS: 52% of investors believe that tomorrowвҖҷs data will be risk-on. 31% believe risk-off and 17% believe mixed/negligible. 40% of investors believe OTHER people think the data will be mixed/ negligible. Risk-on is the surprisingly popular answer since its prediction exceeds its popularity, and it therefore may carry more informational weight. 



Risk-on gained traction this month (52% versus 25% last month). 



The ranges for risk-on and risk-off payrolls estimates are much wider than last month, underscoring how uncertain investors are about the marketвҖҷs reaction function. Investors are split on whether stronger or weaker prints should be treated as risk-on or risk-off.



The same trend holds true for the unemployment rate. 



Labor market data is being watched the same as normal for the sixth month in a row with a tail towards more than normal. 



Source: 22V Research

InvestorsвҖҷ focus remains on Payrolls, but AHE is increasing in importance. 



DATA ESTIMATES: Our survey consensus for Payrolls is 133k, above BloombergвҖҷs 115k (113k at time of survey). 60% expect above sell-side consensus. 



Survey consensus for AHE is in-line at 0.3%. 



Survey consensus for the urate is 4.2%, lower than BloombergвҖҷs 4.3%. However, there is a tail to a higher number. 



Investors think that a Urate of 4.0% implies inflationary pressure. This is higher than last monthвҖҷs 3.9%. There is more of an even skew between higher and lower Urate versus previous months.

emails.subject: MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Will Be On Hold As Oil Shock Unwinds- Levy
emails.body:
<https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakesI6aJhck8kHg~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJCjptJe2eJOIQ> 	 <https://emedia.marketnews.com/marketnewsintl/mni-pr-01-25.png?t=1> 	


MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Will Be On Hold As Oil Shock Unwinds- Levy 

By Evan Ryser
Jul 1, 2026


The Federal Reserve is likely to keep interest rates on hold as the oil shock from the Middle East conflict unwinds and a temporary drop in prices raises the real fed funds rate, Mickey Levy, an outside advisor to several Fed Banks, told MNI.

"I doubt if the Fed would vote to raise rates in an environment in which the general price level is declining," said Levy, a member of the Shadow Open Market Committee, a group of private academic economists that acts as the FedвҖҷs outside watchdogs. 

"The Fed's not going to raise rates when the when the CPI or PCE price index are declining," Levy said. Financial markets are pricing in only about a 30% chance вҖӢof a rate hike at the central bank's July 28-29 meeting. (See: MNI INTERVIEW: FedвҖҷs Next Rate Move Most Likely Upward-Kohn <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakesI6aJhck8kHw~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJCjptJe2eJOIQ> ) 

Levy said this FOMC is on hold waiting for more data with the hope that core inflation recedes a bit, rather than having "its finger on the trigger to hike rates." 

UNWIND

The dramatic spike in oil prices generated three months of outsized increases in CPI and PCE inflation that pushed up their year-over-year measures. Oil prices have fallen sharply, and if current prices stick close to current levels, which is uncertain as inventories need rebuilding, continued rapid price declines in gasoline and other energy may result in months of decreases in the CPI and PCE price index.

"I think June's going to be the transition month in terms of the monthly inflation data, but in July and August you could have outright declines in the CPI and maybe the PCE," he said. "I can't remember when we've had such a period with oil where there's been a surge like we had on the way up and then a collapse on the downside. It's just striking."

This would ease some price pressures on consumers and let the Fed breathe more easily. The consumer in the last three months has been resilient, Levy said. "Consumption has been rising in real terms each month and you've got a nice bounce in employment." (See: MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Hike Plausible As Early As July - George <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakesI6aJhck8kHA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJCjptJe2eJOIQ> ) 

Levy, a visiting fellow at the Hoover Institution, also noted that retail gasoline prices have fallen rapidly and the labor market is at maximum employment. 

In its updated June Summary of Economic Projections, the FOMC members estimated that a rate hike may be appropriate to be consistent with its dual mandate goals. "But the FOMC member dots are not binding, and subject to changing conditions," Levy said. 

DELIBERATE

Levy has known Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh, a former Hoover visiting fellow, for many years. He congratulated the move to establish five task forces, but Levy also suggested the task forces won't by themselves preclude any potential interest rate adjustments. 
 
"I like the idea. Having outside advisors is wise," he said. "In its deliberations and conduct of monetary policy, the Fed has to deliberate well before the task forces come together with some concrete recommendations," Levy added. 
For more information, please contact:
sales@marketnews.com <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakesI6aJhck8nTA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJCjptJe2eJOIQ>  
www.mnimarkets.com <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXakesI6aJhck8kGg~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CCJCjptJe2eJOIQ>

emails.subject: North America Internet: Meta Compute Unlocks Capacity (and FCF) for Meta's Superintelligence Ambitions
emails.body:
With the potential launch of Meta Compute, per Bloomberg, Meta appears to be planning to offer access to its AI compute and infrastructure, including the potential to offer multiple models, similar to AWSвҖҷ Bedrock. CEO Zuckerberg suggested this could be an option at MetaвҖҷs annual shareholder meeting in May and given recent capacity partnerships valued at ~$50B/GW, Meta Compute could significantly shore up its FCF while funding its capacity builds (Prometheus and Hyperion DCs) and newer products under MSL. And to this, we continue to expect Meta to launch multiple new AI products by year-end and has the infrastructure capacity to do so, as Meta Compute becomes a new business unit. In this note, we include thoughts from CitiвҖҷs Software (Tyler Radke) and Telecom (Michael Rollins) Analysts, whereby they suggest AI demand continues to exceed supply despite overcapacity concerns. We reiterate our Buy rating and $850 TP on META. 
 	
Viewpoint | Citi Research 	
North America Internet 
Meta Compute Unlocks Capacity (and FCF) for Meta's Superintelligence Ambitions 
CITI'S TAKE 

With the potential launch of Meta Compute, per Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/meta-is-building-a-cloud-business-to-sell-excess-ai-compute> , Meta appears to be planning to offer access to its AI compute and infrastructure, including the potential to offer multiple models, similar to AWSвҖҷ Bedrock. CEO Zuckerberg suggested this could be an option at MetaвҖҷs annual shareholder meeting in May and given recent capacity partnerships valued at ~$50B/GW, Meta Compute could significantly shore up its FCF while funding its capacity builds (Prometheus and Hyperion DCs) and newer products under MSL. And to this, we continue to expect Meta to launch multiple new AI products by year-end and has the infrastructure capacity to do so, as Meta Compute becomes a new business unit. In this note, we include thoughts from CitiвҖҷs Software (Tyler Radke) and Telecom (Michael Rollins) Analysts, whereby they suggest AI demand continues to exceed supply despite overcapacity concerns. We reiterate our Buy rating and $850 TP on META.

Read Full Report <https://www.citivelocity.> 	

*	More Details on Meta Compute: Selling Model Access & Compute Capacity: Per Bloomberg, Meta is potentially establishing Meta Compute to monetize its AI infrastructure with plans to sell both model access (including Muse Spark and potentially 3P models) as well as excess GPU compute capacity. The article suggests Meta Compute would be run via MetaвҖҷs MSL and Infrastructure teams and reporting to Meta President, Dina Powell McCormick. CEO Zuckerberg has said that Meta would consider selling excess compute or launching API-based services if Meta builds out more capacity than it needs internally (per MetaвҖҷs shareholder meeting <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/27/mark-zuckerberg-says-meta-starting-cloud-business-on-the-table.html>  in May вҖҷ26 [CNBC, 5/27])вҖ”either for core advertising or its Personal Superintelligence product roadmapвҖ”and while we believe MetaвҖҷs internal demand for compute remain high and its project roadmap robust, we view Meta Compute as a newer business unit that can fund MetaвҖҷs broader AI ambitions (vs. MetaвҖҷs more traditional product launch approach of build, launch, grow awareness/adoption, and monetizeвҖҰ).

*	MetaвҖҷs Capacity is Likely Still Constrained Today, but Capacity is Expanding: With Meta Compute established in Jan. вҖҷ26 to develop 10s of GWs by 2030 and hundreds of GWs LT, Meta is building compute capacity in excess of current internal operational needs. As part of this roadmap, Meta is building out Prometheus (1GW+ cluster coming online in вҖҷ26) and Hyperion (5GW+ by вҖҳ30, initial deployments in вҖҷ28) data centers, an MTIA (Meta Training & Inference Accelerator) family of chips (MTIA 300, 400, 450, and 500), as well as $100B+ in spend towards compute partnerships over the next 7+ years (Figure 1, including Google Cloud, CoreWeave, Nvidia, AMD, among others). Bigger picture, while Meta is likely still capacity constrained today, given reports of Meta encouraging employees to be more efficient with tokens, we believe MetaвҖҷs capacity is ramping significantly through Prometheus and newer partnerships.

*	Monetizing MetaвҖҷs Compute to Drive FCF Returns Long-term: Underpinning plans to monetize its AI infrastructure, we believe Meta is making a strategic decision to recognize NT revenue & FCF as it builds out MSL and its goal of reaching Personal Superintelligence. We currently project вҖҷ26E and вҖҷ27E Capex of $139B and $171B and FCF to be $14.6B and $8.5B, respectively. (ContinuedвҖҰ)

	
Meta's Infrastructure Roadmap & Partnerships

emails.body:
US Insight Daily Note вҖ“ 7/1

________________________________

This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research. 

For Institutional Investor Use Only.

 

Good morning вҖ“ here are the key items from across the BofA Franchise:

 

META, HYPERSCALERS, AND SEMISвҖҰ Meta is up +10% after headlines that they are building their own Cloud business (here <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=iKYWFmL&sig=QsZ8yR3siIwVmdtsrdYWm9lkZKg&v=2&url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/meta-is-building-a-cloud-business-to-sell-excess-ai-compute> )вҖҰ and the SOX is down almost -5%. Specialist Brian Fenske continues to defend the Semis trade here/now, pointing to the enduring strength of demand for AI compute. On Meta, Bulls point to improving ROIC and potential value in their proprietary model/infrastructure stack. Bears were more concerned on a capacity overbuild and could be a sign of weak internal AI products. Brian and Annie spoke with 20+ clients this morning, and have aggregated all the feedback in their morning note, see it all: HERE <mailto:Fenske,%20Brian%20%3cbrian.fenske@bofa.com%3e;%20Villani,%20Nicholas%20A%20-%20Eqty%20NY%20%3cnicholas.villani@bofa.com%3e;%20Liu,%20Annie%20%3caliu11@bofa.com%3e;%20Ruiz,%20Jason%20%3cjason.ruiz@bofa.com%3e;%20Orlov,%20Daniel%20%3cdaniel.orlov@bofa.com%3e?subject=Pls%20send%20me%20META%20AND%20SEMIS%20FEEBACK> .

 

TRADING IDEA: LAGGARDS LEAD THE WAY IN 2H26вҖҰ Specialist Michael Dick looked at 230 Consumer names with >$1.5b mkt cap and isolated them by the BEST 10% and WORST 10% performers for each 1H26вҖҰ Then, looked at their performance early in each 2HвҖҰ Mike foundвҖҰ Laggards tend to lead and this played out in a big way this time last yearвҖҰ in fact itвҖҷs been 8 STRAIGHT JulyвҖҷs of Consumer YTD Losers outperforming YTD Winners (ex. 2020)вҖҰ so, whatвҖҷs the hit-rate? Around 95%! This dynamic also plays out across the broader S&P 500. Get his CONSUMER SNAPSHOT <mailto:michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20diya.panjabi@bofa.com;%20nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20jason.ruiz@bofa.com?subject=Hi%20Pls%20send%20me%20CONSUMER%202H%20TRENDS>  in that link. 

 

THE NEXT BofA GLOBAL BIG IDEAS LIGHTNING ROUND: JULY 8TH AT 8AM ETвҖҰ things are moving/changing every hour, but the entire BofA Specialist Sales Team plans to speak to major sector themes, shorter-term tactical ideas, things with duration, and International stocksвҖҰ AS USUAL, it will be accompanied by a vibrant Slide Deck. You can sign up in this link: JOIN THE BofA IDEAS LIGHTNING ROUND <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=R4cBYya&sig=LaHmfreRQPGvgYmB8lFFDyiJGFl&v=2&url=https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/QAO/id4a6380.cfm> .

 

SANDISK: RAISING ESTIMATES ON PRICING STRENGTHвҖҰ analyst Wamsi Mohan sees a stronger for longer pricing cycle through mid-2027. He is raising his Fiscal Year 2027 EPS by +24% to $233.07 and Fiscal Year 28 EPS +14.5% to $254.24. For the June quarter, Wamsi is expecting +13% QoQ bit growth and +35% QoQ ASP growth. On the call, the key focus for investors will be the level of long term New Business Model (NBM) contracts signed on the quarter to give longer term visibility on demand sustainability. Recall, itвҖҷs roughly one third of 2027 revenues are in NBMs currently. NIKE: CHOPPY RECOVERYвҖҰ Overall, Q4 was in line with expectations: EPS was lifted by Tariff Refunds, China remains pressured, and Wholesale sell-through remains a key debate. The biggest changes? They reduced their sales guidance (but left EPS unchanged) and confirmed an Investor Day on November 16th/17th. Bulls sayвҖҰ numbers may be bottomingвҖҰ margin recovery and cost discipline could offset weaker revenuesвҖҰ if 2027 estimates stabilize, the stock may have reached trough valuationвҖҰ upside risk could be meaningful. Bears sayвҖҰ DTC trends remain concerning (sequential slowdown despite easier comps)вҖҰ managementвҖҷs product turnaround narrative is losing credibilityвҖҰ concerns around inventoryвҖҰ no clear revenue floorвҖҰ valuation not attractive if earnings remain depressed (views <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=QwGKHHe&sig=2glbujGtIs35aeYZZH0AFyNEfs6&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3DCdwdqD3v354l!Wppcej5zg%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3DtMDvbQ> ). 

 

HIMS & HERS HEALTH: RISK TO вҖңPEPTIDE OPTIONALITYвҖқ THESISвҖҰ after the FDAвҖҷs initial stance calls for a ban across 7 peptides ahead of their Advisory Committee (on July 23rd-24th). Which peptides? Contrary to retail belief, peptides can be used for more than just WEIGHT-LOSS with this particular ban applying to peptides used against Ulcerative Colitis, Wound Healing, Opioid Withdrawal, Insomnia and MORE. OKвҖҰ that said, 1 of the 7 peptides, are primarily used for Obesity. On what basis? The Administration cited key concerns centered on limited clinical evidence, unresolved safety/quality questions, insufficient support for proposed use cases. Implications? WELL, by вҖңbanningвҖқ peptides it removes them from the 503A Bulk List (a required prerequisite for pharmacies to compound them). While no FINAL decision has been made yet, it sets a negative tone (ahead of the meeting) and precedent for the rest of to-be-reviewed peptidesвҖҰ [potentially] harming the Long-Term вҖңpivot-into-peptideвҖқ thesis (details <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=uMKYAba&sig=W4d9kmC19a6CvAr4UaOOtKY6ty4&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3D3uijckBr-bAl!Wppcej5zg%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3DzJhhJw> ). THAT SAID, the Near-Term thesis seems to be intact after an sales acceleration in early-June according to (Third-Party Data <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=FKqyfaN&sig=ABPGLqZ10SGc3Dn5HVwZoLN1iYP&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3D3DQzsSAUDNgl!Wppcej5zg%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3DtVx3Ig> ).

AIRLINES Q2 EARNINGS PREVIEW: FAVORABLE TRENDS WHILE WATCHING FOR CAPACITY GROWTHвҖҰ analyst Andrew Didora RAISES earnings estimates and price targets across his Airlines coverage, calling it вҖңthe perfect environmentвҖқ. ItвҖҷs a simple storyвҖҰ demand is strong and helped by significantly lower fuel prices, as industry pricing has remained firm following Spring fare increases, and booking trends suggest a greater share of Q3 demand remains exposed to higher fares. Combined with relatively modest capacity growth through the summer, Andrew believes these dynamics support continued unit revenue strength into Q3. ButвҖҰ watch as capacity growth accelerates into Q4, which would likely moderate unit revenue strength later in the year. See the full take on every US Airline in the PREVIEW <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=xj4G-OT&sig=EhSeh1symZHtyJ9KHDda4MsqiwL&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3DsfuE6-8ayjcs0iMj9GhimQ%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3DfMVDLg> . 

 

NON-AUTO OPPORTUNITIES FOR AUTOMAKERS AND SUPPLIERSвҖҰ these companies are leveraging their industrial footprint and scale to expand into faster-growing, higher-margin end markets. Analyst Alex Perry sees Power Gen and Energy Storage (especially Data-Center) as the most tangible near-term opportunities, given that Automakers/Suppliers can scale quickly in a market where speed-to-power is the number one purchasing criteria. AIR LIQUIDE: FROM ENERGY TRANSITION TO AI GROWTH STORYвҖҰ Analyst John Campbell double upgrades Air Liquide to Buy with a вӮ¬200 price target (15% upside) on a clear pivot to an AI-led growth narrative. Air LiquideвҖҷs Electronics division (#1 supplier of gas used in semiconductor manufacturing) is becoming increasingly material, with AI-driven semiconductor capex now accounting for 40% of backlog despite only 10% of current sales. This supports a step-up in growth visibility, with Electronics forecasted to grow 14% annually (2x consensus) and driving a disproportionate share of group growth and margins. The contractual, multi-year nature of fab projects further strengthens earnings quality and should support a re-rating (Air Liquide <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=NTdOC43&sig=FCsYviv2sooqVfRcRIhvTSIRnDO&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3DPwtdX1QIRTC0Euvfy9DIuw%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3D3ZBNjQ> ).

 

EUROPEAN CHEMICALS SHORT IDEAS: HORMUZ REVERSAL EXPOSES STRUCTURAL WEAKNESSвҖҰ The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz removes a key temporary support, with Q2 benefiting from tightness and share recapture now set to reverse into destocking and price pressure. German Chemical companies BASF and Lanxess both face a return to oversupplied markets, with margins likely to retrace as Asian supply normalizes. While expectations remain elevated (BASF) and leverage remains high (Lanxess 5x), neither shows clear self-help levers, leaving earnings exposed to weak end markets and limited pricing power (Chemicals Short Ideas <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036800&ad=1000000003928029&h=H3Btzpw&sig=CmNHbpEK3rlbhdzHOXzFgyEXOG2&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/r?q%3D96h3-GKbTViIpuEl!9FHCg%26e%3Dbofa.insight%2540bofa.com%26h%3DzAl9bQ> ).

emails.subject: Midday Market Intelligence: rise up!
emails.body:
Print PDF <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ2ixWGbK3s9a7F6I7x0hVgWwD8jBA6u42Z6QjuJqZuyDQIFjBbAACwWVQVD4sZnmuQjznBe_kbaUP0Cs1m41gH57u3xHUYuBTPWmhiY-DYEe2iJfPK_oIODitivdXd36nUTmAjPxUai3o7LzRqOLAg5akpiEB9te8bWQnPPYg0RxxtUTVx1O9xkiXn0Q7L1O2LAfGLvvHtgiD8x5CDD6sSqmf3mIiHKTZB82X0PxynlKbTISpGCbJfHVY-UCGvlpbSz7YEsFKyA1KxutzKUm1hwkr3jBuQIGU*%26id=1c1f9435-8878-4bb0-8e02-cb21cca86d61%26utm_medium=pa%26renditionType=pdf/1/0100019f1e76f0d6-0c5fb71c-3350-47b4-96a0-ac96bd35dfe6-000000/JEnAK2BWUB4jOHbHSlCQ3x25poDnFX-jVQy4Q7Rys2E=452>  	 	 | 	 	 Read <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ2ixWGbK3s9a7F6I7x0hVgWwD8jBA6u42Z6QjuJqZuyDQIFjBbAACwWVQVD4sZnmuQjznBe_kbaUP0Cs1m41gH57u3xHUYuBTPWmhiY-DYEe2iJfPK_oIODitivdXd36nUTmAjPxUai3o7LzRqOLAg5akpiEB9te8bWQnPPYg0RxxtUTVx1O9xkiXn0Q7L1O2LAfGLvvHtgiD8x5CDD6sSqmf3mIiHKTZB82X0PxynlKbTISpGCbJfHVY-UCGvlpbSz7YEsFKyA1KxutzKUm1hwkr3jBuQIGU*%26id=1c1f9435-8878-4bb0-8e02-cb21cca86d61%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1e76f0d6-0c5fb71c-3350-47b4-96a0-ac96bd35dfe6-000000/069IlPnOtpec2D4QW7xRxtQadrCunRkOyggDbYqrjr0=452>  	
 	 
	
 	 
Midday Market Intelligence: rise up! 
	
1 July 2026 | 12:11PM EDT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
US stocks are kicking off the second half of the year on the same theme that we saw in 1H26 -- higher highs -- as investors digest today (Wednesday) a slightly weaker than expected ISM Manufacturing Index and a whole lot of Fed speak that is not having much of an impact on rates (10-year yields are little changed at 4.46%).

 	 

and not throwing away my shot 

Our strategists have written extensively about how the rally we have seen this year in stocks has been driven by earnings, not multiple expansion, as a combination of accelerating AI infrastructure investment, tax cuts, and improving business sentiment has helped earnings growth to step up. 1Q S&P500 EPS grew by 17% -- accelerating from the 12% pace we enjoyed in 2025.

And looking forward to 2Q26 results, Ben Snider points out that bottoms-up consensus EPS growth is now expected to come in at 22% -- an acceleration from the acceleration we saw in 1Q26 (see last Friday's Kickstart, "S&P 500 Q2 2026 earnings season preview <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQI-ZEhBxaSQmZcvscB49hyM8upDzRBAOPomsx96Wul6Fp_ON7BMAACwTMGrWBpWiuBlAgbKZFNsjVIrMNAPDGmiRC-Op-LDn2KcxdPpxYHaff5ALez96cxlooYSltJ_Ij42eq6imQK1IFcsQc1u4ELjN1LsYTsgHNnTyTaqAtbyVS_0biWwvQ44QGpX37iy1jQtVdMFnfQa09UwI0upp-hEGfOYTdOVyfHpzw6NfM6icrdB9JZ_Vt4Cs7nsxZPt3CGyhhL-jV6iXfkm3DvhLhZK_t-sE2MlrNY*%26id=a6df8040-3cf2-4b0b-a7d3-a2ea8042108d%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1e76f0d6-0c5fb71c-3350-47b4-96a0-ac96bd35dfe6-000000/zfIBr20KtIgAjjVbVBsM7C3LLrD82EyhYWqEgd_pBZc=452> ." For some investors, such a high bar may prove daunting. But another common theme of the past 3+ year stock market rally has been FOMO (fear of missing out). A lot has been written about how AI is falling short of delivering the enterprise efficiency gains that it has promised. But AI has not fallen short of delivering the AI infrastructure investment that it never promised -- but is nevertheless required to power the large language models being developed around the world. And as long as AI infrastructure investment remains intense, earnings growth may be buoyed alongside it.

A separate theme permeating markets in 2026 has been HALO investing - leaning into the stocks of companies with high asset intensity and low obsolescence (in other words, traditional industrials). And today, we got an update on business sentiment across the US manufacturing complex with a fresh ISM Manufacturing Index (see "USA: ISM Manufacturing Somewhat Below Expectations; S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Revised Down; Construction Spending Increases in May While Benchmark Revisions Raise Spending Level But Lower Recent Growth <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKVe723ZTAfybZfJjRASMF8tMv-QxC0fE1pW_xv2V27J1Y8i5wMAACwzeIghRcSuPxrbaVel319xS6DjHrv_EliwYeZrQG1BI8xTPnzr_KLvpC0ngV90bgT9oVWPIc98xzr_U3WQ4sII9njsJWoaToYl1AWgnH51hplSYXpQ42I13HENyXK9GOcxY2HO5ec_I_RcjTkiRJDyvQ_MgwrGfph8xzOTmy111N7ynOwUxK3FvuPkmtEnJmb4MvvNB8TsHvjmt8mjTiEYuKhoH9c3A8Njwl3nzX32aA*%26id=1677fecc-a8dd-448d-83d2-3946fc5a51fb%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1e76f0d6-0c5fb71c-3350-47b4-96a0-ac96bd35dfe6-000000/pzaOkBGyyj-XIelo3fXzhWFZ4mE46eylsrqCCxktajw=452> "). The Index fell to 53.3 from 54.0 a month ago but remains elevated relative to what we have seen through most of the post-pandemic echo-boom when manufacturing business sentiment has signaled a contraction.

 	 

chart of the day 

Consumer confidence has also improved across the world, particularly in the US writes Megan Peters in "G10 Consumer Dashboard: June 2026: Resilience in Japan <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQI66PDLfMJ8JCjEsTbgtUxMuOk4kxC8yX0ieBq47gm9F85mJ7TjAACw6sxKot3G-cIsp6W5MHxUfdjNzpRh6Zsp5g92Ex7MekFirj52j9z6POM2LzO6Sel_qYG-yCndYk27w9fYLAi3EPv_3ZbOUdX2w5lv0Yd0Jg0qUojobJ_xVa7d5LSLCGRBP1-y66t2g2h9ptvsOLrgGEmOmsvbDHsRQM1v7lqc8ZDElhLqtMx-5FjDIySqEz-golQD8b1XsrhP88BNE4wQiauWOW7l_REfSsX8qUHkHOk*%26id=496a304b-5a23-4fca-a7e8-f9c1972ac649%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1e76f0d6-0c5fb71c-3350-47b4-96a0-ac96bd35dfe6-000000/NuJRVFazEb5mSlqr91Z12VBHaHTZ4aCZt1En2rnjO4o=452> ." Encouragingly, there is still a lot of upside to consumer sentiment which is at or below the 20th percentile of this measure almost everywhere in the world (see chart below).

 	 
Exhibit 1: Consumer Balance Sheets Remain a Source of Strength, but Sentiment Is Weak 	
 	
 	 
Source: Haver Analytics, Goldman Sachs Global Investment Research 	
 	 
 	 

What to watch for 

Focus on June Payrolls on Thursday, as well as weekly jobless claims and May Factory Orders. In micro, GBX reports after the close today. 

 	 

Today's market performance - July 1, 2026, 12:00 PM
