emails.subject: BofA TMT: 7/1 - META, STX-WDC-SNDK Number Raise, CHTR, Bogey Polls, Best Ideas Lightning Round Call
emails.body:
BofA TMT: 7/1 - META, STX-WDC-SNDK Number Raise, CHTR, Bogey Polls, Best Ideas Lightning Round Call 

________________________________

This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research.  

 

MACRO: Late noteвҖҰ.Nasdaq down (0.6%) The META article is the big mover of stocks this AM, weighing on Semis/Compute/Neoclouds. Thoughts and feedback below. Separately Wamsi Mohan is raising FY28 EPS on STX and WDC by 40-50% this AM. Ya know. Just a sleepy Wednesday and a 40% upward revision! I am a DEFNEDER of Semis (shocker) as I believe the real SIGNAL in the META story is about confidence in ENDURING DEMAND for AI compute, not some improbable idea that thereвҖҷs a ton of shadow вҖҳcapacityвҖҷ laying around. That part simply does not pass the smell test for me based on the last 500 mgmt comments, earnings calls, podcasts, research reports, articles IвҖҷve read. But this market is humbling. 

 

UPCOMING CALLSвҖҰ.

 

SIGN UP FOR THIS!! WED, 7/8, 8AM EST вҖ“ GLOBAL BEST IDEAS LIGHTNING ROUND. Join the BofA Specialist Sales Team for a call to discuss major sector themes, longs, shorts, things with duration, tactical moves, and International stocksвҖҰ As usual, it will be accompanied by a vibrant Slide Deck. 

CLICK HERE TO REGISTER <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=95znuWq&sig=3Il0MCpd2t4BFmDyHYOcdxWIt29&v=2&url=https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/QAO/4a6380.cfm> 

 

THURS, 7/9, 8AM EST вҖ“ ON CEO/CFO Takeaways. Vivek Arya will be hosting a group call to discuss takeaways after hosting ON management in NYC. 

CLICK HERE TO REGISTER <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=6wpaLC-&sig=AKhHHpi3oqbrgsqpdg1ER1gpcNT&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/events?aid%3D12341097%26did%3D23070514> , get Zoom info

 

BOGEY POLL - PLS FILL OUT! WHERE IS THE BUYSIDE ON BIG TECH EPS AND CAPEX? Below is a link to a quick survey weвҖҷre conducting to gather BUYSIDE EXPECTATIONS on upcoming Big Tech Q2 Earnings results and guides. In it weвҖҷre polling for quarterly/annual expectations on GOOGL, META, MSFT, AMZN, AAPL, ORCL, CRWV and NBIS

CLICK HERE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE BIG TECH EPS and CAPEX SURVEY <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=WTMoRP4&sig=ZpzwHlOFlAKmRZDZx3g6nLOS7Tw&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a%3D1000000000036547%26ad%3D1000000003860887%26h%3D3RVIccv%26sig%3DDJfxS0fomZhzot5ahnL8PWpyZ7z%26v%3D2%26url%3Dhttps:**Abankofamerica.co1.qualtrics.com*jfe*form*SV_02rkSQkmSFovjim__;Ly8vLy8!!I2XIyG2ANlwasLbx!TtE0wPfqUd5lQEdkj3nsnvAD7UEF8mUf-0lAZcQSL2OUzgbOXv2zSScTeO8nbpu3IN4P1rpS5BlbjpCB-4J9jA$> 

 

THE META NEWS & INVESTOR FEEDBACK ON META, SEMIS, NEOCLOUDS. META now up 11% on the THIS ARTICLE <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=v-2WlKo&sig=3kb90w04p7UUBjafj4XhXnTF7UK&v=2&url=https://blinks.bloomberg.com/news/stories/THHY59KJH6V4> , and SEMIS down (-4%). Market reaction in general reads like 1) Good for META, cheap/beaten up, was not pricing in AI вҖҳsuccessвҖҷ anymore, and direct ROI from its capex is bullish for them, plans to вҖңsell access to AI computing power and modelsвҖқ. 2) Market reacting negative to notion that there is вҖңexcessвҖқ or вҖңsurplusвҖқ of compute given how virtually all other ecosystem participants have said the exact opposite.

If META is selling access to вҖңitвҖҷs modelsвҖқ thatвҖҷs not ecosystem negative at all. I would actually suspect their models crowd out this brewing вҖңopen sourceвҖқ mkt share,  not crowd out OpenAI and Anthropic and therefore we might have BETTER visiblity moving forward into they broader ecosystem. 


(Interestingly, just yesterday the FT reported that GOOGL had put caps on METAвҖҷs use of Gemini AI from Google вҖңafter the social media giant sought more computing capacity than the rival tech group could provide, in the latest evidence of the infrastructure constraintsвҖҰ.вҖқ)

 

MY 2 CENTS. One view I have is that while the mkt is fixating on whether METAвҖҷs вҖңplanвҖқ shows there was shadow supply of compute laying around and is triggered by the words вҖңexcessвҖқ & вҖңsurplusвҖқ (improbable based like, every data point/comment from other ecosystem partners, public companies, mgmt. teams, etc)..that the real SIGNAL HERE is that Zuck would likely only do this if he truly believed there was such massive, enduring demand for AI compute that being 18 years late to the game (relative to say, AWS), and facing a highly competitive, well-funded fieldвҖҰwould STILL leave META with an attractive ROIC business. Anyway. Big topic. Lots of diff angles. 

WHAT DO YOU THINK? EMAIL ME <mailto:brian.fenske@bofa.com;%20aliu11@bofa.com?subject=Hey%20-%20my%20thougts%20on%20the%20META%20article%20and%20semis%20today....> . 

 

In my IB chats I asked investors for feedback and received a lot of great points/views. HereвҖҷs a MSFT copilot (using ChatGPT 5.5) summarization of what we heardвҖҰ 

These comments are NOT my personal views and certainly not research, just conveying summary of what I heard from 20+ investors this AMвҖҰ.

BULLISH POINTS WE HEARD FROM INVESTORS

*        Leasing compute could reduce the вҖңwhere is the ROIC?вҖқ debate by creating a monetization path for unused or underutilized capacity.

*        Bulls see this as evidence that compute demand remains extremely tight and valuable globally.

*        Short-term, above-market compute leases could help self-fund METAвҖҷs AI infrastructure buildout while its internal AI products/models continue to mature.

*        Some investors argued this reduces the risk that META needs to cut capex, and instead supports continued AI investment.

*        Incremental revenue/EBIT from compute leasing is likely not in current models, so even modest contribution could be positive to FCF.

*        Several investors emphasized this is not just raw capacity monetization вҖ” it could reflect the value of METAвҖҷs proprietary model/infrastructure stack and create an AI-lab-like revenue stream.

*        Some viewed the market reaction in NVDA/infrastructure names as overdone, arguing this validates compute scarcity rather than undermines demand.

 

BEARISH POINTS WE HEARD FROM INVESTORS

*        The main bear read is that META may have overbuilt capacity faster than internal AI demand could absorb it.

*        If internal AI products are not generating enough return, leasing compute could reinforce concerns around AI ROI, D&A burden, and capital intensity.

*        Building a cloud/neocloud-like business is not easy and could require more spending on software, orchestration, storage, support, and sales.

*        Some investors questioned whether customers will pay premium pricing for compute tied to Llama/META infrastructure versus more standardized hyperscaler or frontier-lab environments.

*        There was also concern this could increase, not reduce, METAвҖҷs future capital needs if it decides to support external compute customers at scale.

*        One more cautious read: this may be an early sign META is trying to appease investors on cost discipline, meaning the next capex raise could be smaller than feared.



OTHER KEY POINTS / COMMENTS

*        Several investors thought the market overreacted to the word вҖңexcess.

*        Zuckerberg had previously described leasing compute as an option if META overbuilt, so some investors viewed the report as less surprising and not necessarily a strategic reversal.

*        Others argued META recently raised capex and has continued discussing its model roadmap and supplier LTAs, which is inconsistent with the idea that it is abandoning the AI race.

 

WAMSI RAISING ESTIMATES ON SNDK AND HDDs. See below for the key takeaways. 

 

SNDK вҖ“ raising FY27 numbers on better than expected pricing <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=gMuyHwf&sig=525GoxdYvPMuzvFqqPHt8GIEsR6&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DAJQPjmRMTB0hu1B374SdnQ%26e%3Danthony.cassamassino2%2540bofa.com%26h%3D89IVgA> , stronger for longer through mid-2027. Wamsi is modeling +13% QoQ bit growth and +35% QoQ ASP for the June qtr. Focus will also be on the percent of annual rev under NBMs. 

*	Raising FY27 EPS by +24% to $233.07
*	Raising FY28 EPS by +14.5% to $254.24
*	On the qtr, expect Q4 Revs $9.1B (vs Street $8.35B) and EPS $37.01 (vs Street $34.26)
*	Expect management to guide Q1 Revs to $11.25-11.75B, GM of 84-86%, and EPS to $47-50
*	PO raised to $2500 on 10x CY27 P/E

 

STX вҖ“ seeing pricing increases, with revenue and profitability to increase sequentially <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=1A7nPz3&sig=EEvBxmOUXy4M1B8w5Z3WFnStoJR&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DktCcCbqzDGEhu1B374SdnQ%26e%3Danthony.cassamassino2%2540bofa.com%26h%3DFDRe9w>  every quarter in FY27. HDD industry is being more disciplined about adding EB capacity without adding unit capacity. Wamsi sees the majority of STX's nearline HDD volume as likely under build-to-order contracts. As these come up for renewal, pricing can increase in a measured and sustainable way. This is also the last quarter of favorable HAMR pricing given to their first hyperscaler customer. 

*	Raising FY27 EPS by +9.5% to $26.88
*	Raising FY28 EPS by +42% to $50.75
*	On the qtr, expect Q4 Revs $3.51B (vs Street $3.49B) and EPS $5.12 (vs Street $5.11)
*	Expect management to guide Q1 Revs to $3.7B, GM to 51-53% and EPS to $5.75 at midpoint. 
*	PO raised to $1150 on 19x CY28 P/E

 

WDC вҖ“ Wamsi sees further upside to $/TB <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=ea7Og-j&sig=1k9LMYUuZtK7unz5EpATJVcNAJB&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DjOLeXQaRFMshu1B374SdnQ%26e%3Danthony.cassamassino2%2540bofa.com%26h%3DW5Rydg>  to grow LDD YoY. Additionally, there can be upside to managementвҖҷs guidance of 25% EB CAGR on AI demand for storage, deeper customer engagements, and improving multiyear visibility. 

*	Raising FY27 EPS +13.5% to $18.72
*	Raising FY28 EPS +48% to $32.89
*	On the qtr, expect Q4 Revs $3.75B (vs Street $3.71B) and EPS $3.32 (vs Street $3.31)
*	Expect management to guide Q1 Revs to $3.9-4.1B, GM to 53-54% and EPS to $3.80 at midpoint. 
*	PO raised to $732 on 19x CY28 P/E

 

IBM, SNOW, SPOT, V вҖ“ THE Q3 BEST IDEAS LIST. A quarterly list of best ideas from BofA Research (note that this is different from the US 1 List). Here are the ideas <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=Rqnr0sg&sig=IiBZ4YzYPaduucAih7d6K8Xnsws&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DIMFVHuU92jUlmGSxo0xLDA__%26e%3Daliu11%2540bofa.com%26h%3D5BS!Dw> , with a one-pager pitch for each. 

*	IBM вҖ“ Wamsi Mohan expects it to be setup supported by Q2 results with more durable Software results, reset Consulting expectations, and continued momentum in RHT/CFLT. IBMвҖҷs Software portfolio is focused on the Infra layer where there will be more AI adoption-driven demand. 
*	SNOW вҖ“ Koji Ikeda sees potential for continued Product Rev acceleration, AI tailwinds driving faster consumption, Cortex Code product cycle. 
*	SPOT вҖ“ Jessica Reif Ehrlich views the investor day as a clear strategic shift to monetize engagement through pricing and higher value experiences. Launch of the AI-driven music tier will be key catalyst. 
*	V вҖ“ Matt OвҖҷNeill views V as a durable DD compounder, with this FQ3 as the cyclical low for cross-border travel as Middle East disruption peaked. 

 

CHTR: CUTTING BROADBAND ESTIMATES. Jessica Reif Ehrlich views the Q2 setup as challenged on elevated broadband losses <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=lRIsmny&sig=Vdo7nnGMPsZIeV0m7sW3hB8GH1I&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DjcYWyzP-uxfMF6LWpD!XFA%26e%3Danthony.cassamassino2%2540bofa.com%26h%3Dy8ao5A> , ARPU pressure, and continued competitive intensity. Jessica expects residential broadband net losses of 155k, with video trends comparatively better as Charter's seamless entertainment offering continues to support retention (modestly raising residential video net losses to -65k vs -70k prior). CHTR is likely continuing to explore ways to enhance its mobile value prop through broader connectivity ecosystem. Lowering Q2 rev to $13.27B (-2% YoY vs -1% prior) on lower broadband ARPU of -0.8% and lowering EBITDA to $5.53B on lower high-margin residential revs. 

 

CHART: 2026 CONSENSUS ESTIMATES FOR TOTAL BROADBAND SUBS---FROM 2019 to NOW, REVISIONS



Source: Bloomberg

 

CHART: CHARTERвҖҷs EV/EBITDA SINCE 2012



Source: Bloomberg, ASKB

 

PLS TAKE A FEW MINUTES ON THIS FOR US---BIG TECH EARNINGS & CAPEX BOGEY POLLS. WeвҖҷre doing our Q2 buyside bogey poll on Big Tech earnings, with a special focus on CAPEX expectations looking out the next few years. Please take a few minutes. Below that weвҖҷre also freshening up buyside expectations on key Semi/Memory names. Appreciate your input. Fill in as few/as many as youвҖҷd like. We will share with respondents first. 

 

POLL #1 (MOST IMPORTANT): WHERE IS THE BUYSIDE ON BIG TECH EPS AND CAPEX? Below is a link to a quick survey weвҖҷre conducting to gather BUYSIDE EXPECTATIONS on upcoming Big Tech Q2 Earnings results and guides. In it weвҖҷre polling for quarterly/annual expectations on GOOGL, META, MSFT, AMZN, AAPL, ORCL, CRWV and NBIS

CLICK HERE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE BIG TECH EPS and CAPEX SURVEY <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036794&ad=1000000003924438&h=WTMoRP4&sig=ZpzwHlOFlAKmRZDZx3g6nLOS7Tw&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a%3D1000000000036547%26ad%3D1000000003860887%26h%3D3RVIccv%26sig%3DDJfxS0fomZhzot5ahnL8PWpyZ7z%26v%3D2%26url%3Dhttps:**Abankofamerica.co1.qualtrics.com*jfe*form*SV_02rkSQkmSFovjim__;Ly8vLy8!!I2XIyG2ANlwasLbx!TtE0wPfqUd5lQEdkj3nsnvAD7UEF8mUf-0lAZcQSL2OUzgbOXv2zSScTeO8nbpu3IN4P1rpS5BlbjpCB-4J9jA$>

emails.subject: U.S. Software: Reported Meta Cloud Business Plans Risk Turning Major AI Infra Customer into Competitor
emails.body:
<https://barclaysresearch.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519884&ad=4541297084&h=rFzc11J&sig=4WjtnhaO0XV0JKfqcyiHxnpO1yI&v=2&url=https://live.barcap.com/go/BU> 	Hello, Felipe	
	

	
	
Equity Research	

U.S. Software	

Reported Meta Cloud Business Plans Risk Turning Major AI Infra Customer into Competitor <https://barclaysresearch.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519884&ad=4541297084&h=isCkrGj&sig=VFRWFkzJUPrOQfACXmAMsZcUjYY&v=2&url=https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID%3DFC6a45298d95951c12ce43d92e%26sourceArea%3DEMAIL_L2> 	

Bloomberg reported that Meta is developing plans for a cloud infra business to sell access to AI compute and models. Meta has been a large consumer of AI infra and this pivot, if confirmed, could reduce its demand for large AI deals in the ST and increase competition for the neoclouds/hyperscalers.	

1 July 2026  |  3 MIN READ 	

		
	Read the Report <https://barclaysresearch.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519884&ad=4541297084&h=LHp-t4q&sig=UPPidgLTKYbV49VPno7sOa5WKy7&v=2&url=https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID%3DFC6a45298d95951c12ce43d92e%26sourceArea%3DEMAIL_FULL_REPORT> 		
	
	

	
	
This morning, Bloomberg reported <https://barclaysresearch.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519884&ad=4541297084&h=aYuiUwt&sig=1wRbCb5CB02izBpKJTet888kS6N&v=2&url=https://blinks.bloomberg.com/news/stories/THHZ6MT9NJLX>  that Meta Platforms (META, covered by Ross Sandler) is "developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business that will sell access to AI computing power and models." We note Meta has been a major consumer of AI infrastructure deals, pursuing large selfвҖӢ-вҖӢbuild AI data center projects in addition to signing $20bn+ TCV deals with neoclouds CoreWeave and Nebius Group (NBIS, not covered) and large deals multiвҖӢ-вҖӢyear deals with Oracle, Google Cloud and AWS (GOOG and AMZN, covered by Ross Sandler). While the potential for Meta to pursue a cloud strategy was recently alluded to by the company (see quote below in response to the question, "would you consider building a cloud to compete with AWS, Azure, etc.вҖӢ?вҖӢ"), we see the news weighing on the neocloud names and ...

emails.subject: UBS TMI: META Madness - What to make of it?
emails.body:
<https://analytics.ubs.com/b/ss/ubsextern-cca-prod/4/REDIR/?v5=Click&v6=SMEP&pe=lnk_o&pev2=link&events=event3&url=https%3A%2F%2Feur01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fneo.ubs.com%252Fr%252F7%252F%253Fid%253Dh6f9f9149%252Cf75fdc89%252C4ba4f723%26data%3D05%257C02%257Csh234045%2540UBSCloud.onmicrosoft.com%257C7a37565e6f644f1150a808ded77dc02b%257Cfb6ea4037cf14905810afe5547e98204%257C0%257C0%257C639185132328578950%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata%3D4F8hD%252BGCvoDZaSO%252FpLjrHPt73UCA5VEwzCkxtny%252BHH0%253D%26reserved%3D0&v2=4698&v3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202528948&v4=false&c3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202528948> 

**UBS TMI: META Madness - What to make of it?

 UBS Sector Sales вҖ“ Sales Commentary for Institutional Investors Only вҖ“ This is not a product of UBS Research 

This communication has been generated by Global Markets and has not been vetted for Global Wealth Management end client distribution. You are responsible for making sure the communication complies with regulations and internal policies before sending to a Global Wealth Management client

  <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h76cbc981,ffb51c52,1> 

Another wild morning in a week that SHOULD have been nothing but BBQs and some much-needed rest into another long earnings seasonвҖҰMETA +9% with headlines hitting that the company apparently building out a cloud business to sell excess cloud computeвҖҰhmmвҖҰ Okay, so first thing that catches my eye here is the term вҖңexcess capacityвҖқвҖҰ yes, while that isnвҖҷt really the peachiest datapoint when we think through AI demand trends (IMO probably reads MOST negative CRWV/neoclouds), I think the kneejerk read (and the biggest reason why the stock is up +HSD%) is just that this moves shows at least SOME financial constraint from Zuck so capex is NO longer biased to the upside. So now the story can shift to FCF stabilization, a potential new revenue stream, and all of it at what many would argue are near-trough valuation multiples. It also raises an interesting question heading into earnings: if META can post in line 2Q/3Q guide while reiterating FY capex, is that enough to cut it?? ItвҖҷs feeling like itвҖҰ 

  

Now for context, this idea was at least flirted by mgmt. earlier this spring <https://analytics.ubs.com/b/ss/ubsextern-cca-prod/4/REDIR/?v5=Click&v6=SMEP&pe=lnk_o&pev2=link&events=event3&url=https%3A%2F%2Feur01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fwww.cnbc.com%252F2026%252F05%252F27%252Fmark-zuckerberg-says-meta-starting-cloud-business-on-the-table.html%26data%3D05%257C02%257Csh234045%2540UBSCloud.onmicrosoft.com%257C7a37565e6f644f1150a808ded77dc02b%257Cfb6ea4037cf14905810afe5547e98204%257C0%257C0%257C639185132328610190%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata%3DctAZMlzGGj15MJGfNm2XmRP5MKN6KVtuHHTeROPr1Pw%253D%26reserved%3D0&v2=4698&v3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202606446&v4=false&c3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202606446>  so not really a HUGE shock to the system but still an uncomfortable datapoint heading into earnings preview season especially when we think about what it means for the compute/memory bottlenecks and thus pricing trends. So with that in mind, see below for early convos on read throughs. 

  

READ THROUGHS

*	SEMIS TRADE: A lot of debate as to what this move really means for the semis tradeвҖҰjust looking at how SOXX is trading out of the gates this morning, investors are pretty clearly connecting the dots as to what METAвҖҷs move means for father capex increases and thus compute/memory bottlenecks (and thus pricing). Now I get the pushback that even if META theoretically eases, rest of hyperscalers will snap it up that capacity quickly. But at the same time the demand curve still shifts with the duration above trend demand shorter. In other words, so instead of compute shortages through CY29, what if its only CY28??? Nevertheless, IвҖҷm not a semis expert by any means so very open to thoughts here 
*	NEOCLOUDS: A negative read no matter how you cut itвҖҰ no pushback there. 
*	HYPERSCALERS: Feedback more mixed... of course this means weвҖҷll have another competitor on the table but to the extent compute bottlenecks ease, bulls are arguing these players can also benefit from a cost perspective. Cue the second question вҖңwhat does this mean for everyone else's capex plans?вҖқ My initial read from conversations this morning is that the overbuild risk feels much more META-specific than industry-wide just considering how *arguably* behind investors believe they are in their AI effortsвҖҰ I mean just days ago GOOGL cut off META too because they're so constrained <https://analytics.ubs.com/b/ss/ubsextern-cca-prod/4/REDIR/?v5=Click&v6=SMEP&pe=lnk_o&pev2=link&events=event3&url=https%3A%2F%2Feur01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fx.com%252Fnoxflux%252Fstatus%252F2071197823085765006%26data%3D05%257C02%257Csh234045%2540UBSCloud.onmicrosoft.com%257C7a37565e6f644f1150a808ded77dc02b%257Cfb6ea4037cf14905810afe5547e98204%257C0%257C0%257C639185132328637151%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata%3DWgaVtqM7f4SQ%252Fn%252FTJHuiLMna5tMYJvzKOUWP0pJb2S4%253D%26reserved%3D0&v2=4698&v3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202716229&v4=false&c3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202716229> . DoesnвҖҷt read the sameвҖҰ  

  

From: Dwyer, Christina 
Sent: Wednesday, July 1, 2026 7:43 AM
Subject: UBS TMI: 07/01/2026: Happy July, Heightened AI Volatility (Time to Trim?), Ad Check Feedback #7, Wireless Weakness, FOXA, APP, PB Update, AAPL Smartphone Data, Cannes Feedback Calls, Key Events, Access, Headlines & More 

  

 <https://analytics.ubs.com/b/ss/ubsextern-cca-prod/4/REDIR/?v5=Click&v6=SMEP&pe=lnk_o&pev2=link&events=event3&url=https%3A%2F%2Feur01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fneo.ubs.com%252Fr%252F7%252F%253Fid%253Dh6f9f9149%252Cf75fdc89%252C4ba4f723%26data%3D05%257C02%257Csh234045%2540UBSCloud.onmicrosoft.com%257C7a37565e6f644f1150a808ded77dc02b%257Cfb6ea4037cf14905810afe5547e98204%257C0%257C0%257C639185132328668664%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata%3DY1XhzNXpxtMTUfRsRLSRf3tdXwaDOxVSEBTCIZMYkNI%253D%26reserved%3D0&v2=4698&v3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202827093&v4=false&c3=1f17559287716cf09ce0105163a4d1d5~4082~384074202827093> 

**UBS TMI: July 01st 2026

 UBS Sector Sales вҖ“ Sales Commentary for Institutional Investors Only вҖ“ This is not a product of UBS Research 

This communication has been generated by Global Markets and has not been vetted for Global Wealth Management end client distribution. You are responsible for making sure the communication complies with regulations and internal policies before sending to a Global Wealth Management client

  <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h76cbc981,ffb51c52,1> 

New month, new quarterвҖҰnew trends?? Guess weвҖҷll see. IвҖҷve felt like a broken record ever since we came out of 1Q earnings but investor breadth in TMI remains incredibly narrowвҖҰ.and if anything, it feels like it's getting even narrower. Internet stocks just donвҖҷt really react to headlines or thirdвҖ‘party data nearly the way they used to with the market way more focused on terminal value concerns + 2H/2027 IPOs + massive capex commitments from the hyperscalers. Telco appetite ebbs and flows with defensive trade but clearly has taken a breather with competitive concerns and terminal value conversations reaching new highs. Meanwhile media has a whole list of challenges, with choppy consumer trends, lingering oil impact on cruises/parks, the AI debates across music/content and yet againвҖҰ further industry consolidation. Against that backdrop, I feel like sentiment heading into 2Q almost mirrors that of 1Q. As we get closer to reporting season, single-stock dispersion should pick up meaningfully, but outside of AMZN and GOOGL, most ideas feel more like trades than true fundamental investments. Right now, conviction feels scarce, and the market seems much more interested in tactical opportunities than long-duration stories.

  

Similarly, from a positioning standpoint, you havenвҖҷt needed to look that far to see the growing angst around the highly crowded and ever so concentrated AI trade. If AI has felt more volatile, itвҖҷs not just a feeling.  As noted by Aaron Nordvik, realized volatility for the AI 100 {UBXXA100} has diverged markedly from that of the Ex-AI 400 {UBXXX400}, which represents the broader non-AI universe. That said, while he doesnвҖҷt recommend investors to fully get out of the trade, more vol for less upside is at least a reason to take some risk off especially as we could head into some choppy waters into an вҖҳAI news vacuumвҖҷ until 2Q results. Chart below 

  

PB Update: As momentum recovered, HFs selectively rotated back into higher beta segments yesterday, with the strongest buying in Growth (>3std), High Momentum (+1.3std), broad US Tech (+1.8std) and the most AI-exposed US names ({UBXXA100}, +1.9std). However, the broader trend remains one of de-risking. Since early May, HFs have reduced ~6% of US GMV from high-momentum exposures, and yesterdayвҖҷs activity barely offsets this unwind. Resumed shorting of US Software appears to have been short lived. HFs have now covered ~70% of the shorts added in mid-June, including +1.3std of covering yesterday, suggesting limited follow-through.

MICRO: NO mercy for wireless stocks for a second day in a rowвҖҰ. UBS AD CHECK FEEDBACK #7: Stephen Ju wrapped his 2Q Ad Expert Call Series yesterday with his 7th expert who noted e-commerce growth and world cup digital content proliferation should drive positive 2Q26 and 2026 trends. Single stock feedback as follows. Friendly reminder that Stephen and team keep an extensive excel file tracking just how each of these expertsвҖҷ budgets have changed QoQ вҖ“ lmk if youвҖҷd like to receive a copy.

*	2Q26вҖү(Directionally +ve): 2Q26 US &вҖүEurope budgets exceeded prior estimates, coming in at 21%-22% YOYвҖү / 18% YOY (vs prior 19% / 16% respectively). Strength was driven by: 1) growing e-commerce spend, 2) increased spend around the world cup, and 3) resilience in performance spending despite macro conditions and higher inflationary costs. Our expert echoed other feedback that with increased world cup content proliferation into other media (YouTube, social, etc.) vs prior iterations, ad dollars are following accordingly. 
*	2026 (Directionally +ve): 2026 digital growth forecast increased for US /вҖүEurope to 21%-22% YOYвҖү/ 18%-19% YOY vs prior 21% / 17%,вҖүrespectively. US / Europe growth for 3Q26 was unchanged at 23.5% / 17%, respectively. 
*	GOOGLвҖү(Directionally +ve): Search trends tracking ahead, with 2Q26 growth 10% YOY in the U.S. (vs 9.5% prior) and 9% in Europe (vs 8.5%). Our expert noted that paid search continued to perform well. 2026 growth expectations for US/ Europe were hiked to 11% / 9%-9.5% (vs prior 9.5% / 8.5%). YouTube was in line with 2Q26 growth for US / Europe landing at 8% / 7% (both as expected), with our expert noting that stronger competition in CTV was holding back YouTube's share capture. 2026 expectations for YouTube for US / Europe was also unchanged at 8.5% 7.5% respectively. 
*	METAвҖү(Directionally +ve): 2Q26 growth ahead of expectations for both geographies, with US at 10% YOYвҖү(vs 9% expected), and Europe at 9% (vs 8% expected). Budgets were helped by greater world cup linked campaign activations as well as increased digital ad footprint in categories such as retail, packaged goods, luxury and fashion. 2026 was revised higher for the US /вҖүEurope close to 10% / 9%-9.5% vs prior 9% / 8%, respectively. 
*	AMZNвҖү(Directionally +ve): 2Q26 outperformed with growth of 33% / 30% for US / Europe (vs prior 32% / 29%, respectively). 3Q26 growth tracking at 33% / 31%, which means that 2026 growth expectations were revised to 33% / 32% for US / Europe, respectively. Sponsored ads continues to perform well for endemic advertisers, Prime Video is scaling rapidly especially given lower CPMs (vs NFLX)вҖүand broad user base, and our expert's DSP spend is growing due to superior inventory access (with PINS, NFLXвҖүand others now integrated) and lower take rates vs peer TTD. The expert highlighted faster growth vs TTD and DV360, with AMZN DSP benefitting at the cost of TTD. 
*	TTD (Directionally slightly -ve): 2Q26 was mixed with 8% YOY growth in the US (vs prior 9%) and 9% YOY in Europe (vs prior 7%). Our expert noted that GOOGL's DV360 remained the main DSP for most clients, but that AMZN was replacing TTD as the #2 destination. 3Q26 is forecast to be 7% for both US and Europe, with 2026 growth now expected to be 7.5% for US and 6.5%-7% for EUвҖү(vs prior 7%-8% and 6% respectively). 
*	APPвҖү(Directionally +ve): With spend in the U.S. only. 2Q26 growth came in at 34% YOY (vs 30% in 1Q26) and accelerated QOQ. The platformвҖҷs user base provides audience and conversion incrementality with decent ROAS, our expert stating, adding that growth was being driven by both new advertisers moving onto the platform and existing advertisers increasing budgets. 3Q26 expectations were set at 35%, whereas 2026 growth expectations were increased to 35%-36% from 33%-34% earlier. 
*	PINSвҖү(Directionally mixed): Performance was slightly weaker, with 2Q26 growth tracking at 10% / 9% US / Europe (vs 11% / 9.5% expected, respectively). 2Q26 and 3Q26 trends are softer as ad dollars move to wider audience platforms such as TikTok or Instagram to capture world cup related sales, but our expert noted that a potential bounceback of dollars in 4Q26 remains critical for premium activations. 3Q26 growth for US / Europe is expected to be in line with 2Q26 performance, whereas 2026 forecast were increased to 12% / 10% for US / Europe, respectively, from 11.5% / 9.5% earlier. 
*	SNAP (Directionally +ve): 2Q26вҖүgrowth came in at 5% for both US and Europe vs 3%-4% expected, whereas 3Q26 growth was increased to 4.5% / 4% for US / Europe, respectively. That said, Snap's competitive position vs TikTok, Meta remains challenged as pace of innovation notably lags. 2026 expectations now stand at 4.5%.

AAPL SMARTPHONE DATA: David Vogt digs into UBS Evidence Lab latest smartphone data, which surveys over 7,500 smartphone users in the U.S., U.K., China, Germany, and Japan to gauge smartphone demand. The key takeaways is that Apple Intelligence is not driving upgrade activity as total respondents indicating that they would upgrade sooner for Apple Intelligence features declined 500 bps HoH while those noting no impact on their purchasing decision rose +300 bps HoH. Other than that, key takes include a) 12 month global iPhone purchase intent in the US rose b) the average age of an iPhone in the user base down ticked to nearly 23 months c) the total net interest (delta between interested and not interested) in an Apple foldable declined vs the prior survey. <https://open.substack.com/pub/fundaai/p/researchmeta-building-a-neocloud?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> 
READ IN APP <https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ET-_!,w_36,c_scale,f_png,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack.com%2Ficon%2FLucideArrowUpRight%3Fv%3D4%26height%3D36%26fill%3Dnone%26stroke%3D%2523808080%26strokeWidth%3D2> 
 	

Pre-market this morning, news reported that Meta is considering commercializing its surplus AI compute externally, potentially building it into a NeoCloud-style business. The marketвҖҷs first reaction was sharp: Meta rose nearly 6% pre-market, while AI compute and NeoCloud-related names took a hit from the negative narrative. The worry is simple: if Meta also starts selling GPU compute externally, does that directly cause a compute glut?

The reaction is understandable, but we think the market has oversimplified the question.

First, this is not about вҖңAI compute demand peakingвҖқ or вҖңMeta no longer needing to buy compute.вҖқ On the contrary, Meta is still locking in very large amounts of new compute at the same time. Per Bloomberg/Reuters, Meta recently signed a new AI computing capacity agreement with Crusoe, taking roughly 1.6 GW of combined capacity from two Crusoe data centers in Childress, Texas, and Warrenton, Missouri.

Meta is also still buying compute from other NeoClouds. In our 3Q25 META Preview last year, we noted that Meta was seeking to purchase 3 GW of compute capacity from NeoClouds.

Just a week ago, there was also news that GCP was throttling MetaвҖҷs compute resources because MetaвҖҷs consumption had exceeded expectations.

So, on the surface, there does seem to be a contradiction: if Meta already has surplus compute, why keep renting 1.6 GW from Crusoe?

Our read is that this is not a contradiction. It is a generational shift in compute.

Subscribed <https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9mdW5kYWFpLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MjA0NDQ3NjgxLCJzIjozMjgxMDExLCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MzAyOTUxNDQyLCJpYXQiOjE3ODI5MTY2MzYsImV4cCI6MjA5ODQ5MjYzNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.yLOlHISXV81qmliarfBkm-buOncNMsPZ5TwGeHT-LM4?> 

Over the past two years, Meta has bought and deployed large quantities of H100/H200. These GPUs are far from worthless. On the contrary, they remain highly valuable for inference, fine-tuning, enterprise model serving, image/video generation, and traditional ML workloads. But for next-generation frontier model training, especially 3T+ parameter MoE, long context, multimodal, and RL-heavy post-training, the training economics of H100/H200 decline noticeably.

The point is not that the H100 cannot train. It is that the cost per effective token rises meaningfully.

Once models cross the 3T+ threshold, the bottleneck is no longer just single-chip FLOPS. It becomes HBM capacity/bandwidth, GPU-to-GPU communication, the scale-up network, checkpoint/restart, expert routing, sequence parallelism, pipeline bubbles, and large-scale collective communication. An H100 cluster can, of course, still run, but the training wall-clock time is longer, communication overhead is higher, and cluster utilization is harder to sustain. The net result is that to train the same frontier model, H100 trails GB200/GB300 on both cost and time, and will trail Vera Rubin by an even wider margin.

Meta now faces a fairly classic asset-allocation problem: The most advanced GB200/GB300/Rubin should be reserved first for next-generation model training; the prior-generation H100/H200 should be shifted as far as possible toward inference or external commercial monetization. This is exactly why вҖңbuilding a NeoCloudвҖқ and вҖңcontinuing to rent 1.6GW from CrusoeвҖқ can coexist.

MetaвҖҷs continued 1.6 GW lock-in with Crusoe is fundamentally a preparation for longer-term, more advanced, larger-scale AI infrastructure. For Meta, this capacity is strategic supply for the coming GB200/GB300/Rubin era rather than a simple patch for an H100 shortfall.

On the other side, having already bought large quantities of H100/H200, Meta will not let those assets sit idle once frontier training moves to the next generation. Meta of course runs plenty of internal inference workloads across ads, recommendations, and content ranking, but that is not quite the OpenAI/Anthropic model of selling tokens directly to external customers via LLM inference. If Meta lacks enough external token demand it can monetize directly, turning H100/H200 into cloud capacity or a hosted model API for external sale is a very reasonable way to recycle capital.

This actually aligns with the logic of xAI/SpaceX. xAI publicly announced a compute partnership with Anthropic this year, supplying Anthropic with Colossus 1 compute; xAI states that Colossus 1 contains more than 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs, including H100, H200, and GB200, and can support training, fine-tuning, inference, and HPC workloads. This shows that even frontier AI companies may rent out part of an existing GPU fleet while keeping the newest, scarcest, most training-efficient next-generation clusters for their own frontier models.

So todayвҖҷs worry that вҖңMeta entering NeoCloud will crush every NeoCloudвҖқ strikes us as overdone.

A more accurate read: the AI compute market is moving from a single GPU shortage into a phase of multi-generation, tiered pricing and tiered usage.

The first tier is the newest training compute: GB300, Rubin, and later systems with even larger scale-up domains, serving mainly frontier model training. Supply here remains scarce, and customers will continue to lock in capacity with Crusoe, CoreWeave, Nebius, Oracle, Microsoft, and other suppliers.

The second tier is prior-generation high-end compute: H100/H200 and some GB200, better suited to inference, fine-tuning, enterprise AI, hosted models, agent workloads, and small- to mid-model training. Demand here has not disappeared; it has shifted from вҖңthe scarcest training resourceвҖқ to вҖңan inference resource that can be commercialized at scale.вҖқ

The third tier is more general-purpose GPU cloud and long-tail enterprise workloads, more price-sensitive but also more elastic in demand.

Within this framework, MetaвҖҷs move is quite rational: it is not halting AI infrastructure buildout, but placing different GPU generations into their best-fit economic uses.

So we do not see this as a major problem for AI infrastructure. The judgment that really matters: demand for next-generation frontier model training for GB200/GB300/Rubin remains very strong; at the same time, prior-generation GPUs like H100/H200 will not be scrapped but will shift to inference monetization and external compute sales.

The implication for the whole AI supply chain is, if anything, the opposite of the bearish read: the GPU fleet is becoming a multi-generation asset rather than a single-use training tool. Old GPUs do not reach zero, and new GPUs remain scarce. Meta building a NeoCloud is not a sign that demand has collapsed. We believe it is a sign that compute assets are finally being financialized and commercialized.

emails.subject: IGNORAR VERSГғO ANTERIOR: ECB Forum on Central Banking 2026 - Resumo e transcriГ§ГЈo (01/07)
emails.body:
ECB Forum on Central Banking 2026 (01/07)

 

Warsh (Fed)

Postura de polГӯtica e compromisso com a meta

*	Ele descarta qualquer conforto com inflaГ§ГЈo acima de 2% e diz, de forma direta, que quem apostava em tolerГўncia vai se decepcionar: "would be disappointed. We're going to deliver price stability in the US."
*	Como sinal de progresso jГЎ no inГӯcio do mandato, ele aponta melhora nas expectativas e nos riscos de inflaГ§ГЈo: "Inflation risks have come down" e "expectations of inflation... they've come down."
*	Ao descrever a economia, ele repete o retrato do Гәltimo comunicado sem atualizГЎ-lo, sinalizando estabilidade: "labor markets are steady" e "the demand side of the economy was solid."
*	E coloca a independГӘncia como limite inegociГЎvel, inclusive frente ao Executivo: "No matter what the President wants... we've been an independent central bank for a very long time."

AI, capex e produtividade como o centro da leitura macro

*	Ele lГӘ a AI, num primeiro momento, como um choque de demanda: o boom de investimento aparece antes de qualquer efeito sobre a oferta. Nas palavras dele, "the AI shock is leading to a boom in capital expenditures. We see that first and foremost in demand."
*	A expansГЈo da oferta Г© uma expectativa, nГЈo um fato observado, e ele reserva o julgamento sobre se isso Г© inflacionГЎrio: "I'm confident we're going to see it in supply at some point" e "it's up to the Central Bank to decide whether it's inflationary."
*	O otimismo dele se apoia em produtividade que jГЎ apareceu nos dados, e que em boa parte antecede a AI. Ele cita produtividade estrutural na casa dos 2 e poucos por cento, um potential growth em alta e horas trabalhadas estГЎveis: "structural productivity is in the high two % range", "potential growth looks like it's trended up" e "hours worked are relatively flat."
*	Ainda assim, ele separa o diagnГіstico da decisГЈo, recusando-se a antecipar a polГӯtica com base nesse otimismo: "does that optimism convey into policy in the next six or nine months? Still too soon to say."
*	Sobre emprego, o raciocГӯnio Г© que nГЈo existe um estoque fixo de vagas a ser destruГӯdo pela tecnologia: novas tecnologias criam funГ§Гөes que ninguГ©m previa, e ele avalia que estamos no inГӯcio desse ciclo. Ele resume como "first or second inning of this revolution", sendo o "timing" a variГЎvel que realmente importa, dado o dual mandate.

Balance sheet (6.7tn)

*	Ele subordina o balanГ§o aos juros, que devem ser o instrumento principal de polГӯtica: as taxas "should be the dominant means through which we make monetary policy."
*	Sobre como o balanГ§o afeta a economia, ele diz que o canal Г© indireto, via preГ§os de ativos e sinalizaГ§ГЈo, nГЈo via crГ©dito direto: "the balance sheet works mostly through asset prices... through signaling effects."
*	Ele chega a classificar um balanГ§o grande como quase polГӯtica fiscal, o que motiva o desejo de encolhГӘ-lo: "in my biased view, borders on fiscal policy."
*	Mas condiciona qualquer reduГ§ГЈo a duas coisas, previsibilidade para o mercado e tempo, deixando claro que nГЈo serГЎ abrupta: "will not be implemented until financial markets have come to understand" e "it'll take us more than 18 weeks."

ComunicaГ§ГЈo, funГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo e a agenda de dados

*	Ele argumenta que os mercados jГЎ entendem como o Fed reage, e usa a prГіpria reaГ§ГЈo dos preГ§os como prova de que mais guidance seria desnecessГЎrio: "volatility's not up, it's down... yields aren't up, they're down... inflation expectations are down."
*	E propГөe uma agenda de dados em tempo real como resposta aos problemas de mensuraГ§ГЈo das estatГӯsticas oficiais, para enxergar a economia de forma contemporГўnea: leitura "in a contemporaneous real-time way", reduzindo a dependГӘncia de dados "with mismeasurement problems."

 

Lagarde (ECB)

Como o ECB estГЎ decidindo sob o choque de oferta

*	A alta de juros foi enquadrada como resposta ao choque em si, e nГЈo a uma leitura pontual da inflaГ§ГЈo. Ela situa a decisГЈo no ambiente de choque de oferta: "in the context of a supply shock that led us to the decision that we made."
*	A funГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo foi verbalizada de forma explГӯcita, com quatro insumos que o comitГӘ pesa em conjunto: "inflation outlook, balance of risk, underlying inflation, transmission of monetary policy."
*	O balanГ§o de riscos mudou nas Гәltimas semanas: antes assimГ©trico, ela agora o vГӘ mais equilibrado, com risco de inflaГ§ГЈo para cima e de crescimento para baixo se compensando. Nas palavras dela, os riscos estГЈo "more broadly balanced than they were a few weeks ago."
*	O que destravou esse reequilГӯbrio foi a velocidade da queda da energia, que ela cita diretamente: "$72 a barrel now when it was 120 back in March."
*	Os cenГЎrios (mild, severe, adverse) sГЈo usados como teste de robustez da decisГЈo, para que ela se sustente independentemente de qual mundo se materialize: "to make sure that our decisions are solid and robust across scenarios."

Por que ela descarta stagflation

*	Ela rejeita o paralelo com os anos 70 por razГөes estruturais, nГЈo porque a inflaГ§ГЈo esteja baixa. O argumento Г© que as condiГ§Гөes da Г©poca nГЈo se repetem: "stagflation is a concept of the 70s in circumstances that are not replicated."
*	A Гўncora do argumento Г© o mercado de trabalho, que segue forte: desemprego perto do menor nГӯvel histГіrico e participaГ§ГЈo ainda subindo, "almost lowest historical levels of unemployment" e "employment participation continues growing."
*	E ela apresenta o compromisso com a meta como algo que jГЎ estГЎ em execuГ§ГЈo, nГЈo como promessa futura: "We will take the necessary steps and we have."

AI vista como um choque de produtividade que ainda precisa ser medido

*	Ela trata a AI como objeto de mensuraГ§ГЈo para a polГӯtica monetГЎria, nГЈo como narrativa de mercado. O ponto Г© entender o efeito lГӯquido sobre preГ§os ao longo do tempo: "we need to measure the disinflationary, inflationary impact it will have over the course of time."
*	E ela Г© honesta quanto Г  incerteza sobre a ordem desse efeito, deixando aberto se vem alГӯvio ou pressГЈo primeiro: "it might be disinflationary first and inflationary second, or probably more so the other way around."
*	Sobre a posiГ§ГЈo europeia, ela recusa a leitura de simples atraso e descreve uma dependГӘncia mГәtua com os EUA, jГЎ que a Europa responde por parte relevante da receita das grandes plataformas: "When Europe represent 25% of the revenues... we depend on each other."

Estabilidade financeira e o risco de simplificar demais a regulaГ§ГЈo

*	Ela alerta que desregular pode apenas empurrar o risco para fora do sistema bancГЎrio, para estruturas menos visГӯveis, em vez de eliminГЎ-lo. A preocupaГ§ГЈo Г© com risco que "have moved and traveled afar through different structures, bodies and different names."
*	Por trГЎs disso estГЎ um princГӯpio que ela reafirma: quem assume o risco deve arcar com a responsabilidade correspondente.
*	E ela deixa claro que os motores de crescimento da Europa estГЈo fora da polГӯtica monetГЎria, na agenda de integraГ§ГЈo de capitais: "Capital market union. 28th regime and boost the venture capital."

 

Bailey (BoE)

Postura de polГӯtica e transmissГЈo

*	Ele justifica o hold pela atividade, nГЈo pela inflaГ§ГЈo corrente: a economia estГЎ esfriando, o mercado de trabalho amolecendo e um hiato comeГ§ando a se abrir. Nas palavras dele, "a softening economy... a softening labor market... a bit of an output gap opening up."
*	E frisa que essa fraqueza Г© anterior ao choque de energia, ou seja, nГЈo foi causada por ele: "we had that before the hostility broke out in the Gulf."
*	Apesar da inflaГ§ГЈo acima da meta, ele mantГ©m a convicГ§ГЈo de que ela converge, e diz que os dados recentes reforГ§am essa visГЈo prГ©via: "I was very much of the view that we were going to have inflation back at the 2.0 target... in the late spring."
*	Um ponto sutil: ele considera que a polГӯtica jГЎ apertou sozinha, porque o mercado tirou os cortes do preГ§o e isso subiu o custo do crГ©dito hipotecГЎrio: "the mortgage rate went up 1%. So you can argue that we've actually tightened policy."
*	Ele tambГ©m distingue o caso britГўnico pela defasagem da energia, que chega ao consumidor com atraso por causa do sistema regulado de cap: "a delayed reaction mechanism to energy prices because of... our household energy price cap system."
*	O foco de risco dele sГЈo os efeitos de segunda ordem, ou seja, o choque de energia contaminar preГ§os de forma persistente: risco de "pass through of the energy prices" que ele "doesn't want it to become embedded."

AI e emprego

*	Ele se recusa a dar uma resposta Гәnica, porque a tecnologia atua em direГ§Гөes opostas ao mesmo tempo, criando, destruindo e substituindo vagas: "channels which will create jobs... destroy jobs... substitute jobs" e "I wouldn't generalize."
*	Para ele, o resultado final depende de escolhas de polГӯtica pГәblica, sobretudo em qualificaГ§ГЈo e formaГ§ГЈo: "we have choices in terms of public policy mixes... particularly... skills and training."
*	E, na prГЎtica, ele ainda nГЈo vГӘ o efeito nos dados: o prГіprio BoE estГЎ em fase de teste e o impacto nГЈo aparece no agregado, "still really in the experimentation phase" e "we don't see it strongly in the overall economy-wide data."

Estabilidade financeira (ele preside o FSB)

*	A primeira preocupaГ§ГЈo Г© a alavancagem curta de hedge funds no mercado de dГӯvida soberana, que ajuda a distribuir a emissГЈo mas cria fragilidade: "very large growth of hedge funds in the sovereign debt market", com "a lot of leverage, very short-term leverage."
*	O ponto frГЎgil Г© o financiamento no repo overnight, onde um estresse pode forГ§ar uma liquidaГ§ГЈo rГЎpida: "a lot of it's overnight in the repo market" e "you could get a rapid unwind."
*	Ele faz a distinГ§ГЈo que importa para um regulador: cada fundo parece seguro isoladamente, mas o risco vira sistГӘmico quando todos fazem a mesma operaГ§ГЈo: "very low risk for each hedge fund, but when they're all doing something similar, there could be a systemic overlay."
*	E estende a vigilГўncia a aГ§Гөes e private credit, com um exercГӯcio dedicado a este Гәltimo: "increase in leverage in equity markets... leveraged exchange traded fund markets... private credit", alГ©m de "a second systemwide exploratory scenario... about private credit."
*	Sobre a divergГӘncia entre juros longos e bolsa, ele a considera economicamente justificГЎvel pela AI, mas mantГ©m sob observaГ§ГЈo: "explicable in broad economic terms."

Balance sheet e forward guidance

*	Ele define o tamanho do balanГ§o pela demanda do sistema por reservas, e nГЈo por uma meta prГіpria: "we will meet the system's demand for reserves because that's the system's demand for liquidity."
*	O objetivo estrutural Г© tirar o risco de taxa de juros de dentro do banco central, migrando para operaГ§Гөes de repo: "I want to take interest rate risk off the central bank's balance sheet" via "a repo asset side."
*	Sobre guidance, ele Г© declaradamente relutante, porque o custo de sair dela Г© maior que o de adotГЎ-la: "it's much easier to put it in place than it is to take it away" e "it overstays its welcome."
*	E separa explicar a funГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo de se comprometer com uma trajetГіria de juros, sendo o segundo o que ele evita: "we have to be very careful about... getting tied into views on where rates are going to go."

 

Macklem (BoC)

Postura de polГӯtica e o dilema central

*	Ele mantГ©m o juro parado mesmo com a inflaГ§ГЈo acima do range, algo que nГЈo ocorria hГЎ tempos: "inflation is clearly above the target, 3.2% in May."
*	A taxa estГЎ no piso da faixa neutra, oferecendo apenas suporte marginal, o que ele avalia ser o nГӯvel certo para conter a inflaГ§ГЈo: "our range for neutral is 225 to 325 or 225. So we're kind of at the bottom end", nГӯvel "about the right level to keep inflation contained."
*	Ele expГөe o dilema nos dois sentidos com clareza: subir juros aprofunda a fraqueza da economia, cortar arrisca deixar a inflaГ§ГЈo grudada. Subir "is going to weaken the economy further", cortar traz "risk that the rise in inflation becomes more persistent."
*	O que sustenta o suporte Г© o hiato: economia fraca, excesso de oferta e folga no mercado de trabalho, "the economy's soft... there's excess supply... there's some slack."
*	E o comГ©rcio pesa diretamente sobre a atividade, dado o grau de integraГ§ГЈo com os EUA: "you certainly see the impact of US tariffs on our exports", num "very integrated business model in North America."

AI e produtividade

*	Ele separa adoГ§ГЈo de integraГ§ГЈo de verdade: quase todo mundo usa AI, mas poucos a incorporaram ao processo produtivo. A integraГ§ГЈo material estГЎ em "something like 10, 15%."
*	Ele admite um upside ainda maior caso a AI acelere a prГіpria capacidade de inovar, e nГЈo sГі a produГ§ГЈo: "if it actually accelerates the process of innovation... it could be even bigger."
*	Mas mantГ©m o timing do alГӯvio de preГ§os em aberto e aponta pressГЈo de curto prazo nos bens, com efeitos ainda por passar pela cadeia: "You look at computers, up 10%. How's that going to feed through the supply chain?"

Estabilidade financeira (ele preside o comitГӘ de vulnerabilidades do FSB)

*	Ele usa a analogia com a dot-com para separar duas coisas: a tecnologia pode ser real e, ainda assim, o preГ§o pode se descolar. "we still got the dot-com bubble."
*	E sinaliza valuations esticados pelas mГ©tricas usuais: "P/E ratio, forward ratios... things look stretched."
*	Ao mesmo tempo, delimita o papel do banco central, que nГЈo Г© dar recomendaГ§ГЈo de investimento: "we're not in the business of giving investment advice."

Balance sheet

*	Ele explica o balanГ§o enxuto pelo histГіrico de uso restrito de QE, usado apenas na pandemia: "In Canada, we didn't do QE in 08-09" e "We did use QE in the pandemic. It's the only time."
*	E dГЎ a dimensГЈo relativa, bem menor que a do Fed em proporГ§ГЈo do PIB: "Bank of Canada's balance sheet as a percentage of GDP is about a third of the Feds."

 

 

Iuri Honda
iuri.honda@capstone.com.br <mailto:iuri.honda@capstone.com.br> 

Capstone
Av. Bartolomeu Mitre, 336 вҖ“ 2Вә Andar вҖў  Leblon 
Rio de Janeiro  вҖў  RJ  вҖў  CEP 22431-002
Tel.: + 55 21 3550-6521

emails.subject: ECB Forum on Central Banking 2026 - Resumo e transcriГ§ГЈo (01/07)
emails.body:
ECB Forum on Central Banking 2026 (01/07)

 

Warsh (Fed)

Stance e price stability

*	Rejeita conforto com inflaГ§ГЈo acima da meta: quem esperasse tolerГўncia acima de 2% "would be disappointed. We're going to deliver price stability in the US."
*	Reporta melhora nas expectativas no inГӯcio do mandato: "Inflation risks have come down" e "expectations of inflation... they've come down."
*	Descreve o quadro do Гәltimo comunicado sem atualizГЎ-lo: "labor markets are steady" e "the demand side of the economy was solid."
*	Situa a independГӘncia como restriГ§ГЈo vinculante: "No matter what the President wants... we've been an independent central bank for a very long time."

AI, capex e produtividade como eixo do outlook

*	LГӘ AI primeiro como choque de demanda via investimento: "the AI shock is leading to a boom in capital expenditures. We see that first and foremost in demand."
*	ExpansГЈo de oferta Г© esperada, nГЈo confirmada, e o juГӯzo inflacionГЎrio fica reservado: "I'm confident we're going to see it in supply at some point" e "it's up to the Central Bank to decide whether it's inflationary."
*	Ancora o otimismo em produtividade jГЎ observada, majoritariamente anterior Г  AI: "structural productivity is in the high two % range", "potential growth looks like it's trended up" e "hours worked are relatively flat."
*	Separa diagnГіstico de decisГЈo: "does that optimism convey into policy in the next six or nine months? Still too soon to say."
*	Sobre emprego, invoca lump of labor fallacy e estГЎgio inicial: "first or second inning of this revolution", com o "timing" como variГЎvel central sob dual mandate.

Balance sheet (6.7tn)

*	Subordina o balanГ§o aos juros como instrumento primГЎrio: taxas "should be the dominant means through which we make monetary policy."
*	Descreve o canal de transmissГЈo do balanГ§o: "the balance sheet works mostly through asset prices... through signaling effects."
*	Qualifica o balanГ§o grande como quase fiscal: "in my biased view, borders on fiscal policy."
*	Condiciona a reduГ§ГЈo Г  compreensГЈo prГ©via dos mercados e a um horizonte longo: "will not be implemented until financial markets have come to understand" e "it'll take us more than 18 weeks."

ComunicaГ§ГЈo, reaction function e dados

*	Argumenta que os mercados jГЎ leem a funГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo, dispensando mais guidance: "volatility's not up, it's down... yields aren't up, they're down... inflation expectations are down."
*	Agenda de dados real-time como resposta a mismeasurement oficial: aspiraГ§ГЈo de captar a economia "in a contemporaneous real-time way", reduzindo dependГӘncia de dados "with mismeasurement problems."

 

Lagarde (ECB)

Reaction function e decisГЈo sob choque de oferta

*	DecisГЈo apresentada como resposta direta ao choque, nГЈo a um nГӯvel de inflaГ§ГЈo isolado: "in the context of a supply shock that led us to the decision that we made."
*	FunГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo com quatro insumos formais: "inflation outlook, balance of risk, underlying inflation, transmission of monetary policy."
*	BalanГ§o de riscos reponderado, com risco agora incorporado Г  prГіpria avaliaГ§ГЈo de inflaГ§ГЈo: riscos "to the upside on inflation and to the downside of growth are probably more broadly balanced than they were a few weeks ago."
*	ReequilГӯbrio atribuГӯdo Г  velocidade do movimento de energia: "$72 a barrel now when it was 120 back in March."
*	CenГЎrios como mГ©todo de robustez da decisГЈo, nГЈo como mero exercГӯcio: "milder, severe, and adverse scenario to make sure that our decisions are solid and robust across scenarios."

RejeiГ§ГЈo de stagflation via mercado de trabalho

*	Recusa do enquadramento com base estrutural, nГЈo conjuntural: "stagflation is a concept of the 70s in circumstances that are not replicated."
*	Ancora a recusa no estado do emprego: "almost lowest historical levels of unemployment" e "employment participation continues growing."
*	Compromisso apresentado como reativo e jГЎ em curso: "We will take the necessary steps and we have."

AI como choque de produtividade a mensurar

*	Trata AI como objeto de mensuraГ§ГЈo de polГӯtica, nГЈo como narrativa: "we need to measure the disinflationary, inflationary impact it will have over the course of time."
*	Assume incerteza sobre a ordem do impacto: "it might be disinflationary first and inflationary second, or probably more so the other way around."
*	Enquadra a dependГӘncia UE-EUA como estrutural, limitando a leitura de simples atraso europeu: "When Europe represent 25% of the revenues... we depend on each other."

Estabilidade financeira e cautela regulatГіria

*	Alerta contra simplificaГ§ГЈo que desloque risco: preocupaГ§ГЈo com risco que "have moved and traveled afar through different structures, bodies and different names."
*	Reafirma o princГӯpio de alocaГ§ГЈo de risco: quem toma o risco deve arcar com a responsabilidade correspondente.
*	Levers de crescimento situados fora da polГӯtica monetГЎria: "Capital market union. 28th regime and boost the venture capital."

 

Bailey (BoE)

Stance e transmissГЈo

*	Justifica o hold pela atividade, nГЈo pela inflaГ§ГЈo corrente: "a softening economy... a softening labor market... a bit of an output gap opening up."
*	Data a fraqueza como anterior ao choque: "we had that before the hostility broke out in the Gulf."
*	MantГ©m convicГ§ГЈo de convergГӘncia Г  meta apesar do desvio: "I was very much of the view that we were going to have inflation back at the 2.0 target... in the late spring."
*	Trata a retirada do easing precificado como aperto efetivo: "the mortgage rate went up 1%. So you can argue that we've actually tightened policy."
*	Distingue a dinГўmica britГўnica pela defasagem regulada de energia: "a delayed reaction mechanism to energy prices because of... our household energy price cap system."
*	Foco de risco em second-round effects: risco de "pass through of the energy prices" e efeitos que "we don't want it to become embedded."

AI e emprego

*	Recusa generalizaГ§ГЈo, elencando canais opostos: "channels which will create jobs... destroy jobs... substitute jobs" e "I wouldn't generalize."
*	Atribui parte do resultado a escolhas de polГӯtica pГәblica: "we have choices in terms of public policy mixes... particularly... skills and training."
*	Reporta ausГӘncia de sinal agregado: BoE "still really in the experimentation phase" e "we don't see it strongly in the overall economy-wide data."

Estabilidade financeira (preside o FSB)

*	Alerta para alavancagem curta de hedge funds na dГӯvida soberana: "very large growth of hedge funds in the sovereign debt market", com "a lot of leverage, very short-term leverage."
*	Localiza o risco no repo overnight e no unwind: "a lot of it's overnight in the repo market" e "you could get a rapid unwind."
*	Distingue risco idiossincrГЎtico de sistГӘmico: "very low risk for each hedge fund, but when they're all doing something similar, there could be a systemic overlay."
*	Estende o monitoramento a equities e private credit: "increase in leverage in equity markets... leveraged exchange traded fund markets... private credit", com "a second systemwide exploratory scenario... about private credit."
*	Interpreta a divergГӘncia bond-equity como economicamente explicГЎvel, mas sob vigilГўncia: "explicable in broad economic terms."

Balance sheet e forward guidance

*	Ancora o balanГ§o na demanda por reservas: "we will meet the system's demand for reserves because that's the system's demand for liquidity."
*	Objetivo de migrar o interest rate risk para fora do balanГ§o: "I want to take interest rate risk off the central bank's balance sheet" via "a repo asset side."
*	RelutГўncia assumida com guidance, por custo de saГӯda: "it's much easier to put it in place than it is to take it away" e "it overstays its welcome."
*	Separa funГ§ГЈo de reaГ§ГЈo de compromisso com trajetГіria: "we have to be very careful about... getting tied into views on where rates are going to go."

 

Macklem (BoC)

Stance e dilema de polГӯtica

*	Hold com CPI acima do range: "inflation is clearly above the target, 3.2% in May."
*	Posicionamento no piso do neutral: "our range for neutral is 225 to 325 or 225. So we're kind of at the bottom end", nГӯvel avaliado como "about the right level to keep inflation contained."
*	Dilema explicitado nos dois sentidos: subir juros "is going to weaken the economy further", cortar traz "risk that the rise in inflation becomes more persistent."
*	Fundamenta o suporte marginal no hiato: "the economy's soft... there's excess supply... there's some slack" no mercado de trabalho.
*	Trade como headwind direto sobre a atividade: "you certainly see the impact of US tariffs on our exports", dado o "very integrated business model in North America."

AI e produtividade

*	Contrasta adoГ§ГЈo difusa com integraГ§ГЈo efetiva baixa: uso quase universal, mas embedding material em "something like 10, 15%."
*	Admite upside maior se a AI acelerar a prГіpria inovaГ§ГЈo: "if it actually accelerates the process of innovation... it could be even bigger."
*	MantГ©m o timing da disinflation em aberto e aponta pressГЈo near-term nos bens: "You look at computers, up 10%. How's that going to feed through the supply chain?"

Estabilidade financeira (comitГӘ de vulnerabilidades do FSB)

*	Usa a analogia dot-com para separar tecnologia real de precificaГ§ГЈo: "we still got the dot-com bubble."
*	Sinaliza valuations esticados por mГ©tricas convencionais: "P/E ratio, forward ratios... things look stretched."
*	Delimita o mandato: "we're not in the business of giving investment advice."

Balance sheet

*	Explica o balanГ§o enxuto pelo histГіrico de uso restrito: "In Canada, we didn't do QE in 08-09" e "We did use QE in the pandemic. It's the only time."
*	Contextualiza o tamanho relativo: "Bank of Canada's balance sheet as a percentage of GDP is about a third of the Feds."

 

 

Iuri Honda
iuri.honda@capstone.com.br <mailto:iuri.honda@capstone.com.br>

Rio de Janeiro  вҖў  RJ  вҖў  CEP 22431-002
Tel.: + 55 21 3550-6521

emails.subject: MediaTek Inc.: Addressing Key Investor Questions
emails.body:
Asia Pacific Equity Research 	

Hi Felipe,


MediaTek Inc.


Addressing Key Investor Questions

 	 
By Gokul Hariharan AC <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=_RmhlU0&sig=C932lidatqzMfHCIadOoXJKQSLG&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/U315071> , Jennifer Hsieh <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=4TSKviV&sig=SveX5jPDzlo2EaodQLL4lRuuB4S&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/V768229> , David Chou <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=YYIqW1y&sig=Xg6Me5xrX1qgYdWf8MfREDd680z&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E950102> , Jason Chen <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=aQ1khGi&sig=SgzNoKIx0kfe1s1g7SJUXueH8w6&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/N801118> , Subham Singhania <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=sazfa5u&sig=M3FboU9YbsZ93kORP5uhFdFekmI&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/analyst/E878020>  

Click here for the full document and disclaimers <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=025g769&sig=VkWoReeIjOMK4sq4z1qSo7VhqZN&v=2&isig=T1rWNggzEqAdgJlmCQpianbIdaW&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/7ugrbmiv/GPS-5354216-0/8988d5fe-e16b-420b-bedf-229b801d3347> 

Overweight	 
2454.TW, 2454 TT	 
Price (01 Jul 26):NT$4,335.00	 
Price Target (Jun-27):NT$5,300.00	 
	

1.	TPU v8t (Zebrafish) ramping up on track, with potential for upside to 2027 revenue guidance: We believe TPU v8t is on track for a volume ramp-up in 4Q26, supporting the company's target of US$2bn in ASIC revenue (or 400-500k unit shipments) this year. For 2027, management has guided to US$7-12bn in ASIC revenue (implying 10вҖ“15% share of a US$70вҖ“80bn ASIC market), underpinned by strong customer demand and confidence in material availability. We have seen Google TPU demand skewing toward our previous bull-case scenario and have revised up our TPU estimates in May (now forecasting 8mn market TPU shipments in 2027 vs. 5mn units expected six months ago (latest CoWoS report <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519653&ad=4541188886&h=nvtAA2A&sig=ZmZqGKSpzhJE0jgCuxjqjDovLu5&v=2&isig=SZat2Y9SJDgbWjnbyCykdpQP5QB&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/sa7ft16j/GPS-5305898-0/8988d5fe-e16b-420b-bedf-229b801d3347> )). However, we still see upside to our current MediaTek enterprise ASIC revenue estimate of US$10.8bn in 2027 (~2.5mn TPU v8t units, ~30% share of 8mn total TPU shipments), with further upside beyond US$12bn achievable if MediaTek can secure additional substrate and 3nm wafer capacity. 
2.	TPU v9 Humufish design status on track to tape out in the next 2-3 quarters, market share likely to skew to the upside, given challenges with competitor program: We believe TPU v9 (Humufish) is on track to tape out in late 2026-early 2027, with revenue contribution starting in late 2027 or early 2028. We believe that v9 Humufish ASP should be 2-3x that of v8t (US$12k+ vs. ~US$4.3k), reflecting significantly greater design complexity (4 N2 compute dies, 4 I/O dies, and 4 memory I/O dies vs. 1 N3 compute die and 1 I/O die in v8t). Meanwhile, our supply chain checks continue to indicate that the key competing project (Pumafish) from US Fabless competitor has been either canceled or postponed, creating an opportunity for MediaTek to gain share in 2028-29. While we do not expect the competitor to be entirely absent - likely launching Sunfish v8i variant with 4 N3 based compute dies - we still expect MediaTek to benefit from an earlier massвҖ‘production timeline (tapeвҖ‘out at least 1-2 quarters ahead of peersвҖҷ v9). We are currently expecting ~2M units for Humufish v9 units in 2028, but do expect further upside, depending on Intel EMIB-T packaging yield progress. Our recent research indicates an extension of v9 Humufish (called Triggerfish), with specific design changes (including more SRAM and potentially a controller die within the package) to extend Humufish to accommodate inference needs as well. This could also result in upside to MediatekвҖҷs TPU v9 family shipment expectations in 2028-29. 
3.	TPUv10 RFQ in progress, engagement model similar to v9, confident of securing a key project: MediaTek is currently engaged in the TPU v10 RFQ process, based on our checks, with results expected in the coming months. We see a high probability of MediaTek securing at least one TPU v10 (Icefish) project, with Broadcom supporting another v10 project. While there is some discussion around additional v10 variantsвҖ”potentially including a full CoT modelвҖ”our checks indicate that GOOG is still embracing a semi-COT model for the v10 family. Key IP that Mediatek is providing in the v10 generation would be 448 G High Speed electrical SERDES, die-to-die interconnect and experience in 3D packaging вҖ“ we still do not see Google embracing CPO for its TPU projects in the v10 generation. We do see the possibility of further accelerator variants in the v9 family, including a fast-inference chip likely awarded to Marvell (SRAM based inference, codenamed Merope), as GoogleвҖҷs Compute program expands in scope and attempts to match workloads with specific ASICs. 
4.	More progress on new customer RFQs; increased rack-level design from smaller customers; Tesla most likely in the next 1-2 quarters: We believe MediaTek is in RFQ discussions with several hyperscalers (we believe Tesla, Meta, and Microsoft, etc.). While all are still in RFQ stage, we see the best chance of a win at Tesla, given its need for more frontвҖ‘end design support, which aligns with MediaTek's strengths. While any win would not contribute revenue before late 2028-2029, confirmation would be positive for sentiment as it demonstrates diversification away from a single large customer. We believe that MediaTek is now securing multiple RFQs from various ASIC design service projects, after the successful tape out of TPUv8t. Several of these projects are also seeing some design engagement at a rack-level basis, rather than just for ASIC chip design. 
5.	Intel EMIB-T likely for TPUv9; a return to TSMC 3D SoIC is possible for TPU v10: We now believe that MediaTek has finalized Intel EMIB-T as the primary packaging choice for TPU v9 Humufish, with CoWoS based solution unlikely to be pursued. Based on our checks, EMIB yield for largeвҖ‘size packaging remains low (likely around 60%), but MediaTek appears to be confident that yield can improve before mass production ramps up in late 2027 and the chance of meaningful execution risk remains low. However, for TPU v10, we believe Google is considering a 3D packaged IC solution for logic-logic integration, which may suggest a return to TSMC SoIC packaging, along with CoWoS-L. We believe that IntelвҖҷs 3D packaging (Foveros Direct) is still at a very early stage of ramp as compared to TSMC SoIC. For logic-HBM integration, we also believe that TSMC is now aggressively pushing for larger reticle size CoWoS-L / CoPoS in the 2029-30 timeframe, which will align with the mass production ramp timeline for TPU v10. 
6.	Concerns about competition from QCOM in Datacenter ASIC are early and overblown: QCOM (covered by Joseph Cardoso) announced last week at its investor day that it is expanding into the DC field, partnering with Meta (on C1000 datacenter CPU) and Microsoft (on its AI inference chip using HBC memory). Meanwhile, QCOM highlighted two custom silicon hyperscaler clients (one likely ByteDance, the other likely AWS, in our view) targeting US$1+bn revenue each in 2027. We agree that the recent Alphawave acquisition gives QCOM access to SerDes IP, and its chipвҖ‘design pedigree is strong given the long experience in smartphone/CE. However, prior experience in smaller consumer CPUs may take time to translate into a reliable track record in large AI accelerators. MediaTek, in comparison, has been engaged in TPU projects since 2020 and has been developing its Serdes IP since 2018, before gaining firm traction. QCOM has two advantages at this point, in our view: (1) Architectural license access for ARM instruction set, which can help it differentiate in ARM CPUs over time, and (2) Strong N3 wafer allocation at TSMC, which, in the current shortage environment, could translate into datacenter ASIC revenues, especially from customers like AWS, even if design engagement is not very high. Based on our checks, we do not see any large AI ASIC projects based on CoWoS or 2.5D packaging, where QCOM is the lead design service partner. 
7.	Gross margins to dip modestly, while OP margins should ramp up meaningfully as DC ASIC business ramps up: We expect MediaTek GM to dip by 2-3ppts YoY in 2026 to 45-46%, reflecting ASIC dilution and a smaller smartphone revenue base amid weak end demand. Rising input costs (wafers, OSAT, substrates), on the other hand, should be broadly offset by the lateвҖ‘2026 price hikes, keeping margins stable throughout 2026. While ASIC GM is below the corporate average, its OPM contribution is accretive. As DC ASIC business ramps, we expect GMs to settle at ~42% in 2028, while OP margins could move up to ~25%. We model the datacenter ASIC business to represent ~50%/~70% of company OP in 2027/28, helping expand OPM to ~20%/~25% (vs. 17% in 2025). 
8.	A long-term pursuit of full-COT from GOOG is unavoidable, but will take time: A key question on the durability of MediaTekвҖҷs ASIC business is GOOGвҖҷs pursuit of a full-COT (Customer Owned Tooling) business model, similar to AWS for Trainium. In the very long term, we do believe GOOG will attempt to move towards a full CoT model to take more control of the chip design, but for designs until TPU v10 we are not seeing any change from the current semi-CoT approach. In the next 3-4 years, we believe that MediaTek is likely to benefit from the rising adoption of this semi-CoT model, and should gain meaningful share against Broadcom, given the 40-50% cheaper offering. At the same time, smaller projects within GOOGвҖҷs ASIC portfolio (such as Server CPU, video accelerators) could migrate to a full-CoT model much more quickly, and more design service vendors are also entering the supply chain, such as Marvell and Alchip. We believe that MediatTek is fully cognizant of this longer-term tail risk and is cultivating other ASIC projects to mitigate exposure, as well as move into rack-level design to enhance its value add to new ASIC customers. 
9.	Investor feedback вҖ“ increased interest from Global Tech investors, next watch point is second customer confirmation: We have seen a much broader set of global tech investors express interest in MediaTek stock in the last two months, after the successful tapeout of its TPUv8t project for GOOG. If MediaTek is able to move TPUv9 to tapeout on time, we expect global tech investor interest to surge meaningfully, given its position as a credible competitor to Broadcom. For Asian investors, TPU expectations have already moved up meaningfully and the next watch point would be the confirmation of a second ASIC customer (we believe Tesla is quite likely), and potential details of project award for TPU v10. 
10.	Core business units struggling but broad-based price hikes to support revenues and gross margins: Unit demand in core businesses (smartphones, TVs, and consumer IoT) continues to be challenging due to weaker consumer demand and impact from rising memory prices, but MediaTek is still able to see healthy growth in computing, 5G thin modems, and automotive. In addition, MediaTek is raising prices across its core business, averaging 5-10%, on our estimates, with the increases flowing through revenue in late 3Q26 to early 2027. This should stabilize GMs in core businesses, as the increases are largely intended to offset rising costs from OSATs, substrates, and foundry price adjustments. We expect non-ASIC revenue to grow mildly in 2026, despite challenges in consumer demand. 

  




Investment Thesis, Valuation and Risks 


MediaTek Inc. (Overweight; Price Target: NT$5,300.00)


Investment Thesis 


We are Overweight on MediaTek, given (1) MediaTekвҖҷs v9 TPU project (Humufish) appearing to emerge as the primary v9 family in 2028-29, (2) MediaTeksвҖҷs smoother execution with v9 positioning it well to maintain its leadership in v10 competition, and (3) solid prospects in datacenter solutions, ranging from XPUs to interconnect solutions, custom HBM and full-rack-level enablement, with strong engagement for new XPU projects from most hyperscalers. Key catalysts from here on, in our view, are (1) indications of a larger role with TPU v9 if EMIB-T packaging execution goes on track, (2) the announcement of new customers before the end of the year вҖ“ we believe Meta, Tesla are key potential customers, (3) the expansion of the datacenter TAM to include interconnect solutions and rack-level implementation beyond the top 4 CSPs. 


Valuation 


Our Jun-27 PT of NT$5,300 is based on our SoTP valuation (consisting of 12x 12M fwd core earnings + 40x 12M fwd ASIC-related earnings). 


Risks to Rating and Price Target 


Key downside risks include: (1) further pressure on Smartphone SoC GMs due to cost escalations and increased competition; and (2) slower progress on the NVDA partnership, given NVDAвҖҷs expanded engagement with Intel and other chip makers.

Important Disclosures
