emails.subject: *TODAY* JEF Access: Update on Utility Scale Solar+BESS w/ Primergy Solar - July 1st at 12:00pm ET
emails.body:
WEBINAR

JEF Access: Update on Utility Scale Solar+BESS w/ Primergy Solar

Speakers: Ty Daul вҖ” Chief Executive Officer, Primergy Solar

 

Host: Julien Dumoulin-Smith: US Power, Utilities, Midstream & Clean Energy вҖ“ Lead Analyst

 

Background

We will be hosting Primergy Solar, a U.S.-based renewable energy developer, owner, and operator focused on utility-scale solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage projects, on July 1st at 12:00 PM ET. During this discussion, we will explore PrimergyвҖҷs growth trajectory, solar + storage deployment trends, financing innovation, and broader implications for U.S. power markets and clean energy investment.

 

Founded in 2020 and headquartered in Oakland, California, the company develops, finances, builds, and manages large-scale clean energy infrastructure across major U.S. power markets.

 

Backed by significant institutional capital and partnerships, Primergy has rapidly scaled a multi-gigawatt portfolio spanning solar and storage assets across development, construction, and operations. Its flagship Gemini Solar + Storage project in Nevada is among the largest co-located solar-plus-storage facilities in the United States, highlighting the firmвҖҷs role in advancing grid reliability and energy transition efforts.

 

Ty Daul brings over three decades of experience in power generation and renewable energy development, having been involved in more than 10+ GW of projects representing over $12B of investment. Prior to Primergy, he held leadership roles at Canadian Solar, Recurrent Energy, and SunPower, and is widely recognized as an experienced operator in large-scale energy development

 

Date: Wednesday, July 1st, 2026

 

Time: 12:00 PM ET

 

 <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519561&ad=4541144848&h=We4LGez&sig=B1bbp8hrgz5rBPXx0cR1PHm5bQ&v=2&url=https://jefferies-us-power-utilities-clean-energy.open-exchange.net/session>

emails.body:
EVENTS: 10:00am ET вҖ“ Pharma 101 Register here 10:00am ET вҖ“ Residential HVAC Expert: Channel check on residential HVAC for the busy season Register her  <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/i/dQ2K0uJFO0KSpAW9douAWOMhvpX3Eao37fVOdRzkcCoGfGDf86AgLxW6jxVDgKiImFFWhp4Pu858nnN3HC8xs20zKBUWN75xKY5ICA495rtzsUdVb5VIi75MzgqraNv7>  <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/r/i/dQ2K0uJFO0KSpAW9douAWOMhvpX3Eao37fVOdRzkcCoGfGDf86AgLxW6jxVDgKiImFFWhp4Pu858nnN3HC8xs20zKBUWN75xKY5ICA495rtzsUdVb5VIi75MzgqraNv7> 

EVENTS:

10:00am ET вҖ“ Pharma 101 Register here <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=rkIeXG1&sig=KGAFvB73EG3maVerVwliJagzFpq&v=2&url=https://events.bernsteinresearch.com/Registration/EventAccess?id%3D8270> 

10:00am ET вҖ“ Residential HVAC Expert: Channel check on residential HVAC for the busy season Register here <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=V-xaVJr&sig=OI5ElJo8luShpHdJDT2XPzWckJL&v=2&url=https://events.bernsteinresearch.com/Registration/EventAccess?id%3D8443> 

10:30am ET вҖ“ HOLCIM вҖ“ H1 PRE-CLOSE FEEDBACK Register here <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=77TAFA2&sig=9P3FJm6XVIFsjE6CjrnHnc4ex73&v=2&url=https://events.bernsteinresearch.com/Registration/EventAccess?id%3D8358> 

11:00am ET вҖ“ Metals & Mining 101 Register here <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=O8CYkwT&sig=6MWJlSUU5apkPMqwD2UQJENT1gk&v=2&url=https://events.bernsteinresearch.com/Registration/EventAccess?id%3D8372> 

 

RESEARCH:

CRCL <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=yKGX8Pc&sig=Z3SFqd3Qt1b283BM8sVZMPxsT0g&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DDKAYWQ0WKvdwFVRHPFP8ZdUd160F8xqXHDD7VGzceSzkytHL51wQRh951F22ifgP>  - Gautam Chhugani sees Open USD as further validation for stablecoin going mainstream... OUSD is credible, but no change to his l-term thesis on CRCL (already has scale, liquidity and distribution). The 17.5% selloff following the Open USD announcement is well understood, but Gautam sees it as additional validation that stablecoins are entering the financial mainstream. While Open USD's consortium of 140+ partners across payments, financial services, tech, and crypto represents the first credible challenger to the Circle/Tether duopoly, stablecoins remain a network-effects and liquidity business where incumbency matters. Circle and Tether have spent years building liquidity, trust, distribution, and partner integrations, advantages that are difficult to replicate even with a powerful founding consortium. Open USD likely increases both the long-term TAM and competitive intensity, but the announcement is ultimately a sign of the category's growing strategic importance rather than evidence that Circle's position is suddenly impaired.

 

Payments (OUSD) <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=7FpW5CW&sig=KxlwA3x1pe9qVZNUms52Y9IaRei&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DdsAJSQ%25252bFzwu78tNf5BTVBkUFcYWpOmYOfGFIbRSpFysTEGBsmZvkNP9NCfsSCKNC>  - Harshita Rawat says Open USD announcement highlights a growing battle over stablecoin economics and governance. The effort is backed by Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Stripe, Coinbase, BlackRock and 100+ partners, though notably absent are JPM, BofA, Citi, PayPal and incumbent stablecoin issuers. She views stablecoins primarily as settlement rails rather than payment products, with the biggest opportunities in treasury management, cross-border payments/transfers and financial infrastructure modernization. Harshita pointed to Google as a potential AI/agentic commerce angle and Stripe as a potential infrastructure winner. The key question is whether 100+ partners can align on governance, economics, and distribution. Despite those uncertainties, Harshita remains bullish on Visa, arguing stablecoins are more likely to enhance than disrupt the card networks' position. Across her conversations with investors it seems there is strong interest to own Visa.

 

Datacenters & Neoclouds <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=-_CJRmb&sig=AVOwWTOhV33qBn5wFEYpj5wWlTK&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DKyhe%25252bXDn4M2JeyIijDGs36IfykbC4NH2sTME3R64VYlN2%25252fI%25252fYK0QOXk3X3IPKzre>  вҖ“ Madison & Gautam build on their $/MW analysis note <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=-_CJRmb&sig=AVOwWTOhV33qBn5wFEYpj5wWlTK&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DKyhe%25252bXDn4M2JeyIijDGs36IfykbC4NH2sTME3R64VYlN2%25252fI%25252fYK0QOXk3X3IPKzre>  to unpack margins and unit economics. The traditional data center REITs (EQIX, DLR, CoreSite), neoclouds (CRWV, NBIS, IREN), and Emerging AI Infra providers (CIFR, WULF, CORZ, RIOT) are situated at different locations in the ecosystem, but trying to solve a similar problem: enabling compute for AI (and non-AI) workloads. In this note, we walk through five major comparisons across this company set: operating margin, revenue yield, financing, capex, and risk profiles. Within emerging AI Infra, CIFR and WULF stand out with strong leasing margins, declining cost of funds and hyperscaler counterparties. Within Cloud names, IREN demonstrates strong margins due to vertically integrated landlord economics, but is behind on scale and building an enterprise business (relative to CRWV and other clouds). We are also constructive on the colocation REITs, which offer a compelling combination of strong credit-quality counterparties, contractual revenue visibility, and moderate capex intensity.

 

NKE <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=Gz_5NNC&sig=V92EfPDWaipKmJXIhdUH30br7AU&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DPWePlT3417626L770c6lpv7tsc4adrn23lqzhdnsoenfmpq4ywc6bs5tygqulf6lcv7zrokowi5nh26uczuisg64lvzqto1%26amp;pn%3Dpn%26amp;ct%3DR%26amp;downloadid%3DP20KADTLCZBASL25A499ZVA349GAL3%26amp;h%3D1%26amp;f%3D1%26amp>  вҖ“ Aneesha Sherman cuts #вҖҷs after a mixed quarter...optimistic that EPS guidance is unchвҖҷd and Nov analyst day maintained. Nike faces near-term pressure with revenue declines expected through FY27 as management reduces promotions, cuts low-quality sales, and prioritizes brand elevation. This вҖңquality over quantityвҖқ approach should lead to two more quarters of declining sales but an earlier inflection in margins. Management is more optimistic heading into calendar 2027, highlighting a strong innovation pipeline and the end of its transformation program by late 2026, with FY28 expected to mark a return to normal growth and continued margin expansion. While earnings stabilization is a positive signal, meaningful EPS growth is unlikely until at least the second half of FY27 given ongoing revenue pressure. The next key catalyst is the November Investor Day where management is expected to provide updated guidance and detail the innovation roadmap.

 

UAL & AAL <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=nDbZrL6&sig=AO89LN8iYRZglajTgRQZTZPrQ7B&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DPWePlT3417625L770b7hzij4sj6vlc3tja6ukxr6uqkmgl5xhfqz2majqaoxvcbz4fuaa2etfajqvgsoyvhuk7fa2gfqma1%26amp;pn%3Dpn%26amp;ct%3DR%26amp;downloadid%3DNZXTC643NSRBLXOZ2YG2UYNI1PBAMG%26amp;h%3D1%26amp;f%3D1%26amp>  вҖ“ Dave Vernon raises #вҖҷs ahead of earnings and expects to see significant earnings revisions in the quarters ahead. Dave is revising estimates to reflect resilient demand, a more favorable fuel backdrop, and modest 3Q capacity cuts at UAL, with fuel emerging as the key driver of upside versus consensus for both UAL and AAL. For 2Q, UAL is expected to report in-line results with slightly improved TRASM driven by solid premium and corporate demand, while AAL is positioned to beat expectations on better unit cost control despite steady top-line assumptions. The outlook for 3Q improves meaningfully as fuel prices have declined sharply, driving EPS estimates well above consensus for both airlines, even without stronger revenue assumptions. The primary driver of this upside is lower operating expenses (particularly fuel) rather than top-line growth. As a result, earnings revisions are becoming increasingly tied to fuel trajectory, making cost dynamics the key variable to watch near term.

 

 

 <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=KT6XW40&sig=PFuh0VzkGQGjrVoiHVJdz0OJRXy&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/Home.aspx%23> 

 

WEDNESDAY 1 JULY 2026


Key Research

________________________________

Real Estate

*	Real Estate monthly review: June 2026 <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=-iRHpnt&sig=GB7Jlh9vwGVyUjsdsnVZEqETtYg&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/viewresearch.aspx?cid%3Ddf%2554e%2547y%2555czzvi%2546%25252bxuar%2546l5%254Cxhf%2546k%2555c%2559e1%2541d78%255A%2545h%2557%2556%2550zd1%254D%2557v5%2554p%2541%2553qo%254D%2547%254E%254D1%2541a4%2541> 

Strategy

*	India Portfolio: 11 Bottom-up ideas <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13519530&ad=4541082814&h=a5zK6Vx&sig=LpUSayjLclXYqLl8KXZ6ZkWFw6X&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/viewresearch.aspx?cid%3D%25252b%2557n%2545r%2552%2551g%2548%2554%254Bl%2554qmpqs%2544el5%254Chm5mts82%2549gfj%2547su2%2545m%2546haq%254A%2557i%25519lvj%2551yrmn%2554%25252b%2554ri%2557>

emails.subject: Starting Now- Is MELI Ad Tech Seeing a Boost from the World Cup? Conf Call Starting now
emails.body:
Is MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Division Seeing a Boost from the World Cup? Starting Now

 

US investors вҖ“ please dial 866-306-3230 Pin: 214018

Brazil investors вҖ“ please join via Zoom https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85781588299 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/us02web.zoom.us/j/85781588299__;!!E9kdcDHgb3V80Z1Q1L9jhgg!WntLsQc8oBI1-CMCm0LSG6D1mpsXjJu3mFysK3iANtbCCKT4dcyo4FwTwo5J9il4XF5dYpD0E4--nsDpAXG4ABMU$>  Meeting ID: 857 8158 8299

 

 

What are global CPG companies buying on MELI for the World Cup?

	*	Commerce data?
	*	Brands Ads? Display Ads? Product Ads?
	*	Off platform inventory?
	*	Closed loop measurement?
	*	Retail Media? 
	*	AI Optimization?

 

Our speaker is a Programmatic Buyer for the Consumer Product Goods (CPG) practice at a major ad-tech company. In this role, he advises clients on campaign setup and optimization techniques for the LatAm market.

 

 

 

 



	
SUSQUEHANNA AND JAMIE FRIEDMAN INVITE YOU TO PARTICIPATE ON A CONFERENCE CALL TO DISCUSS:

MELI Ad Tech & The World Cup:

A Programmatic BuyerвҖҷs Perspective

 

Is MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Division Seeing a Boost from the World Cup?

 

Date + Time
July 1, 2026
10:00 a.m. ET

Conf Call Info

Dial-in: 866-306-3230

Pin: 214018

 

About the Speaker

Our speaker is a Programmatic Buyer for the Consumer Product Goods (CPG) practice at a major ad-tech company. In this role, he advises clients on campaign setup and optimization techniques for the LatAm market.

 

Update on MELI AdTech (from a past earnings call) вҖңвҖҰwe have a decent and big advertising business, almost $1 billion in revenue last year, but still very small compared to what it can beвҖҰwe disclosed that our revenues over GMV are around 2%. If you are to compare that with global benchmarks, that number could be 3x or more.вҖқ

Discussion Topics

*	MELI Ad Tech and the World Cup?  Is MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Division seeing a boost from the World Cup?
*	MELI Ad Tech and the World Cup?  What parts of MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech solutions are seeing the World Cup increase? 

	*	Retail Media? 
	*	Commerce data?
	*	Closed loop measurement?
	*	AI Optimization?

*	MELI Ad Tech and the World Cup? What are global CPG companies buying on MELI for the World Cup?  

*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiative: Customer Feedback? How do large advertisers view MELI today? How does it stack up on ROI, pricing, and ease of use?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Focus Areas? How would he describe MELIвҖҷs advertising strategy today following the recent acceleration in ad revenues? What does he think is driving the recent growth?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Targeted Customer? Who is MELI targeting today as their target customer? Are SMB sellers still the core driver, or are enterprise and multinational brands becoming more important?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Recent Developments? What are the most important recent developments in MELIвҖҷs ad business? What really matters here from your perspective?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: How are they doing in: Brand Ads? Display Ads? Video Ads? Off Platform inventory? How credible is MELI today as a brand advertising platform? Are advertisers using it to create demand, or is it still primarily performance-driven?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Data? How strong is MELIвҖҷs data advantage today? Where is it differentiated, and where does it still need to improve?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: AI & Automation? How is MELI applying AI across bidding, targeting, and campaign management? Are these tools improving outcomes yet?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Self-Serve & Platform Scalability? What progress has MELI made on self-serve tools? Are you seeing improvements in adoption, and what friction still exists?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Geographical Reach? Where is MELI seeing the most traction for ads today? Is growth still concentrated in core markets?
*	MELIвҖҷs Ad Tech Initiatives: Next Steps? If he were running MELIвҖҷs ad business, what would you focus on next? What are the key unlocks from here?

 

For more information

Please contact your SFG salesperson for additional information.  

 

Susquehanna International Group, LLP is comprised of affiliated entities, including Susquehanna Financial Group, LLLP (SFG). SFG is a provider of research and execution services. MediaTek (2454.TW): Stronger AI ASIC momentum to reshape earnings growth trajectory; reiterate Buy with TP up to NT$6,800 
	
1 July 2026 | 9:47PM CST | Research | Equity 	
	
	
We now see much stronger AI ASIC momentum at MediaTek beginning in 2027, and we are raising our AI ASIC revenue to US$20.3bn (vs. US$12.3bn previously), accounting for 49% of its total revenue in 2027E. Our forecast is significantly higher than the company's high end guidance of US$12bn as we see a stronger demand upward revision from customer over the past 2 months. For 2028, we also tweak up our AI ASIC revenue to US$52.5bn (from US$48bn previously), accounting for 69% of total revenue, as we expect to see incremental shipment units from the current project, on top of the next-gen project.

We are also factoring in a 5% price hike for MediaTek starting 3Q26. The increase mainly reflects the rising cost along the supply chain. However, as the adjustment is intended to cover cost inflation, we expect MediaTek's GM to remain largely steady at the current level. 

Net net, we are now modeling MediaTekвҖҷs 2027-28E revenue to grow by 95%/83% YoY (vs. 61%/111% previously) with OpM expanding to 25%/33% (vs. 15% in 2026E), and EPS of NT$181.92/NT$422.17 in 2027/28E. We raise our 12m TP to NT$6,800 from NT$5,000 (still based on 25x 2H27-1H28E EPS). With 57% implied upside, we reiterate our Buy rating on MediaTek.

emails.subject: Coal inventory at Indian IPPs down 28% y/y, biggest 3 month drop, 16 days of use end-JuneвҖҷ26 close to average
emails.body:
While the market has well noted the unusual volatility between Chinese domestic and seaborne coal prices due to the power crisis in China in summer of вҖҳ21 and geopolitics in Europe last year, the big (and unseasonal) drawdowns in coal inventories in India went relatively underappreciated and was a contributory factor to the strength in seaborne coal prices in вҖҷ22вҖ“вҖҳ23. At end-JunвҖҷ26, Indian inventories were 41.8mn, lower than the peak above 58.1mn tonnes in JunвҖҷ25. Inventories have come down by 13.5mn tonnes in the last three months, the biggest three-month drop and exceeding the 11.2mn tonnes drop over JunвҖ“AugвҖҷ20 during the pandemic lockdowns. They were down 28% y/y and 11% m/m in June (compared with average seasonality of 4% m/m decrease during the month). Inventories are now at 14 days of use (vs long-term average of 13 days), down from a peak of 19 days in March. 
 	
Viewpoint | Citi Research 	
Global Metals & Mining 
Coal inventory at Indian IPPs down 28% y/y, biggest 3-month drop; 16 days of use end-JuneвҖҷ26 close to average 
CITI'S TAKE 

While the market has well noted the unusual volatility between Chinese domestic and seaborne coal prices due to the power crisis in China in summer of вҖҳ21 and geopolitics in Europe last year, the big (and unseasonal) drawdowns in coal inventories in India went relatively underappreciated and was a contributory factor to the strength in seaborne coal prices in вҖҷ22вҖ“вҖҳ23. At end-JunвҖҷ26, Indian inventories were 41.8mn, lower than the peak above 58.1mn tonnes in JunвҖҷ25. Inventories have come down by 13.5mn tonnes in the last three months, the biggest three-month drop and exceeding the 11.2mn tonnes drop over JunвҖ“AugвҖҷ20 during the pandemic lockdowns. They were down 28% y/y and 11% m/m in June (compared with average seasonality of 4% m/m decrease during the month). Inventories are now at 14 days of use (vs long-term average of 13 days), down from a peak of 19 days in March.

Read Full Report <https://www.citivelocity.> 	
India is often an underappreciated factor in global seaborne coal markets вҖ” While the focus of the coal market usually is on China, India is close behind as one of the top 3 importers of both coking coal and thermal coal (c.15вҖ“17% share of global seaborne demand for both)

Coal inventories at Indian power plants down 18% y/y as of end-JunвҖҷ26 вҖ” Inventories at Indian IPPs typically rise during NovвҖ“Apr and draw down in the subsequent six months. Inventories declined by 33% during AprвҖҷ24вҖ“OctвҖҷ24 but has since increased by 62% to its peak in and are now down 28% y/y.

June-end inventories down 11% m/m vs average seasonality of down 4% m/m вҖ” Inventories normally come down in June (by an average of 4% m/m over the last decade), and hence the down 11% m/m in JuneвҖҷ26 is greater than normal seasonality. 

Absolute level of inventories coming down вҖ” The demand weakness due to lockdowns took inventories to 50.7mn tonnes at end-AprвҖҷ20, then an all-time high. More recently, inventories peaked at 58.2mn tonnes in JuneвҖҷ25 and have since come down to 41.8mn tonnes by end-JuneвҖҷ26.

Inventories only marginally above average levels in terms of days of use вҖ” 41.8 mn tonnes of inventories at power plants translate to 14 days of burn at the power plants, higher than the 13 days of burn over the last decade, having come down from the peak of 31 days of burn in AprвҖҷ20. The 21 days of use at end-JunвҖҷ25 is also the highest since DecвҖҷ20. The lowest level in the last decade was 5 days of burn during the вҖңcoal crisisвҖқ of SepвҖ“Oct'14.

emails.subject: SNDK, STX, WDC Preview & Quantum Expert Call Today 12PM ET
emails.body:
Good morning, please see our preview recap for SNDK, STX, WDC:
 
SNDK: F4Q26 preview: Supply/demand imbalance persists, pricing strong; PO to $2500 <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=s-Gyj-wckXqPJurxA6SRsQ&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=pgCWtg> 
*	We see the pricing environment remaining healthy and model 13% q/q bit growth and 35% q/q price increase for the June qtr.
	*	We model bit growth of 13% q/q and ASP growth of 35% q/q (higher than NAND bit growth reported recently by MU, but lower than the q/q ASP growth reported by MU).
*	Over time, a majority of SNDK's annual revenue could be tied to long-term contracts which offer better visibility.
	*	We like that fact that SNDK is locking-in an increasing portion of its business under NBMs which offer visibility past F28.
*	Please see our SNDK model here <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=s-Gyj-wckXqPJurxA6SRsQ&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=pgCWtg> .
 
STX: F4Q26 preview: pricing can drive upside; PO moves higher to $1150 <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=qtyKH45KEcbwHFkwdLJh4Q&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=4Kzq2Q> 
*	We expect a strong F4Q report and F1Q27 guide as HDD demand continues to outpace supply.
	*	We expect revenue and profitability to increase sequentially every quarter in F27.
*	We see STX's factories as effectively full, so the next leg of growth should come less from utilization and more from higher capacity per drive, continued favorable pricing, and lower cost per terabyte as the product mix migrates from PMR-based drives toward HAMR.
	*	We believe the majority of STX's nearline HDD volume is under build-to-order contracts.
*	Please see our STX model here <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=W946lgjl2-rwHFkwdLJh4Q&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=TbASXQ> .
 
WDC: F4Q26 preview: expecting a solid print and guide; PO to $732 <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=b5LhFKsyBPvwHFkwdLJh4Q&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=WZqcQw> 
*	We expect management to guide F1Q rev to $3.9-4.1bn, GM to 53-54% and EPS to $3.80 +/- $0.15.
*	While our estimates assume 25% HDD EB CAGR, we see the potential for stronger growth for the next 3-5 years as storage.
	*	Most large hyperscale customers are now placing orders about a year in advance, while some are seeking supply commitments that stretch into 2027, 2028, 2029 and even beyond.
*	Please see our WDC model here <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=qIPJb3B6g4TwHFkwdLJh4Q&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=g137zQ> .
 
BofA Hardware Expert Call Series
*	TODAY 12PM ET: Quantum through the user lens with Alexey Galda вҖ“ sign up here <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=h7eB7gn0MEfAyrTQ-VoxNg&e=wamsi.mohan%40bofa.com&h=9SWbMQ> 
	*	Dr. Alexey Galda is currently Associate Scientific Director of Quantum Algorithms and Applications at Moderna.
	*	Discussion will be centered around comparing and contrasting modalities as well as understanding how algos are mapped to hardware and what technical constraints (error correction, runtime, qubit quality, etc) remain as bottlenecks before broader commercial adoption.
 
If you want to dig in more, just shoot me an email at Wamsi.mohan@bofa.com <mailto:Wamsi.mohan@bofa.com>  or give me a call at 646-855-3854 and I am happy to go over each of the topics in more detail.
 
Wamsi Mohan
Managing Director
BofA Securities
Equity Research, IT Hardware and Technology Supply Chain 
Phone: 646-855-3854 (Direct) 
Wamsi.mohan@bofa.com <mailto:Wamsi.mohan@bofa.com>

emails.subject: Barclays Energy, Utilities & Renewables Desk Commentary 7/1 - VST, BE, TALO, RIG
emails.body:
U.S Equities Sales & Trading 

Market Commentary вҖ“ Energy, Utilities & Renewables Desk Based Analyst



  Stan Solomon | 212-526-3090

  For Institutional Investors Only 

 вҖңThis is not a Product of Barclays Research. This is a product of Barclays Desk Analysts and Trading. For Institutional Investors Only.

 

Barclays 40th Annual Energy-Power Conference  

Tuesday, September 8 - Thursday, September 10, 2026

https://events.barclays.com/website/94932/home/

https://events.barclays.com/website/94932/register-now/

WeвҖҷve launched the conference website вҖ“ so you can get on there, see the schedule, register etc.   

We keep adding companies, so make sure you check the website before making meeting requests

 

Oil

 

Brent and WTI are trading lower and there are no major changes to the narrative.  Time spreads and physical markets have weakened as the Strait has partially opened with Russia crude oil exports at the highs and ChinaвҖҷs purchases remaining well below pre-war levels.  Yesterday, the EIA monthly had U.S. production up by 216k/d sequentially to record levels, above where the weeklies were.   Refined product futures are higher this morning in the front which roles to the August delivery contract today вҖ“ this is the bull case for oil at the moment, along with the risk that the U.S. and Iran canвҖҷt get a final deal done and spec positioning which is extremely light on the long side.   The WSJ had an article out last night that said, according to unnamed U.S. officials, that President Trump had decided against a broader military campaign and didnвҖҷt mind if negotiations drag beyond August 18 (60 days after the MOU).  President Trump said publicly that he could give Iran more time, вҖңif they behaved.вҖқ  Vice President said yesterday that Tehran has not yet made the concessions to fully honor the MOU (not yet вҖңbehavedвҖқ in other words.)  He said that the administration has two options if Iran fails to honor the MOU, and вҖң.both those options are the table.вҖқ

https://www.moneycontrol.com/world/deal-or-more-military-action-jd-vance-says-us-has-two-options-if-iran-backs-out-of-post-war-mou-article-13963574.html
IranвҖҷs Deputy Foreign Minister is meeting with Qatari ministers today to discuss implementation of the MOU.  Jared Kushner and Steve Witfkoff met with mediators yesterday. 

Weekly API Crude Oil and Refined Products Inventories (according to Bloomberg) vs. Consensus for the DOEs

*	Crude oil вҖ“ 6.072 mm vs. -2.902 mm
*	Gasoline -2.106 mm vs. -560k
*	Distillate +2.9 mm vs. +10K
*	Cushing +550k
*	The DOE released 5.5 mm barrels from the SPR to the market last week.

 

*	This was out yesterday intraday and may have been part of the reason that crude oil sold off- Total U.S. oil production hit 13.93 mm bbl/d in April, up 216k/d sequentially.
*	The Kremlin confirmed yesterday that itвҖҷs in talks to import gasoline

https://www.politico.eu/article/russia-import-gasoline-ukraine-strikes-fuel-shortage/

President Z said today that Ukranine hit RussiaвҖҷs Ufa refinery for the second time this week.

 

PJM News

 

There was a lot of noise out there yesterday regarding the IPPs with clients asking why the group suddenly sold off in the afternoon.  Yesterday, PJM held a closed at 2:30.   Bloomberg and reporters reported that they voted in favor of a proposal by the Data Center coalition and regulated utilities to procure power through the вҖңbackstopвҖқ auction. Bloomberg reported, вҖңProposal includes a вҖңsubscription basedвҖқ procurement target that recognizes the benefit of data centers securing bilateral contracts for power generation.вҖқ  None of the other proposals passed, including a curtailment proposal.  Yesterday, several clients sent me a letter from most of the governors of PJM states to PJM asking that PJM adopt curtailment requirement for large loads that werenвҖҷt buying from new build generation.  Curtailment may be a part of the solution, but nothing has been decided yet.
https://blinks.bloomberg.com/news/stories/THGMKET96OSH

https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/biggest-us-power-grid-pjm-vote-managing-data-center-demand-2026-06-30/

 

I had the opportunity to sit in at a dinner last night with a former FERC Commissioner hosted by Nick Campanella and Betty Jiang with a small group of clients.  There were some interesting elements of the discussion, but to me the primary takeaway is that although she thought the recent FERC orders were a positive step, the regulatory process is incredibly complex and variable in the RTO regions, and until there is regulatory clarity, large loads may continue to migrate to BYOG and regions were stuff can get done fastest like TX, GA etc.  Nothing earthshaking here.  IвҖҷm happy to discuss.  

 

META News

META announced that itвҖҷs looking at building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing power and models. The fact that they have excess computing power or think that they will have excess capacity may raise a few eyebrows.   META is the only one of the big companies that doesnвҖҷt have a cloud business and id could be a diversification strategy into a very good business.  These companies need to generate a return on investment for the spend theyвҖҷre making (I think?) and this helps in that regard.  They are not deffering projects, but investors could start thinking more about long term investment growth plans.  Feedback here is most welcome.

 

Company News

 

VST is selling 3 вҖңconventionalвҖқ power plants with a combined capacity of 752 MW to Winslow Power.  The assets are the 541 Casco Bay combined cycle facility in Veazie, ME, the 108 dual fuel combustion turbine facility in Croghan, NY and the 103 MW dual fuel combustion turbine plant in Sovay, NY.   The press release came from the buyers and had no mention of asset prices.  VST also announced an expansion of its revolver from $3.44 B to $5.50 B.  

 

BE and Brookfield announce a 5 five fold expansion to their JV from $5 to $25 B.

 

EDF agreed to sell its renewable power business in the U.S. and Canada for about $4.2 B.  ThereвҖҷs also an earnout of as much as $390 mm

 

TALO and Ridgewood are buying GOA assests from SHEL for $850 cash net to TALO.  TALO expects its final net cash consideration to be $450-500 based on estimated cash flow from the assets from the effective date of July 1.  TALO is getting 16,000 boe/d (77% oil) of production and 23 mmboe of proven reserves plus 10 mmboe of probable reserves.  This is net, based on Q1 production.  TheyвҖҷre also getting 25% interest in the Na Kika platform and four associated fields.  TALO also said in the release that it completed the Genovesa workover and returned the well to production during Q2 and, as indicated by the operator, the Monument development well encountered 245 feet of net pay as expected.  A second well will be drilled and first production is expected in late 2026.

 

RIG announced 3 contracts with EQNR for harsh environment semis worth over $1 B in contract backlog over 7 rig years excluding any additional services.   The effective day rate for each is $400,000 per day.  The Enabler gets three years starting in Q1 28.  The Encourage gets 2 years starting in Q1 28.  The Endurance gets two years starting in Q2 27.

 

The FT reported that ENI IM and Mercuria forming a commodity trading JV, and ENI IM put out a press release confirming this.

 

NG LN is investing $1.75 B to secure a 35% interest in Joulent LLC.  Joulent focuses on developing contracted power generation and high voltage infrastructure for US large load customers.  The company has 50% of CVXвҖҷs Project Kirby, the 2.7 GW project with MSFT.

 

From Barclays Research

 

Dave Anderson has a note out after traveling with HAL COO Shannon Slocum.  Some of the takeaways:  In NAM, full utilization of equipment in frac has led to positive pricing conversations.  Dave noted that Q2 Middle East impacts appear to be within the guided range and that Mr. Slocum expects improvement in H2, but indicated that itвҖҷs difficult to predict how much.  They discussed international unconventionals which are ramping up, particularly in Argentina, but also in preliminary work in Algeria and the UAE.  Dave notes that industry sources have said that HAL is starting up the Saudi Jafurah field in a few weeks.  Latin America in general continues to be a strength for HAL with activity in Mexico likely to increase next year and Venezuela which could be back for HAL in late 2026.

https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a4483985c169a5c41aa9ec8

 

Eddie Kim hosted meetings with RIG CEO Keelan Adamson and CFO Thad Vayda last week and came away more positive on RIG shares as the offshore thesis remains on track despite the recent decline in oil prices.  Management said it expects industrywide deepwater rig utilization to surpass 90% by early 2027, setting the foundation for pricing increases next year.   Further, the company is on track to achieve a proforma net leverage target of 1.5x by the end of 2028 from 3.0x with the potential to reduce even further to ~1.0x.  From a deepwater rig fundamentals perspective, supply/demand stats still remain strong with a limited number of high-spec drillships available/idle today and open tenders still supportive of a 2027 activity inflection.   Eddie believes the selloff in RIG and the offshore drillers is overdone at current levels and would be buyers here.

https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a43febc8f4a9e339fb832e0

 

Theresa Chen has a note out on VLO in which she writes that the supply set up for refined products is likely to remain favorable through summer even if the current interim peace deal holds and activity through the Strait of Hormuz normalizes permanently.  The timing of when damaged refining capacity in the Middle East will return is uncertain and UkraineвҖҷs continued attacks on Russian refining infrastructure compound the global supply issues.  Theresa notes that U.S. gasoline and diesel inventories are well below the five year averages and weвҖҷre in peak gasoline demand season.   Theresa writes that VLOвҖҷs Q2 indicator currently tracks at $30.21 cracks, up from $17.86 and crude differentials have been relatively favorable, though down recently.  Theresa raised her EPS estimate for Q2 to 10.48 vs. consensus 10.13.  She has refining EBITDA at $4,688 mm and DGD EBITDA at $400 mm.  She raised her price target to $279 on the same 7 X multiple of 2027 EBITDA (which she raised from $10.6 B to $11.0 B)

https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a4460bb5c169a5c41aa9e30

 

Nick Campanella previews Q2 for PNW.  He sees an in-line quarter with EPS guidance reaffirmed.  He expects H2 to be more interesting in terms of new news and with AZ elections and the APS rate case and a new plan around Q4.

https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a442eb395951c12ce431603

 

Nick has a note out on the TXNM deal.  He still sees a path to approval.

https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a445b2fe7de2a2c16faece8

 

Mike Lonegan previews FTS CNвҖҷs earnings.  He expects TEP to be the primary focus of earnings.  HeвҖҷs modestly below consensus for the quarter.
https://live.barcap.com/go/publications/link?contentPubID=FC6a4421ffe7de2a2c16facc64

 

Stan Solomon

emails.subject: How Elon Musk Is Building the Terafab Team Inside Tesla
emails.body:
Expectations are growing that Elon Musk will propose a merger of SpaceX and Tesla. But a close look at TeslaвҖҷs org chart, including the $55 billion Terafab semiconductor manufacturing project, a Tesla and SpaceX collaboration, shows that the two companies are already operating as one entity in key ways. Several people, aside from Musk himself, have senior responsibilities at both companies, including Charlie Kuehmann, who has held leadership roles in materials engineering across both companies for more than a decade. At SpaceX, several Tesla executives, including Ashok Elluswamy, vice president of AI software, and Silvio Brugada, senior director of vehicle software, have taken on roles within the companyвҖҷs AI unit, xAI.

emails.subject: Vital Knowledge: Meta follows SpaceX in shifting toward a hyperscaler/neocloud model
emails.body:
<https://vitalknowledge.net/assets/images/resources//section1.png> 	
 <https://vitalknowledge.net/assets/images/resources//vertical_divider.png> INTRADAY COMMENTARY 	
Jul 01, 2026 8:47:32 AM EST 	
Meta follows SpaceX in shifting toward a hyperscaler/neocloud model 	

*	META (Meta) вҖ“ according to a Bloomberg report <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-01/meta-is-building-a-cloud-business-to-sell-excess-ai-compute>  this morning (at ~8:35amET), Meta is forming a new cloud business to sell computing capacity to external customers, entering a market currently dominated by Amazon (AWS), Microsoft (Azure), and Google (GCP). 
*	What bulls will say: Meta has been one of the heaviest spenders (in terms of capex/revenue) and many feared it was building way more capacity than it could ever use internally, so this external cloud business will help monetize all that infrastructure, bolstering revenue, margins, and cash flow. Since the industry in aggregate still seems to be capacity constrained, this Meta compute infrastructure will likely be quickly utilized by others.
*	What bears will say: the formation of an external cloud platform is a tacit admission from mgmt. that it overbuilt capacity and/or is falling short on its own internal AI model initiatives. Meta isnвҖҷt the first company to make this transition вҖ“ SpaceXвҖҷs xAI also appears to be dialing back expectations for its internal AI tools (Grok and Cursor) and has started selling capacity to external customers (including Google and Anthropic). If Meta and SpaceX slow the pace of capacity additions over the coming months and quarters, it would deal blow to the pick-and-shovel providers capitalizing on the data center boom.
*	What we think: the Meta news is great for that company specifically, but negative for sentiment toward pick-and-shovel providers (and it could weigh on other hyperscalers/neocloud companies too).

emails.subject: R&Co US Riser: Pharma Global Monthly Report; TKO (Buy), Bloom Energy, CrowdStrike, FactSet, Nike (all Sell), Albemarle, Dynatrace, Klarna, Shell (all Neutral), Economics, Charts, IDEAS
emails.body:
Fact of the Day: Turkish striker Hakan ЕһГјkГјr scored the fastest goal in World Cup history in just 10.8 seconds during the 2002 tournament

 

Research Summary:

Pharma: Vital Signs Global Monthly вҖ“ June Edition <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-j/> 

UK Banks: What Burnham Means For Banks <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-r/>  

Economics: Germany вҖ“ Concrete Ambitions, Contested Multipliers <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-i/> 

Economics: Japan вҖ“ A Weak Yen To Force The BoJ's Hand <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-d/> 

Albemarle (Neutral): Earnings Higher In Fy26, Lower In Fy27

Bloom Energy (Sell): Brookfield (n/r) Partnership Expanded To $25b

CrowdStrike (Sell): Stock Split 4:1

Dynatrace (Neutral): Announces Board Appointments

Economics: Trump & Bessent Zone In On Gas Prices

Economics: US Rates вҖ“ Data-Dependent Danger

FactSet (Sell): Q3 2026 Earnings Slight Beat, Guidance Unch, Margins Behind

Klarna (Neutral): Google (Buy) Ordered To Pay Klarna $1.5bn In Anti Judgement

Nike (Sell): Less Sales; More Margin

Shell (Neutral): Agrees To Sell Us Gulf Assets For $1.7bn

TKO (Buy): Completes $800m Buyback

 

Heard this morning on the sales deskвҖҰ

*	James Moore likes SchneiderвҖҷs (Buy) acquisition of Cognite: This is not a defensive software purchase; it is Schneider buying the scarce, hard-to-replicate layer, a proven industrial knowledge graph paired with an agent build and governance environment. Investors should treat this as thesis confirmation for the industrial AI stack. 

 

Research Updates:

PHARMA: VITAL SIGNS вҖ“ JUNE EDITION <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-j/>  - We present the 26th edition of Rothschild & Co RedburnвҖҷs monthly publication, which provides a snapshot of where global biopharma is and where it is headed. Using a diverse range of data sources, we offer insights into what biopharma companies are doing today and tomorrow across multiple activities (be it R&D or M&A) and how those efforts are recognised, or not, by investors, with analysis in absolute terms and relative to their peers, other healthcare sectors and the rest of the market. This month, we look at probabilities of pipeline success, showing that the most popular areas may not be the easiest.

 

ALBEMARLE: EARNINGS HIGHER IN FY26, LOWER IN FY27 - Our view on FY26-27 earnings trajectory of ALB stands, but we see a more pronounced bullwhip effect. We see lithium prices falling from c$25/kg in Apr 2026 to c$15/kg by YE2026. The price impacts ALBвҖҷs top line w/ only a 1-quarter lag, while COGS impact of high spodumene prices comes w/ a 2-3-quarter lag. We thus see FY26 earnings disproportionately elevated (adj. EBITDA c$3bn, 5% above cons.) and FY27 earnings disproportionately depressed (adj. EBITDA c$1.4bn, 53% below cons.). We lower our PT from $188/sh to $170/sh, with a lower multiple attached to FY26 earnings. Our Q2 2026e adj. EBITDA est. is c$1bn, 34% above cons (high pricing, no spodumene COGS drag). At the current stock price ($135/sh), Q2 earnings could serve as a catalyst for a short-term outperformance.

 

BLOOM ENERGY: BROOKFIELD PARTNERSHIP EXPANDED TO $25B - After US close yesterday, Bloom announced the expansion of the strategic partnership with Brookfield to $25bn, as Brookfield increases its framework to finance power projects for AI infrastructure. This represents a fivefold expansion since the original $5bn partnership was announced ~9 months ago in Oct-25. We still have very little detail over the partnership details, but making an assumption that ~50% of the $25bn is upfront costs, this would imply 3.0-3.5GW of fuel cell capacity orders. No timings or project specifics were provided in the release. The stock was up 8% in aftermarket trading, having already been up 10% on the day with the whole space strong. These large partnerships with Brookfield and Oracle help build backlog and justify manufacturing expansion plans. It' is likely that Ceres Power (Buy) trades up today in response to another large order in the SOFC space, with the news highlighting the longer term potential as Ceres' partners grow and compete with Bloom in the growing SOFC market.

 

CROWDSTRIKE: STOCK SPLIT 4:1 вҖ“ CRWD will execute a 4-for-1 stock split after market close today. With the stock trading circa $760 per share, can expect to be around $190-200 per share. CrowdStrike trades at 33x EV/sales 12months fwd (3x that of the cyber peer average) and 1% fcf yield.

 

DYNATRACE: ANNOUNCES BOARD APPOINTMENTS - The platform has announced the appointments of George Riedel and Dan Streetman to its board of directors, effectively immediately вҖ“ this follow engagement with Starboard Value. Both carry security and IT operations pedigree, aligning closely with Dynatrace's narrative. Riedel possesses a strategy background, with deep public and private board chairmanship experience вҖ“ with prior seats at Cerner and Xperi (n/r). Streetman has experience scaling go-to-market motions under private equity ownership. Notably, Dynatrace has also announced plans to hold an investor day following 2Q27 results, to outline its path to the "rule of 50" in FY29 вҖ“ Dynatrace and Starboard intend to engage substantively in the coming months. Mr Connell has confirmed a commitment to refining their equity investor communications. An incremental positive for shareholders considering historic struggles regarding the stock's narrative - shares are +3% pre market.

 

ECONOMICS: TRUMP & BESSENT ZONE IN ON GAS PRICES вҖ“ In a Truth Social post (Monday, Jun 29), Trump told gasoline retailers to cut prices "IMMEDIATELY," arguing they are "too high considering that Oil is now at $68 a Barrel, and heading south." He warned there would be "no gauging (sic), which is totally illegal," and that "If Retailers don't do this, big problems lie ahead!" Bessent also urged gas retailers to drop prices for the US 250th birthday and said "we're watching." He said the administration has "a chart of how quickly the prices went up and how they followed crude, and we're going to hold them accountable on the other side." He argued retailers "probably had record profits on gasoline retailing" during the Iran war and that "now it's time to do something for the American people."

 

ECONOMICS: US RATES вҖ“ DATA-DEPENDENT DANGER: A benign rate outlook can hide a dangerous path. Counter to market expectations, we anticipate a dovish Fed, but the real story is what that does to the yen carry trade just as the Bank of Japan keeps hiking. The twist is that Warsh may have built the trigger himself: by de-emphasising forward guidance and forcing markets to focus on the incoming data, he has left rate expectations hostage to every release. A single soft US print could spark the kind of rapid repricing and violent carry unwind we saw in August 2024 вҖ” only harder-hitting now the US current account leans on flighty equity inflows. After a long lull, expect bond and equity volatility to return.

 

FACTSET: Q3 2026 EARNINGS SLIGHT BEAT, GUIDANCE UNCH, MARGINS BEHIND вҖ“ FDS reported its Q3 results this morning вҖ“ revs slightly above cons by 1.1% (+6.4% YoY), driven by strength in institutional buy-side and wealth management clients. Org ASV growth of 7.1% was slightly above cons by c57bps, accelerating sequentially and driven by strength in EMEA. Operating margins were behind cons by c83bps, owing to higher SG&A. Adj. EPS +6.1% YoY, beating cons by 1.8% driven by lower share count (net income in-line). Guidance unch. Expect shares small o/p today. Call at 9am EST (2pm UKT).

 

KLARNA: GOOGLE ORDERED TO PAY KLARNA $1.5BN IN ANTI JUDGEMENT вҖ“ The Patent and Market Court in Stockholm has ordered Google to pay SEK 14.4bn ($1.5bn) to Klarna subsidiary PriceRunner in an anti-trust judgement. PriceRunner was seeking $8.3bn in damages for alleging that Google systematically demoted competing price comparison services in its search results while favouring its own Google Shopping product. RDBN VIEW: The $1.5bn award is equivalent to 60% of KlarnaвҖҷs equity and 20% of KlarnaвҖҷs market cap. Even with the courtвҖҷs ruling, Google is expected to appeal, which they must do so within 3 weeks. If the Patent and Market Court of Appeal rejects the appeal, this is generally final, with a case only reaches the Swedish Supreme Court if the Court of Appeal itself grants authorisation to appeal further. This means Google's realistic route is a single appeal to the Patent and Market Court of Appeal (subject to obtaining leave to appeal), which would be a full re-hearing on liability and quantum. Expect the shares to be up 10%+ today.

 

NIKE: LESS SALES; MORE MARGIN вҖ“ Q4 to May yielded a dramatic EPS beat but tariff refund driven. Progress in Performance but not sufficient with overall sales trends pressured from mid-April, particularly Sportswear & Jordan streetwear (c50% of group revenue). Has encouraged mgnt to take a more cautious view on top line in the next 2 qtrs. Instead of being down low single digit Q1 expected down low to mid-singles (inc World Cup) with Q2 sequentially worse (comp etc) with unclear inflection beyond. Gross mgn & opex expected to compensate in H1 (so EPS still flattish over the 3 qtrs. to Nov) but revenue recovery uncertain & mgn recovery pulled fwd to compensate implying pressure on outer year EPS ests. Investor day 15/16 Nov.

 

SHELL: AGREES TO SELL US GULF ASSETS FOR $1.7bn вҖ“ Shell will sell 50% non-op stake in Na Kika platform and its 100% owned Coulomb tieback to subs of Talos Energy (n/r) and Ridgewood Energy. Total consideration is $1.7bn subject to adjustments and contingent payments. Effective date is 1 July 2025 and the sale exp to close by end of 2026. Shell's entitlement prod'n from Na Kika was 37kboe/d in 2025. Shell note that per co modelling the contribution to prod'n in 2030 and beyond is exp to be minimal. Would not expect a meaningful share price rxn today on the news.

 

TKO: COMPLETES $800M BUYBACK вҖ“ First announced in March, TKO has completed its accelerated $800m Class A share repurchase programme, acquiring c4.2m shares in line with its mid-June target. The company remains active under its 10b5-1 trading plan and expects to repurchase a further $200m of Class A shares by 31 August, completing its initial $1bn buyback authorisation. A further $1bn repurchase programme, announced alongside Q1 results in May, remains available.

 

International Research:  

UK BANKS: WHAT BURNHAM MEANS FOR BANKS <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-r/>  - If Andy Burnham can deliver growth and more infrastructure/housebuilding then this would be good for banks' loan books and revenues. But a change of PM (and maybe chancellor) could reignite concerns about bank taxes. In this report, we highlight that UK banks already pay a lot of tax versus other countries; that higher bank taxes could harm credit supply (currently working well) and assess the leading six candidates for chancellor of the exchequer. Aside from the politics, we show that UK bank fundamentals are in pretty good shape with 5% loan growth, stable mortgage spreads, repricing structural hedges and strong credit quality. We reiterate Buy ratings on LLOY/NWG. 

 

ECONOMICS: GERMANY вҖ“ CONCRETE AMBITIONS, CONTESTED MULTIPLIERS <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-i/>  - Germany's fiscal turn is real but unbanked: the budget pushes spending to вӮ¬572bn by 2029 and the deficit toward 4% of GDP, yet the growth payoff hinges entirely on a contested multiplier (Bundesbank 0.6 vs KfW up to 1.2). On that range the stimulus lifts the level of German GDP by ~1.5вҖ“3.0% by 2029 вҖ” the gap is all about execution, and the Infrastructure Future Act cleared the Bundestag on 26 June but still needs the Bundesrat on 10 July. Even fully delivered, it won't fix the deeper problem: Germany's ~вӮ¬1.06tn TARGET2 claim has crept up again, a reminder that completing EMU вҖ” not just German spending вҖ” is what would make the euro area's capital actually circulate.

 

ECONOMICS: JAPAN вҖ“ A WEAK YEN TO FORCE THE BOJ'S HAND <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wozkk-dyiuuktjju-d/>  - The Japanese yen is trading at a multi-decade low against the US dollar, currently Y162. Such a level raises expectations about currency intervention by the Ministry of Finance вҖ“ Y160 has been a previous trigger point. But at most a burst of yen buying would provide temporary relief. The Bank of Japan (BoJ) needs to increase the pace of interest rate hikes.

Market Intelligence: US Morning Update 
	
1 July 2026 | 8:27AM EDT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
Click "here <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2Fcontent%2Fproducts%2Fd036cb0f-b058-490d-9e12-e7b8862ebdfd.html/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/SEKD1b6_a5EwZkBNpfUpPHlkW3DJ3UC3bNYWlARjClo=452> " to listen to the US Morning Call.

Stocks in Asia started 2H26 mixed amidst a flurry of macro data across the region. Japan and Mainland China saw modest gains, whereas Taiwan outperformed and Korea ended lower driven by moves across major tech stock in both markets. Sentiment in Japan improved as reflected in the modest rise in consumer sentiment index and significant improvement in business conditions sentiment measured by the BOJ Tankan survey (see "Japan: June Consumer Sentiment Index Improves Slightly; Expected Inflation Remains High but No Acceleration <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLMBxxwBw7F5AD9A4q57alVhlT0RxCL1Ml2uiwA5zhDlsZmzYUfAACwqUtQUIkpNboNoE-VsqKPb_xE5BhTtfoY60ue7b_rMLmkTtZOu6viDwkdu-pXtfhalXu5x49HAR_qASZ0JHJfdqdt_Kl1XHV07JuijYvsTOZKh5SNXHB64qt8e8BvvgHHNIoXh3qk7oIfie0QCBdPw1XNGaqIp7VAd_SGHFrQwworN-UKx9x5PmDle0eSSKvmjkbLBofqNy7FLu-s-aAhQa_xz0aRq-ggsN_Zbu1DiSk*%26id=cc2a0b69-5f03-4c63-b719-f8ab1e571490%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/T0sx6McERDJMldmrKGpIYKXDC5tWAsJZlh7Kl4h34L8=452> " and "Japan: Jun BOJ Tankan: Large Manufacturers' DI Improves Significantly; FY2026 Capex Plan Revised Up Sharply as Usual <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJkQR28KbgsYJwe1uqcjNm9aYKiTBChurH-dxJcxjPrTK8UKC-MAACw0xgLNPyl-Qp5O7kUTHpqr_xnqO0Q1haw9Ie_DUGqv8A78FZUzKMkpkDLTN_OB7uKWwT3GUqAlvEVZPDkia5-532AHx9jZOlX3B4mDFB5AcBdG_Caw_xGBSXk3MNVcubrBiRwqfQRhqr-3mQKVcOeYg7ygRJ4cZGC6bnkREyUCZNABtmzsOF2tVQrKV2v0H_4cBxZ5sDCblgFoJsFvKBGs35xeQGrb2DGCb-E2HPz7mw*%26id=8b7de4e3-10ca-4a72-a178-091c7367bdab%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/2ql-bUmjeeTP347oyJJe8uOXiJXNXOkpLc6V9I3-nPU=452> "). Manufacturing PMI declined in Mainland China and India (see "" and ""). In Korea, exports accelerated to their strongest pace since early 2010 (see "South Korea: Surprise and Broad Gains in June Exports, with a record trade surplus of $36bn <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKlsPo8-9iw47v5B_0JMHuMTiZZwxAkkAlVUwcatNHRhT1GmYGWAACwRj0wJAtxglVpA0rDzxJ1CGNDsKNkY67XpIY1LvyyBDD1m2kTiWd-Ugpytc5hsp6t5Zi3BhkWaibZoqcStDYmeinIYEvwyIjo8oWnysrXcWmXE2sI8eZy8NjuND_NG3IkfwVEHiBWGSQD8W6pT0nXJOJTloddhX01V73C9qe2cfcX9_92uXyTcqj0MJd5vFr7VumAb-wyJZ5ZJ4lSzdkto9P7_UzKG6vT4NwsKoQ2Lq4*%26id=cc425b8d-0a8f-4e52-ab72-3b52438834ae%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/8HJTtSkuKWw8ijuFBgxtuPbYWSTmnphWobyxJAzePJA=452> "). Hong Kong was closed for a holiday. 

European equities are trading lower today, in a tight range following, yesterday's record close for the regional STOXX 600. The focus remains on cooling inflation prints.

Yields on 10-year US Treasuries are holding steady following their ~10bps move up yesterday to 4.47%. Front month Brent oil is back down to $72/bbl after its short-lived flare up yesterday. 

Broadening out across EM equities in 2H. EM equities have rallied strongly in 1H26, but the gains have been narrow and earnings-led, and concentrated in AI/tech, while the broader market has lagged. Looking ahead to 2H26, Sunil Koul remains constructive because forward EPS has risen faster than prices, leaving valuations lower despite the rally, as discussed in an overnight note, "EM Equities: 2H Outlook: Broadening Gains <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIqCr0KtGc4NHedZKn8XOW5U7cEMhDJr53gHFWZjL7ZppDXeMf8AACw0jQkVUYVLVFRWoOeOPojaY3Etrggs6OPipGV8SI_X4fFP1BiqBMsIjPb6Ii4IlOf6QYAaTP9gdyFNwv3v3B5SOJHFMUOm8DpXbzk_MVM0KZxdgeKpNX_nJNxQlfM88lLLrSNqNCrP4Yel4X8qt__H6U_8JRKMn30-lXpiI4VyDawkmu_q_XpgNC4Gp3jokjrCu4Qy9YJUi56uY6jCQQSvxxqVkrtUREw_-Z8TV_qr2c*%26id=95526063-b5a2-4843-b526-1ccfa1598591%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/-zAcSMXymaIPVMOFLpkATQCRqxq5Na2AAMGPLrhgCCg=452> ." With the US-Iran agreement reducing oil tail risks, look for a broader cyclical recovery, favoring oil importers such as India, Turkey, and Egypt, plus rate-sensitive markets like Brazil and South Africa. North Asia should continue to lead on memory-driven earnings, while our sector preference shifts toward Banks and Capital Goods and away from Energy and Internet.

Fresh high conviction ideas to start 2H. Steve Kron and team add EL, NXT and WFC to the US conviction list, while removing BRX, DUK and NVT, in "US Conviction List - Directors' Cut: July 2026 Update <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQL3nyua9JXYD2Bdcu-Zk-UOd-95XxC0A17E--0ZLJJ_oXbKjC-bAACw-MaF94xMwxR6xjj9WdmUWPrQD3oxyBy5vKLte8RA2fnoaBCqILme_deD9ACcOODSChrDfCBVIuHRYNzh2tyEuKy-Xifld38pKYbkVzhtcbZyva4F5enreE6V8dhLO_fb9bp-aVW3vZ_1qCWrwS9o3IJ6NxmJ6oyu4etvDbwulH72KfKh_PqWNLCs-x4SAT0ctEgdaOqlgjb6WP2DKYQKDdOANtgsPJR7BEjhDaG6b6M*%26id=2f096fb4-8219-4915-b6d5-86f134ccf93f%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/e41sTBQSbArg_bRZBHko1Kp0d3bNibxP6PdvAtnRueE=452> ." Listen in on our podcast at "Research Unplugged: US Conviction List - Directors' Cut: July 2026 Spotlight <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKm1Z5qJNoT9kWWdd7t9Cs1CgohjRAWkWSv0u8n_S7RoD6CgxJJAACwRE5y3Sd4LORapvtlOaiYxIzvXPR9IQF_L62ck9ro4dZPFXm_MUYNY00dQhlG7S8-3znQGLbarDXDZQtUyl9VAnA_XVe56_AJU5dM29aU9ShsMp_aSKE6mAlSUzwBud8mDrb5LUixqXWEEm2G-pAVElhBR8txEJQr2sLqqqcPYBU4vB87kyruVt9uPqXnoV8qBeN3JKkxJKdoDaCSvLuFYRCu_YEj8Gm2oVFkDAwVvQ4*%26id=b7071176-f416-4fb9-84a6-44fdc362b0e8%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/TWEFDmSUY38eh7rvM1NuW28nrcsTZuh_z7KYKSDXbt4=452> ." See our morning call section below for more.

In Europe, Jess Graham and team add TGS and Halma to the conviction, while removing Bayer, ABI and DT, in 'European Conviction List - Directors' Cut: July 2026 Update <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIkUO8ggJW0bSyKUj3O0h0zXllnUBAcaWQ5-pgDUVx7VP3ZqU65AACwDw5OoyfeLrB8AnhlgztDW_VBKXoF_J1sGO5rR_d8BoftMDkHmdA4IvJ5ioUMHTI-pO13NVg0NaCBR7mTokDwvn3syxldK2IFC_Sqf1dOLRF-2B3UGGwlAIF8nAMQhkY6piBjsyfdEsNBZf6PwDFzuhkN4Gk-g2ilM7QqJgMgQ8ugHuL9fzFJXMX4s1Tkh8Hx1RgmWPJ3TKIUJ9QGfbdDam24VhcbpUrKpJEOIgmfE6U*%26id=67b2fab9-d021-448c-8742-e20718e19a79%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/VgN4-e2G-xC6bxAfaA2dZdYUbRHG2M8ZXWHO-bjGC_A=452> ."

 	 

Looking Ahead 

We kick off July today, with a fresh ISM Manufacturing Index for a real-time snapshot of business sentiment. FDS and GIS report before the open today. GBX reports after the close. 

 	 

Performance of Global Indices (as of July 1, 2026)

9:00 AM: Fed Chairman Warsh speaks

US Morning Call for July 1, 2026: 

Click "here <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2Fcontent%2Fproducts%2Fd036cb0f-b058-490d-9e12-e7b8862ebdfd.html/2/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/l6AyrwHWAWpDd954f6mGIIQJ_c441b5OaR79jyQRP-E=452> " to listen to the US Morning Call.

US Conviction List - Directors' Cut, Kron/Herzog/Ramsden/Lee: July 2026 Update. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQL3nyua9JXYD2Bdcu-Zk-UOd-95XxC0A17E--0ZLJJ_oXbKjC-bAACw-MaF94xMwxR6xjj9WdmUWPrQD3oxyBy5vKLte8RA2fnoaBCqILme_deD9ACcOODSChrDfCBVIuHRYNzh2tyEuKy-Xifld38pKYbkVzhtcbZyva4F5enreE6V8dhLO_fb9bp-aVW3vZ_1qCWrwS9o3IJ6NxmJ6oyu4etvDbwulH72KfKh_PqWNLCs-x4SAT0ctEgdaOqlgjb6WP2DKYQKDdOANtgsPJR7BEjhDaG6b6M*%26id=2f096fb4-8219-4915-b6d5-86f134ccf93f%26utm_medium=pa/2/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/O06mlpguhpru6JQPqpmjUh6KoI5V98rJB0hVzcevREA=452> 

Research Unplugged, Herr/Herzog/Ramsden/Lee: US Conviction List - Directors' Cut: July 2026 Spotlight. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKm1Z5qJNoT9kWWdd7t9Cs1CgohjRAWkWSv0u8n_S7RoD6CgxJJAACwRE5y3Sd4LORapvtlOaiYxIzvXPR9IQF_L62ck9ro4dZPFXm_MUYNY00dQhlG7S8-3znQGLbarDXDZQtUyl9VAnA_XVe56_AJU5dM29aU9ShsMp_aSKE6mAlSUzwBud8mDrb5LUixqXWEEm2G-pAVElhBR8txEJQr2sLqqqcPYBU4vB87kyruVt9uPqXnoV8qBeN3JKkxJKdoDaCSvLuFYRCu_YEj8Gm2oVFkDAwVvQ4*%26id=b7071176-f416-4fb9-84a6-44fdc362b0e8%26utm_medium=pa/2/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/V2zuqgExZHSsBNiOSOVzMPQa7q1IMGANMJf9zKczVco=452> 

We add EL, NXT, and WFC to the US Conviction List, while removing BRX, DUK, and NVT. For EL, look for an innovation-led sales inflection, improving trends in China, and internal margin expansion initiatives to help restore confidence and support multiple expansion. For WFC, look for balance sheet expansion, capital markets strength, and cost discipline to support continued margin improvement and higher returns. We also highlight the key themes shaping the second half of 2026, including AI capex, broader market leadership, inflation and rates, equity supply, and earnings durability. For NXT, look for the companyвҖҷs evolution beyond solar tracking into a broader power technology platform to drive a stronger long-term growth and earnings profile.

Brown-Forman Corp. (BF_B, Sell), Herzog: Reinstate at Sell given an unfavorable risk/reward on a challenged whiskey outlook and muted top- and bottom-line growth. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLnvHQ8QYxAx_rgx_CId1n5XvMJxxBT6hMU8WPlvDK4DyozNIR8AACw8jc-q0bmWaXRpgKE5N8HNA_7y-fUzghvzml63mPILQrdnIUWtJy9XJNPSBygx7IMJzMjZ3t5kw7lyMLotlC6aRm9MalbyqoE78j8uFKhkMwi9KPmY7CV8c2byC8VpwNCBjrgp_v9qovW7NTiaVhqm8AEI3MEScr_rifvHT9U8fMAWLxJgVkWZ5AY7wH348oeiH0rQmXZIts5owu85uNoOsCoIGrPecLdFOsfCdBmVnc*%26id=2cb95e41-6288-4715-a93b-a0f12b1a9159%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1dab81de-74b16402-2f25-4dfb-94d2-fb648c8724ec-000000/ONoLGijpNWoNt3b79cvXtFDHdImDBlAeHq1Ljn6dmfo=452>  Reinstate BF_B at Sell as the spirits as we think the combination of structurally lower demand for alcohol, plus impending oversupply from aging whiskey barrels coming to market, will weigh on revenue growth and profitability. Look for ~350bps gross margin compression over the next two years. 12m price target, $24.

emails.body:
MACRO WEEK AHEAD: WEEK OF JULY 6, 2026


 

US CALENDAR

ECONOMICS

*       JUL 6 вҖ“ PMI-Srvcs (revision) and PMI-Composite (revision) at 9.45am ET. ISM-Srvcs (New Orders/Employment/Prices Paid) at 10am ET.

*       JUL 7 вҖ“ ADP Weekly Employment Change at 8.15am ET. Trade Balance, Imports and Exports at 8.30am ET. NATO Summit (July 7-8).

*       JUL 8 вҖ“ Mortgage Applications at 7am ET. FedвҖҷs Williams will speak at 9am ET. Wholesale Trade Sales and Inventories (revision) at 10am ET. FOMC Meeting Minutes at 2pm ET. Consumer Credit at 3pm ET.

*       JUL 9 вҖ“ Initial/Continuing Jobless Claims at 8.30am ET. Existing Home Sales at 10am ET.

*       JUL 10 вҖ“ N/A

 

EARNINGS

*       JUL 6 вҖ“ N/A

*       JUL 7 вҖ“ MKTX, PENG

*       JUL 8 вҖ“ AEHR

*       JUL 9 вҖ“ NRIX, PEP

*	JUL 10 вҖ“ DAL

 

GLOBAL CALENDAR

*       JUL 6 вҖ“ (Germany) Factory Orders at 2am ET. (United Kingdom) PMIs at 4.30am ET. (Japan) Labor Cash Earnings at 7.30pm ET. (China) Foreign Reserves at TBA ET.

*       JUL 7 вҖ“ (Japan) Leading Index at 1am ET. (Germany) IP at 2am ET. (Japan) BoP Current Account Balance and Trade Balance at 7.50pm ET.

*       JUL 8 вҖ“ (United Kingdom) RICS House Price Balance at 7.01pm ET. (Japan) Money Stock M2 and Money Stock M3 at 7.50pm ET. (China) CPI/PPI at 9.30pm ET. (China) Money Supply M2 at TBA ET.

*       JUL 9 вҖ“ (Japan) Machine Tool Orders at 2am ET. (Japan) PPI at 7.50pm ET.

*       JUL 10 вҖ“ (Germany) CPI/PPI (revision) at 2am ET. (France) CPI/PPI (revision) at 2.45am ET. (Canada) Net Change in Employment and Unemployment Rate at 8.30am ET.

 

NEAR TERM CATALYSTS вҖ“ CPI (Jul 14, Aug 12), ISM-Mfg (Jul 1, Aug 3), ISM-Srvcs (Jul 6, Aug 5), PMI-Mfg and PMI-Srvcs (Jul 24, Aug 21), NFP (Aug 7, Sep 4), PCE (Jul 30, Aug 26), Retail Sales (Jul 16, Aug 14). FED DATES: Jul 29, Sep 16

 

POLITICAL CATALYSTS

*       JUL 1 вҖ“ The required six-year formal joint review of the USMCA officially begins on July 1, 2026.

*       2H 2026 вҖ“ XiвҖҷs potential visit to the US (POLITICO <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13518533&ad=4540491421&h=pvh22aF&sig=SUN8DNqrew1mBw7qniqzk2ygUUa&v=2&url=https://www.politico.com/news/2025/10/30/amazing-meeting-trump-touts-progress-on-multiple-fronts-with-china-after-meeting-xi-00629580> ; Oct 30, 2025).

*       OCT 4 вҖ“ Brazil General Election.

*       NOV 1 вҖ“ Expiration of the one-year trade truce agreement between Trump and Xi.

*       NOV 3 вҖ“ US Mid-term Election.

*       DEC 15-16 вҖ“ G20 Summit in Miami (here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13518533&ad=4540491421&h=oTjVfhn&sig=B9mzqCpwzv8l6amsx2mh2TsgN2L&v=2&url=https://www.state.gov/releases/2025/12/united-states-hosts-first-g20-sherpa-meeting%23:~:text%3DUnder%2520President%2520Trump's%2520leadership%252C%2520the%2520United%2520States,14%252D15%252C%25202026%252C%2520at%2520Trump%2520National%2520Doral%2520Miami> )

emails.subject: Research Summary: CRCL INITIATION, DKNG, PENN, TSEM, VIAV, Supply Chain Data | Upcoming Corporate Access/Expert Calls
emails.body:
SusquehannaвҖҷs 2026 Industrials, Energy + Travel Conference August 5 (NY) + August 6 (Virtual) REGISTER HERE! <https://sig.dealogic.com/clientportal/Conferences/37/IndustrialsEnergyTravelConference/5/RegistrationForm> 

 

TodayвҖҷs Susquehanna Corporate Access/Expert Calls/FIFA WC

вҖў 10:00am ET: MELI Ad Tech & The World Cup: A Programmatic BuyerвҖҷs Perspective hosted by Jamie Friedman. Our speaker is a Programmatic Buyer for the Consumer Product Goods (CPG) practice at a major ad-tech company. In this role, he advises clients on campaign setup and optimization techniques for the LatAm market. Dial-in: 866-306-3230; Pin: 214018

вҖў 10:00am ET: CTV and Programmatic Ad Spend Trends (Jun/Jul) With a Leading Advertising Platform hosted by Shyam Patil. Relevant for: TTD, ROKU, MGNI, GOOGL. Dial-in: 866-283-7699; Pin:  666035

вҖў 12:00pm ET: Search and YouTube Trends (Jun/Jul) With a Large Global Holding Company hosted by Shyam Patil. Relevant for: GOOGL, AMZN. Dial-in: 866-283-7699; Pin: 439806

вҖў 1:00pm ET: AppLovin and Unity Ad Spending Trends (Jun/Jul) With a Leading Independent Agency hosted by Shyam Patil. Relevant for: APP, U. Dial-in: 866-283-7699; Pin: 148651

вҖў 10:00pm ET: Inventec hosted by Mehdi Hosseini. https://sig.zoom.us/j/95829139483?pwd=FX6MtT5x6gSnmSIOQT0tE7bHhgPQcG.1; Meeting ID: 958 2913 9483, Passcode: 612604

вҖў 12:00pm ET вҖ“ England vs. DR Congo рҹ“Қ Atlanta; 4:00pm ET вҖ“ Belgium vs. Senegal рҹ“Қ Seattle; 8:00pm ET вҖ“ United States  vs. Bosnia and Herzegovinaрҹ“Қ Santa Clara, California.

 

Susquehanna Research Summary

вҖў CRCL INITIATION | Jamie Friedman: Circle is a first mover in digital finance with significant long-term upside if USDC becomes the global digital dollar, diversifies beyond reserve income through payments, and expands its ecosystem via Arc Blockchain. However, the business remains heavily dependent on interest-rate-sensitive reserve income, shares economics with Coinbase, and trades at a premium valuation, leading us to initiate with a **Neutral** rating and **$69 price target** while awaiting a better entry point. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/8a68e5ac-1832-45a3-9374-88f7a032ed07> 

вҖў DKNG | Joe Stauff: DKNG's PM Launch + OSB Peer Outperformance Sets It Up For 2H26+ Upside. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/4035025f-6281-412c-aea4-85f06747d7dc> 

вҖў PENN | Joe Stauff: Raising Regional Target Multiple To 7x On PENN's Projects Inflection. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/85645f3e-1463-453d-b9e6-9d8bf29ed5b4> 

вҖў TSEM | Mehdi Hosseini: Tech Talk: Inside the Transceiver Manufacturing Supply Chain. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/0501755e-aa7e-4cc5-bdc9-7497c789ecd8> 

вҖў VIAV | Mehdi Hosseini: Calibrating Field Instrument Assumptions, Updating Estimates. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/93cc9c5f-6bfb-4902-b48c-e185784b0d95> 

вҖў Logistics & Trucking | Harrison Bauer: Framing Freight: High-Frequency Supply Chain Data. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/7b8d8785-16ae-4cf7-b8ec-1e4aa5e5e152> 

 

Market Summary

вҖў Maintain a constructive equity outlook for 2H26, supported by resilient economic growth, healthy consumer spending, easing inflation expectations, and a strong earnings backdrop, with any near-term volatility viewed as a buying opportunity.

вҖў AI remains the dominant investment theme, with continued upside expected across hyperscalers, semiconductors, memory, data centers, power infrastructure, and industrial AI as enterprise adoption accelerates and capital spending remains robust.

вҖў Broaden exposure beyond mega-cap AI leaders by adding healthcare, defense, power/grid infrastructure, industrial automation, and national security themes, while maintaining selective cyclical exposure.

вҖў Consumer fundamentals remain strong, with record household cash balances and resilient spending supporting above-trend GDP growth and corporate earnings despite higher interest rates.

вҖў Key risks to monitor include inflation and bond market volatility, geopolitical developments, AI positioning/crowding, and upcoming macro catalysts including labor market data, earnings season, and central bank decisions.

 

Macro & Market Outlook

вҖў Constructive outlook for 2H26: Markets remain supported by resilient economic growth, easing inflation expectations, robust corporate earnings, and continued AI-driven capital investment despite elevated geopolitical and policy uncertainty.

вҖў Near-term catalysts: Labor market data, July earnings season, inflation releases, Fed meetings, USMCA review, and geopolitical developments remain the key market drivers.

вҖў Market backdrop remains favorable: Strong corporate fundamentals and healthy consumer balance sheets continue to offset higher rates and geopolitical risks.

 

AI & Technology

вҖў AI investment cycle remains the dominant structural theme, with hyperscalers, semiconductors, memory, optical networking, power infrastructure, and data center equipment expected to remain primary beneficiaries.

вҖў US export restrictions on advanced AI models were eased, improving global commercialization opportunities while reinforcing AI as a strategic national priority.

вҖў Industrial AI consolidation is accelerating, highlighted by strategic acquisitions of AI/data platforms as companies seek to own industrial data layers required for enterprise AI deployment.

вҖў Enterprise AI adoption continues to broaden across information services, professional services, finance and education sectors.

 

Consumer & Economy

вҖў US consumer remains resilient, supported by record household cash balances, healthy corporate balance sheets, and continued consumption strength.

вҖў Retail spending growth remains materially above pre-pandemic trends, supporting above-trend nominal GDP and earnings growth.

вҖў Main macro risks remain inflation persistence and bond market volatility.

 

Investment Themes

вҖў Expect leadership to broaden beyond the largest AI names as earnings season progresses.

вҖў Preferred structural themes: AI infrastructure, Power generation & grid modernization, National security & defense, Industrial automation, Bioprocessing, Semiconductor supply chain, Quantum computing, Critical materials & energy security.

вҖў Growing investor interest in diversification within AI rather than concentration solely in mega-cap technology.

 

Key Risks

вҖў Higher-than-expected inflation or hawkish central bank actions.

вҖў Geopolitical escalation (Middle East, US-China).

вҖў Crowded AI positioning increasing volatility.

вҖў Elevated market leverage amplifying drawdowns.

вҖў Regulatory risks surrounding AI, digital platforms, and technology exports.

 

Bottom Line

вҖў Overall stance remains constructive: Favor equities over the medium term, led by AI investment, resilient consumer spending, strong earnings growth, and infrastructure investment. Expect higher volatility than in prior years, but view pullbacks as opportunities to selectively add exposure to long-term structural growth themes while maintaining appropriate portfolio hedges.

 

Sports

вҖў Happy Bobby Bonilla Day! Through today, the Mets have paid Bonilla $19.09M to not play the 2000 season. He is owed another $10.7M over the next 9 years.

вҖў Pete Crow-Armstrong of the Cubs is one of three players in MLB history to have a month with: .375+ batting average, .775+ slugging pct, 80+ total bases,15+ walks, 10+ home runs AND 5+ stolen bases. The others are Babe Ruth (June 1920, July 1920, May 1930) and Lou Gehrig (June 1930).

вҖў Only twice in American League history has a team had its starter strike out 9+ batters while allowing 2 or fewer hits in 3 straight games (reg or post). Both times it was the Tigers: Oct. 10-13, 2013 (Verlander, SГЎnchez, Scherzer) and June 28-30, 2026 (Flaherty, Mize, Skubal).

 

Corporate Access/Expert Calls                                                                     

TMT

Our speaker is a Programmatic Buyer for the Consumer Product Goods (CPG) practice at a major ad-tech company. In this role, he advises clients on campaign setup and optimization techniques for the LatAm market. Dial-in: 866-306-3230; Pin: 214018
