US MKT INTEL VIEW

We maintain our Tactical Bullish call despite the potential for some additional choppiness into month-end from both period-end rebalancing and the US / Iran conflict. We look for markets to stabilize and trade higher into earnings which unofficially kick off on July 14. A few thoughts followed by the Monetization Menu and the balance of the note.

 

*         NEW MONETIZATION MENU вҖ“ We think it makes sense to maintain the Tech / Cyclicals barbell while adding Healthcare exposure as low correlated long. Within Tech, we like AI, Memory, and Semis despite the recent pullback and elevated vol regime, so we would consider adding some hedges from other parts of Tech or factor hedges such as Beta / Momentum. Regarding Cyclicals, a combination of Airlines, Banks, Homebuilders, Retailers, and Transports; we would avoid Autos given USMCA risk. In Healthcare, we like broad-based exposure across the sector including Biotech, MedTech, and Pharma. Internationally, we Korea, parts of Latam, and Taiwan and would consider hedging with Indian Equities. For book hedges, consider SPX puts with July expiry.

*         DELTA-ONE TRADE IDEAS (Manish Sinha, June 26) вҖ“ (i) Short Beta (JPBPURE Index) and Momentum (JPMPURE Index) factors to hedge alpha dispersion risk and to play tightening financial conditions. (ii) Short Residual Vol aka ResVol (JPRVPURE Index) to hedge a hawkish Fed and worsening liquidity; (iii) Short Low Profitability Crowding (JPLPCRW2 Index) as a crowding hedge for AI-heavy exposure; (iv) +Long Dispersion Laggards (JPDISPL Index) vs. -Short Dispersion Winners (JPDISPW Index) to play a dispersion reversal.

*         OLD MONETIZATION MENU (JUNE 23) вҖ“ Unchanged WoW, we would like to return to the Tech and Cyclical barbell but stay nimble on the rate-sensitive or Hormuz reopening trades: stay long in the global AI theme with heavier weight on US vs. Asia. We do think the initial вҖңeverything rallyвҖқ should benefit Airlines, Housing and Retail but historical patterns reflect that the rally could be short-lived and more vulnerable to the shifting headline volatility. We like a tactical long in Financials into the July earnings season and expect it to catch up with the broader Cyclicals, as strong fundamental growth remains intact.

Wesfarmers Ltd. (WES.AX): Downgrade from Neutral to Sell on valuation 
	
1 July 2026 | 7:35PM AEST | Research | Equity 	
	
	
We downgrade WES from Neutral to Sell on valuation and a deteriorating risk reward. WES has significantly outperformed since 18th May +27% vs. the ASX200 +3%. We think this is partly on the back of the US market lead with HD +18%; BBY +31%; TGT +6%. We think the US retailer stock prices are reflecting a better consumer environment (lower gas prices, better retail spending, world cup driven enthusiasm) which is not the case in Australia (spending is slowing modestly, house prices are declining and petrol prices are set to increase). We are within 1-2% of consensus EPS across 2H26-FY28E but note recent tailwinds are moderating. FX has become less of a tailwind since mid-May (AUD/USD now 69c vs. 72c) and Lithium prices are moderating (also peeking in mid-May). WES now trades on ~34x GSE FY27E EPS vs the 10 year average 24x (or ~26x since COL demerger) and at close to a record premium to the ASX200. Our PT increases from $76 to $78 on higher market multiples; hence, with 14% downside we downgrade to Sell from Neutral.

emails.subject: TMT Daily | Rothschild & Co - AMZN, META, Meituan, SAP, DDOG, Semis datapoints, Ayar, WBD, TKO
emails.body:
<https://i1.cmail20.com/ei/d/28/FE1/AA0/193336/csfinal/RothschildandCoRGB.png> 
 
 

TMT Specialist Sales


Rothschild & Co TMT Daily

 
 <https://i1.cmail20.com/ei/d/28/FE1/AA0/193336/csfinal/AdobeStock_330323095-e1d277cfd690303b.jpeg> 
 

A little delayed today, but better late than never!

 

What caught our eye?


Thunderbirds are go!

 <https://i2.cmail20.com/ei/d/28/FE1/AA0/193336/csfinal/anth-99028a0a2804513c.png> 

Market Colour


Another day with bulls in the driving seat, SPX +80bps, NDX +1.7% and SOX +3.9%. Lots of price highs too incl AMD, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, PANW, APH. Nasdaq equalweight made a 2 year relative high. Factor-wise, high-beta momentum up 3.2%, like the SOX it banked 2 strong back-to-back days. Semi caps led the way again, continuing to trade up on the propsect of DRAM caapcity additions. Kospi down 2% with Hynix and Samsung down 3.4% and 5.6% respectively. Kospi is still > its 50d, so nothing to trouble the chart watchers yet. In macro, Oil stable above key $70 support. Dollar steady after recent pullback. Bitcoin holding on for dear life - if we get a break then $52k or even $50k could be next. US 10yr up 8bps yesterday and remains under pressure below 4.5%. 

12m Rel Highs: AMAT, AMD, LRCX, KLAC, PANW, ALAB, ASML
3m Rel Highs: APH. TTWO, PSON
12m Rel Lows: ORCL, CTSH, NFLX, DIS, TMUS, T, CAP, DTE, CLNX
3m Rel Lows: VZ, ORA, SCMN, VOD, BT, TEF, TELIA, TEL2


Software & Internet


Amazon (Neutral) / IT Services - AWS investing $1bn on FDEs
AWS is investing $1bn to launch a Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) unit, embedding AI experts into enterprise client teams. This Palantir-style approach bypasses traditional consulting to co-develop agentic AI solutions in days, leaving clients with self-sufficient systems running natively in their AWS environments. This is a strategic land-grab to solve the "last-mile" AI deployment bottleneck. By directly engineering workflows on its own infrastructure, AMZN is locking in customer stickiness. This is an incrementally negative read for IT services, where their consulting businesses are increasingly competing in a more fragmented market, with many of those emerging competitors better positioned to help the end customer

Meta (Buy) - Judge denies bid to dismiss state AG child addiction lawsuit
A federal judge rejected MetaвҖҷs final attempt to toss a multi-state lawsuit alleging Facebook and Instagram intentionally addict teens and violate privacy laws, clearing the path for an August 18 trial. Despite the alarming headline, this regulatory overhang is largely background noise. We believe MetaвҖҷs overarching legal strategy remains completely unchanged: systematically settling bellwether cases - like the recent Kentucky school district settlement - to kick the can down the road and delay sweeping precedents. An out-of-court settlement in this impending California trial is highly probable; failing to settle would be the actual surprise. 

Meituan (n/r) - launches 1.6T parameter model trained entirely on Chinese silicon
MeituanвҖҷs launch of LongCat-2.0, a 1.6-trillion-parameter LLM trained and run entirely on domestic Chinese chips, marks a potentially critical milestone. This is the first model of this scale to achieve full training and inference without Nvidia hardware. Lots of things to consider here, but it seems clear to me the regulatory protectionism around chip/manufacturing IP is having the opposite effect vs what the politicians intended!

SAP (Buy): management responsibilities formally adjusted ahead of CPO departure
Bloomberg reports (here <https://specialistsales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-woedt-hjyuihouk-r/> ) that Chief Product Officer Muhammad AlamвҖҷs product and engineering responsibilities will be split between CEO Christian Klein and COO Sebastian Steinhauser and that an external search for a new product lead is ongoing. The company announced in March that Alam would not renew his three year contract when it expires in March 2027, for personal reasons, and that Klein would assume his responsibilities in the short term. This article effectively formalises the temporary reporting structure during an external search. The CPO role is important for the equity story because it includes oversight of AI product development, where SAP has lagged peers in direct AI monetisation вҖ“ the calibre and background of an external hire will therefore be closely watched as a potential indicator of a shift in strategy. 

Datadog (Buy) - Acquires Adaptive ML 
The platform has acquired Adaptive ML, a Paris-based AI startup developed the world's first reinforcement learnings operations (RLOps), enabling enterprises to build and deploy their own agents based on open-source LLMs. The platform centres around long-term adaptability and continious learning; the founders had previously contributed to projects such as Falcon and BLOOM, holding R&D roles at Hugging Face. The team will join Datadog AI research, focusing on technical problems and collaborating with product & engineering teams to develop new capabilities. Deal terms remain undisclosed, with a reported $100m valuation in the seed round raised in 2024.


Tech Hardware


Semi-cycle Tracker
Smartphone users face higher handset prices as chip supply remains constrained into 2027.  ASICs and GPUs are ramping adoption of 2nm processes for their newest chips. Qualcomm (n/r) and MediaTek (n/r) reportedly shifted their smartphone chips to 2nm in 2026, after 3nm capacity became crowded with AI chips. This could lead to another leg of smartphone price increases going forward. RDBN View: We view that AI-driven demand has already increased prices across servers and PCs. However, the impact on end-market dollar revenue growth has been relatively modest. A separate data point worth flagging is that Taiwanese component maker Yageo (n/r) expects strong earnings this year, driven by its 50% revenue share from capacitor products. Price increases in passive components is underway as AI demand continues. YageoвҖҷs production of multilayer ceramic capacitors (MLCC) utilisation rates have reached 80% with premium MLCCs seeing 90% utilisation. RDBN View: We expect price increases across capacitors (including MLCCs, tantalum, aluminium electrolytic and film capacitors) 

Ayar Labs (n/r) вҖ“ latest funding round implies c$5bn valuation
The Information is reporting that Ayar Labs is raising at least $200m at a valuation of around $5bn. This is one-third higher than the $3.75bn valuation in its previous funding round a few months ago. Ayar Labs focuses on using high-speed, light-based connections instead of traditional copper wiring to move data between AI and memory chips in data centres. As of early June, Ayar had joined the NVIDIA NVLink Fusion ecosystem вҖ“ making its products optically and electrically compatible with NVIDIA optical and SerDes technologies.  Ayar LabsвҖҷ TeraPHY Optical engine places I/O optical chiplets on the edges of the chips themselves вҖ“ shifting the optical boundary closer to the source of compute. Ayar claims their chiplets deliver 10x higher bandwidth than conventional copper and optical pluggables. Questions of reliability and scalability of packaging have dogged CPO вҖ“ and we would expect AyarвҖҷs optical chiplets to face similar questions. Nevertheless, shifting the optical boundary to the chip edge, in conjunction with their SuperNova Light Source product suggest that significant strategic questions around SerDes moats and photon production will likely unfold over the coming 1-2 years.

NVDA (Buy) - startup 'Etched' raises capital
AI chip startup Etched has raised $800 million from backers including Jane Street and TSMC. Etched was founded in 2022 and designs chips to run artificial intelligence models at inference. Etched is reportedly testing its products and says it has signed $1 billion of sales contracts.  Etched claims it offers a low-voltage chip architecture that combine HBM and SRAM memory. The companyвҖҷs rack-scale product includes networking and cooling elements. 


Media


Warner Bros. Discovery (Neutral).  UK raises media ownership concern over Paramount deal
UK Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy yesterday wrote to Paramount saying she is вҖңminded to interveneвҖқ on public interest grounds in the companyвҖҷs purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery, although she has not taken a final decision.  Her concerns centre around needs for вҖңsufficient pluralityвҖқ of view in news media and control of media enterprises.  A combined Paramount/Warner would rank number three in terms of overall viewing, ahead of both Sky and Channel 4.  A Bloomberg article also reported the combined entity will hold a significant proportion of childrenвҖҷs linear channels.  Paramount has a deadline of 6th July to respond and has already assured the company it has вҖңno plans for material changes to linear TV channels and on-demand services operated by WBD insofar as they relate to the UKвҖқ.  The UKвҖҷs Competition and Markets Authority has already started an initial investigation into the deal and will decide by August 7th whether to launch an in-depth probe.  The deal has been cleared by numerous regulators around the world leaving the UK and EU some of the last hurdles, although the EU appears to be on track. Overall, we continue to believe the deal will ultimately receive approval, but this news, along with the recent legal action from California, and potentially New York, suggest Paramount will miss its end of Q3 closing target.

TKO (Buy) вҖ“ completes $800m buyback
First announced in March, TKO has completed its accelerated $800m Class A share repurchase programme, acquiring c4.2m shares in line with its mid-June target. The company remains active under its 10b5-1 trading plan and expects to repurchase a further $200m of Class A shares by 31 August, completing its initial $1bn buyback authorisation. A further $1bn repurchase programme, announced alongside Q1 results in May, remains available.

emails.subject: Apple Inc. "UBS Evidence Lab Inside: iPhone purchase intent mixed as the US..."
emails.body:
Our latest survey results suggest that Apple Intelligence is not driving upgrade activity, consistent with our expectations. 
 <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h79912267,3b41e15,4be20007> 	 	 Global Research 
01 July 2026 	
Apple Inc.	в—Ҹ	(AAPL.O) Neutral	 	
UBS Evidence Lab Inside: iPhone purchase intent mixed as the USвҖүand Europe rise but China falls <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h79912267,3b41e15,4be20008&e=ZGlkPUFDNy1FLTYyMTM1ODI5Jm9mZl9pZD1BQzctRS0xNTg5NTg2OTc0Jm1hPVk0Njc4NTM0OTQ5Nzg1QTQ1JmNhbXBfaWQ9RU06VU5LVzoyMDI2LTA3OjAxOkM&s=u3WmalAp45CmDWbhm2Tn2kCXD9-slErlGCjyCKjCjpc>  	
Our latest survey results suggest that Apple Intelligence is not driving upgrade activity, consistent with our expectations. 


Read Report   > <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h79912267,3b41e15,4be20009&e=ZGlkPUFDNy1FLTYyMTM1ODI5Jm9mZl9pZD1BQzctRS0xNTg5NTg2OTc1Jm1hPVk0Njc4NTM0OTQ5Nzg1QTQ1JmNhbXBfaWQ9RU06VU5LVzoyMDI2LTA3OjAxOkM&s=TAiWgxTc3xhfTVIEjw52eEBZSDxQQ1ufilJefj-CT6g> 		
'12-month purchase intent' indicates solid interest in the USвҖүand Europe 

UBS Evidence Lab (> Access Dataset <https://neo.ubs.com/r/7/?id=h79912267,3b41e15,4be2000a&e=ZGlkPUFDNy1FLTYyMTM1ODI5Jm9mZl9pZD1BQzctRS0xNTg5NTg2OTc2Jm1hPVk0Njc4NTM0OTQ5Nzg1QTQ1JmNhbXBfaWQ9RU06VU5LVzoyMDI2LTA3OjAxOkM&s=VfnPcBJGK3bHmFjY7hpzayATVS3oG-UUgcBUNxdLioQ> ) recently surveyed over 7,500 smartphone users in the U.S., U.K., China, Germany, and Japan to gauge smartphone demand. The key takeaways from the survey pertaining to Apple:

1.	12-month global iPhone purchase intent in the US rose ~300 bps YoY to ~20%, as China fell ~100 bps YoY to ~15%. The UK (+600 bps YoY) and Germany (+400 bps YoY) saw material increases as the change in Japan (+100 bps YoY) was more modest in our view.
2.	The average age of an iPhone in the user base downticked to ~22.9 months from last survey (-0.8 monthsвҖүHoH) but still near survey highs.
3.	Apple Intelligence is not driving upgrade activity as total respondents indicating that they would upgrade sooner for Apple Intelligence features declined to ~24% (-500 bps HoH) while those noting no impact on their purchasing decision rose to ~31% (+300 bps HoH). 
4.	Total 'net interest' (the delta between 'interested' and 'not interested') in an Apple foldable declined ~100 bps from the prior survey to ~39%, but the favorability spread over a foldable smartphone rose ~600 bps HoH. 

Apple foldable interest ticks lower but spread widens vs foldable smartphone 

As Apple is expected to launch a foldable iPhone at its annual September event, we highlight interest in both a foldable smartphone and a foldable specifically by Apple. 'Net interest' in a foldable smartphone notably declined ~600 bps HoH to (8)% in aggregate. While 'net interest' in an Apple foldable also declined (-100 bps HoH), we note that the favorability spread of a foldable specifically by Apple over a foldable smartphone widened by ~600 bps HoH to ~48%. We believe that the sentiment towards an Apple foldable is directionally positive for iPhone demand, particularly as AI features recently announced at WWDC26 are unlikely to be a material driver in our view. 

Our Sept-26 quarter and FY26 iPhone forecasts are unchanged 

We estimate Sept-26 quarter and FY26 iPhone rev / units of $56.0B / 55.1MвҖү(Consensus $55.3B / 57.9M) and $251.6B / 261.6M (Consensus $250.8B / 257.7M), respectively. 

Valuation: Our PT of $296 is 30x our CY27 EPS est of $9.86 

Our $296 price target is ~30x our CY27 EPS est of $9.86. At 30x, Apple shares would be trading roughly in line with the trailing 3-year average and a 1 turn discount to the trailing 12-month average, reflecting solid demand balanced by uncertainty around the company's AI strategy.

emails.subject: EG Geo-technology, EU: AI dependency draws Europe toward Washington despite tech sovereignty push
emails.body:
Geo-technology, EU


01 Jul 2026 05:13 EDT

	  	 
 	
 	 
AI dependency draws Europe toward Washington despite tech sovereignty push

 	
 	 
 	 ________________________________

 	 
 	 
*	The Fable/Mythos shut-off highlighted EuropeвҖҷs dependency on Washington for access to US frontier models, leaving it exposed to unpredictable White House decisions. 
*	The EU will remain far behind the US in total compute, and a megaproject to build an Anthropic-level lab is almost certainly politically and economically unfeasible. 
*	European leaders will seek to anchor model access guarantees in the transatlantic allianceвҖ” even as the bloc pursues tech sovereignty in parallel.

 	 
 	 ________________________________

 	 
 	 
European countries are now dependent on a volatile White House for access to frontier models (see note: Anthropic cut-off will boost tech sovereignty efforts abroad <https://library.eurasiagroup.net/go/wfpub_eurasia_26606_bfa35aab/wfpub_eurasia_26606_bfa35aab.html?token=dd305963-00b2-4e59-b92e-1bd3f80cc27c&extension=.html&click=15fc251c-f8a6-4c28-8606-31aab923c628> , 19 June 2026). The recent Fable/Mythos shut-off appeared to confirm a key prediction in Europe 2031 <https://europe2031.ai/summary/> , a viral essay laying out a scenario in which Europe is reduced to a vassal of the US due to AI. The paper was widely circulated in European tech circles and amongst officials. Shortly afterwards, reports emerged that the White House had struck a deal with OpenAI to vet access to its most advanced model, GPT-5.6.

This will further motivate Europe and other middle powers to find ways to ensure access to cutting-edge AI. At the G7, European leaders and AI CEOs pitched a US-led coalition for AI governance and a вҖңtrusted partnerвҖқ scheme. But there was reportedly little enthusiasm from the US government. The European Commission is preparing an action plan to ensure that advanced AI cyber models are available in the EU, and help European industry defend against them. 

Some European policymakers nonetheless fear the cyber capabilities of the most advanced models such as Mythos could amount to a decisive strategic edge, analogous to nuclear weapons. If so, neither access agreements with Washington nor domestic вҖңnear-frontierвҖқ models may be good enough, leaving the EU with no alternative but to invest hundreds of billions of euros in AI chips and datacenters and its own frontier model. However, the EU is highly unlikely to pursue this вҖңmoonshotвҖқ.

Europe will not catch up in the compute race absent unprecedented new private sector investment. The centerpiece of BrusselsвҖҷs AI strategy, the AI gigafactories, will not begin construction until next year, while their financing will depend on tense and already well-advanced negotiations over the next seven-year budget. But these will pale in comparison to the largest data centers under construction in the US and the Gulf. Building world-class AI clusters will therefore depend on attracting many more big-ticket private sector investments like SoftbankвҖҷs вӮ¬75 billion data center project in northern France, and upgrading the blocвҖҷs energy grid (see note: Energy constraints will limit but not collapse EuropeвҖҷs AI infrastructure growth <https://library.eurasiagroup.net/go/wfpub_eurasia_26704_107d2907/wfpub_eurasia_26704_107d2907.html?token=af720f04-ec50-48a9-be97-5055154101f5&extension=.html&click=15fc251c-f8a6-4c28-8606-31aab923c628> , 30 June 2026). 



A megaproject to build a European Anthropic or OpenAI is almost certainly out of reach. Besides the compute requirements, such an initiative would also require poaching elite researchers from around the world with sizeable compensation packages. No funds have been set aside for this, while the bloc must also finance other pressing priorities. The winner of the commissionвҖҷs Frontier AI Grand Challenge, a consortium led by the Italian company Domyn, will build a model with more than 400 billion parameters, compared to 10 trillion for Mythos.

Brussels will instead focus on AI adoption, deregulation and industrial applications. President Ursula von der Leyen has met on multiple occasions with a group of leading European tech CEOs, who have emphasized deregulation, consolidation and completing the single market, with sovereignty a secondary concern. While these would improve competitiveness, it would not deliver frontier-level capabilities. Her new industrial AI advisor, Siemens Chairman Jim Hagemann Snabe, will issue recommendations in September. 

Mistral will remain EuropeвҖҷs best asset for sovereign AI. Having focused on enterprise deployment over top-tier model development, it has fallen in global LLM rankingsвҖ”but will release a powerful new model this summer, and is backed by major European tech players such as ASML. As the only top LLM-builder in Europe left standing following the acquisition of Aleph Alpha by CanadaвҖҷs Cohere, the company will benefit from the uncertainty surrounding access to leading US offerings. 

While Chinese open-weight models such as ZhipuвҖҷs GLM 5.2 are a high-performing alternative for businesses concerned about access to US models, there is similarly no guarantee that future releases will also be freely available (see note: Zhipu's gains point to escalating competition and supply-chain decoupling <https://library.eurasiagroup.net/go/wfpub_eurasia_26674_2bc43336/wfpub_eurasia_26674_2bc43336.html?token=729830f2-91f9-4eac-92c0-63953c8e8cd1&extension=.html&click=15fc251c-f8a6-4c28-8606-31aab923c628> , 26 June 2026). Chinese foundation models are also likely not compliant with the EU AI Act, according to some EU officials, and significant questions remain open around their safety and security.

But if true sovereignty means having capabilities on par with the very best LLMs, Europe will fall short. Even Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg have failed to match Anthropic and OpenAI, despite their immense resources. A middle power coalition along the lines suggested by Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney would likewise struggle.

European leaders are now committed to pursuing tech sovereignty. But when it comes to frontier AI, they will likely conclude that their best option is to build leverage elsewhere in the AI stack, and use diplomacy to anchor model access in the transatlantic allianceвҖ”binding Europe closer to Washington just as the bloc pursues greater strategic autonomy.

 	 
 
 	 
EG Geo-technology

geo-technology@eurasiagroup.net <mailto:geo-technology@eurasiagroup.net> 

 	 
 	 
Nick Reiners

Senior Analyst, Geo-technology

reiners.ne@eurasiagroup.net <mailto:reiners.ne@eurasiagroup.net> 

 	 
 	 
Heather West

Practice Head, Geo-technology

west@eurasiagroup.net <mailto:west@eurasiagroup.net> 

 	 
 	 
Xiaomeng Lu

Director, Geo-technology

+1 202.316.7259

lu@eurasiagroup.net <mailto:lu@eurasiagroup.net> 

 	 
 	

 <https://apps.apple.com/us/app/eurasia-group/id1477168765?ls=1>        <https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.worldflow.wmd.research.eurasia>  	
 	 PDF or Print  <https://library.eurasiagroup.net/document/savepdf/wfpub_eurasia_26718_6442b95f/wfpub_eurasia_26718_6442b95f.html?token=dd805725-2ec9-490d-ad69-25f711293a7d&extension=.html&click=15fc251c-f8a6-4c28-8606-31aab923c628>  | Get the EG iOS <https://apps.apple.com/us/app/eurasia-group/id1477168765>  or Android <https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.worldflow.wmd.research.eurasia&hl=en_GB>  mobile Apps for content on the go

Explanation of Political Trajectories (ie, вҖңTrajectoriesвҖқ)

Eurasia Group's (EG) Political Trajectories are indicators of the net impact of political factors on the macro business environment in a country over specific timeframes. For each country, two Trajectories are provided, a short-term (6 months) and a long term assessment (24 months). Trajectories are assigned by Eurasia GroupвҖҷs country analysts through a structured qualitative process. Each Trajectory represents a net assessment, indicating how political changes in a country impact the macro business environment, defined as the economic and investment climate in the country. Once initiated, Trajectories are updated both periodically and as events warrant. Trajectories may be suspended from time to time if there is no active analyst opinion or analyst coverage, but the opinion or coverage is expected to resume. Trajectories can be:

Positive: Political factors in the country are anticipated to have a positive impact on the macro business environment

Neutral: Political factors in the country are anticipated to have a neutral or negligible impact on the macro business environment

Negative: Political factors in the country are anticipated to have a negative impact on the macro business environment

Disclaimers: Trajectories are solely for informational purposes and should not be relied upon as investment advice. Trajectories do not indicate asset price movements, and have no value in forecasting market prices. Trajectories are based on data and analysis we deem to be reliable; they are not guaranteed as to accuracy or completeness. The Trajectories indicate the political outlook in a single country, over time. Trajectories do not address sector-specific or city/regional political risks that are often different than the country-level outlook. Trajectories represent an analystвҖҷs net qualitative assessment of a country's political outlook. EG sales, analytic staff and products may provide analysis reflecting different opinions than those expressed by the Trajectories.

emails.subject: markets / macro :: observations on H1'26.
emails.body:
1. at the halfway mark, S&P has generated a total return of +10%. while thatвҖҷs somewhat anticlimactic relative to the convexity of recent years -- and

1. at the halfway mark, S&P has generated a total return of +10%.  while thatвҖҷs somewhat anticlimactic relative to the convexity of recent years -- and it lags equal-weight S&P (+12%) and small cap (+23%) -- the fact is +10% is very solid relative to the long arc of market history (and is stronger than the first half of last year). 

  

2. hereвҖҷs another framing: if S&P closes the year over the 7530 level, that will mark FOUR consecutive years of double digit returns.  in the 68-year history of the index, that has only happened ONCE before (technically, it was five straight years -- from 1995 through 1999, of course).  

  

3. yes, some high profile stocks have sold off meaningfully from their respective highs.  with that said, consider this: beneath the surface of S&P, 44 stocks are up > 50% YTD (in contrast, only 6 are down > 50%).  in addition, 29 are up > 75% (none are down that much).  finally, 22 are up > 100% (as you would probably guess, none are down that much).  so, there have been some enormous moves within the index, which have clearly and heavily favored the right tail.   

  

4. the AI buildout is rapidly accelerating, which has clearly accrued to the immense benefit of the AI infrastructure providers, and is a theme thatвҖҷs shot through this entire note.  consider these YTD returns of GS custom baskets: memory +250% ... data centers +115% ... AI semis +101%.  with that said, the market narrative hasnвҖҷt necessarily followed a straight line (the most recent development being tension between US frontier models and Chinese open-weight).  

  

5. a rich opportunity set doesnвҖҷt mean the ride has been easy.  again, trading is about navigating the path, and the downside gaps in March -- or the upside gaps in April -- bear witness to the degree of difficulty.  away from stocks, the macro landscape hasnвҖҷt been all that straightforward, given the swing in front end rates (from around 50 bps of expected cuts in 2026 to 37 bps of expected hikes), the dollar (from rampant bearishness in March to general bullishness today) and commodities (the SoH was closed for nearly four months, yet oil has given back most all of its rally).  

  

6. point-to-point, North Asian equity markets have stolen the show this year.  measured in total returns and local currency, hereвҖҷs the very top of the H1вҖҷ26 leaderboard: Korea (KOSPI) +102% ... Taiwan (TWSE) +62% ... Japan (NKY) +40%.  while each of these markets has a specific story to tell, the throughline is they all sit at the coalface of the AI infrastructure story.  for what itвҖҷs worth, note that Asia also commands the very bottom of the global leaderboard: India (NIFTY) -7% ... Hong Kong (HSI) -10% ... Indonesia (JCI) -33%.  

  

7. one additional point on Japan to underline: NKY rallied 37% in Q2, which is the single biggest rally in any given quarter in market history (data back to 1970).  

  

8. one additional point on Korea that I find interesting: for as good as the headline returns have been, this year has also seen five intraday trading halts, which is nearly half of the total halts from this entire century (thanks Matt Mason).  spot up / vol up, by any other name. 

  

9. a final point on Asia.  for a discussion of what happened in the first half and what we expect in the second half, this weekвҖҷs podcast is with Tim Moe, who brings to bear 40 years of experience in the region: link <https://marquee.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmarquee.gs.com%2Fresearch%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQL5byDr-pJNZaIkf7V6mo_UcS2cGRDdgm6_bAJs_31FQZGEyThIAAEAu0TRSie9qFD1JQi-x94EoiPAa6b-W-s5K0qjHG_WjDkBvg9ydQKGZat19JUS9eQxHvDCI3l4VwOzephdctUu_CyxAEWqGRr46VdgL49N-mTwC_djx8KD9fszSTHiY7jOrAB-CvL9BbfE6HMjag7WQlTz1pgZbu_ck5GxY10wPSV5RmZWnEZU2i55n1Bk5yqqsyY3dXbDCbm1KzlodJKzv5BHwIqUMwJ-vyqU-xCKMRctWcn0n5upIXkInlaGAAcb8IV_4SX2FlF0ApOON6HrHiqsOkR6XD6MRzeTV3ikEyPJNZNksv9_Ih6SyzYxqWakNAq7Z3yq2p88vcUeyWOgjA**%26id=%2Fs%2Fcontent%2Fmarkets%2F56286519-6dc4-4a31-b911-f3f1d3ee82de%3Fmq_utm_source=article%26mq_utm_medium=email%26mq_utm_content=emailBody%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1cf45357-0ea19c89-9359-4649-9bdb-13bafc4bf5b2-000000/A9qeAqTEoTZRZOkV6DHfZmdEZH-AdEvNMUv02BeAw1A=452> .  to summarize his views in one line: stronger-for-longer markets, but with an increase in volatility. 

  

10. what else stuck out in the first half?  I certainly didnвҖҷt see it coming, but small cap has performed masterfully, and is tracking for its largest annual outperformance over S&P since 2003.  ex-post, I chalk this up to a few factors: the cyclical growth environment (this is evident in several parts of the market, simply pull up a chart of CAT) ... some gearing towards biotech and select AI infrastructure beneficiaries (mostly the form of power and data center plays) ... as well as positioning (recall a record short base in RTY futures just one month ago).  

  

11. in an environment of high dispersion (and, thus, low correlation), the momentum factor continued to shine.  after a 48% rally in 2024 and a 30% rally in 2025, our flagship pair trade is up 57 YTD%.  without question, the perceived set of leaders and laggards from AI has been stimulative for this factor.  with all that said, IвҖҷd be remiss if I didnвҖҷt note that the realized volatility of this factor increased noticeably in the past few months, and that the fundamental community has plenty of leverage to it (link <https://marquee.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmarquee.gs.com%2Fresearch%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLEVMfWrw1h5HwNTt0oxvePV0-PTBANMmnxc0bAjHSvqj6EjhfNAAEAQ8rMLvKKokx1hs2OoBpqnwZt9uojiUWYPko5wO7uBqxX1hXK-qccklc1GDNBDpRPNUzCyMttqsPxuqlME9zbodu0BKeB36LvdtWUJ6MOSD6xJeS-TIHC6a0AQc65JYhEFs-xb8WeEEvvCiGrKJ1cS99zZgNBvPsVtvv7NaPa1U_13HGEyEuTOZVsJI_oK2fzFcbAxqnHi5_hx4-yF9K8pFmEmKoPzoIWL3w7C-5ix_Kx4z_12ScQ8cj3kkvtCWN080VCojaVze5KUYJ0FW8TdTWI6PWSgSrNxPOhcyfdc8H4qmMBSbaPbHs5jYkFaJG6OiAyudakG_W0Z9_DhNoUCA**%26id=%2Fs%2Fcontent%2Fmarkets%2F983cc511-35c9-4fdf-a0fe-e2df174a0b8a%3Fmq_utm_source=article%26mq_utm_medium=email%26mq_utm_content=emailBody%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1cf45357-0ea19c89-9359-4649-9bdb-13bafc4bf5b2-000000/1fL3NLzm0z5loYhZtyXmk5nw8p7mNtA-qFEqvfZe7Vg=452> ). 

  

12. coming off their best year since 2009, IвҖҷd argue the hedge fund community continues to do their job well (in turn, so does the GS hedge fund VIP basket, +22% YTD).  we track nine distinct hedge fund cohorts, and all have produced positive YTD returns, with significant strength in fundamental long/short and the macro space (link <https://marquee.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmarquee.gs.com%2Fresearch%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKx4soTOe7A5Kdj17t9hxbgm6GoeRCc8vsdbGMczvZt3cN1jczMAAEAsLbXW0k8iOUut_5JSxKXk90LRSz4yN2TBeQhDl-8lrjHrWf5zHOcX2ca-2bz42DQ49_0pfuFdMaqZCq_FSq9f3pMKMtDQjlfKbKdwSC9FHLvlaTvR0kWxwiF7wynDs2NRKZ9rmrUuNQjK0k6h-GRYkax1Funu0WUGl7B9Q2BDvBd-UL-qisbLF5pTcvbpALEQ4dCbk5hv4aUIaG8XLCFAg38AU7g7Ks-VGF5BD4gbRhk_A6l2vQepCEuQHFP2r3oOibEvxjT9v4pY7lKp8S9u4SwMUUPKZ3ed5kmDCpid9Hl_4Q5ilvVl8n37eTZl_9EBrCG6nmqrIKc2GDylXzLMQ**%26id=%2Fs%2Fcontent%2Fmarkets%2F7ead6462-ef57-412a-a494-df3d43bf0cb2%3Fmq_utm_source=article%26mq_utm_medium=email%26mq_utm_content=emailBody%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1cf45357-0ea19c89-9359-4649-9bdb-13bafc4bf5b2-000000/Yn0vTimle82LFDAw7FZcucEmvFSCXsorkiR8oVXF27E=452> ).  given how fast things are changing these days -- such that predicting what the world will look like in 5 or 10 years is an exceedingly difficult task -- I think thereвҖҷs a lot to be said for active managers who can move their feet and quickly adjust risk. 

  

13. in no particular order, some additional quick points before I wrap things up: 

  

i. while the US tech space has broadly done very well once again -- witness the SOX is up 102% YTD, and just recorded its best single quarter in history -- I admit that I didnвҖҷt see this coming: Magnificent Seven flat YTD; S&P 493 +16%. 

  

ii.  earlier in the year, I borrowed an analog from John Arnold that illustrated how powerful an equal pairing of tech stocks PLUS energy stocks has been.  hereвҖҷs the mark-to-market since the end of 2020: a daily rebalanced mix of those two sectors has cumulatively returned +204% -- on zero down years -- which compares to +114% for S&P.  

  

iii. IвҖҷd be remiss if I didnвҖҷt mention that European equities have done just fine so far this year -- SXXP has returned 11%.  as is often the case, Europe finds a way to muddle through better-than-expected. 

  

iv. for all of the action these days, and for all of the new issue thatвҖҷs coming off the assembly line, I find it notable that the IG credit index has barely budged from where it was at the start of the year.  

  

v. gold and silver were enjoying a Nantucket Sleigh Ride to higher all-time highs ... then peaked immediately before the news that Mr. Warsh would be handed the keys ... and have been trending lower ever since.  while the first leg of that was a washing out of speculative enthusiasm, and the second leg was a function of the Iran war, I suspect the last leg is more fundamental: the days of the Fed responding to every major problem with money printing are perhaps over.  

  

vi. in the context of how the US economy is constantly creating and destroying jobs, this was the best stat I saw in H1: in 1960, there were around 1.5mm workers in the US healthcare industry; today, there are over 18mm. 

  

vii. as many folks are rightly mentioning, I suspect the politics of AI are going to become a much bigger storyline in the second half.  in that vein, I thought this recent quote was cause for consideration: вҖңthe AI industry today is the only industry in America that has less regulations than sandwich shops.вҖқ 

  

viii. one note on my personal use of AI: I find the advancement in the tools that I use to analyze the markets is nothing short of stunning.  for example, I have a few different mechanisms to run market analogs which have a depth -- and a voice -- that is totally remarkable.  with that said, IвҖҷll repeat a past pledge: every single character in this email is written by me, unless noted with a specific quote, and that will always be the case.  

  

14. stepping back, this is the most dynamic market environment that I can recall in my 27 years.  the drivers are not hard to identify: weвҖҷre witnessing immense technological change -- alongside significant political / geopolitical fragmentation.  in practice, it is a tumultuous time -- while also a profoundly pro-cyclical time (as both the public sector and the private sector are on the gas).  to repeat a line from a few weeks back, it is both the rate of change AND the scale of deal flow that have been so stimulative for the marketplace. 




15. a few final thoughts as we look forward.  said plainly, weвҖҷre witnessing the greatest industrial undertaking that the private sector has even attempted.  in turn, an enormous amount of market cap has been created (and impaired) in the expectation that a productivity miracle will follow.  and thatвҖҷs where the setup for the second half gets so interesting.  I thought this take from our macro strategy team was on point (link <https://marquee.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKzzUeYs0hZK8b7oVSJ8Hec4u-r-RCxH1sZ2Rmlv9fECmXDYxiSAADAJXHASTA7lyD_vA8PEjalbT5xokA_vjZpOtA6Sq9N8W5arF7_a0LPps9MzDCw-RgdxCT_7s6aGdoijaRZnO8aWDxgm9xALYEqSpyq4j-KKz_OTs0aclNHFt0MDFcKvZosyK8vLEh0KLmiaio6RoQ-VBKPzDzYAQKIC7nzXUhc3hLNy0sxt6I94gdV7lkbgtR1cCsAHSpI-X63hifOnUzX0IUBisYTXLTy_agj4mg4apqacQq4hRBnY7ACo7gu_x6w%26id=1934f841-17e4-4176-8abe-f93ee2186464%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1cf45357-0ea19c89-9359-4649-9bdb-13bafc4bf5b2-000000/3mSV2fCsDH0TjOPn0SLii92_YVoHqUSLlHqXUxvDZ-U=452> ): 

  

to square the value added in AI-related names with the potential gains from AI for the broad economy needs increasingly optimistic (though not yet implausible) assumptions.  the growing risk is that the market is overestimating the persistence of above-average earnings, especially those generated by the investment boom itself.  as long as that investment boom looks secure, we expect the positive impact on near-term earnings to dominate valuation concerns, but pricing makes us more vulnerable to challenges to that optimistic view. 

  

16. working from there, my expectation is global equities will trend higher in H2вҖҷ26.  this view is rooted in the house call that double digit S&P earnings growth will persist through 2027.  at the same time, my instinct is the trading environment will have only more of вҖңlive by the sword / die by the swordвҖқ feel to it.  why?  the ante has been upped everywhere I look: record deficit spending by the G-3 (and beyond) ... an unrelenting commitment to AI capex by the hyperscalers ... the engagement of leverage by the trading community (witness $570bn of global exposure in levered ETFs).  in the end, long delta / long vol was my general posture for most of the past few months -- taking this entire note together, IвҖҷm sticking with that construct for the second half.    

  

17. as we approach the 250th birthday of my country, IвҖҷll end this note with two quotes from the annual letters of the great Warren Buffett (link <https://marquee.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fberkshirehathaway.com%2Fletters%2Fletters.html/1/0100019f1cf45357-0ea19c89-9359-4649-9bdb-13bafc4bf5b2-000000/F3iHiHMn1098i2Mb7F6sUMbvtqVniEdosAeCAKX7L3Y=452> ): 

  

i. this from 2008: вҖңin the 20th century alone, we dealt with two great wars (one of which we initially appeared to be losing); a dozen or so panics and recessions; virulent inflation that led to a 21.5% prime rate in 1980; and the Great Depression of the 1930s, when unemployment ranged between 15% and 25% for many years.  America has had no shortage of challenges.  without fail, however, we've overcome them.  in the face of those obstacles -- and many others -- the real standard of living for Americans improved nearly seven-fold during the 1900s, while the Dow Jones Industrials rose from 66 to 11,497.вҖқ 

  

ii. this from 2020: "Charlie and I have always acknowledged that Berkshire could not have achieved its results in any locale other than America ... our country would have done splendidly in the years since 1965 without Berkshire.  absent our American home, however, Berkshire would never have come close to becoming what it is today."

emails.subject: JEF SILK ROAD | UPGRADING USHIO | SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS  | KOREA INVESTMENT HOLDINGS | THE BEAR CASE FOR AI | 1 JULY 2026
emails.body:
This material is a product of Jefferies Equity Sales and Trading Department. It is not a product of Jefferies Research Department, and it should not be regarded as research or a research report. Please take a look at the additional disclaimers at the end of this email.

 

MORNING PICCOLO | Good start to the new half. Japan is the story today, everything in the AI supply chain is up at the same time - wafers, substrates, MLCCs, ABF. Sumco +11%, Ibiden +9%, Taiyo Yuden +15%. The US handed them a great boost overnight and then a wave of price target hikes on WFE names this morning kept it going. NKY is 30% above the 200DMA at this point, worth keeping in mind. Taiwan is very similar. TSMC +3% as people position for what could be a 70% gross margin print next quarter. The interesting move is probe cards - MPI, Winway, Gallant all up 10% - which fits with Fubon's July call on advanced packaging. Korea though is a different story. KOSPI -1.8%, SEC -4.3%, SK Hynix -2%, which is pretty strange given what Nasdaq and SOX did overnight. The reasons going around are a bit thin to be honest - NPS rebalancing starting today, a small dip in June DRAM export data, some unconfirmed post on X about a memory efficiency breakthrough. Foreigners have now sold for nine straight sessions. Power equip and defence are catching the rotation again - LS Elec +10%, HW Aero +10%. KOSDAQ at +1.4% , Jusung Eng +24% on the Samsung/SK Hynix capex story. China basically flat, HK closed for a holiday. India bounced back above 24,000 off the 50DMA, IT services a bit soft after KPIT flagged a weak outlook, autos doing well, MCX +5% on the Jefferies BUY initiation. Australia was all about banks - ANZ -3.6%, NAB -2.8% - and house prices just had their biggest monthly drop since 2022 which isn't helping sentiment there.

 

 

 

 KOREA CONFERENCE - 9-10 SEPT

	

 

Join our KB/Korea Conference in (9-10 Sept) вҖ“ Register here <mailto:as_asia_ldn@jefferies.com?subject=Please%20register%20my%20interest%20to%20join%20the%20Jefferies/KB%20Korea%20Conference%20in%20September>  вҖ“ positioned as the largest in the industry вҖ“ with momentum buildingвҖҰ

 

*	148 corporates confirmed 
*	39 C-level corps 
*	11 pre-IPO/unlisted
*	Speakers include Louis Gave (Gavekal) and Chris Wood, with more to follow вҖ“ See conference here <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13516661&ad=4538999186&h=ckiMaFY&sig=99aE82Cv7jpzQwoXIL5mlNDNKK2&v=2&url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v%3DHPQpS40WvKs> 

 

 RESEARCH HIGHLIGHTS                                                                                                                                                             

	

 

*	USHIO: Nakanomyo upgrades to BUY, TP ВҘ5,500 (26% upside) as selloff creates entry 
*	SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS: KB maintains BUY, Memory shortage runs 2+ years, DRAM +308% in 2026, memory's share of AI capex goes from 14% to 50% by 2027; SEC is the prime beneficiary, buy the dip. 
*	KOREA INVESTMENT HOLDINGS: BUY, TP KRW350,000 (75% upside) with 22.5% ROE at 0.8x P/B and 3.8x PE, 2Q26 earnings 39% above consensus; absurdly cheap. 
*	THE BEAR CASE FOR AI: Ed Zitron - OpenAI/Anthropic burning cash at a scale the AWS analogies can't justify, open source is the st threat, and $748bn of hyperscaler obligations tied to two unprofitable companies is not a self-sustaining industry. 

 EVENT HIGHLIGHTS

	

 

ANALYST ACCESS (IN PERSON)

*	Chris Wood - Global Head of Equity Strategy: Wed 8th July вҖ“ Milan

 

CORP ACCESS (IN PERSON)

*         SoftBank (9984 JP): 30 June вҖ“ 1 July вҖ“Vienna, Milan

 

CONFERENCES/TOURS

*         Asia and WCLC Tour: 7-18 Sept

*         Korea Conference: 9-10 Sept

*         Jefferies 5th India Forum: 16-18 Sept

 

 RESEARCH                                                                                                                                                             

	

USHIO | 6925 JP; BUY ; TP 5500 | UPGRADE TO BUY | LINK <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13516661&ad=4538999186&h=8lxQWMk&sig=6uDY8B1V8LJvGYdExcxBwFVBfnl&v=2&url=https://content.jefferies.com/links2/pdf/1ccf2d99-6dfb-49a5-a935-2306fa31555d?firmId%3D32195>  

*	JEFs Nakanomyo San upgraded Ushio to Buy (vs HOLD) with a JPY 5,500 target (26% upside), arguing the recent sector-driven selloff has created an attractive entry point.
*	The main driver is Industrial Process, where management targets 2x sales and >2x margins versus today, with growing confidence the targets are achievable.
*	Ushio is a direct beneficiary of AI server and advanced packaging demand through substrate lithography, Adtec and the Applied Materials DLT partnership, with EMIB-T adoption providing an additional catalyst.
*	The company also has an 85% share in semiconductor UV lamps, ongoing OSRAM synergies, and FY27 EPS estimates that remain well above consensus.

SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS | 005930 KS; BUY; TP KRW550,000 | AI INVESTMENT RUN TO CONTINUE | LINK <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13516661&ad=4538999186&h=XRb9ta8&sig=aQlCnOcOqboRKl3fD6uU9IlXUy7&v=2&url=https://content.jefferies.com/links2/pdf/62a08b07-5b49-4998-a025-46a295af990f?firmId%3D32195>  |

*	Jeff Kim at KB maintains BUY on SEC with a TP of KRW550,000, arguing the recent correction is a buying opportunity as AI investment accelerates into 2H26 with rate hike fears fading and the Fed likely on hold through year-end.
*	The memory shortage is not going away soon. Supply is constrained through 2027 with limited capacity additions, while demand keeps compounding: agentic AI triples memory content in data centres, physical AI boosts it by 5x, and edge devices (PCs, smartphones) are adding to the load. A minimum two years to resolve the shortage is the base case.
*	DRAM prices are forecast to rise 308% in 2026 and NAND 256%, with further upside in 2027 as the shortage deepens. KB forecasts SEC operating profit of KRW375tn in 2026 (+761% YoY) and KRW547tn in 2027.
*	Memory's share of total AI capex rises from 14% in 2025 to 36% in 2026 and 50% in 2027, as HBM, DDR5 and eSSD all scale simultaneously and memory capacity becomes a direct determinant of AI service quality. Global AI investment is forecast to exceed $800bn in 2026 and $1.1tn in 2027, which at 3% of US GDP is still the lowest GDP share of any major tech revolution in the last two centuries.
*	SEC is the prime beneficiary given the largest output capacity in the world. Mag 7 FCF takes a hit in 2026-27 from the capex cycle but recovers to 2024 levels in 2028 on a 91% YoY jump as AI profitability improves, keeping the investment cycle intact.



 

KOREA INVESTMENT HOLDINGS | 071050 KS; BUY; TP KRW350,000 | STILL CHEAP DESPITE CONTINUED EARNINGS BEATS | LINK <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13516661&ad=4538999186&h=IXSDlvG&sig=aDiLjjcbUCtacG8fm5S9DuhIzio&v=2&url=https://content.jefferies.com/links2/pdf/48523a35-5c38-4ed6-83cf-6285128c7985?firmId%3D32195> | 

*	Earnings momentum remains exceptional. KIH is expected to deliver 2Q26 earnings 39% above consensus, with 1H26 profits already reaching 91% of FY25 earnings, driven by market share gains in ETFs, strong margin loan growth and continued strength in wealth management and structured finance. 
*	A clear winner from Korea's retail trading boom. Brokerage earnings are expected to surge 170% YoY, supported by rising trading activity, expanding market share and a structurally stronger earnings base from trading and capital markets businesses. 
*	Valuation remains remarkably cheap. Despite generating a forecast 22.5% ROE, KIH trades on just 0.8x P/B and 3.8x FY26E P/E, while offering a 6.6% dividend yield, making it one of the cheapest high-quality financials in Korea. 
*	Buy maintained with 75% upside. Concerns around a potential insurance acquisition continue to weigh on sentiment, but stronger earnings and improved funding capacity suggest the risk is increasingly priced in, leaving significant room for re-rating. 

 

 





THE BEAR CASE FOR AI | EXPERT CALL RECAP (ED ZITRON)

*	The market is conflating an AI capex bubble with the underlying industry. OpenAI and Anthropic are deeply unprofitable, have raised $200bn in six months, and don't look like previous tech cycle winners. 
*	A $200 subscription costs Anthropic ~$8,000 and OpenAI ~$14,000 to serve. The unit economics are largely hidden.
*	The AWS/Uber comparisons are not fair. Amazon's inflation-adjusted cumulative capex was $29bn. AI capex dwarfs that, and unlike cloud, model costs are going up not down. Training costs are permanent, inference is expensive, and compute must be bought ahead of demand nobody can accurately forecast.
*	Open source is the threat. Customisable open-source models may be the single biggest risk to OpenAI and Anthropic, who need sustained revenue growth to justify $1.1tn of committed compute. Price cuts don't solve it if there's no measurable ROI on the other side.
*	Amazon, Microsoft and Google have ~$748bn of remaining obligations, much of it tied to OpenAI and Anthropic. A self-sustaining industry wouldn't need this level of interconnected demand creation. The catalyst to watch is a meaningful capex pullback from any hyperscaler.

Wednesday, July 1 2026
 
*** PLNW Q2'26 preview, KPIT profit warning/readx CAP, ATE, BC8 call TMRW, SAP/mgmt. AI reshuffle, SAP/NOK/MSFT, TIETO/CFO exit, ADYEN/CeX Malaysia, Open USD, WLN/CrГ©dit Agricole ***
 
To discuss our Global SW AI impact assessment framework <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=tCpvRuFn3YfoUgqoWd44FQ&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=yCMbrw> , Q1 2026 wrap <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=15Sq9CcEBZ!oUgqoWd44FQ&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=rx9-iw> , or SAP, DSY, ADYEN, CAP: book a call with Fred Boulan using this Self Booking Link <https://rsch.baml.com/events?aid=12341058&did=23065165> .
For calls on Payments <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=zKKKPcvSAoxX5U!inye6iQ&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=Xe0rTA>  / Wise <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=cUdkqb8iX81FZQKdnkB0Qw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=9ULEkw>  / IT Services <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=F-8UNsRWa3BFZQKdnkB0Qw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=Sx9tAQ>  / Alten, Bechtle, Kainos, Sopra Steria and TietoEvry: Book a call with Aditya Buddhavarapu using this Self Booking Link <https://rsch.baml.com/events?aid=12341061&did=23065168> .
For calls on HBX <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=0TvCncIyucdiAf!213RB6A&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=PVM!3A>  / Amadeus <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=eDm8OkY!rYqFQMWpR0Cbew&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=ChBjeA>  / Sabre / Nemetschek / TMV / Sinch: Book a call with Victor Cheng using this Self Booking Link <https://rsch.baml.com/events?aid=12341067&did=23066059> .
 
UPCOMING EVENTS
*	*** TOMORROW *** Bechtle вҖ“ Virtual Group Meeting with IR | Thursday, July 2 2026 | 10:00 AM UKT | Please join us for a virtual group meeting with the Bechtle IR team on Thursday, July 2nd at 10am UKT / 11am CET. We will discuss growth drivers across key markets, investment priorities, competitive landscape, and GenAI implications. Please register here <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=MKwFRkW7GqCFQMWpR0Cbew&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=Yd3IRw> .
London TMT Conference: We hosted our London TMT Conference on June 9-11th, 2026, with > 70 TMT companies in attendance including SAP, OpenAI, DSY, Sage, Amadeus, ASML, ASMI, IFX, STM, AIXTRON, RELY, WISE, RELX, PUB, WPP, BT, DT, ORA, TI, VOD. 
Fireside chats replay links: OpenAI <https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/csuiteTMT2026/agenda/0C1EC923A95CC0.cfm#/page/agenda/eventid/1017138> ,  SAP SE <https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/csuiteTMT2026/idP5yImU.cfm> ,  Sabre <https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/csuiteTMT2026/id20B3M9.cfm> , Remitly <https://bofa.veracast.com/webcasts/bofa/csuiteTMT2026/1EYTwD.cfm> 
Feedback reports: European technology recap <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=v8KT2fY69KTzlE8-FkIFrA&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=VD1S8A>  , European IT Services <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=F-8UNsRWa3BFZQKdnkB0Qw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=Sx9tAQ>  , SAP <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=beu7U6gWfYs7mmrsGEf5KA&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=Skvdbw>  , Dassault Systemes <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=bihmMJY2uro7mmrsGEf5KA&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=cXm!DA>  , Sage <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=s7LoJgkyBenXOnZK1bwtlw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=18b!IQ>  , Sabre <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=tPajOhbkQ1bXOnZK1bwtlw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=8OfyyQ>  , Remitly <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=MgNCpW9WRtAFIEaL5o-aBA&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=ZM9C7A>  , Wise <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=cUdkqb8iX81FZQKdnkB0Qw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=9ULEkw>  , OpenAI <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=VzWnEh8wLyrglbImREndRw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=tLgVkg> 
 
NEWS вҖ“ EMEA
Planisware SA <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=3tmzqFg!QpTglbImREndRw&e=frederic.boulan%40bofa.com&h=U-S2nA> : Strong growth profile, AI upside вҖ“ Reit Buy - Reit Buy, вӮ¬24 PO on strong growth profile at 14% revenue CAGR to 2028, 6pp above peers, for broadly aligned 11.6x '27 EBITDA. Q2: forecast 12.8% top line, FY 12.5% accelerating to 14% in 2027 on improving pipeline conversion. FY2026 guidance prudent. AI position: Exchange26 showcased Prisma AI agent, reinforcing differentiated AI strategy and unified platform positioning.
 
Relevant Alten, CAP вҖ“ KPIT profit warning yesterday on weaker autos end market
Indian engineering services company KPIT yesterday downgraded its outlook for Q1 FY27 (June end) and the rest of the year citing a sudden drop in revenue in the last few weeks. The company stated that there is an expected decline of ~ 1% in USD reported revenues for Q1FY27 as compared to Q1FY26 (YoY) primarily due to sudden actions by some European OEMs triggered by their recent profit warnings/ adverse business outlook. This impact was not seen coming earlier and has been realized only in the recent weeks. (KPIT <https://www.bseindia.com/xml-data/corpfiling/AttachLive/bee7f0cf-3011-46be-b131-6409fc46b980.pdf> )
>>> BofA comment: While the customer is not named in the release, we note that BMW, which profit warned recently, is a KPIT customer. Both Alten and Capgemini are exposed to automotive clients in the engineering R&D space. 
 
SAP gives AI product oversight to CEO and COO in reshuffle
According to Bloomberg, SAP is divvying up responsibilities for its product and engineering functions in its second top-level reorganization this year, as EuropeвҖҷs largest software company grapples with staying ahead of rising AI competition. SAP has decided to split the responsibilities of Chief Product Officer Muhammad Alam among existing executives as Alam prepares to leave the company in March, rather than name a new CPO with those duties. CEO Christian Klein will take over most of AlamвҖҷs teams, while COO Sebastian SteinhГӨuser will handle industrial AI. (Bloomberg) <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-30/sap-distributes-ai-product-oversight-to-ceo-coo-in-reshuffle?srnd=phx-technology> 
>>> BofA comment: Head of Product and Engineering Muhammad AlamвҖҷs decision not to renew his contract post 2027 was announced back in March.
 
Nokia, SAP and Microsoft enter strategic multi-year agreement to advance Cloud- and AI-driven business transformation
SAP announced that Nokia has signed a new multi-year agreement with SAP to help accelerate its enterprise transformation using RISE with SAP Methodology, with its SAP S/4HANA software environment hosted on Microsoft Azure. The agreement, concluded at the end of 2025, marks a significant step in migrating NokiaвҖҷs SAP landscape to the RISE with SAP journey. By adopting RISE with SAP Methodology, Nokia will follow a structured, end-to-end approach to migrating its ERP landscape covering processes, data, applications and operating models while gaining continuous access to innovation and embedded AI capabilities delivered through SAPвҖҷs cloud ERP portfolio. (SAP) <https://news.sap.com/2026/06/nokia-sap-microsoft-strategic-multi-year-agreement-advance-cloud-ai-driven-business-transformation/> 
 
FitsAir and Amadeus sign global distribution agreement to expand international reach
FitsAir, Sri LankaвҖҷs privately owned international airline, and Amadeus, a leading travel technology company, announced the signing of a global distribution agreement. The agreement will make FitsAirвҖҷs full range of content available to travel sellers worldwide through the Amadeus Travel Platform, supporting the airlineвҖҷs continued international growth. (Amadeus) <https://amadeus.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/fitsair-amadeus-global-distribution-agreement-international-reach> 
 
Tieto CFO departure
Tieto announced yesterday that CFO Tomi HyrylГӨinen has decided on his own initiative to leave the company to pursue new opportunities outside Tieto. He will continue in his position until the end of December 2026. The recruitment process for a new CFO will commence immediately. (Tieto <https://www.tieto.com/en/newsroom/stock-exchange-releases/2026/change-in-tietos-group-executive-team-cfo-tomi-hyrylainen-to-leave-the-company/> )
 
Nemetschek Arabia and KEO Group sign strategic collaboration agreement to advance digital facilities management across the GCC
Nemetschek Arabia, part of the Nemetschek Group, one of the worldвҖҷs leading software providers for the Architecture, Engineering, Construction and Operations industry, has signed a strategic collaboration agreement with KEO Group, a leading multidisciplinary consulting firm. emails.subject: JPM International Market Intelligence | Morning Briefing: 20 Seconds. June Thematic Pulse - Global Thematic Flows, Industrial Policy and Global Consumer Part 2. Drew's NFP scenarios. Cybersec is urgent. Italian Politics
emails.body:
JULY 1, 2026

 

20 SECONDS:

*         Quiet morning. Focus today = PMIs, EA CPI and panel with Lagarde, Warsh, Bailey & Macklem at SintraвҖҷs forum (2pm CET)

*         Memory down vs Beneficiaries of KoreaвҖҷs mega investment plans (JPG1KCPX) up 5% - the hyperscaler curse?

*         We have published our June Thematics Pulse. Three key themes: 1) flows into global thematics (top 3 = AI, Infrastructure and Quantum vs bottom 3 = Blockchain, Space and EV & Battery); 2) SRI вҖ“ focus on Industrial policy; 3) Global Consumer (part 2).

*         Fun stats on AI spillovers: Courts across the U.S. are now seeing a steady rise in filings that include made-up or inaccurate references. Looks similar to the growing use of American idioms by UK MPs.

*         Drew is out with his NFP scenario analysis

*         JPM Research: Urgency for Cybersecurity is going up and our Analysts added TENB to AFL and upgraded QLYS. Chinese AI models are closing the gap in vulnerability discovery capabilities at a time when vulnerability is visibly accelerating. Mariana discusses Italian politics ahead of elections in 2027. 

 

 

MKT INTEL VIEWS: Quiet news flow. US negotiators say talks in Qatar are off to a positive start while Iran is more vocal in its demands to control and impose tolls on SoH.  Equity funding costs are finally starting to come down.  USDJPY keeps going (in slow motion) but again alongside broader dollar strength. Gold is below $4000/oz once again.  

 

In Asia, Memory down vs Beneficiaries of KoreaвҖҷs mega investment plans (JPG1KCPX) up 5% - the hyperscaler curse? 

 

Focus today = PMIs, EA CPI and panel with Lagarde, Warsh, Bailey & Macklem at SintraвҖҷs forum (2pm CET).  The likelihood that todayвҖҷs flash Jun EA CPI will be up by less than consensus has increased (after the drop in national CPI reports from  Germany, France <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=QJIiktJ&sig=NXbyP3AKxGCdLfZLTuolhSstwJv&v=2&isig=GeB8a5mhNMgms2YFYdNVi4jfl9p&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/1oop6ega/GPS-5352664-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559> , Italy <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=UtiwrTv&sig=XtpB0IuTeBQYe4lIAJFj1dhTJur&v=2&isig=3bUumFGN1U5xjp2N9LgsPAEaYXv&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-vnflpiu/GPS-5352721-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559> , and Spain <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=-cxiC4P&sig=ZjdSqVIScjO0kdiWuE5Voflup4S&v=2&isig=QToxnwJACcXEtdCoWJQoTR45L1l&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-e492a2q/GPS-5351248-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559> ) but  LagardeвҖҷs Sintra speech <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=mjXdbop&sig=U82YIwSRtnpPMOzc4xbdk9xToy7&v=2&isig=8QpLNSJLzsVZUyPedScaaMuBF9H&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/aje8idgo/GPS-5352224-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559>  left GregвҖҷs call for a final 25bp hike in September intact. 

 

Memory vs Memory Capex Beneficiaries



 

We have published our June Thematics <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=HNIRvd9&sig=RuNsHfgl4FdPQYFzMHDkiuhPXOz&v=2&isig=HvvvoZIWDw9pPFcKcubIg5kFANX&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-lko56u5/GPS-5353255-0.pdf/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559>  note. Three key themes: 1) flows into global thematics; 2) SRI вҖ“ focus on industrial policy; 3) Global Consumer (part 2). 

 

TRACKING FLOWS IN THEMATICS: We track flows into 16x Secular Equity Themes. The AUMs are relatively small ($500bn) but are rising fast (25% organic inflows in June, 2-2.5x the rate for broader US Equities). Over the past month, the Themes with the strongest inflows have been AI, Infrastructure and Quantum while those with weakest inflow have been Blockchain, Space and EV & Battery.

 



 

SECURITY & RESILIENCE INVESTING (вҖңSRIвҖқ) вҖ“ A FOCUS ON INDUSTRIAL POLICY: Government priorities will remain drivers of equity returns. We anticipate Physical AI becoming more salient in US vs China competition. EU is moving on 2 axes, specifically Tech Sovereignty and Protectionism, but unlikely to do anything bold. China will continue to emphasize self-sufficiency in core technologies, supply-chain resilience, and strategic buffers in food and energy. The next China tech giants will be in upstream equipment/materials, the wafer-fabrication вҖңfoundation,вҖқ domestically memory, advanced packaging & testing, and compute/chips. Other Asian Economies will fight for their edge (Korea & Japan) 

 

 

 

GLOBAL CONSUMER вҖ“ PART 2:  Our key argument is that the global macro regime should shift back to recovery/expansion (from slowdown/stagflation) and should drive an inflection in Consumer Sentiment and related Equity market themes. The Consumer also benefits from attractive price relatives and low positioning. Give the lack of earnings momentum we would still say selective and favor names with at least one of the following characteristics:  a) target more resilient upper-end  consumer (given wealth effects); b) capture the shift to spending  on experiences; c) are exposed to regions with solid wealth and cyclical backdrops (US,  Korea, Taiwan); and d) stand to benefit from a substantial decrease in oil prices in 2H26. 

 



 

Fun Facts on AI Spillovers: Courts across the U.S. are now seeing a steady rise in filings that include made-up or inaccurate references produced by AI tools. These issues are showing up more often in certain states, especially where AI adoption is moving faster than review practices.  Looks similar to the growing use of American idioms by UK MPs.

 



 

 

US MKT INTELвҖҷS NFP SCENARIO ANALYSIS

FeroliвҖҷs full NFP preview is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=-epNX-G&sig=IVerPmfk1frQ6d9kduzLrP7RgDb&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/nahe0bhi/GPS-5321939-0/893d2f56-aeec-4768-aa11-fd18125ba32e> . He sees 125k jobs being added, versus the StreetвҖҷs estimate of 110k and 172k jobs in the prior print. For the unemployment rate (U.3) he sees 4.3% vs. 4.3% prior. For Average Hourly Earnings, he sees +0.2% MoM and +3.4% YoY.

 

The following scenario analysis is NOT A PRODUCT OF JPM RESEARCH, this is a trading desk view from JPM US Market Intelligence. 

 

В·       [5%] NFP prints above 160k. SPX loses 50bp to gains 1.5%

В·       [25%] NFP prints between 130k вҖ“160k. SPX gains 75bp вҖ“ 1.25%

В·       [40%] NFP prints between 100k вҖ“ 130k. SSPX gains 50bp вҖ“ 1%

В·       [25%] NFP prints between 70k вҖ“ 100k. SPX loses 50bp вҖ“ 1.25%

В·       [5%] NFP prints below 70k. SPX loses 1.25% вҖ“ 2%

В·       WHAT ARE OPTIONS PRICING? For options expiring on July 2, 2026, using data from June 29, show a 1.2% implied move.

В·       US MKT INTEL ON NFP вҖ“ With period-end rebalancing appearing to be completed, we look for the bullish trend to resume. Dip-buying behavior remains prevalent, which is why the outcomes here skew bullishly, especially when considering neutral aggregate positioning. When considering macro fundamentals, FeroliвҖҷs estimate reflects a resilient economy, and our latest Consumer Cash Pile (here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=ywOk65V&sig=6VCQFM4vhK9mJzwcxCrOkJiwsSo&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/893d2f56-aeec-4768-aa11-fd18125ba32e> ) reflects ample levels of cash across the income brackets that drive the majority of US consumption with minimal signs of aggregate consumer stress. Combine that with a likely robust 26Q2 earnings season and that should drive stocks higher, ceteris paribus. For 26Q2 earnings season, FactSet expectations are for revenue growth of 12.1% (vs. 11.9% in 26Q1), earnings growth of 22.0% (vs. 28.8% in 26Q1), with 14.1% margins (vs. 14.8% in 26Q1, which was the second consecutive record).

 



 



 

 



 



 

 

IDEAS & INSIGHTS вҖ“ IN BRIEF 

*         CHINA AI MODELS | Add TENB to AFL, U/G QLYS to N, CRWD & PANW Well Positioned => Open-weight Chinese AI models are closing the gap with frontier models in vulnerability discovery capabilities. This comes at a time when enterprises are preparing to assess and manage a вҖҳtsunami of vulnerabilitiesвҖҷ expected across their IT estates, and the progress of the Chinese models likely accelerates <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=Y3S9zkN&sig=Yp7JtBSiHuM8vVFSDfPdTzpWhUl&v=2&isig=EgcnxZp9U2Tdodisb2yu4L11Aux&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-4rentqn/GPS-5351448-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559>  levels of urgency. Accordingly, add TENB to our Analyst Focus List + upgrade QLYS from Underweight to Neutral as we think the company will also benefit across its core installed base. PANW & CRWD also well positioned

*         Italy: Politics and budget to come back into focus <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=tCM1991&sig=A6x5nOMpt3whJ86tPsxhMfu7gZe&v=2&isig=WNLLnVUoamfv4ssr7NQaPOufxxD&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/3c6tpoi8/GPS-5353117-0/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559> . Italian politics has been relatively quiet, but three interrelated issues are coming up. First, there has been some speculation of early elections before the likely date of autumn 2027. Second, major electoral overhaul is underway as fragmentation on the right grows. Third, 2027 budget discussions will generate noise, but a rupture is not expected. Growth to hold up in 2027 despite further consolidation and elections, though it will continue to trail regional peers. 

*         TODAYвҖҷS CATALYSTS:  China RatingDog Mfg PMI; Global Final PMIs; EA CPI; South Korea trade; Japan Tankan; US ADP Employment, ISM Mfg; Japan PM Takaichi visits New Delhi for a summit with Modi; deadline for US, Mexico and Canada to decide on USMCA extension. Lagarde, Warsh, Bailey & Macklem in panel at SintraвҖҷs forum.

 

IDEAS & INSIGHTS вҖ“ IN DETAILS 

MONETIZATION: We are bullish on Global Equities

-       Macro & thematics still impose the relative regional preference (long US & Asia вҖ“ JA, SK & TW - vs EU). The US is overheating; Europe is in slow recovery from stagflation & Asia has pockets of both. Further, secular themes carry less weight within EU Equities.  That said, we have been tactically bullish on EU Equities <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13515993&ad=4538467075&h=SlDmj3a&sig=G7p74Z8IQ9tL5hrtjRXAvOv6EFp&v=2&isig=80xtTh2cdNnnJmMhmHMorpvZoMW&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-22cvp0m/GPS-5291960-0.pdf/6f1d827c-76da-4bc3-b566-83c3877ea559>  since early May on positioning, underperformance and regime switch on SoH reopening. We continue to play this via options given attractive vol levels and extended outperformance in the near-term. 

-       LONGS

o   Supercycle themes - AI bottlenecks/facilitators, Cyber, Physical AI, Clean Energy (EU Nuclear, EU Renewables, Asia ESS), Modern Warfare and Critical Minerals - are key Longs within a portfolio of International Equities. We continue to think that a multi-week factor rotation is still in the cards, and this could keep the complex choppy/vulnerable to drawdowns. Hence, we stay hedged via options on momentum, but we are minded to buying dips. 

o   Cyclicals but with a secular tailwind. EU & JA Banks, EU Industrials & JA Machinery; GE Infra. EU Miners are looking attractive right now.

o   We would stay selectively long on the Consumer and look in regions anchored by wealth effects вҖ“ notably the Korean Consumer (JPKBWEFT Index). We continue to see a tactical trade in EU Luxury. 

-       SHORTS

o   Span AI Vulnerable (ex-Software), EU Insurance, challenged Industries (Autos, Chemicals, Asian Consumer Electronics, MedTech) & MDAX. 

o    We are high conviction on EU Chemicals and Asia Consumer Electronics currently/

-       Option hedges: Keep 1) long dollar (given cheap vol) either in vanilla or dual digi format (FX & Equity leg) for an H4L air pocket; 2) MCX < UKX until clarity emerges on spending, pragmatism and ideology; 3) Puts on Momentum Factor to hedge mean reversion (seasonality + SoH reopening)

 

POSITIONING INTELLIGENCE

POSITIONING INTELLIGENCE IS NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH

 

Report: Weekly Wrap | Mixed Tech Flows as AxJ, Semis, and Tech HW Positioning Stay Elevated

 

Summary:  

*	Gross leverage easing (as expected): Down WoW from 361% to 338% across All Strategies, but still 98th %-tile vs last 5yrs. Move looks driven by post-expiry roll-offs vs clear active de-grossing 

 

*	Potential for active de-grossing into late July is high: 1) weвҖҷll soon be past the main index rebal events, 2) the starting points of gross flows and leverage are at highs (long and short additions over the past 4wks have been +2z level), and c) July is approaching, during which weвҖҷve seen HF de-grossing each time over the past 5 years (around -1z to -3z events).

 

*	Crowding still working, but could reverse soon: Crowded stocks strong past 5d and MTD (e.g. +14% MTD in N. Am., +5% EMEA, and +10% in APAC). With sharp US crowding rebound MTD, next move could be lowerвҖ”US 20d crowding performance turned negative in each of past 5 Julys; strong 1m/3m performance can raise mean-reversion odds.

 

*	AxJ Tech / Korea:  HFs still bought Korea over the past week. HF AxJ long/short ratio is quite elevated and is up from 1.5x at the end of 1Q26 to 2.2x currently (93rd %-tile since 2020).  HFs mostly sold Tech outside AxJ this past week.

 

*	Positioning extremes within Tech globally: 6m z-scores of positioning + performance getting extreme. Semis ~+3z (moves above +2.5z have historically coincided with a pause/reversal), Tech HW >+4z (prior peaks often ~+2z and marked highs).

 

*	Momentum exposure still stretched: 6m z of HF positioning/performance touched +2z again this week (similar to mid-May). With price z-score > positioning z-score again, forward returns could skew negative. US non-Pure Momentum (JPPQMO) is pulling back per typical seasonality and could remain choppy.

 

*	Overall flows/positioning: HFs were net buyers globally (buying N. America & APAC, neutral EMEA). Financials consistently bought across regions. US Tactical Positioning Monitor: +0.4z WoW, -0.6z over 4wks; level ~neutral vs 10+ yrs. HF net leverage basically unchanged WoW (slight increase All Strats, reduction Eq L/S).
