TokenBudgeting: Our Conversations with Enterprises on Token Spend <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6349492&post_id=203595758&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=3qce8x&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMjU2NDY4ODEsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwMzU5NTc1OCwiaWF0IjoxNzgyODQ0NDYxLCJleHAiOjE3ODU0MzY0NjEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02MzQ5NDkyIiwic3ViIjoicG9zdC1yZWFjdGlvbiJ9.gsnZNLCPKms7Jm_lO5OaCfR8lR-ECdc5PTbUeDbiazA> 


Was Widespread TokenMaxxing Ever Really Here?

Crystal Huang <https://substack.com/@crystalh1229> , Joey Brookhart <https://substack.com/@joeysemianalysis> , and Dylan Patel <https://substack.com/@semianalysis> 
Jun 30
	вҲҷ
	Preview
 	 
 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6349492&post_id=203595758&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMjU2NDY4ODEsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwMzU5NTc1OCwicmVhY3Rpb24iOiLinaQiLCJpYXQiOjE3ODI4NDQ0NjEsImV4cCI6MTc4NTQzNjQ2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTYzNDk0OTIiLCJzdWIiOiJyZWFjdGlvbiJ9.8FO3cYo4RUaAk4PlegPZP8okz1C2OTCgPqvES_SAswE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=3qce8x> 	 
	 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6349492&post_id=203595758&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMjU2NDY4ODEsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwMzU5NTc1OCwiaWF0IjoxNzgyODQ0NDYxLCJleHAiOjE3ODU0MzY0NjEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02MzQ5NDkyIiwic3ViIjoicG9zdC1yZWFjdGlvbiJ9.gsnZNLCPKms7Jm_lO5OaCfR8lR-ECdc5PTbUeDbiazA&r=3qce8x&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> 	 
	 <https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6349492&post_id=203595758&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=3qce8x&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoyMjU2NDY4ODEsInBvc3RfaWQiOjIwMzU5NTc1OCwiaWF0IjoxNzgyODQ0NDYxLCJleHAiOjE3ODU0MzY0NjEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi02MzQ5NDkyIiwic3ViIjoicG9zdC1yZWFjdGlvbiJ9.gsnZNLCPKms7Jm_lO5OaCfR8lR-ECdc5PTbUeDbiazA> 	 
	 <https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvc2VtaWFuYWx5c2lzL3AvdG9rZW5idWRnZXRpbmctb3VyLWNvbnZlcnNhdGlvbnM_.l-LeQLQeKlwpTQrCuH2VAjGLz3nQt3TOjaIKE4cOCo0?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email> 	 
<https://open.substack.com/pub/semianalysis/p/tokenbudgeting-our-conversations?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> 
READ IN APP <https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ET-_!,w_36,c_scale,f_png,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack.com%2Ficon%2FLucideArrowUpRight%3Fv%3D4%26height%3D36%26fill%3Dnone%26stroke%3D%2523808080%26strokeWidth%3D2> 
 	 

ItвҖҷs been reported that token consumption inside of enterprises is hitting a budgeting wall after unhinged consumption earlier this year. The SemiAnalysis team talked with over 50 customers by slack, phone, and at the Databricks AI Summit to understand trends within the enterprise.

*	Widely reported responses to Tokenmaxxing budgets from companies like Meta and Uber are overstated and stem from poor incentives and employee allocation we didnвҖҷt find present at other organizations

*	Budgets are now the new norm, but thereвҖҷs no consensus number with budgets starting at $250 and going up to tens of thousands a month.

*	Companies are downgrading default models and turning off premium tiers while employeesвҖҷ game subscription M365 Copilot usage to stretch their token allowance.


Rise and Fall of Tokenmaxxing 


Tokenmaxxing started earlier this year when companies like Meta and Salesforce began encouraging their employees to consume as many AI tokens as possible to boost productivity. At Meta, an employee even built a вҖңClaudeconomicsвҖқ dashboard that ranked the top 250 power users in the company. The results showed that Meta employees consumed over 60T tokens over a 30-day period, with the single highest individual accounting for roughly 280B tokens. Employees started competing for rankings like вҖңToken LegendвҖқ and вҖңCache WizardвҖқ by having agents do research for hours simply to burn tokens. 

The dashboard was shut down 2 days later after The Information reported the spend. 

That episode was just one amongst others in the enterprise tokenmaxxing trend in 1H26. Companies are now shifting focus from tokenmaxxing to token budgeting. Most recently, Uber made headlines for burning through their Claude Code and Codex annual budget in four months. In response, the company imposed a $1,500/month/employee limit, with over-limit requests allowed and approved on a case-by-case basis. To see if the news reports on early 2026 tokenmaxxing and now tokenbudgeting were true, the SemiAnalysis team conducted on-the-ground conversations at the Databricks AI Summit and talked with large enterprises to understand the trends.

SemiAnalysis is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support our work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

Upgrade to paid <https://substack..5c2gS6GO662XUJdx_Lmh8se91XlhPEGXQKihQYx2gto?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=203595758> 

Our View of the Data & Narrative


There are many news stories out there on tokenmaxxing and resulting budget blowouts. However, in our work in the Tokenomics Model <https://substack.com/redirect/bf96e487-bb92-48ce-9d0f-59e40da35e5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> , we estimate that 90th+ percentile customers make up most of the revenue and are at very little risk to API revenue cuts through the rest of the year. Even Meta, who was burning through 70T tokens per month in February and is spending close to at least $50,000/year per employee (at list price) is only a 3-5% customer for Anthropic per our estimates. Ramp data shows a similar trend amongst the top customers. 99th percentile customers spend almost $90,000/yr per employee while 90th percentile customers spend ~$7,300. This is a stark contrast to the median Ramp customer spending just $136. Note Ramp customers are generally way more tech forward so it's already a high spend skewed distribution. The media fortune 500 is well below $100 per employee still.

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/189f0adb-b1d8-4517-8bb7-2847077a20f0?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> 	 	
Source: Ramp Economics Lab

Our conversations with enterprise customers, including many Fortune 500s, followed this split. Many tech forward Fortune 500s spend well under $2,000/year per employee in AI, with the larger spend mostly in the engineering and data science departments. This suggests that the s-curve of growth in enterprise usage still has plenty of runway. TodayвҖҷs market is driven by the coding vertical explosion and other VC-backed AI companies whose products build on top of Anthropic or OpenAI models (90th-99th percentile customers who are seeing their revenue accelerate too).

What the coding market has done to AI Lab ARR will be repeated with Cyber (Mythos re-release dependent) at an even faster pace than Claude Code and again with white-collar knowledge work as Cowork, CoPilot, Codex, and Computer type products penetrate the enterprise.

The Tokenomics Model <https://substack.com/redirect/bf96e487-bb92-48ce-9d0f-59e40da35e5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> estimates coding related spend at the AI Labs on a 1st and 3rd party basis and ARR and margins at the application layers (companies like Cursor, Loveable, GitHub CoPilot, and more) to help investors and corporates track growth of and within the vertical. We believe that over 70% of ARR today across OpenAI and Anthropic can be attributed to coding use cases with Anthropic spend higher than OpenAIвҖҷs given the difference in B2B vs B2C mix (Anthropic 90%+ B2B vs OpenAI 60%).

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/cf76572d-ffff-48e8-a309-055d3c546ac7?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> 	 	

Still, cheap tokens are in widespread demand. We see massive growth in spend across both the for the Token-as-a-Service (TaaS)/API Endpoint Market for both frontier models and open-source models. Our estimates for AWS Bedrock this quarter drive our total AWS growth rate number well above street. The Tokenomics Model <https://substack.com/redirect/bf96e487-bb92-48ce-9d0f-59e40da35e5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> also forecasts massive demand for TaaS providers like Together, Fireworks, Baseten, and others who make up over $4B of ARR today.вҖӢ

	 <https://substack.com/redirect/9b380c83-130b-4147-8dcb-0667e79c092e?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> 	 	
Source: SemiAnalysis Tokenomics Model <https://substack.com/redirect/bf96e487-bb92-48ce-9d0f-59e40da35e5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> , TaaS Tab

Thus, to say, our work suggests that headlines are overblown, enterprises continue to spend, and new use case/verticals for demand and token consumption are keeping the AI train moving forward at an aggressive pace.


Budgeting: Pick a Number, Any Number


Most companies we talked with (n>50), also impose a hard cap on AI usage - though there doesnвҖҷt seem to be a token number that companies are converging towards. At the lower end, we spoke with the head of AI at a top 3 US aerospace and defense manufacturer and one of the worldвҖҷs largest pharmaceutical companies which cap employees at $250 and $500 a month, respectively. At the higher end, our conversations with companies like Workday and Stripe revealed that their employeesвҖҷ budgets are about $2000 a month.

A subset of companies has not yet imposed any limit at all, for what seems like two possible reasons:

1.	EmployeesвҖҷ access to AI tools is so limited that cost simply isnвҖҷt a concern; or

2.	The company has derived enough additional output from employees to justify the spend.

The financial industry is a clear example of the former. Much of it has been slow to adopt AI, and those that have moved have done so minimally. In conversations with various data scientists, analysts, and AI leads across a range of asset managers, regional banks, and auto-finance firms, the same pattern kept surfacing: employees boxed into the tools provided through MicrosoftвҖҷs platform.

The ROI, where it exists, can be dramatic:

*	A recruiter at Amazon responsible for scouting and placing principal engineers within the company noted that the process from initial screening call to team placement used to take 6-9 months, but with AI tools to take interview notes and create reports, that timeline been cut in half.

*	An employee at a data and analytics provider serving 85% of the Fortune 500 said what used to take them a week to do, can be done in just a few hours now.

The most mature companies are implementing a soft limit, which should be viewed by employees as guidelines rather than a hard rule. At a public cybersecurity company, the Director of Analytics, who oversees all developers and data scientists, said they set a вҖңlimitвҖқ of $800 a month for juniors and anywhere from $1,600-$4,000 a month for more senior staff. Data scientists are given the largest budget, as they tend to work with larger datasets requiring more tokens, a pattern that recurs across companies with flexible budgets. Should employees exceed their allowance, managers are alerted to have a conversation rather than cutting them off until the count resets.

SemiAnalysis is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support our work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

Upgrade to paid <https://substack..5c2gS6GO662XUJdx_Lmh8se91XlhPEGXQKihQYx2gto?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=203595758> 

The Art of Token Conservation


With rising token spend and increasing executive consciousness towards that spend, employees are starting to learn to adapt. When that same aerospace and defense manufacturer first introduced its $250/month limit, some of the power users burned through it in four days. Employees can now request a higher budget, but that was not always an option. Employees now must get creative to conserve tokens. The company has disclosed that although employees have access to Claude, it has вҖңturned-offвҖқ Opus 4.8 and Fast-Mode deeming it unnecessary. Management believes that handing employees larger token budgets would push them to automate tasks that shouldnвҖҷt be automated at all, like writing emails. We believe this anti-automation view by management teams is naive. Email, much like Slack with connectors from the AI Labs, will become more automated and AI native over time enabling greater productivity, visibility, and collaboration throughout the organization. 

One global travel-tech company took a slightly lighter-touch approach. With 800 engineers out of 1,500 total employees who collectively spend a little under $10M a year on AI, it recently switched the default Claude model for all staff from Opus to Sonnet. Opus remains available but using it now requires a conscious choice. Most employees have a $200/month budget by default, though it can be increased to tens of thousands of dollars depending on seniority and role, with expected budget increases coming soon.

As more companies impose limits, many employees are unbothered as they donвҖҷt come close to the cap. Our conversations with customers at the Databricks AI Summit and in research calls showed that at many organizations most employees do not come close to the limit.

This mirrors the power usage we see here at SemiAnalysis where a handful of employees spend 4 to 5 figures per day, and others spend close to 0. Those who approach or exceed it have found ways to stretch their tokens. We have not impossed limits as the top performers are the ones utilizing a lot of tokens.

Employees of companies on the Microsoft 365 Enterprise subscription receive free, unlimited access to the standard Copilot chatbot. Because that usage isnвҖҷt tracked against the monthly budget, employees can game the system by using CopilotвҖҷs 365 chat to draft and synthesize ideas first, before spending metered tokens on Claude or Codex.


AI As Headcount Lever


The cost of AI tools sits with the company, but so does the benefit. A big 3 US airline stands apart from the others we met with in how it budgets. Its token allocation is tied to the specific project and the revenue that project is expected to bring in. When a project comes in, the financing team decides what percentage of revenue is set aside for expenses like travel and contractor fees, and now that same budget must cover token usage as well.

This reframes AI spend entirely. Companies that hand out generous allowances do so on the expectation that employees will work faster. Output expectations rise to match spend, and many workers have found themselves putting in even longer hours than before. Across over 50 conversations with medium to large enterprises, one thing became clear: the headline Uber, Meta, and other Fortune 500 tokenmaxxing stories were a result of poor incentives and lax oversight rather than an absence of high ROI activities/projects to spend those tokens on. The clearest expression of the ROI dynamic is at Amazon. Despite its widely reported layoffs, the company is hiring at a much faster pace due to efficiencies unlocked by AI tools.

SemiAnalysis is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support our work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

Upgrade to paid <https://substack..5c2gS6GO662XUJdx_Lmh8se91XlhPEGXQKihQYx2gto?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=203595758> 

Budgeting Here to Stay


Caps, soft limits, and conservation tricks are all ways of managing the bill at the end of the month. In contrast to the high numbers of token spend in the news flow, AnthropicвҖҷs own documentation says the average Claude Code usage per developer is between $150-$250 per month, and only 10% of users spend over $30 per day. One thing is clear after our on-the-ground work talking with customers: there is not a material risk present to 2H26 AI budgets and we expect Anthropic and OpenAIвҖҷs API business to continue to grow at their current net new rates m/m for the foreseeable future.

To learn more about the Tokenomics Model <https://substack.com/redirect/bf96e487-bb92-48ce-9d0f-59e40da35e5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> , including our estimates on AI Labs and Hyperscaler financials, email sales@semianalysis.com <https://substack.com/redirect/e1b48cb8-d411-46ce-b565-ec9ccfe38a54?j=eyJ1IjoiM3FjZTh4In0.MU_lxJejkzuw1QU6zGUKkYnWxSX80sQRqtYB1XpD6gQ> 


Summary of Selected Conversations

emails.body:
This is a really fun event вҖ“ a smart group of investors in a closed-door room free to ask questions of some of or favorite names. Hope you can join!

 

 



 

J.P. Morgan is delighted to invite you to participate in the J.P. Morgan Hardware & Semis Management Access Forum hosted by research analysts Harlan Sur, Joseph Cardoso, and Samik Chatterjee, taking place on Wednesday, August 12 in Palo Alto from ~7:30am вҖ“ 2:00pm PT. This is an in-personвҖ“only event and will include 45-minute, analyst-hosted chat-style sessions offering deep-dive insights into select companies. The day will also include two panel discussions: one with 650 Group and another with industry experts representing optical companies. This is a custom and intimate event, and we hope you will join us. 

  

Event Format: 45-minute analyst led вҖҳfireside chatвҖҷ + Investor Q&A.

 

Location: J.P. Morgan | 3223 Hanover Street | 1st Floor | Palo Alto

 

Date: Wednesday, August 12, 2026, at 7:30am вҖ“ 2:00pm PT

 

 

AGENDA & PARTICIPATING COMPANIES (Pacific time)

List below as of 6.29 вҖ“ additional companies to be confirmed 

7:30am | Check-in and Breakfast

7:45am | 650 Group Panel

8:30am | Lumentum

9:20am | Lam Research

10:10am | Cadence Design Systems

11:00am | Applied Optoelectronics

11:45am | Lunch

12:10pm | TBA 

1:05pm | Optical Companies Expert Panel (featuring speakers from Ranovus and Avicena).

 

Registration: Please register HERE <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514345&ad=4536954511&h=kA3YmnY&sig=V49dQepVxTIhNkn4pOWQLJAfXeZ&v=2&url=https://jpm.buythesell.com/Registration/EventAccess?id%3D300> .

 

Hosted by:

Harlan Sur, Semiconductors & Semiconductor Capital Equipment / IT Hardware | harlan.sur@jpmorgan.com <mailto:harlan.sur@jpmorgan.com>  | 415.315.6700

Joseph Cardoso, IT Hardware/ Telecom & Networking Equipment | joseph.cardoso@jpmchase.com <mailto:joseph.cardoso@jpmchase.com>  | 212.622.9036

Samik Chatterjee, Software - Large Cap / Mid & Small Cap | samik.x.chatterjee@jpmorgan.com <mailto:samik.x.chatterjee@jpmorgan.com>  | 212.622.0798

 

Event Coordinator: Grace Snyder

 

*Please note, this event takes place the day before the Apple (AAPL) headquarter visit on August 13. Clients planning to attend the headquarter visit are encouraged to register for the forum as well.

 

 

 

All materials and information shared with you is, unless otherwise indicated to you, proprietary and confidential to J.P. Morgan.  You are hereby notified that any disclosure, dissemination, copying, distribution, or use of the information provided to you, other than as expressly permitted by J.P. You are permitted to disclose the materials and information to your officers and employees on a need to know basis only but further disclosure or dissemination to any third party is STRICTLY PROHIBITED.  Should you have any questions regarding this, please contact your usual J.P. Morgan contact.

On January 3, 2018, MiFID II came into effect. Therefore, you may only be eligible to participate in this event if you have the appropriate level of access to J.P. Morgan research. Please check your eligibility before participating/accessing.

emails.subject: JPM Global Market Intelligence | Global Thematic Views: June 2026
emails.body:
SUMMARY OF THEMATIC VIEWS

Below is a summary of the views which we investigate in this note with the вҖҳDeep DiveвҖҷ section including (i) a detailed rationale for the theme; (ii) key charts; and, (iii) color on positioning.

 

*         SECURITY & RESILIENCE INVESTING (вҖңSRIвҖқ) вҖ“ A FOCUS ON INDUSTRIAL POLICY: Government priorities will remain drivers of equity returns. We anticipate Physical AI becoming more salient in US vs China competition. EU is moving on 2 axes, specifically Tech Sovereignty and Protectionism, but unlikely to do anything bold. China will continue to emphasize self-sufficiency in core technologies, supply-chain resilience, and strategic buffers in food and energy. The next China tech giants will be in upstream equipment/materials, the wafer-fabrication вҖңfoundation,вҖқ domestically memory, advanced packaging & testing, and compute/chips. Other Asian Economies will fight for their edge (Korea & Japan)  

*         GLOBAL CONSUMER вҖ“ PART 2:  Our key argument is that the global macro regime should shift back to recovery/expansion (from slowdown/stagflation) and should drive an inflection in Consumer Sentiment and related Equity market themes. The Consumer also benefits from attractive price relatives and low positioning. Give the lack of earnings momentum we would still say selective and favor names with at least one of the following characteristics:  a) target more resilient upper-end  consumer (given wealth effects); b) capture the shift to spending  on experiences; c) are exposed to regions with solid wealth and cyclical backdrops (US,  Korea, Taiwan); and d) stand to benefit from a substantial decrease in oil prices in 2H26. 

*         TRACKING FLOWS IN THEMATICS: We track flows into 16x Secular Equity Themes. The AUMs are relatively small ($500bn) but are rising fast (25% organic inflows in June, 2-2.5x the rate for broader US Equities). Over the past month, the Themes with the strongest inflows have been AI, Infrastructure and Quantum while those with weakest inflow have been Blockchain, Space and EV & Battery.

 



 

THEMATIC DEEP DIVE

SECURITY & RESILIENCE INVESTING (вҖңSRIвҖқ) вҖ“ A FOCUS ON INDUSTRIAL POLICY. 



*         The world is becoming multi-polar and structural reorganization is underway in public and private investment from efficiency/globalization-first towards building greater national and economic security, resiliency and reliability. Hence, government priorities have become dominant drivers of equity returns globally. Priorities cluster around 5 key themes/areas <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=TiWj4Q9&sig=BMbL7gMj809k8ksJkl2ZeL7SPID&v=2&isig=VlvBj91InWXReAC7EvN3gKvYV4S&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/a3irdl74/GPS-5274031-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> : 1) Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains; 2) Aerospace and Defense; 3) Energy Independence and Resiliency; 4) Frontier and Strategic Technologies; and 5) Pharma and Healthcare. Previous editions of this Thematic Pulse <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=roeqiCB&sig=S4uNfNMT8eoCvKtP5goVsi8FazB&v=2&isig=2ZwZunkrlqiPbHQZW7XMAzCAu3R&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/87jukur1/GPS-5284019-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  have covered 2) & 3) in details while below we run an update of whatвҖҷs in focus right now.  

*         US вҖ“AI is seen as a two-country race between China and the US with both countries moving to vertical integration to limit external influences and to establish a dominant position on future industries. While the race on LLMs is more socialized, we think вҖҳPhysical AI <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=_ZJSciJ&sig=4kyKo1TUUyVwpXzCBvjYaYFhqcJ&v=2&isig=aFeGOzW4MUE7dhNzYgWxMz7We98&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/a9qhgorl/GPS-5346537-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> вҖҷ is going to become the evolution of US-China competition. US officials could soon take action <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=_rXs6Lf&sig=OXS5aCdcr5gOkVmmkp9DBEYKVkT&v=2&url=https://www.politico.com/news/2026/06/23/lutnick-china-robots-commerce-00972576?nid%3D0000018f-3124-de07-a98f-3be4d1400000%26nname%3Dpolitico-toplines%26nrid%3D6689c2df-5a4c-473b-9b52-93479349fd17>  on Chinese Robotics as they increasingly see ChinaвҖҷs state-backed robotics industry as a national security threat, fearing subsidized robots could dominate global markets before U.S. manufacturers have the scale to compete. China accounted for ~80% of all humanoid robot shipments in 2025, while it also dominates supply chains for drones. 

*         EU вҖ“ the bloc is moving forward on two axes. 1) On AI/Tech, there is growing recognition <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=Isy0zoV&sig=3WAz84f3MHC8HjyEsO6OCAitaUN&v=2&url=https://europe2031.ai>  that dependence on others for AI technology has to be reduced. The response has been the EC Technological Sovereignty Package, which includes the Chips Act 2.0 and the Cloud & AI Development Act. The Chips Act 2.0 aims to build capacity in advanced semiconductor technologies, speed up permitting and support strategic projects. The Cloud and AI Development Act target a tripling of European data center capacity over the next 5-7 years and introduces a single EU-wide framework to assess cloud and AI sovereignty.  The initiatives so far are encouraging but consensus is that targets are unlikely to be met <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=J5E-srC&sig=Tnp9qlXyTx0nrex65BxL8YPyCwZ&v=2&isig=FkVVoEZajcFjTYPZtLLH5Y6DOFT&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/5vn7ua27/GPS-5342249-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  and actionable ideas spans only a handful of names in Cloud and Satellite space. 2) On trade, focus is on the вҖҳsecond China shock <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=P0gh0zC&sig=83bLjuUxgVImE3YL3S0fRGjg1KY&v=2&url=https://www.politico.eu/article/eu-and-china-seek-to-ease-tensions-with-high-level-meeting-maros-sefcovic-wang-wentao/?secureweb%3Dblpcbbap%26__cf_chl_f_tk%3DRCHWGMJNkld4BIR8dEczuoTWhbyNduCwqITc0dEiig8-1782825680-1.0.1.1-cHDd4W7VKA5bqKNgE.ZyxDhyjmEZ8UNdOt_ZSf0hrik> вҖҷ. The EU has significantly increased its willingness to use targeted protectionist measures, especially in strategic sectors. Notable actions include the Industrial Accelerator Act (which mirrors aspect of ChinaвҖҷs state-led model such as local content rules) and recent move to increase steel tariffs and reduce duty free quotas to protect industry. More recently, the stance on China has also been hardened <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=P0gh0zC&sig=83bLjuUxgVImE3YL3S0fRGjg1KY&v=2&url=https://www.politico.eu/article/eu-and-china-seek-to-ease-tensions-with-high-level-meeting-maros-sefcovic-wang-wentao/?secureweb%3Dblpcbbap%26__cf_chl_f_tk%3DRCHWGMJNkld4BIR8dEczuoTWhbyNduCwqITc0dEiig8-1782825680-1.0.1.1-cHDd4W7VKA5bqKNgE.ZyxDhyjmEZ8UNdOt_ZSf0hrik>  (with a notable shift also from Germany) but a broader S301-style tool hasnвҖҷt been deployed. Instead. EU and China have agreed on an October deadline <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=ozQXHxQ&sig=M5vSy4gCn9ym9o3ZQMp9bYfaLOI&v=2&url=https://www.politico.eu/article/eu-china-dialogue-trade-war/>  to recalibrate bilateral trade relationships. Here, we would note that a previous вҖңEU-China High Level Economic and Trade Dialogue,вҖқ launched in 2008, achieved no lasting breakthroughs. 

*         China вҖ“ The 15th Five-Year Plan (2026вҖ“2030) marks a decisive shift toward вҖңsecurity-firstвҖқ <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=CNkHAAW&sig=86w7LJ9lAmKe2GeTBOnCRI7b23J&v=2&isig=7mmT1nn7L0fya8EtoYpJIdbqUx9&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-fgsco5j/GPS-5301801-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  economic organization, emphasizing self-sufficiency in core technologies, supply-chain resilience, and strategic buffers in food and energy. The AI buildout is best viewed as a state-guided macro industrial program - not just a private tech capex cycle - with the ambition to embed AI across 90% of the economy by 2030. Unlike the US, national and local agendas are driving investment, financed through state directed channels such as government guidance funds and infrastructure programs. As a result, comparisons based only on private AI spending understates ChinaвҖҷs true investment scale.  Government funds are estimated to have deployed roughly $912bn across industries between 2000 and 2023, including about $184bn towards AI companies. In that context, on June 9, Bloomberg reported a potential RMB 2tr (1.4% of GDP) five-year, state-led AI data-center / computing-hub buildout, with total investment possibly rising to ~RMB 5tr (3.4% of GDP) if power-grid integration is included. Policy anchored localization and crucial roles in the global supply chains (e.g. Apple seeking approval to buy memory from China <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=hY5kCtq&sig=DxQlnqp7yvpZEdEjuLup2ObWn4j&v=2&url=https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/apple-seeks-approval-buy-chips-blacklisted-chinese-company-ft-reports-2026-06-27/> ) means that China AI ecosystem <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=zoZRuEw&sig=54MTPUgtHjaG3BwwOsHc3zT8iny&v=2&isig=T1okHjDnAewOEjkXutfsf2sszXt&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-3u2n66c/GPS-5338648-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  will remain in focus. Over the next 3вҖ“5 years, the companies most likely to emerge as long-term China tech giants <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=pinwCBZ&sig=2ADZ9VCVcE8IDxgAq9pesZoTn1p&v=2&isig=YjWEXfNDZcb13w0zZBf7eMOXLDI&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-hj4f6s9/GPS-5352560-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  will be concentrated in five high-barrier tracks: upstream equipment/materials, the wafer-fabrication вҖңfoundation,вҖқ domestically memory, advanced packaging & testing, and compute (CPU/AI chips). 

*         Other Asian Economics are fighting for their edge. The region is seeing a rapid increase in investment ex-China: (i) On June 24, JapanвҖҷs government called for over ВҘ370 trillion public-private growth investment in 17 strategic areas by FY2040 . Investing in strategic areas is a key theme for enhancing JapanвҖҷs growth potential and supporting the Japanese equity market. Putting aside the feasibility of the long-term target of over ВҘ370 trillion, we believe this investment could serve to increase JapanвҖҷs growth potential, corporate profit growth, and valuations. The government did not present a specific order of priority for investment areas, but we expect accelerated capital investment in the next few years in areas related to economic security and infrastructure, including AI / semiconductors (physical AI), cybersecurity, materials, resource and energy security, and disaster prevention and national resilience. (ii) in South Korea, the government and major AI ecosystem C-level executives have announced a mega-investment master plan (worth US$3.1T <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=6hCTWLN&sig=TWq7MPA0kSyTdwLtz1a0K7rkd2k&v=2&isig=InGfxFamoT0vCnspRAdqbNgeD8s&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-2r0cjdm/GPS-5350909-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> ) establishing a) semiconductors; b) AI robotics and physical AI; and c) AI datacenters as the three major growth pillars. The genesis of the investment stems from retaining the current AI leadership (especially in AI semiconductors) and leapfrogging as an AI export country through nurturing and developing various AI-derivative businesses including robotics and AI datacenters. Within the US$3.1T long-term investment plan, JPM <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=pinwCBZ&sig=2ADZ9VCVcE8IDxgAq9pesZoTn1p&v=2&isig=YjWEXfNDZcb13w0zZBf7eMOXLDI&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-hj4f6s9/GPS-5352560-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  estimates 60-70% to be allocated to front-end wafer equipment spending, 20-30% for infrastructure and cleanroom construction, and the rest for back-end packaging facilities but we expect to hear more details in the upcoming result season and follow-up corporate events. Our D1 Team has come up with a basket of Capex Beneficiaries from KoreaвҖҷs mega investment plan (JPG1KCPX).

GLOBAL CONSUMER вҖ“ PART 2

*         We have discussed and turned more constructive on the Global Consumer in our May Thematic Pulse <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=OJZW6uu&sig=IC4Hht8cbzhlWySwFOA6FAVPEjp&v=2&isig=QXM1i5uuxGBrWAxjVnVejA3eAOa&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/crj918dt/GPS-5317240-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> . Of note, our Global Equity Strategists <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=D9FGFqx&sig=NsmXTR70sPVE4IlDpsjpFhuTW9i&v=2&isig=VhqODxC8drLUTRPpeAW6vPkFlQW&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/772r32ip/GPS-5338264-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>   now see certain beaten down Consumer plays to show better performance in 2H. 

*         Our key argument <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=VlQbAfu&sig=1Qlhvg9qEYdyQgtdljgvOlUQag0&v=2&isig=8X2YZRAK1kC3mN6tVyuKC6LqP8v&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-c6oklj2/GPS-5351031-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  is that the global macro regime should shift back to recovery / expansion <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=uYL7AQN&sig=IDhQmb5HFKq403Etr2vFMgMb4Hn&v=2&isig=Mp0wm4d5yviUrjZRFwt3hM8Fy93&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/4d110166/GPS-5339502-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  (from slowdown/stagflation) and should drive an inflection in Consumer Sentiment (which in most parts of the world is near-record low). We have four main arguments for this: 

1.    The US vs Iran MOU & reopening of the SoH and the strong case for oil supply to expand (higher OPEC quotas, the UAEвҖҷs exit from OPEC, IraqвҖҷs threats to leave the cartel, the full removal of Iranian sanctions, increased Venezuelan output etc.). This eventually translates to a tailwind for real income

2.    Normalization in business sentiment from last yearвҖҷs trade war shock lifting ex-AI capex and labor demand (& with powerful base-effects)

3.    Supportive financial conditions alongside lower pressure on global CB hikes and a strong consumer wealth backdrop

4.    The strength of the US Consumer to drive global consumption. Detailed color may be found in the latest Consumer Cash Pile note, here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=RdT9eEp&sig=7IZyuVEaWFtJxpsLDOnPDboxshO&v=2&isig=VVNrpFYuyrBG2POO4md1nYzHjhB&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> . The main takeaway is that in 26Q1, another record was set for cash levels with 4 of the top income quintiles having 8% - 29% more cash today than pre-COVID, on an inflation adjusted basis.

*         In this respect, DM Flash PMIs and some early Consumer Confidence readings are already signaling improvement, with Services output rebounding from recent lows in June (UK the exception). Meanwhile, Chase US credit card data sees Q2 spending averaging at 5.1% - well above Q1 average of 4% - and Discretionary spending at +5.1%. By income bracket, all cohorts show broadly stable discretionary spending trends vs last month, with middle- and upper-income consumers remaining resilient and running at 6-7% growth so far in June and at c.7% QTD. In this context, Research <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=kMdQtxE&sig=HBc9lrCFRtuWGelsNLMLbqez4De&v=2&isig=FBniLpfk89RQXWLzKhdYhN69eOg&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-50v0lhj/GPS-5318332-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  continues to expect ongoing support from American consumers for luxury companies and think this cohort will once again emerge as the best performing.

*         The Consumer complex also benefits from attractive price relatives (generally at multi-year low) and undemanding  valuations  Of note, while Cyclical sectors have strongly outperformed this year, Consumer Cyclical <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=D9FGFqx&sig=NsmXTR70sPVE4IlDpsjpFhuTW9i&v=2&isig=VhqODxC8drLUTRPpeAW6vPkFlQW&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/772r32ip/GPS-5338264-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857>  is the one area that has struggled most, with most consumer subsectors weak since CovidвҖҷs V-shaped move. 

*         Wealth effects remain a key point. 

o   In Korea <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13514284&ad=4536925850&h=8U-fbvi&sig=FiBrcMR8nhRfucc6Rhp0Bx4zlaL&v=2&isig=NV7ICIIOWfHObU4Px1UHUq7buf4&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/n5uur722/GPS-5300075-0/2be655df-041a-4849-b44b-6d4930f16857> , the ongoing wealth effect (from higher incomes and stock wealth) means the propensity to consume and invest in equities is likely to be higher going forward, particularly given the constraints on property investments. Within Korea, this can be played via department stores, cosmetics, travel, brokers, construction.

o   Note KoreaвҖҷs Tech Boom is now large enough to be meaningful at a macro level, creating a wealth boost not just for corporates and households, but even for the government - enabling long-term measures and planning. This should continue to trickle through consumer strength.

 

Further, the Consumer is a consensus UW globally and therefore could have a tailwind from POSITIONING

*         In Europe, Luxury net buying picked up over the last month, rising to 0.7zs (from -0.2zs prior). positioning remains low at the 21st  percentile on a long-term lookback, but has slightly increased vs May (12th percentile). While HF exposure is not looking stretched, Luxury has outperformed SXXP by nearly 5% since start of May.

*         US Travel (JP2TRL) saw 1.3zs of net buying last month, unwinding the prior monthвҖҷs net selling. Overall positioning remains low at the 21st percentile. 

*         As oil prices continue to fall we have seen net buying in Airlines (JP2AIR) accelerate (0.9zs), but overall positioning is on the higher side (81st percentile). 

*         Positioning in our US Low End Consumer (JP77LCON) basket is extremely low, at the 7th percentile. We saw -0.9z of net selling over the past month. We also saw net selling in US Retail (-0.9zs), but positioning is higher on a longer term lookback (63rd percentile).

 

MONETIZATION

*         Given the lack of broad earnings momentum, we would still stay selective. In our view, investors should favor Consumer Stocks with at least one of the following characteristics:

o   Target more resilient upper-end consumer (given wealth effects), with Luxury a key beneficiary (JPLX); 

o   Capture shift to experience-oriented spending ex. Hotels and Travel & Leisure (JP2TRL Index) - PMI bounce in June driven by Services recovery;

o   Are exposed to regions that have solid wealth and cyclical backdrop already (Korea, Taiwan, US) 

o   Stand to benefit from a substantial decrease in oil price in 2H26 вҖ“ e.g. Airlines (JP2AIR)

TRACK FLOWS IN THEMATICS

*         We have started tracking flows into 16x Secular Equity Themes. 

*         The AUMs are relatively small (c. 500bn vs c. 9.2tn for US Equities) but are rising fast. The cumulative daily net organic flow (in % of AUM) for Thematic ETF is 40% since June of last year. This is approximately 4x-5x what we are tracking for US Equities. 

*         Over the past month, organic flows have been slowing pace (25%ar) but are still running at 2-2.5x whatвҖҷs seen across US Equities. 

*         In June, the Themes with the strongest inflows have been AI, Infrastructure and Quantum while those with weakest inflow have been Blockchain, Space and EV & Battery.

*         This trend underscores the growing importance of Thematic Investing

emails.subject: Exclusive: Anthropic Preps Claude Agent in Microsoft Teams
emails.body:
Salesforce and Microsoft may be worried about threats posed by AI upstarts like Anthropic to their core enterprise apps, but theyвҖҷre taking a conciliatory approach. Even though the two incumbents sell proprietary AI agents that can be used inside their Slack and Teams messaging apps, for instance, theyвҖҷve decided the apps ought to be a вҖңplatformвҖқ or app store for any AI agents their customers might want, including AnthropicвҖҷs Claude. A partnership Anthropic and Salesforce announced last week lets Slack customers summon Claude in their group chats to review their communications and handle workplace tasks, as long as they connect it to the other apps or databases their business uses.  And Anthropic recently told Microsoft it is planning to develop such a feature for Teams, according to someone briefed on the discussions.

________________________________

Welcome back!

Salesforce and Microsoft may be worried about threats posed by AI upstarts like Anthropic to their core enterprise apps, but theyвҖҷre taking a conciliatory approach.

Even though the two incumbents sell proprietary AI agents that can be used inside their Slack and Teams messaging apps, for instance, theyвҖҷve decided the apps ought to be a вҖңplatformвҖқ or app store for any AI agents their customers might want, including AnthropicвҖҷs Claude. A partnership Anthropic and Salesforce announced last week lets Slack customers summon Claude in their group chats to review their communications and handle workplace tasks, as long as they connect it to the other apps or databases their business uses. 

And Anthropic recently told Microsoft it is planning to develop such a feature for Teams, according to someone briefed on the discussions.

The upcoming Claude agent in Teams would likely compete directly with MicrosoftвҖҷs own Copilot AI tool, which is a key to MicrosoftвҖҷs revenue growth strategy. But Teams, like Slack, already allows customers to use external AI agents from coding app Cursor, product development app Linear and search app Perplexity, whose capabilities overlap with that of Copilot. (Teams has several times more users than Slack.)

Similarly, the new Claude agent in Slack, Claude Tag, would seem to cut against SlackвҖҷs effort to make its competing AI agent, Slackbot, a big business in its own right. Yet Salesforce promoted Claude Tag heavily in social media.

That prompted plenty of Salesforce employees to worry about whether Anthropic was akin to a fox entering their hen house, we reported over the weekend <https://url3396.theinformation.com/ls/click?upn=u001.71kYkaWDpGOJSzbGrs4y1TNF0-2FB-2Bh5pDUdkL0JSEoBmbIlaCwUAb-2B2vhxG1AFBaNonpHefRpRHnEXQdr33ZUZUEOSDmVH4VlGlCdIkjEP2CvdUwM1CKbC-2FTyo0WtsMDPVSuZ8qbRwKQZG8xYADxAIFZ-2FPh5OH9rsbipYs9yvW38LJ9rz-2FTbqJRQTTgOM246hLEw09WkkxyCjBvYBTjhOOP4xLhGEgU-2BEz9dxZvOzHp10OO-2BWRXkkIqqmFhvqDu0yVt2Z60IS-2BH3AtPwcTIpvUw-3D-3Dvdbc_m62Kq-2BZVlqTCB7tW71uBlxzucvmcl39EI5mlNrCbXb2EsQ9L-2FX-2Fo5QkOPg8S9quK5BsflXFxY9iTjXFjDcOJqz-2ByoeK6iLgeLS6yJqPGUCOb2wH6c0KKiMI5o0beN-2F7U1qon3UrWvVU1LncFlZerI3WHJlZOEAM9f-2FzYpKSs9paBQERQ62pBl-2BG94ffLLUvK1RuwKl6-2B1Ai9X6uApxrsnJqg97swJHDtBzVVwSCxWNgRQiR8K6m561WdILJFmvYs61BVDqqtUYc9cKOifTIvcUbv-2BcHBeXDJbPunaWBqpX3uX-2FZJmrx1paEMt6knltOjDMbh5W4UCeEPNDMQfBYvLDhqgXA7KG6WXaPXcVftQPM-3D> . Their concerns were understandable: As some of you may recall, an Anthropic official even said publicly last fall that the AI firm used its Claude to create a workplace chat app similar to Slack <https://url3396.theinformation.com/ls/click?upn=u001.71kYkaWDpGOJSzbGrs4y1TNF0-2FB-2Bh5pDUdkL0JSEoBkayEPumDOht6B6TI0Zt47OOPdyFmh0DcQxoqsLQlgwtFi2TBqIQkVoHMnnmb7zshsi2zTAKag43WP2JaAK9TS4TuqUiHyQgA8nY8QFgQ4jQmawVI6Vhtvgq16dz5E-2FWhpQn0xpyYlFbQHStdGCCxN4tBDl5BjM7kRuFODAYX8rKDQtJriB9Dpil-2ByY0F-2FRZ3nqUCuLpU9Iej3qFgxWjqYR1w2KGYeJPCEKmRBstkIYhz2EVNPjaihWBInFc8xHhm4-3DOiWi_m62Kq-2BZVlqTCB7tW71uBlxzucvmcl39EI5mlNrCbXb2EsQ9L-2FX-2Fo5QkOPg8S9quK5BsflXFxY9iTjXFjDcOJqz-2ByoeK6iLgeLS6yJqPGUCOb2wH6c0KKiMI5o0beN-2F7U1qon3UrWvVU1LncFlZerI3WHJlZOEAM9f-2FzYpKSs9paBQERQ62pBl-2BG94ffLLUvK1RuwKl6-2B1Ai9X6uApxrsnB7lvk1edil7nSpSm4KpR3fJ5HtHXCp9mqUHMNRVjjtkRd9nNqHXiEdK3fuhJja7uAvBg-2BLva9PLT-2F-2B33hkJne2gOZA6eeyk0WAcNp4MeVpljoQsnI19z3H5d5uJBM0-2FGhItOpT3IuByZj-2ByMCbGLR8-3D> !

It doesnвҖҷt help that Andrej Karpathy, an Anthropic staffer and AI celebrity, last week appeared to dismiss вҖңSlack botвҖқ in comparison to the Claude agent for Slack, which he referred to as a вҖңnew paradigmвҖқ in AI-human relations.

Needless to say, other software executives had strong reactions to these developments.

Salesforce opening up Slack to Anthropic seemed вҖңcrazy...like a real misstep that salesforce would allow this,вҖқ Parker Conrad, CEO of HR app Rippling, said in a post on X in response to our story over the weekend.

A New вҖҳLikeвҖҷ Button?

Another major software CEO told us privately that the dynamic reminded them of when website publishers allowed Facebook to place a вҖңLikeвҖқ button on their sites. While the publishers did so to be more relevant to their visitors, the move gave Facebook tremendous intelligence about the web and its content as it pursued ways to bring such content into the вҖңwalled gardensвҖқ of its social media apps.

Other onlookers viewed things differently. They said the Salesforce move seemed in line with the plan many enterprise apps have to allow their customers to use AI agents made by other companiesвҖ”for a fee.

But the external AI agent providers donвҖҷt need to pay Microsoft for the privilege of operating the agents in their messaging apps, according to a company spokesperson. The same is likely true of Salesforce, as well: sources familiar with two AI app developers, Viktor and Perplexity, told us they pay the software giant nothing to host their agents on Slack. And MicrosoftвҖҷs public app store policies say the company only collects fees when customers spend money through third party apps, which isnвҖҷt the case for such Teams plugins. 

Salesforce declined to comment on the financial terms of its deals with other agent providers.

We donвҖҷt know the details of AnthropicвҖҷs financial arrangement with Salesforce, which is probably fairly complex, given that Salesforce actually uses AnthropicвҖҷs models to power Slackbot and its other key AI agent tool, Agentforce. For all we know, Anthropic could be giving Salesforce a giant discount on that model access in exchange for promoting the Slack integration.

Driving Bot Awareness

The attention Slack got for the Claude Tag integration also appears to have alerted more Slack customers to the existence of AI agents in the messaging app, and that could be great news for SalesforceвҖҷs strategy. 

For instance, Poland-based AI startup Viktor told us it saw its вҖңbest week everвҖқ following the Claude Tag launch, with more than 400 new customers signing up to use its generalized AI agent. The agent can work across Slack channels to help with tasks such as performing a cost analysis of a potential supplier by surfing the web for alternatives, for example. 

AI search firm Perplexity, which launched its Computer AI agent as a Slack app in April, said the number of enterprise users of its agent on Slack has risen 17% per week on average since then. Perplexity also offers Computer through Microsoft Teams but has existed longer in Slack, where customers can use it to handle a variety of administrative and coding tasks, such as creating a coding bug ticket in GitHub or tapping Snowflake databases to analyze a companyвҖҷs performance and to produce slideshows or spreadsheets.

MicrosoftвҖҷs approach is even more strategic. It says it lets virtually any software provider build features for customers that connect to Microsoft applications. For example, Microsoft has long allowed Anthropic customers to use Claude to take over work theyвҖҷre doing in Microsoft apps such as Excel, Powerpoint, and Word <https://url3396.theinformation.com/ls/click?upn=u001.71kYkaWDpGOJSzbGrs4y1TNF0-2FB-2Bh5pDUdkL0JSEoBkayEPumDOht6B6TI0Zt47OiB1Z9q0Df3oOusdqR8T7GflmQU2Ya6NCi2P7DeAb-2B2j8Wkn-2FTDcb-2FIwHj9SN3beKZ-2Br2-2FiPceKWTyZRuqx9jlj2w5Koq-2Fy2QF4NruchEInTe0zUEnsrV8tpjxtGeoTY4dRitnTsiEgniJBM6s3u6MwlQjvzxu3svHPcKuezs8TtHC-2FvLrc7bdsvBkE6Q8Ek8Smdm_m62Kq-2BZVlqTCB7tW71uBlxzucvmcl39EI5mlNrCbXb2EsQ9L-2FX-2Fo5QkOPg8S9quK5BsflXFxY9iTjXFjDcOJqz-2ByoeK6iLgeLS6yJqPGUCOb2wH6c0KKiMI5o0beN-2F7U1qon3UrWvVU1LncFlZerI3WHJlZOEAM9f-2FzYpKSs9paBQERQ62pBl-2BG94ffLLUvK1RuwKl6-2B1Ai9X6uApxrsnAOxpgTAsAlXCji3lCHH9-2BEDO1fgM-2BxfsQPV-2FD8fKVyeGANuPDZs0Q0U4TyGrjQbTAizrjsZ2WHGpopWCvNf7HWeakSKuIp4U9TA-2FuFi8UfianTQzs6HeSgR0SQvMRMxpszEsxf-2Bv1zjl8z5WmDSi6Q-3D> , despite selling Microsoft own competing agent, 365 Copilot.

Without explicitly naming Anthropic, CEO Satya Nadella told shareholders in April that plugins like the Claude Office features would help entrench Microsoft among customers.

вҖңItвҖҷs fascinating that here we are in 2026, and the most exciting things [in AI] are plugins in Word or Excel,вҖқ Nadella said. вҖңWhen you see that, that means we have a structural position in knowledge work.вҖқ

emails.subject: BofA: 2 GREAT NOTES ON CONSUMER & CULTURAL TRENDS IN THE US & CHINA--> Gen Diya and China Cool
emails.body:
Good afternoon, 

IвҖҷve flagged these notes before but IвҖҷm putting them in front of you again, because NEW EDITIONS just hit andвҖҰ..
*	Both are insightful, well written, fun-to-read pieces on key consumer/culture/tech trends that have gotten excellent investor and corporate feedback
*	Both touch on US, European, ChinaвҖҰreally, Global trends that should help with idea generation and more, andвҖҰ 
*	They are a showcase of some rising star talents here at BofA, Diya Panjabi who works on our team as a Consumer Specialist alongside Mike Dick, and Luokun Ren вҖңLKвҖқ on BofAвҖҷs APAC Advisory Sales team. 
 
DiyaвҖҷs вҖңGen DiyaвҖқ piece is below. CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE <mailto:diya.panjabi@bofa.com;%20michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20aliu11@bofa.com;%20brian.fenske@bofa.com?subject=Hey%20-%20Pls%20SUBSCRIBE%20me%20to%20the%20Gen%20Diya%20distro.%20Thx.%20> . 
 
LKвҖҷs вҖңChina CoolвҖқ June Edition is attached. CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE <mailto:luokun.ren@bofa.com;%20aliu11@bofa.com;%20brian.fenske@bofa.com?subject=Hey%20-%20Pls%20subscribe%20me%20to%20LK's%20CHINA%20COOL%20distro.%20Thx.%20> . 

I also love hearing any feedback on these!
 
Thank you, 
Brian
 
From: Panjabi, Diya <diya.panjabi@bofa.com> 
Sent: Sunday, June 28, 2026 10:11 AM
To: Fenske, Brian <brian.fenske@bofa.com>
Subject: Gen-Diya: Part IX.
 

BofA Consumer: Gen-Diya, Part IX
 
________________________________

This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research. For Institutional Investor Use Only. 
 
GEN-DIYA: PART IX.
 
As a recent[ish] Gen-Z college graduate who is now covering the Consumer sector with Michael Dick at BofA, I thought IвҖҷd put my high screen time and shopping addiction to use. With that in mind, here is the next edition of Gen-Diya, a series filled with Gen-Z observations, trends, channel checks, and more. 
 
Subscribe HERE <mailto:nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20diya.panjabi@bofa.com?subject=Hi!%20Please%20Subscribe%20Me%20to%20Gen-Diya> , and request past editions of Gen-Diya HERE <mailto:nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20diya.panjabi@bofa.com?subject=Hi!%20Pls%20Send%20Me%20Past%20Editions%20of%20Gen-Diya> . IвҖҷm also planning on conducting a survey of BofA summer interns and sending it out soon, so please keep an eye out, and let me know if there are any questions youвҖҷd like answered!
 
With that, letвҖҷs get into some of the top Gen-Z trends IвҖҷve been seeing lately:
 
KNICKS FEVER X KALSHI X вҖҰ LOVE ISLAND?
 
WhatвҖҷs better than a Knicks win?? A Knicks win AND free food and drinks! Sounds too good to be true, right? Not for those who watched the game at The Jeffrey, a bar on ManhattanвҖҷs Upper East Side. 
 
During the Eastern Conference Finals, the bar offered customers 1% off of tabs for every point the Knicks won byвҖҰ and took a $4,000 hit as a result. Together, Owner Andy Freedman and Kalshi came up with a brilliant plan to offset the financial hit from promotional strategies like this one: Hedge the risk on Kalshi.
 
For the next big game, The Jeffrey used Kalshi to place a $5,000 wager on the Knicks. Knicks win? The bet pays out, and covers everyoneвҖҷs tabвҖҰ AKA, free food & drinks on the house! Knicks lose? The wager loses $5k, BUT the massive crowd they drew in more than makes up for it in revenue (source <https://fortune.com/2026/06/03/nyc-bar-knicks-kalshi-win-prediction-market/> ).
 
TheyвҖҷre not the only ones. Bars across NYC followed suit, and so did my local bagel shop. Utopia Bagels hopped on the Kalshi train to hedge the market, offering to use the payout to cover everybodyвҖҷs breakfast the following morning. And letвҖҷs just sayвҖҰ IвҖҷm sure that Sunday morning bagel was desperately needed by many.
 
Plus, the energy of the crowd is 10x higher with rewards at stake. Sounds like a win-win to me! I expect (and hope) weвҖҷll see more of this in the future, especially with the World Cup in progress and Football season around the corner (go Seahawks!)
 
What else has Kalshi been up to? TheyвҖҷve created markets where people can bet on Love Island eliminations / top couples / more! IвҖҷll admit, while I havenвҖҷt been a user in the past, this is both (1) extremely tempting, and (2) something I unfortunately have extensive knowledge on. In other wordsвҖҰ if that вҖңPurchases of the MonthвҖқ section gets a whole lot longer, you know whatвҖҷs funding it!
 
For more on Kalshi, check out this awesome private profile <https://rsch.baml.com/r?q=X0MMVc8u9rUc1nf-ZC3WOA&e=brian.fenske%40bofa.com&h=jYZ4Xw>  from analysts Shaun Kelley and Julie Hoover. 
 
THE вҖңFIFA 15вҖқ
 
The latest trend thatвҖҷs taken over TikTok? Food reviews from visiting World Cup fans. TheyвҖҷre immersing themselves in all of the quintessential American food experiences, with the most common stops IвҖҷve seen being ChiliвҖҷs (specifically for the triple-dipper), Taco Bell, In-N-Out, a 1am Waffle House run (classic), Starbucks, Crumbl, Olive Garden, IHOP, Raising Canes, Texas Roadhouse, Chipotle, automated soda machines, ReeseвҖҷs Oreos, and Cheesecake Factory (where most have gotten too full to make it to the actual cheesecake). Their reactions are hilarious, a mix of delight and true awe.
 
Similarly, many are in absolute shock at the bottomless chips and dip at restaurants, experiencing the ultimate вҖңcannon eventвҖқ (Gen-Z slang for an unavoidable and highly common learning experience): eating too many chips, and consequently being too full to enjoy the actual meal. 
 
My favorite comment? With all of the new foods being tried, people are saying it will result in visitors heading back home with the вҖңFIFA 15вҖқ (not to be confused with the Freshman 15).
 
Posts saying things like вҖңTo those heading to America for the World Cup, if thereвҖҷs a Texas Roadhouse near your hotel, head there immediately!!вҖқ and вҖңIs this size normal in America?вҖқ, as well as videos of visiting fans wearing cowboy hats from Buc-ees (another favorite, though my Texas self may be biased here) and calling Costco a вҖңmuseum-like lifechanging experienceвҖқ are also going viral, bringing strangers together and creating wholesome laughter across social platforms.
 
вҖҰ and we canвҖҷt forget about the other fan obsession: вҖңEUROPE WE NEED RANCH ASAPвҖқ. In fact, so many people attempted to bring full size bottles of the condiment back home with them, that the TSA had to issue a warning:
 
"If you're visiting for a very large sporting event & you happen to discover RANCH while you're hereвҖҰ please pack it in your CHECKED BAG on the way home."
 
Followed by:
 
вҖңOk please avoid chugging your ranch outside security, the airlines will check it for you.вҖқ
 
вҖҰ Kraft announced a TSA-friendly ranch dressing kit shortly after. 
 
Fans of Team USA have also dialed up hilarious patriotic comments post-US wins, commenting things like: вҖңCelsius is a drink, not a temperatureвҖқ, вҖңWeвҖҷre gonna win the Soccer BowlвҖқ, вҖңThereвҖҷs no L in United States of AmericaвҖқ, and вҖңWhatвҖҷs 2-0 in Celsius?вҖқ, all accompanied by eagle emojis, of course.
 
FIFAвҖҷS STADIUM SPONSOR COVER-UP
 
Restaurants arenвҖҷt the only ones winning from the World CupвҖҷs popularity. As a part of FIFAвҖҷs efforts to de-brand host stadiums for the World Cup, they had LeviвҖҷs put a massive white tarp over its logo, both inside and outside of the stadium. Rather than just вҖңtaking the L (loss)вҖқ, LeviвҖҷs used it as a moment to create a viral marketing campaign, and changed its social media profile picture to reflect the covered up logo. They even extended the bit to locations around the world, putting the sheet on storefronts in Paris, London, Brazil, Mexico, Hong Kong, and more (source <https://www.sfchronicle.com/sports/article/levi-s-stadium-s-sheet-covered-logo-went-22315774.php> ).
 
Gillette also made a lighthearted joke out of it, posting the below picture with the caption вҖңAt least we got to choose how we cover itвҖқ:
 

 
Heinz Ketchup also fell victim to the stadium de-branding, with FIFA putting black tape over logos on their condiment bottles. They then posted the following:
 

 
VIRALITY vs. BRAND LOYALTY
 
A couple of months ago, I heard the following quote on the Coty earnings call:
 
вҖңWe believe we went too far in terms of spending a lot on Gen Zs, who are by nature the least loyal consumers on earth instead of keeping our key loyal millennial and Gen X users.вҖқ
 
They have a point. In a world where it feels like everyone is rushing to buy whatвҖҷs hot / viral, is Gen-ZвҖҷs consumption more SKU or moment-driven, rather than long-term affinity / brand loyalty? Business of Fashion even put out a social media post depicting how nowadays, conglomerates deploying the most viral products are seeing the highest growth, with LвҖҷOreal, Amorepacific, and Puig mentioned. 
 
Now, there will always be those timeless / classic items, such asвҖҰ LeviвҖҷs 501 Jeans, the Ralph Lauren Polo Bear and cable-knit sweaters, Adidas Sambas and 3-stripe track pants, Nike Air Jordans, Calvin KleinвҖҷs Classic Logo Waistband, etc.. However, those now co-exist with the growing layer of high-velocity, low-loyalty consumption. IвҖҷve heard more and more companies call out how hard it is to (1) attract and (2) hold on to the Gen-Z customer, and it seems like theyвҖҷre now artificially trying to create these вҖңviralвҖқ moments to make it happen, often utilizing influencer partnerships to do so. As another example, e.l.f.вҖҷs new haircare line was dropped on TikTok Shop before it was even available on their website, likely another attempt to curate this sought-after virality. VictoriaвҖҷs Secret was even asked a question about it on their last earnings call:
 
вҖң вҖҰ The younger customer loves the scarcity model. You know, that's almost as important to them as the cool factor. They love to wait in line for things вҖҰ the ability to do collaborations and drops that sell out kind of like the Dot Cakes / Parke sweatshirt model vis a vis either Victoria's Secret or PINK and how that could work?вҖқ
SoвҖҰ what brands feel a bit more immune to this structural shift? The ones that have intense brand loyalty / aura? Here are a few that come to mind (more difficult than expected)вҖҰ feel free to ping me with more.
 
Apple
Ralph Lauren
Spotify
Lego
Amazon
TiffanyвҖҷs 
 
Notice that some of these are Tech or Retail companiesвҖҰ perhaps thatвҖҷs another structural shift to watch.
 
THE COLOR OF THE SUMMER
 
Happy [slightly late] National Pink Day!
 
IвҖҷve noticed quite a few brands dropping pink-themed products this summer, and wanted to flag a few:
 
First, Starbucks is dropping a new pink Bearista cup <https://about.starbucks.com/press/2026/meet-the-starbucks-pink-bearista-glass-a-pink-drink-inspired-collectible-coming-in-july/>  in mid-July, which they say was inspired by their customer-favorite Pink Drink. Recall, StarbucksвҖҷ original Bearista cup from November took the internet by storm and sold out immediately, with people waking up at 2am to get oneвҖҰ it even caused some (small) fights in stores (more <https://www.wsj.com/business/hospitality/starbucks-holiday-bearista-bear-cup-dd6b973b?eafs_enabled=false> )! 
 
Considering (1) I tried HARD to acquire one last time, and was unfortunately unsuccessful, (2) pink is my absolute favorite color, as evidenced by the color of my desktop background, Bloomberg IBs, and calendar, and (3) I am a Starbucks fanatic, I will be doing everything in my power to acquire one of these. Mike even gave me permission to come in late, so that I could wait in line! #bestbossever
 

 
Second, Elle, a series that follows the high school adventures of Elle Woods from [my favorite] movie Legally Blonde, comes out July 1stвҖҰ and PINK strategically collaborated with the creators in order to introduce their own вҖңLegally gorjвҖқ (short for gorgeous) collab. The items resemble the clothes that Elle wore in the original movie, and some even have certain iconic phrases from the movie written across them. View the whole collection HERE <https://www.victoriassecret.com/us/pink/collabs/elle?brand=pink&scroll=true> . вҖңToo much pink? I object!вҖқ
 
Third, Malibu meets Manhattan with DunkinвҖҷ DonutsвҖҷ new Pink Barbie Menu. They even hosted a large pop-up event here in NYC (and yes, I attended)!
 

 
THE EMPTY NESTвҖҰ FILLS BACK UP?
 
According to new data from Realtor.com, one third of young adults are still living with their parents, as вҖңold tabooвҖқ becomes the вҖңnew normalвҖқ amid soaring costs (source <https://www.foxbusiness.com/lifestyle/one-three-adults-under-35-lives-parents-housing-costs-soar-data-shows> ). The article goes on to say that whatвҖҷs holding them back isnвҖҷt a lack of qualifications, but rather a lack of housing they can actually afford. In fact, about 70% of the young adults living with their parents in this survey are employed. 
 
The opening joke on Love IslandвҖҷs first episode was also about affordability issues. Contestants compete for a $100k prize, and the announcer joked that the money would be just enough to cover half a tank of gas. 
 
Further, I ordered a small cup of chicken and rice soup earlier this week because I wasnвҖҷt feeling well. Any guesses on the price?:
 

 
Answer: Now, not only am I sick, but IвҖҷm out a whopping $9. Sad!
 
NIKE: CULTURAL MOMENTUM
 
TheyвҖҷve had a few relevant moments lately that I wanted to touch on:
 
First, their ad about the New York Knicks NBA Championship was quite literally a masterpiece, and was utterly adored online.
 
Second, Fanatics said it took in >8,000 orders per minute following the KnicksвҖҷ win over the Spurs, a new company record, and the Nike NBA Finals Champions T-Shirt (view <https://www.fanatics.com/nba/new-york-knicks/new-york-knicks-nike-unisex-2026-nba-finals-champions-locker-room-t-shirt-black/o-1392+t-47929675+p-4644262658283+z-9-666761875?irclickid=QtHS11y15xyZUU4R-IRg4ytUUkuQmZVeLR%3A2140&irgwc=1&afsrc=1&_s=afl_impact&utm_source=Impact&utm_medium=affiliates&SSAID=10078> ) was one of the bestselling items, among other Nike merch.
 
Third, Nike is bringing its biggest Toma El Juego tournament to Bryant Park; IвҖҷve actually walked past it on my lunch break a few times. ItвҖҷs an immersive 3-night celebration that honors the creativity, freedom, and community that define street soccer (and it looks pretty sick); more here <https://bryantpark.org/calendar/event/toma-national-finals/2026-06-25> !
 
LACROSSE SEGUE PIPELINE FINANCE
 
ItвҖҷs another вҖңFinance BroвҖқ summer! A creator on TikTok has gone viral for making hilarious videos about classic finance archetypes, such as playing lacrosse, visiting Nantucket, living in a place like Darien or Greenwich, etc..

If you havenвҖҷt seen it, itвҖҷs worth looking up for a good laugh. 
 
A FEW ONE-LINERS ON WHATвҖҷS TRENDING RIGHT NOW, THINGS TO WATCH FOR, AND MORE:                                 
 
FoodвҖҰ Tom Brady launched his own coconut water brand called Good Nut, and one of the Vita Coco founders made a viral video essentially calling him The GOAT, but not at coconut waterвҖҰ Shake Shack made a shake and custard based off of the viral dot cakesвҖҰ colleagues and I conducted positive channel checks of the Fried Apple Pie at McDonaldвҖҷsвҖҰ Utz Brands partnered with a beloved couple from the Bachelor on TikTokвҖҰ and thereвҖҷs a trend of letting TikTok decide your Starbucks order.  
 
Functional SnackingвҖҰ the desire for functional food and drinks such as prebiotic sodas, electrolytes, protein drinks, kombucha, energy drinks, fiber, collogen, non-alcoholic drinks, etc. is stronger than ever, enabling brands like Poppi, Olipop, Alani Nu, David Protein, Drizzilicious, and Barebells (which also just released a line of soft candies with 18g fiber and only 100 calories per bag) to take off. It fits in with the broader Gen-Z wellness trend thatвҖҷs been further fueled by the rise of GLP-1s. Brands have even started hosting races or вҖңrun ravesвҖқ with artists such as Diplo, and then handing out their healthy snacks (or athletic gear) afterвҖҰ and the events have been huge hits.
 
TechnologyвҖҰ Instagram Plus was announced, allowing people to extend their stories, access deeper story insights, preview stories without showing up in the viewer list, and moreвҖҰ Kylie Jenner teamed up with Meta to launch AI glasses, and they look more subtle than the original versionвҖҰ and people are excited about the new Oura Ring 5, which is a lot slimmer than the last model. 
 
Clothing and BeautyвҖҰ Target is doing collabs with Hollister and LoveShackFancyвҖҰ people like the Target 4th of July setsвҖҰ capris are coming backвҖҰ Edikted is opening a huge store on Fifth AvenueвҖҰ Dior came out with a luxury fitness and wellness collectionвҖҰ IвҖҷm seeing constant ads for Sambas on TikTokвҖҰ Prime Day is/was here!вҖҰ ULTA had a 20% off everything saleвҖҰ and IвҖҷm noticing a shift towards powdered foundations instead of liquid.
 
ONE LAST THINGвҖҰ
 
PURCHASE(S) OF THE MONTH
 
Considering I am influenced by anything and everything, I thought it could be fun to have a section on my purchase of the month. HoweverвҖҰ as it turns out, I make more than one irresponsible purchase per month (and hearing about all of the cool items being released while listening to earnings calls doesnвҖҷt exactly help). So, introducing a small section on any notable purchases IвҖҷve made lately: 
 
вҖңI keep thinking DiyaвҖҷs shopping, but sheвҖҷs actually writing content.вҖқ вҖ“ Nick Villani, Global Head of Content Distribution
 
(1) Hatch Alarm Clock
 

 
I wrote about this in the Gen-Z Holiday Shopping list and was finally influencedвҖҰ but at least I got a good deal, thanks to Prime Day! Hopefully waking up wonвҖҷt entail a jump scare from the вҖңRadarвҖқ iPhone alarm anymore.
 
(2) Mandatory purchases from the Aritzia Clientele Sale, of course:
 

 
For better or for worseвҖҰ I was a part of the first tier let into the sale. 
 
(3) Ralph Lauren sneakers for the office:
 

 
$70 for cute shoes I can wear everywhereвҖҰ IвҖҷll take it!
 
(4) I honestly Uber EatsвҖҷd wayyy too much this month, plus the $9 soup set me back, so I had to restrain myself from buying more, ha!
 
Thank you for reading! Feedback welcome, as always.
 
Subscribe HERE <mailto:nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20diya.panjabi@bofa.com?subject=Hi!%20Please%20Subscribe%20Me%20to%20Gen-Diya> , and request past editions of Gen-Diya HERE <mailto:nicholas.villani@bofa.com;%20michael.m.dick@bofa.com;%20diya.panjabi@bofa.com?subject=Hi!%20Pls%20Send%20Me%20Past%20Editions%20of%20Gen-Diya> .
