emails.subject: MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Independence Still On Shaky Ground - Judge
emails.body:
<https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXZxukI6aJhchl1Hw~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CC8ehptJe2eJOIQ> 	 <https://emedia.marketnews.com/marketnewsintl/mni-pr-01-25.png?t=1> 	


MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Independence Still On Shaky Ground - Judge

By Evan Ryser
Jun 30, 2026


WASHINGTON - A pair of U.S. Supreme Court rulings Monday on the president's ability to fire officials at independent agencies do not guarantee future Federal Reserve independence and leave Governor Lisa Cook vulnerable to further attempts to remove her, Columbia Law School professor Kathryn Judge told MNI.

"The news is helpful in protecting Federal Reserve independence, but the decisions by no means guarantee ongoing independence, either as a legal matter or as a matter of democratic legitimacy," she said in an interview. 

Cook can remain in her job while her case challenging TrumpвҖҷs attempt to fire her proceeds in district court, the Supreme Court said. That fight will take some time, and the justices left the door open for round two if the Trump administration wants to try again to get rid of Cook, Judge said. 

"There's a number of novel legal issues that are at play in the president's effort to fire Lisa Cook," she said. "There's no case law or precedent to look to." 

The court's other decision Monday in Trump v. Slaughter, allowing the president to fire officials at other independent agencies for any reason, "makes it all the more challenging by suggesting there are significant constitutional considerations, but also historical considerations that shape how cause should be understood."

The question of whether Trump could fire or demote Fed Chair Kevin Warsh remains unsettled, at a time when inflation is high yet the president is calling for rate cuts, Judge said. 

"One of the key issues that does remain open is whether Trump can demote a chair to becoming a pure governor, and there was nothing in today's opinions that resolved those issues, and if anything, the Slaughter case potentially makes it easier for a president to demote a chair."

CHANGING SURROUNDINGS

The Slaughter ruling gave the president sweeping new authority over approximately two dozen multi-member agencies that Congress intended to be independent, and leaves Fed independence on shakier ground than it has been in over 90 years, Judge said.

It wasn't until the court's 1935 decision in HumphreyвҖҷs Executor v. United States -- now overturned in Slaughter -- that Congress put in protections for Fed officials in the Banking Act of 1935. Previously, Congress had removed the "for cause" protection that members of the Fed board of governors enjoyed under the Federal Reserve Act because they didn't think it was constitutional. 

"The Fed really rose to independence alongside and in tandem with the rise of independent agencies generally," said Judge, who is working on a book about how the Banking Act of 1935 reformed the Federal Reserve System. 

"It's important not just as a legal matter, but as a matter of democratic legitimacy, for the public to believe, and for Congress to believe that there is a reason for the Fed to remain independent. The rationale of expertise and long-term decision making that the Fed has traditionally relied on is no longer going to suffice," Judge said. 

Champions of Fed independence should emphasize the Fed's unique structure to make a convincing argument, she said. 

"It's important to lean in to the distinct characteristics, and in particular the Fed's distinct decentralized structure, and the way that the reserve banks can promote accountability in helping the public to understand why the Fed deserves the special treatment that it seems to be getting." (See: MNI INTERVIEW: Fed Regional Banks Key To Independence - Judge <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXZxukI6aJhchl1HA~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CC8ehptJe2eJOIQ> )

REGULATORY POWERS

Whether the exception the court carved out for the Federal Reserve this week extends to monetary policy functions only or shields the central bank more broadly from presidential control remains uncertain, Judge said.

"The Federal Reserve today looks very different than the First or Second Bank of the United States, and in particular it has a whole variety of regulatory responsibilities that are very similar to regular responsibilities being carried out by the Comptroller of the Currency and by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation," she said.

"One of the interesting and challenging questions going forward will be how the Federal Reserve Board carries out those powers, consistent with protecting its independence over monetary policy."

For more information, please contact:
sales@marketnews.com <https://enews.marketnews.com/ct/x/pjJsdwXZxukI6aJhchl1Gg~k1zZ8KXr-kA8x6_CC8ehptJe2eJOIQ>

emails.subject: PULSE: Greater China Technology Semiconductors: Testing Consumables: Potential Price Hike Coming; Reiterate OW on MPI and WinWay
emails.body:
Greater China Technology Semiconductors 	
Testing Consumables: Potential Price Hike Coming; Reiterate OW on MPI and WinWay	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/c4dc0b2a-7485-11f1-9560-223b52d1e427?t=1782829191:2518:45476:5e2e4f67363b&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 	 
(PDF: 9 Pages) 	
Asia Pacific 	
Industry View   Attractive

Tiffany Yeh, Charlie Chan, Daniel Yen, CFA, Daisy Dai, CFA, Lucas Wang, Ethan Jia & 1 more...	 
June 30, 2026

	
Key takeaways	 

*	Rising precious metal prices and a severe test pin capacity shortage point to likely price hikes for probe cards and test sockets, passing costs on to customers. 
*	Very strong June results look likely in view of probe card and test socket demand, driven by a robust AI/HPC ramp and incremental DDIC momentum. 

With precious metal prices continually rising and a severe shortage in test pin capacity, our supply chain checks suggest that probe card and test socket vendors are likely to raise prices, passing costs on to customers вҖ” similar to other segments in the semi industry.

Reiterate OW on MPI and WinWay: With capacity tight and new supply taking time to ramp, we believe probe card and test socket vendors have pricing power. We also think MPI and WinWay are likely to post very strong June results, driven by robust AI/HPC ramp and even DDICs.

MPI's share price has lagged amid weak CPO sentiment; however, we see no change in insertion test demand as optical engines remain necessary regardless of what new technology FAU is adopting. We view 43x 2027e EPS as an attractive entry point to capture the 105% 2025вҖ“2028e earnings CAGR.

WinWay should start fully ramping the new capacity to fulfill strong backlog starting in June. Valuation is 39x 2027e EPS vs. our 2025вҖ“2028 EPS CAGR projection at 111%. Read more... <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/c4dc0b2a-7485-11f1-9560-223b52d1e427?t=1782829191:2518:45476:5e2e4f67363b&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG Autoblast#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==>

emails.subject: BofA TMT: 6/30 - AMZN, GOOGL, Bogey Polls, CHWY CFO Today, SPOT, CMCSA Takes
emails.body:
BofA TMT: 6/30 - AMZN, GOOGL, Bogey Polls, CHWY CFO Today, SPOT, CMCSA Takes 

________________________________

This is Sales Commentary Only, Not Research.  

 

MACRO: Nasdaq down a touch. Huge reversal yesterday AM in Bottleneck/shortage  themed sectors (Memory, HDDs, Optical, Neoclouds). June 30th, last day of month, of quarter of the first half of the year. EU Semis acting well. ASML has a meaningful catch-up trade its due for. 

 

I AM BULLISH HYPERSCALERS (thoughts in yesterdayвҖҷs note, ask for resend <mailto:brian.fenske@bofa.com;%20aliu11@bofa.com?subject=Hey%20-%20Pls%20send%20me%20he%20HYPERSCALERS%20Defend%20piece%20from%20Brian.%20thx.%20> ) вҖ“ but getting very MIXED feedback from you all. Pushback mainly on capex going up, FCF going down, massive investment cycle, whats right multiple, being dragged into tougher/worse business. I LOVE THE TENSION, hasnвҖҷt felt this way in a while. What do YOU THINK? EMAIL ME THOUGHTS <mailto:brian.fenske@bofa.com;%20aliu11@bofa.com?subject=Hey%20-%20My%20thoughts%20on%20HYPERSCALERS%20are...> . 

 

TODAY @ 1PM EST - TMT SUMMER FRIDAYS with CFO OF CHEWY (CHWY), CHRIS DEPPE and HEAD OF IR, LEE HOROWITZ, HOSTED BY MIKE MCGOVERN. Analyst Mike McGovern & Specialist Brian Fenske are hosting Chris Deppe, CFO, and Lee Horowitz, Head of IR & Strategic Finance, at Chewy (CHWY). Mike McGovern will lead the discussion, focusing on takeaways from the latest quarter and other key topics. The session will be interactive with plenty of time for investor Q&A. 

CLICK HERE TO REGISTER <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=fp8Hezm&sig=Kgvk6auAjeh9aO9QFvXspTlHeMM&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a%3D1000000000034422%26ad%3D1000000003631859%26h%3DDPzRVyy%26sig%3DURLrn92whWXUyU76UG4wbNCUuN2%26v%3D2%26url%3Dhttps:**Arsch.baml.com*events*aid*3D12339639*26did*3D23025382__;Ly8vPyUlJQ!!I2XIyG2ANlwasLbx!Rgk4kQDFcGbIC2zFJHvHspyg4QSR9ZW9DCzb52MInKO5z8csdXM5xhHgZETizHB6gDiFdw3e7gJ71adHBBY$> , Zoom details then sent

 

PLS TAKE A FEW MINUTES ON THIS FOR US---BIG TECH EARNINGS & CAPEX BOGEY POLLS. WeвҖҷre doing our Q2 buyside bogey poll on Big Tech earnings, with a special focus on CAPEX expectations looking out the next few years. Please take a few minutes. Below that weвҖҷre also freshening up buyside expectations on key Semi/Memory names. Appreciate your input. Fill in as few/as many as youвҖҷd like. We will share with respondents first. 

 

POLL #1 (MOST IMPORTANT): WHERE IS THE BUYSIDE ON BIG TECH EPS AND CAPEX? Below is a link to a quick survey weвҖҷre conducting to gather BUYSIDE EXPECTATIONS on upcoming Big Tech Q2 Earnings results and guides. In it weвҖҷre polling for quarterly/annual expectations on GOOGL, META, MSFT, AMZN, AAPL, ORCL, CRWV and NBIS

CLICK HERE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE BIG TECH EPS and CAPEX SURVEY <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=WTMoRP4&sig=OLaaiTY25SrCK1gS5kyb1prmTuq&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a%3D1000000000036547%26ad%3D1000000003860887%26h%3D3RVIccv%26sig%3DDJfxS0fomZhzot5ahnL8PWpyZ7z%26v%3D2%26url%3Dhttps:**Abankofamerica.co1.qualtrics.com*jfe*form*SV_02rkSQkmSFovjim__;Ly8vLy8!!I2XIyG2ANlwasLbx!TtE0wPfqUd5lQEdkj3nsnvAD7UEF8mUf-0lAZcQSL2OUzgbOXv2zSScTeO8nbpu3IN4P1rpS5BlbjpCB-4J9jA$> 


POLL #2: WHERE IS THE BUYSIDE ON SEMIS/MEMORY/HDD OUT YEAR EPS? While we have you, weвҖҷd love to freshen up our bogeys some key MEMORY/OPTICAL/AI SEMI/HDD names as well, following MU results last week. In it we asked for CALENDAR numbers for everything, so pls keep that in mind. Names are: MU, SNDK, LITE, COHR, NVDA, AVGO, AMD, MRVL, INTC, WDC, STX, GLW. 

CLICK HERE TO FILL OUT THE SEMIS HARDWARE BOGEY POLL <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=dUK6Y65&sig=Ot7Pvaci4atM1yxE7LoeRq8QBrz&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a%3D1000000000036547%26ad%3D1000000003860887%26h%3DrlYNj4Z%26sig%3DZSV7NWHhQWsrlOeFZJm0zh8oa2C%26v%3D2%26url%3Dhttps:**Abankofamerica.co1.qualtrics.com*jfe*form*SV_5C7cNtw2qP7Tka2__;Ly8vLy8!!I2XIyG2ANlwasLbx!TtE0wPfqUd5lQEdkj3nsnvAD7UEF8mUf-0lAZcQSL2OUzgbOXv2zSScTeO8nbpu3IN4P1rpS5BlbjpDbGkPWlA$>  

 

AMZN вҖ“ PRIME DAY AND AWS PRICE INCREASE. Justin highlighting that the data points to Prime Day sales +9% YoY <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=7RPAx6K&sig=3HwWhj6iHnKWPKAIn0j5wYPHVDO&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DlmgnCFyWfsOpatv5R9BuTQ%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DAH1idQ> , with stable discounts YoY. However, Numerator data points to average order size down -11% (could partially be a mix shift into essentials and grocery spend). There could be risk to Q3 guidance on Prime Day timing shifting forward (could be $7-8bn in sales shifting from Q3 to Q2). Separately, AWS raising fees 20% on select GPU workloads which can translate to a 1-2pt benefit to 2H AWS growth <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=7RPAx6K&sig=3HwWhj6iHnKWPKAIn0j5wYPHVDO&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DlmgnCFyWfsOpatv5R9BuTQ%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DAH1idQ> . AWS can continue acceleration driven by addition of OpenAI models on Bedrock, ramp up of capacity for the $38B OpenAI commitment, and an early ramp of the $100B Anthropic commitment. 

 

SPOT вҖ“ Q2 PREVIEW. Jessica Reif Ehrlich expects a largely inline print <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=oh75x2t&sig=8xE7xO1AF8FebxPX5NSx4buzL8d&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DdBF!!aoU3lwVcH7SxsXmKg%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DrL8biQ> , reflecting stable underlying trends and reported rev acceleration driven by moderating FX headwinds. Expect a ramp in opex driven by AI-related investments and increased marketing spend. The focus is now on execution post investor day, and waiting for more details around key initiatives around launch timing, pricing and uptake. Jessica maintaining Q2 revenue of вӮ¬4.78B, GM of 33.1% and OI forecast of вӮ¬630M, as well as 2026 rev of вӮ¬19.4B and OI of вӮ¬2.85B.

 

CMCSA - JESSICA SEES NBCU/SKY SPIN OFF SHARPENING FOCUS, CREATING STRATEGIC OPTIONALITY and PATH TO VALUE UNLOCK. Jessica Reif Ehrlich, who properly вҖңcalledвҖқ this spin in her January 12th Media Year Ahead piece, is out with her CMCSA thoughts following <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=6kNtaaT&sig=4pBlSBJfsRNvH8Q2KuYoFw9VGPq&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3Dmov5QiQQc9SFei1bPdHKkA%26e%3Dbrian.fenske%2540bofa.com%26h%3DnW4GEw>  the formal announcement yesterday that the company would spin into 2 companies, CMCSA Connectivity and NBCU/Sky (the parks/media assets). 

 

Jessica has extensively written that she believed the CMCSA assets were meaningfully undervalued and sees this tax-free spin as the START of a value unlock path. Key comments from Jessica:

*	MGMT OVERALL ON M&A FUTURE. вҖңOn consolidation, we would not over-interpret Brian Roberts' "absolutely not" response to M&A questions as foreclosing future optionality. We believe the transaction needs to be framed first as an organic value-creation and strategic focus story, not as the first step toward a sale/merger, but separation clearly increases optionality over time, in our view. At cable, consolidation remains logical, particularly if scale can improve mobile convergence, procurement, programming, customer acquisition and operating efficiency. вҖң

*	ON CHTR/CMCSA POSSIBLE MERGER: вҖңCharter's leverage is a key gating factor, and any CMCSA/CHTR scenario would still face regulatory scrutiny, but a cleaner Comcast is a more credible long-term consolidator or acquisition target than the current structure.вҖқ
*	ON FUTURE M&A/TRANSACTIONS RELATED TO THE NBCU SIDE: вҖңOn the NBCU/Sky side, we do not believe there is sufficient scale to stand alone indefinitely in a media landscape increasingly defined by global streaming, escalating sports rights, tech investment, and deeper content libraries. NBCU/Sky will have valuable assets across parks, studios, IP, sports and news, but as an independent public company it may ultimately need to participate in further consolidation to maximize its competitive position and equity value.вҖқ

 

JESSICA!!!вҖҰCan you pls take a second and give Jessica some credit for writing definitive research reports predicting/and deeply analyzing the WBD M&A path and breakup that led to itвҖҰ.and now the CMCSA spin/breakup and how it might evolve into more M&A. I have long known Jessica is the GOAT in Media, so I want to give her credit on all of this. 

 

CMCSA вҖ“ THE CREDIT VIEW. Tom Cucuruto sees limited net impact to credit metrics <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=INAu0-W&sig=O1OH2Zy9FMoQy7DGfZNVREOYvVM&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DHAFyChtcCNhdZDvlV62q4g%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DlTX6FA>  (expect pro-forma Comcast RemainCo gross/net leverage of about 3.1x/2.7xвҖҰ vs 2.3x net at 3/31 LTM), but policy uncertainty and loss of scale could pressure single-A ratings. Suspending share buybacks (~$7B NTM) could reduce net leverage by another 0.3x to 2.4x pro forma, consistent with CMCSA's existing leverage target (2.3x). Though CMCSA did not reiterate their leverage targets or credit ratings.  

 

JESSICA AND HER TEAMвҖҷS MEDIA PREVIEWS SO FAR

*	SPOT <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=RU657yK&sig=ZJ5xt4osWjEjLOFGK8uLqu5fQ28&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DB6pbfZ7kpRdf8h5xd3FB8g%26e%3Dsanchit.kaushik%2540bofa.com%26h%3DehFM1A> . June 30. No change to numbers.

LEVERED ETFs AND REBALANCE IMPACTS. The Derivs Strategist team highlighting that US L&I ETP AUM has reached $180B <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=escdh9_&sig=JOtAc5DrGQxQxrg72AjYr2sI9f4&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3Dn7LGqR1dHne0xrI3Sj6vdw%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DlQnMQQ>  and the total beta-adjusted rebalance is now nearly $27B per 1% SPX move (vs $12B in Nov 25 and tripling from March 2026 lows). Much of this is driven by Semis (SOXX, MU, SNDK and more, see Exhibit 16 for top rebalance per 1% SPX move and Exhibit 18 for the breakdown on biggest contributors). Looking at stocks with less liquidity or higher vol, ETPs have been >4% of a typical days volume on a 1Пғ move for names like AXTI, CRML, SNDK (Exhibit 22). Stress testing the rebalance impacts, a ETP rebalance would be 30% of volumes on a 20% 1day SPX decline with long-levered holders liquidating their holdings (Exhibit 20). That scenario would be market moving but they think unlikely given markets buy-the-dip response and unlikely to drive a runaway price spiral like VIX experienced in 2018. Lots of great charts in the note. 

 

ABNB вҖ“ HOTELS ROOM NIGHT CONTRIBUTION. Justin Post forecasts hotels can add 120bps to 2027-2030 nights CAGR <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=H44zR6M&sig=VQjMOD012DD2PKYfbch3ixCAWd8&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DXKldR1edw9og18puuZi!Cg%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DhUZv6A> , with scenario analysis in the note. ABNBвҖҷs hotels push is driving incremental supply and demand creation in cities with supply constraints, with Justin assuming hotel nights grow at a 30% CAGR to 7.5% of total nights by 2030 (upside scenario to 15% of total nights/210bps of CAGR contribution). OTAs likely face limited competitive pressure on nights, given the small base for ABNB hotels. See more scenario analysis in the note. 

 

IFX вҖ“ AI EXEC TAKEAWAYS, UPSIDE STILL UNDERAPPRECIATED. Didier Scemama hosted Adam White <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=MwzhKe2&sig=CybDXfkmFSH6GXPxQjAGy8oeF1w&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DWH9SE0oCl2TU76-rPrGGtQ%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DW5kAGQ> , President of IFX's Power & Sensor Systems (PSS) division for a fireside chat. Continue to view current content assumptions of $175/kW as conservative with incremental upside from HVDC, SiC/GaN, SSTs and vertical power. Didier continues to see content migrating to the high end, forecasting $235/kW by 2030. Server CPU exposure is also underappreciated, with rising power requirements and evolving AI workloads supporting a structurally higher content opportunity over time. Combined with expanding 300mm capacity, broad exposure across GPU, TPU, ASIC and CPU ecosystems, and a differentiated grid-to-core portfolio, the competitive moat continues to strengthen.  

 

LOGITECH вҖ“ DOWNGRADED TO UNDERPERFORM. Didier Scemama downgrading as rising OEM price increases <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=UH9LN9p&sig=4qJrRvd9Ej1QxuoouzoB23Mwbfp&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3DZVXYUcgZFv4OeZiuLACN8w%26e%3Dbofaml_european_sales%2540bofa.com%26h%3DJO2T6w>  across PCs, tablets, smartphones and gaming hardware are expected to drive demand destruction (PC Units -8% in CY26). He cuts revenue and EPS forecasts by -6-11% from FY27 to FY29 (now -9вҖ“21% below consensus), reflecting weaker end-market demand and an expected decline in PC units, with limited scope for growth recovery despite managementвҖҷs strong cost discipline. Margins should remain relatively resilient, but the risk/reward has deteriorated as slowing demand and higher promotional intensity weigh on the outlook. PO lowered to CHF70 (13x FY28 EV/EBITDA). 

 

CHINA POWER SEMIS вҖ“ TIGHT SUPPLY & BETTER PRICING. Daley Li sees China power semi in a continued upcycle <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=qCuAGL2&sig=Dbg5X4ZPLY4MkF5N99PZ7qgh5aJ&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3D-AHbzFR-rsGV0F1gPyngfw%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DXH4afg>  given AIDC & AI servers growth outlook and GM expansion on price hikes. Expect another price hike in 2H, especially for low-/mid-voltage power semis like MOSFET (see Exhibit 10 for beneficiaries). This is an additional re-rating catalyst for the sector. Daley prefers for NCE Power and upgrades Silan to Neutral. Revising estimates higher, see Exhibit 8. 

 

MEMORY вҖ“ KOREA W800T LONG-TERM MEMORY SPEND COMMITMENT. Simon Woo views South KoreaвҖҷs planned вӮ©800tn investment in a new memory cluster as reinforcing long-term government support <https://insight.email.bofa.streetcontext.com/platform/al?a=1000000000036567&ad=1000000003873101&h=Iu0zA8m&sig=OkzmvIoIw0vgOyCmTTHEsVw5lWB&v=2&url=https://rsch.baml.com/report?q%3D91!r6F8Pdq2V0F1gPyngfw%26e%3Dkushalappa.berera%2540bofa.com%26h%3DgQ9JKA>  for memory leadership, although meaningful supply additions remain many years away given infrastructure and construction timelines. Near-term supply dynamics are largely unchanged, with expansion still centered on Yongin and Pyeongtaek and wafer capacity growth expected to remain measured. Longer term, this should double memory capacity by 2030. Overall, the announcement is strategically positive for the memory ecosystem but does not materially alter the current supply outlook.

IQVIA Institute for Human Data Science

Evolving Use and Spending on Medicines in the U.S.
Navigating Patient Access Barriers and Cost Pressures
U.S. Medicine Use Trends 2026 webinar
Date: July 15, 2026
Time: 10 a.m. вҖ“ 11:30 a.m. ET
REGISTER NOW <https://app.constellation.iqvia.com/e/er?utm_campaign=2026_UseofMedicines2026Webinar_INSTITUTE_SM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Eloqua&s=460529241&lid=33799&elqTrackId=60EAB7980535F1581C2109FC3AF64D1E&elq=759e5e88eb064a2f8b200c7a9284d552&elqaid=31667&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5542D19FA358AB08935A86C758AAB0B20EA6AEC73DBEA98B1F9B602A4354AB561>  
The U.S. prescription medicine landscape is changing rapidly, shaped by rising use of innovative therapies, shifting vaccination trends, changing benefit designs and continued pressure on patient costs.

Join this webinar to explore the key trends defining medicine use and spending in 2025, including dynamics in obesity, immunology, oncology and other high-growth areas. The discussion will also examine access challenges and how policy may shape patient access, net spending and stakeholder decision-making over the next five years.

Speakers:

*	Stacie Dusetzina, Professor, Health Policy Ingram Professor of Cancer Research, Vanderbilt University Medical Center
*	Amanda Frost, Vice President, Research, Pharmaceutical Care Management Association (PCMA)
*	Luke Greenwalt, Vice President, Thought Leadership & Innovation, IQVIA U.S.
*	John OвҖҷBrien, CEO, National Pharmaceutical Council

Moderator:
Murray Aitken, Executive Director, IQVIA Institute for Human Data Science
Research highlights:
Jamie Pritchett, Associate Thought Leadership Director, IQVIA Institute for Human Data Science
REGISTER NOW <https://app.constellation.iqvia.com/e/er?utm_campaign=2026_UseofMedicines2026Webinar_INSTITUTE_SM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Eloqua&s=460529241&lid=33799&elqTrackId=5613A36D8B994E67329EF5FA4D2CD41E&elq=759e5e88eb064a2f8b200c7a9284d552&elqaid=31667&elqat=1&elqak=8AF53975B780ED2518A2594624CFCE14E7FCEA6AEC73DBEA98B1F9B602A4354AB561>  
Join the conversation about this important topic and more on LinkedIn <https://app.constellation.iqvia.com/e/er?utm_campaign=2026_UseofMedicines2026Webinar_INSTITUTE_SM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Eloqua&s=460529241&lid=3150&elqTrackId=B46ECD6154903FB53B44AC3E31EE0E34&elq=759e5e88eb064a2f8b200c7a9284d552&elqaid=31667&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5E1A46B3F7E04DC934D2C1AC585890133EA6AEC73DBEA98B1F9B602A4354AB561>  and X <https://app.constellation.iqvia.com/e/er?utm_campaign=2026_UseofMedicines2026Webinar_INSTITUTE_SM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Eloqua&s=460529241&lid=3149&elqTrackId=A54053553F528C41DF95EDAE91B85DB9&elq=759e5e88eb064a2f8b200c7a9284d552&elqaid=31667&elqat=1&elqak=8AF55708D13748A297D8339C44027EA9A8E0EA6AEC73DBEA98B1F9B602A4354AB561> . emails.subject: R&Co US Riser: Honeywell (Neutral) split, US Macro - Playbook for a rate cut, Ad Agencies - takeaways from Cannes, DoorDash (Buy) POS takeaways, Norwegian Cruise, Uber (both Buy), Music Labels, & US Gambling updates, Charts, IDEAS
emails.body:
Fact of the Day: President Bill Clinton signed a bill designating Lake Champlain the sixth Great Lake in March 1998. The title was rescinded two weeks later following public backlash.

 

Research Summary:

Ad Agencies: <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-k/>  Five Themes From Cannes

Entain (Buy): <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-t/>  Weighing Machine

CTS Eventim (Buy): <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-r/>  Fact from Fiction

Honeywell Technologies (Neutral): <https://research.cmail19.com/t/d-l-wlukdhy-dhkkdhiliy-y/>  Fairly Valued Post Aerospace Spin

Novo Nordisk (Buy): <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-i/>  Wegovy IP plumping up? 

Rheinmetall (Buy): <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-r/>  Choppy Waters 

Siemens (Buy): <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-d/>  To SI For                                 

US Macro: <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-u/>  Is Warsh Looking to Europe for Inspiration?

Norwegian Cruise (Buy): New Chief Marketing Officer appointed

Uber (Buy): Pilot with Waymo in Phoenix ended

UMG (Sell), WMG (Neutral), Spotify (Neutral): Google (Buy) argues fair use on AI training

US Gambling: Michigan gets TRO banning Kalshi  

 

Heard this morning on the sales deskвҖҰ

*	R&Co Redburn Internet and Payments analyst, Dom Ball, has just returned from a RoW marketing trip where he met with 30+ investors over 5 days. DoorDash (Buy) POS not well understood; bearish takeaways on Adyen (Neutral); Shopify (Buy) existential threats; card networks remain tricky. Please reach out to discuss takeaways in-depth.

 

Research Updates:

AD AGENCIES: FIVE THEMES FROM CANNES <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-k/>  - The Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity took place last week, with 500 speakers sharing their views and visions for the future of the marketing industry. We highlight several themes that emerged from the presentations, with AI taking center stage in topics that spanned: humans vs machines, the two-audience problem and the merits of effectiveness vs efficiency. Meanwhile, the growing importance of Creators in marketing briefs was another salient theme, as was the evolving role of agencies in the age of AI. We elaborate on these topics in the short email linked below and present our analysis of the Cannes Lions Grand Prix winners by holding group. 

 

HONEYWELL TECHNOLOGIES: FAIRLY VALUED POST AEROSPACE SPIN <https://research.cmail19.com/t/d-l-wlukdhy-dhkkdhiliy-y/>  вҖ“ On Monday, Honeywell completed the separation of its Aerospace business to leave the remaining company, Honeywell Technologies, as an automation pure play. In our in-depth report during May, we laid out pro forma medium-term estimates for Honeywell Technologies which were broadly consistent with the targets through FY29 that were subsequently provided by the company at its investor day on 11 June. In this note, we are establishing formal estimates for Honeywell Technologies that are not materially different from our pro forma framework. We are also establishing a DCF-based twelve-month price target of $214 which is consistent with our pro forma valuation. Given modest potential downside of 6% versus the current price we remain with a Neutral rating.

 

US MACRO: <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-u/>  IS WARSH LOOKING TO EUROPE FOR INSPIRATION? <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-u/>  - Kevin Warsh's new committee on the Fed's balance sheet is an opening to reassert what the Fed has spent a decade trying to separate: the link between the size and composition of the balance sheet and the level of interest rates. Europe has already shown how well a "demand-led floor" can work, and the Fed is quietly drifting that way вҖ” weekly Treasury buybacks, destigmatised repo, and a Treasury willing to put TGA cash to work have all cut the risk of fresh repo crises. But the more radical prize lies further north: Sweden's Riksbank still runs a "corridor" system, using liquidity-draining operations to hold overnight rates without remunerating every last reserve. If Warsh pushes the Fed down a similar path вҖ” active buybacks, reserve management purchases, open market operations and a shift toward an overnight repo rate as the signalling tool вҖ” the pay-off is a leaner balance sheet and scope to set short-term rates lower through the cycle. 

 

NORWEGIAN CRUISE: NEW CHIEF MARKETING OFFICER APPOINTED - NCLH has appointed Lee Applbaum as Chief Marketing Officer, effective July 6, 2026. Applbaum brings 25+ years building global consumer brands; he was global CMO at PatrГіn Spirits and Bacardi (Tequila PatrГіn and Grey Goose) and former CMO of Wheels Up. His focus at NCLH will be brand strategy, guest engagement and demand generation. The appointment fills a key seat in the senior-team rebuild under Chidsey, perhaps earlier than expected. However, for NCLH it is all down to execution which is still to be proven. We wrote about the opportunity at NCLH in our recent Hitchhiker's Guide, linked here. Trades on 9.7x 26E EV/EBITDA. Let us know if you would like to discuss Norwegian or cruise further.

 

UBER: PILOT WITH WAYMO IN PHOENIX ENDED вҖ“ Waymo AVs are no longer available via the Uber app in Phoenix, vehicles will remain in use to make deliveries with DoorDash (Buy). Phoenix was UberвҖҷs first pilot market with Waymo and was very limited in deployment (low teen number of AVs that were available on the Uber app versus Waymo having 500 AVs on its own app). As it stands the partnership between the two in both Austin and Atlanta still stand. ShouldnвҖҷt be a major surprise given most were not expecting this particular agreement to last very long given how small it was in scale, but may be considered blow to UberвҖҷs position in the value chain so shares likely off this AM. Trades on 11x FY27 EV/EBITDA.

 

UMG, WMG, SPOTIFY: GOOGLE ARGUES FAIR USE ON AI TRAINING вҖ“ In a new policy paper around AI regulation, Google has argued that training AI models on publicly available web data should вҖңremain protectedвҖқ by fair use in the US. The paper also says copyright concerns raised by gen AI are best addressed at the level of outputs, not inputs вҖ“ clearly an attempt to protect Gemini and its own gen AI music model Lyria 3 from rightsholder litigation. RDBN VIEW: Google is now using the same вҖңfair useвҖқ argument as Suno, which adds significant legal/financial heft to their side of the debate vs the major labels. The outcome of any ruling in this area will be very material, but may be drawn out longer now Google is in the fray. Bad news for the majors, Spotify is more agnostic.

 

US GAMBLING: MICHIGAN GETS TRO BARRING KALSHI вҖ“ News out that Kalshi is now subject to a temporary restraining order (TRO) banning it from MI state. Kalshi now needs to ringfence in the state, making this now the second state behind NV where it has to restrict its services. Continue to expect the legal tug-o-war to continue until we get a definitive answer from SCOTUS, but the legal momentum continues to favor the states, not prediction market operators.

 

International Research:  

CTS EVENTIM: FACT FROM FICTION <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-j/>  вҖ“ 1) ANALYSIS: The data shows CTS Eventim taking share in Europe, not losing it. In this report, we challenge several misconceptions about the European live music market, concluding that US players are structurally ill-suited to the region, and that the AI threat is substantially overstated. We see several catalysts in H2 and reit our Buy rating. 2) VS CONS: no fвҖҷcast changes, but +1-6% & +2-7% above cons on revs & EBITDA. 3) VALUATION (FY27e): trades on GFC-trough multiples вҖ“ 8x EV/adj EBITDA (target 15x), P/E 14x, FCF yield (ex WC) 6-7%, divi c4%, c1x ND/EBITDA вҖ“ and an unwarranted 50% discount to Live Nation (Buy)

 

ENTAIN: WEIGHING MACHINE вҖ“ 1) GETTINвҖҷ NO CREDIT: <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-t/>  Entain has never fully got credit for building CEE. The platform was meant to drive a new era of growth for Entain. But CEE never resulted in any meaningful valuation rerating, or higher share price. Instead the share price has fallen c58% since CEEвҖҷs creation. ВЈ1bn of capital allocated to CEE has seen ВЈ4bn of market cap destruction - so looking at divestments makes sense. 2) VALUATION IN FOCUS: With a valuation вҖҳmarkвҖҷ on CEE, we find the RemainCo assets are implicitly trading on just c4x EV/EBITDA. ThatвҖҷs a material discount to the multiples traded across European B2C gambling. 3) ITALY NEXT: With CEE done, we question whether the focus turns to Italy for EntainвҖҷs divestment-specialist CFO Mike Snape. This could unlock a further ВЈ1.2-ВЈ1.6bn of capital and see leverage below the bottom end of the target range. OVERALL: A healthier balance sheet, with a smaller and less complex asset portfolio means investors are likely to ascribe more value to the remaining assets as they come into focus. Equally, it also makes Entain more digestible - Entain on the scales next. Entain trades on 6.4x cons. вҖҳ26E EV/EBITDA.

 

NOVO NORDISK: WEGOVY IP PLUMPING UP? <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-i/>  - It is assumed by us, and most other observers, that Wegovy will face generic competition in mid-2032. However, that is not the extent of the IP covering injectable and oral Wegovy. In this note, we review the IP Novo has been granted in the US for Wegovy and Wegovy pill. We make no change to our base assumptions, but show the impact of additional years of protection on our Novo price target, such that an additional year of delayed generic entry would increase the price target from DKK454 to DKK468. Were it to be extended by eight years, it would increase to DKK500. We maintain our Buy recommendation. Trades on 13.6x 27E P/E, a 20% discount to sector. IDEAs Est Mom 41.

 

RHEINMETALL: CHOPPY WATERS - MAINTAIN BUY; TP REDUCED <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-r/>  - 14 pg note following Germany's decision to cancel F126 frigate programme. Rheinmetall was widely expected to take over as prime contractor. Main takeaways: (1) Minimal read-X to other major contract expected in 2026 for Boxer armoured vehicles; we expect approval this autumn. (2) More muted growth for RHM's Naval Systems from 2026-30E; we cut cumulative divisional revenue forecast by c20%. (3) RHM's domestic market share could prove lower than expected given breakdown in communication with German MoD; we trim group revenue and profit forecasts in 2026-28E, and lower by 5-10% in 2029-30E. (4) Decision to diversify into long cycle equipment commendable, though will face extra scrutiny on future M&A. F126 cancellation means potentially overpaying for NVL. (5) Share price decline looks overdone; cautious forecasts and more prudent valuation still offers sizeable upside having reduced TP from вӮ¬1,900 to вӮ¬1,300. (6) Floor for 2030 exit multiple is 15x P/E offering TSR at 16% CAGR. Would be in line with mid-teens average from 2015-22 (ex. 2020) when RHM had lower earnings growth plus material exposure to autos sector. Per RDBNe RHM trades on 8x 2028 EV/EBIT vs BAE (Buy) on 13x, Thales (Buy) on 11x, Leonardo (N/R) on 11x (per VA), Renk (Buy) on 10x and Hensoldt (N/R) on 15x (per VA). 

 

 

SIEMENS: TO SI FOR <https://sales.cmail20.com/t/d-l-wluhhil-dyiuuktjju-d/>  - In this report, we: (1) raise our 2030 EPS by 18% led entirely by a massive 47% upgrade to our Smart Infrastructure (SI) EBITA , we raise our 2026-2030 SI organic sales from 8.2% to 11.0% (350bp above consensus) вҖ“ led by 25% in datacentres вҖ“ and our SI margin by 340bp from 20.8% to 24.2% (c400bp above 20.0% consensus) вҖ“ SI joins our long-standing view that DI margins can beat consensus by c400bp in 2030 (as first laid out in вҖҳTo DI forвҖҷ in 2021), (2) provide feedback from the recent US CEO roadshow that we hosted, and (3) lay out our 3Q26 forecasts, ahead of 6 August: we forecast orders, sales, EBITA and FCF +15%, +2%, +3% and +3% above consensus respectively; our order beat is led by SI (+34% organic order growth versus +19% consensus led by +160% in datacentres and +14% in non-DC). We raise our 12-month target price from вӮ¬300/sh to вӮ¬355/sh based on our 12-month forward DCF fair value (вӮ¬363/sh) and our SoTP fair value (вӮ¬350/sh), and we maintain our Buy recommendation.

Market Intelligence: US Morning Update 
	
30 June 2026 | 8:42AM EDT | Research | Equity 	
	
	
Click "here <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2Fcontent%2Fproducts%2Fd036cb0f-b058-490d-9e12-e7b8862ebdfd.html/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/2PoCMQQxEgmiKy9MDBWhsqtQfRegnyCiytthq0dpTCU=452> " to listen to the US Morning Call.

Stocks in Asia were mostly higher on Tuesday, except in Hong Kong where weakness in internet stocks persisted. However, tech-oriented Japan, Korea and Taiwan posted gains on positive cues from US markets. Sentiment in China was also bolstered by an increase in official manufacturing and non-manufacturing PMIs (see "China: Both official manufacturing and non-manufacturing PMIs rose in June <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIJzz0sZ6qv1m_lJsncj2YgD8IGcxAK-gTJdI2QGZ2KG0rHg2i1AACwN7kHw7y_DVDYySqugiaAffaRHJALdA2F5b0xlVxETQlmbgQh79I3hYWPNHZcAPTnvJv9MsuKPDsBxIVZ9OE_Etz-0-msUSiL8s3N-bYKsxjNN-wvX3uOpifJ9DW6oJdHacfox0xY135pHnfMgpeItduX0Zi_mLF_6UCdLi9rM_Ttlv_GWTrrr5qcZcajwdOz2IVouBSKY3YUtbYEUfaMbj1m16GARz7vKMq0vhMoCAY*%26id=863ea4d5-ab92-47d7-8881-684744d229f6%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/CgFW0LeZlmDZLBHYCSl3GPO9snhmgTrhdBoCccRskO8=452> "). In Japan, industrial production rose for a second consecutive month (see "Japan: May Production Rises for Second Straight Month, In Line With Market Forecast <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJvtrRJ0votW1t3GoU6-EZH-A79uxATBTBoE-Bmdnn57lMauVLuAACwkYZJ9xh_Q1sAWtDo5xjMHSc9qblQEK2zim4N1KO_im8vIT1c7-F6I-sMYkWR_20PRcZFu87tCTZudYo-BeVBeqI_3zzOivxkjoN3etLKo-rTSir4zahhOXGhMnTcpYzTsr1ayo5-a2K8T8zOT5Re6NdizawyHv56V3lJgAnqdlHTFaXtKvU1D2CmjiNAajJOmIBqrpbtDq7h1iJYBcmyBcRhVB66EuonNsby5G5jadA*%26id=d9362d8d-e88c-4096-a4f8-29a5e4d0b141%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/vwhB2w0xjc1oLkaK58oTMHhUQEh0e2cGgQx4_M2Px3U=452> '), while it declined in Korea, alongside weak consumption and mixed investment data (see "South Korea: A Surprise Contraction in IP, Weak Consumption, and Mixed Investment in May <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKOgvoNYh1rga-BsSoTe1SnSIfXYxDJVC-vx3lKQGhRhDZ7OWaYAACwwwhBRaM5FpJKE8CDrbvZCSSxrysXzui24JVnZSTLwP8LLQyMQopKv_WRr3JSFh2wtLgw9-who4DSoJQgP-bPaoF2JrdLGajHfz6oycVgIe7KpRGS7epMQGmUf3v7WVHqQgIXvog0Be6LOWwx5Vug6_j5WSRv0Bz7bqgW6aY7Afk6bW7EN-wa95G6SNyeNi3uvCfW9c-f2ulSaAB69u0agYgk3swLa6UCsxk8xd93UHM*%26id=63346bed-22b3-48c9-ab78-1f0fd77a35c4%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/MhV3GBeH2P62VRdJD58XQw-ZKcr52RmRg_xdMqfst4E=452> "). 

European equities are also trading higher amid tech strength and weaker-than-expected inflation in France. Our economists nudged down Euro area headline and core inflation forecasts (see "Euro Area: French Inflation Below Expectations; Revising Down Our Area-Wide Tracking <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKTIC-rzp7LUStIIqTCkH0V8LsOLhBOHZDpsPlLip8oA_3R1DmNAACwkCdhmoBYVqEnjqqmqMDH7Avw7-mHVGVTGSIt2FDuh9Auq2Mj1ORf2JK3YIVOewlRcjf-YyqFhXkWsAvx2tNyHS-COrBHQHYh-vWTfCjXFW-SQLaZG8a6iq3zhKgJy9LUtlih5aAvy1Cs19jhyqm1eKN0h7gg6fHEljrOi2Kc7W96-Wl6OKiIMChQ8IN-KQC3-6rDy1MfcLY-MLCPVka9E-M33llPo4UFosFhryhy-7g*%26id=0bb03342-8eec-490a-b868-256158c8253c%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/WCzlRBUHMvnRBvgXOlyzYb-vSpXm5d-KHw0Jdr4eiOg=452> "). They continue to expect the ECB to hike in September, but note that the hurdle for a hold is now low given reduced inflationary pressures from energy prices (see Jari Stehn's "ECBвҖ”Hike or Hold? <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJXnMCQSKHIcQuMK5gU7XM4KTe6TRAtdQpu6gAti57zNAPXXh92AACwlaYm0CLUn5E3hF7lMae_vFhIMJdyOkFxKgdVrtrNAVKnFPe1nLsZnargNzUdBHkFr2sN6m3xWqEU9trx2g41y09mQiptUPkYthruNKabUq-8sD8Y1ULyMsdKgTEvTMv4C_h5eAbeF6hQ7Mf_04-ko0W4y089oxzO4N8ZJVqRPCvaEeQBduDf3EUPFgzLHRB8rJKiA5DpQw0YE9qF0U1T_4gksNHvMBYQBddyn8XtiIM*%26id=7071bde2-6c4e-412b-af5e-de3b324c75ec%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/fc520HFcIOSehccQG5GO2BpVb5p6GmtaEZpgbD--zk8=452> "). 

Yields on 10-year US Treasuries remain anchored at 4.37%, while front month Brent oil edged up, but remains below $75/bbl amidst a swift ramp up in Hormuz flows (see "Oil Comment: Gulf Exports: 2 Steps Forward, 1 Step Back <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQK1hjFkZ7f2DGyBxAx53P1lc9YajhCzJ2HRQF14tkgIwmKAIAPpAACw-rtwHCX5xJugyRsiEeq7QjLt_6brxujKdFJjmSXrFdn_oeghOBTWuL5FdNjhocSwgPPCuQig_2yLIhGDnb7UGZUffXnYwAK_jp6jzIGhiXSDu_3vaL1odNqcW_jGs9K0gPduif1vuxxFbnJBM_rd3We2h83oIaSF8gzmQrHZTWhYG5XxbGByn4lCLA5HPFtKT4WBmlIOQIbxkAZOsqmJbk-xfO6EkBetZszB2IMsN0c*%26id=4635f587-b270-4a6a-97fa-bf6cafc0eff4%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/q_VZ9GdTnm52AAfwvQA-D9FcicX70NQwY3VdeJjbBMY=452> ").

Selective across credit. Amanda Lynam remains constructive on corporate credit, but turns more selective, in a fresh Macro Credit Views, "Dispersion, not disruption <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIDp6el4fbyKAipV5BnklriNWTh9BC4QgUi0dpuQ9pjsVWjtzW-AACwF0jeXcfOtRBXP8KCsmbSTkcef178FQgqVYBNJOIQznGBCiqRYELF2UhMc6FmqoPf9aa6l-M0RgBqjaWngBq8fVkVivycRnZ6-K9_OHoVVxwdTcGy_ozSiiQlafW3VlRRVaMHGtLgMczpxDioPxOB_50d5iMJe_CxWLVpa8rhbuhAnhv_hqLvzIFiLl44W5hjJdSN9zav768aO4IeEeByi9sd8hIVGnZ13AmCDwbE5t0*%26id=bece7ac4-2336-4a8a-8bda-3c5088dd7fc1%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/IfH6E8uQWJcHY7ANFZ0tIT70TWL3aTbNscHfLzOA1ys=452> ." We expect some modest spread widening, especially given tight valuations and heavy supply, yet we think widening should stay contained because all-in yields remain attractive and continue to draw strong demand from pensions, insurers, and other yield-oriented buyers. 

в—Ҹ

Yield demand should limit widening. Tight spreads face pressure from heavy supply, but strong demand from yield-based buyers should keep widening contained.

в—Ҹ

Rates and quality positioning are key. With risk-free rates driving more of total yield, we prefer USD over EUR and BBB over higher-rated IG in the US.

в—Ҹ

Dispersion remains the main theme. In HY and private credit, we see security selection as critical, especially in Software and other AI-sensitive areas, while broad market fundamentals still look stable.

 	 

Looking Ahead 

Focus on the Conference Board's Consumer Confidence survey for June, the April FHFA and Case-Shiller home price surveys, as well as May JOLTS job openings today.

9:00 AM: FHFA house price index, April (consensus +0.2%, last +0.1%)

US Morning Call for June 30, 2026: 

Click "here <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2Fcontent%2Fproducts%2Fd036cb0f-b058-490d-9e12-e7b8862ebdfd.html/2/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/SEFqdnLFK583QCHnITf56lEFHjARcshzbWQnf07kBGs=452> " to listen to the US Morning Call.

Capital Markets, Blostein: Terminal value debates vs. (mostly) strong EPS growth trajectory across the group. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQL9fikKDIyISvy1g2dVd6TonK2FShAm9fTVlMlwovGbq_OCEIasAACw1Z9FKJl1FT1lSBmFf3eUvKJfAmTMqp2eYXJj_iWPiR-F_6itxqECJ7w41nRpDkU-EQkahrbKe77BhDgbViQ7HChsfcZW4r8mvRz0p-XUp9vQX9QmLGqSJh26pg5-hY_SdOmcrvNLPHFTbmx14ILxsJZUgJiAyijHzCrXI3NKRsBQeyNS1ugNBVwcmXRgsJqBHmFFZmWMec4n_l_gRra21GlvSCXi2TgMcfDLLy3i0Bw*%26id=97853aa5-8b09-4267-87b5-db7b86ea3b8c%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/ZQsLJwGNBhGoVqU6FpU46vILdjCdWTm3-qYi3gieyRo=452> 

Tradeweb Markets Inc. (TW, Buy), Blostein: Dragged by Sentiment, Backed by Growth; Upgrade to Buy. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJhaPj5JbXwyIdhmpvXItoEc2wNiBDEhlbty0GKCfBEofMuU5R0AACw_Q7-3g5KAShtdSbKqVDe_KkgeJ0aIJQMzCxEqv2_jWSez0YVOylX7NLZXRqQufhyKd0b45nrFpl7dd5zYLC0dlC1TvTquM1aRVvFAyZSGw4Apcw0UWAy8qaFEaSwE0t3qhmI_DKBc67_-PL7kDCdxLVhYs2E6i9vh7Pcyikj526IZMGb5WJLhmWbF_ULI3qRiKUaaaaLcRyxkqz_RGbaZA3dTwXmZmrrIavM0idKZ3Q*%26id=61962684-3466-4c02-96ce-9606b5bbf097%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/Swy6rxWBWybt-vXKkcF1exQ9GGl_Fab563j267LN_q8=452> 

Capital Markets stocks remain deeply dislocated as terminal value fears about AI, tokenization, and private equity durability weigh on valuations, even as underlying fundamentals across trading, flows, rates, and retail alternatives remain supportive. We see the widest disparity between price and fundamentals in Wealth Managers, followed by Exchanges and Alternative Managers. Best positioned Buy-rated SCHW, LPLA, TW, TPG (on CL), STEP, and HLNE. Separately, we upgrade TW to Buy (from Neutral) as we expect revenue growth to re-accelerate, driven by an underappreciated Rates franchise, and see limited real-world tokenization risk. 12m price target, $146.

Energy, Mehta/Mackay: Closer Look at M&A Potential in Energy and the Case for Continued Consolidation. <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKpILyuyN_0_JqJUxlmSMYFtgGuaxAIRr5-kzN6yCeg9JZaLdnwAACwxdyEUjdEH5GCAQq44j_E5yMu70dS0wTMvkKaCr9Z4Uo0xsJfpi7xzsTuk_66Bq-3xCX2aQl0-DPmRZ2QBG2xKhwPA6aYdKcbF-nlDStQbiNJ27ogMBbqOK3LokegQ50DvlWoTvPfAHFqb9tjoXaD3_DWilVI_n-VKT_qSCLskRiYsaY7WxN9hPpAWmlGNZnIizo0wHHfLLzuFiWTJQSJAncmIGMXQ1iByL7psxngUyI*%26id=2e7b27bc-9196-4876-bf04-49b435116e6c%26utm_medium=pa/1/0100019f1891ee82-5f312a33-e993-479d-b6b4-a482e655c732-000000/8SPHjoeL7xJWK8uLEewOjizdEhh9ohKDXK9ogfi5g4E=452>  We remain constructive on continued oil & gas consolidation, as M&A across E&Ps, Majors, Midstream, Refining, and Services can drive strategic asset alignment, improve cost structures, and strengthen through-cycle free cash flow. We see the longest runway in E&Ps with focus on Buy-rated FANG, OVV, COP (on CL), PR, and DVN. Look for more selective bolt-on potential in KNTK and GLNG (on CL) in Midstream and Refining. We also refresh our M&A rankings across sub-sectors.

Rubrik Inc. Former Director at AMD Views TSMC's CoWoS as Leading Advanced Packaging Technology Over Intel's EMIB <https://research.alpha-sense.com?alertEventId=63c90904-850c-4707-bbbe-ec56f6b6371021443916500&documentId=EC-1000000-223106&source=email_alert&category=main_doc> 	
TSM  вҖў  Customer  вҖў  Expert Insight  вҖў  2 pages  вҖў  30 Jun 2026 	
Call Summary 

1.	The conversation explored the dynamics of advanced semiconductor packaging vendor selection, focusing on the technical, operational, and strategic considerations between leading packaging technologies. It covered the evaluation of TSMC's CoWoS versus Intel's EMIB, qualification processes, switching costs, and the durability of TSMC's competitive position.
2.	Former director with over six years of experience at a leading semiconductor company, focused on AI infrastructure, server-class silicon validation, competitive analysis, and hyperscale platforms, with direct involvement in technical and platform strategy discussions related to advanced packaging supplier evaluation.
3.	The expert views TSMC's CoWoS as the gold standard in advanced packaging, with unmatched reliability, performance, and partnership, making it difficult for competitors to displace in the near to medium term.
4.	The expert is skeptical about Intel's ability to compete at scale in advanced packaging until it can attract significant external customer volume and demonstrate proven success with its latest technology nodes.
5.	Switching advanced packaging suppliers is seen as a complex, costly, and underestimated process, particularly due to the extensive re-qualification required.
6.	TSMC's CoWoS packaging technology is considered highly innovative and reliable, with no real competitor for high-end AI and HPC chips during the expert's tenure.
7.	Performance, reliability, and close partnership were the primary criteria for packaging vendor selection, with cost being a minor factor due to its small share of total system cost.
8.	Intel's EMIB was not seriously considered due to competitive conflicts and perceived technical and manufacturing risks; cost advantages were not compelling enough to offset these concerns.
9.	Switching packaging suppliers involves significant silicon redesign and a lengthy re-qualification process, often underestimated by outsiders and requiring months of engineering effort.
10.	TSMC's dominant position is reinforced by massive capital investment, technological leadership, and a non-competitive stance with its customers, making its moat difficult to erode in the next 5-10 years.

RESEARCH:

SNDK <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511305&ad=4535204566&h=ZwlG6rs&sig=EojDeLWiUY1Xry9OxuJu3BYF5H7&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DR%25252fr0a9rCzwWNeN67TP0wakZrkzr8BYeyH1OgqX7LLBmTcuRTu%25252b0Mo9QhYeKD0sc%25252f>  - Mark Newman raises SNDK PT to $3000 (vs. $1,700 prior), FY27E EPS now at $243 (+28% vs cons), new LTAs provide structural lift. The debate has centered on whether LTAs can structurally reduce NAND cyclicality and support a higher earnings multiple. Mark believes these LTAs are fundamentally different from prior cycles given multi-year duration (3-5 years), price floors, and upfront customer financial commitments that provide increasing downside protection over time. His base case calls for FY27/FY28 EPS of $243/$272, or roughly 28%/27% above Street, while his bull case reaches $350/$400, or 84-87% vs cons. He applies an 11x FY28 EPS and 14x through-cycle earnings, which equates to a 7.5x bull case FY28 EPS. Mark runs a 72% peak to trough ASP decline through his numbers with new LTA assumptions, gets to $214 in FY30E EPS, showing significant downside protection vs older LTA structures. A business with higher growth visibility and cyclicality that has been minimized deserves to trade at a more premium multiple in his view. Mark rates SNDK outperform with a $3,000 PT. 

 

Energy & Transition <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511305&ad=4535204566&h=l3po4V1&sig=VYUkThviUPsCYoPApdCjrK1kG2k&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/ViewResearchStreamer.aspx?cid%3DPWePlT3416092L770odbdm6ncxpspuiy3yyyxutd7blt23rv44lupdj34qsz2ikeqceigxpbmbpfgzeqe4roaipg3inf4u1%26amp;pn%3Dpn%26amp;ct%3DR%26amp;downloadid%3DHUPXKQANL7BH665N0A1WNDNRIENWEI%26amp;h%3D1%26amp;f%3D1%26amp>  вҖ“ Sunaina Ocalan thinks Virtual Power Plants could great opptys for ENPH and GEV. Sunaina dives into Virtual Power Plants (VPP) and discusses the potential opportunities they can create for ENPH & GEV. Recently, Sunrun, Tesla, and Renew Home announced a framework agreement to deliver up to 6.8 gigawatts (GW) of flexible energy capacity to hyperscalers and utilities. These offerings come from VPPs, which aggregate distributed energy resources (i.e., rooftop solar, EV chargers, and smart thermostats) and act as a flexible energy resource. Since most distributed energy resources are small, many do not meet the size and reliability required to participate in the grid. FERC Order No. 2222 (issued in September 2020) aims to ease and standardize rules around DER and VPP participation in regional energy markets. Sunaina believes that the implementation of this order can support the continued growth and integration of VPPs into U.S. electricity markets, creating opportunity for EnphaseвҖҷs product offering as well as for GEVвҖҷs electrification business. 

 

U.S. Food <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511305&ad=4535204566&h=HLlQGtS&sig=Djw2fBLefYnMihUe4sFEzZvgaFT&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/viewresearch.aspx?cid%3D9%2545%2544d%254Ekf%254D%254B8t%2559%254C%2542d%2548nb5%25485aiem%254At75%2551%2546%2548%2556%2545r%2553%2547musp%2550dbu9q0%2558%2550l%2551%2543%25450szic%254E%255377%2551>  вҖ“ Alexia Howard digs into payout ratios... CAG most at risk of cutting its quarterly div and see increasing risk to GIS, CPB, KHC, TSN. We recently became more cautious on the outlook for U.S.-centric packaged food names due to the recent spike in U.S. trucking freight costs (which now typically represent ~10% of COGS) in an environment where the companiesвҖҷ ability to pass on such cost increases may be compromised by heightened consumer and retailer sensitivity to rising grocery prices. In this report, we take a closer look at how dividend payout ratios and leverage are trending across our coverage. We think that Conagra is most likely to cut its quarterly dividend. On an annualized basis, the current dividend payout of $1.40 is over 90% of 2026-2027 average FCF and over 85% of expected EPS. Additionally, the companyвҖҷs net debt/EBITDA is the highest amongst the group at 4.2x and it has $762m (~10% of its total debt) of debt that needs to be financed in the next twelve months. The other companies in our coverage that are currently paying a dividend (SJM, MKC, MDLZ, HSY and LW) all have payout ratios that look very manageable on both a FCF and EPS basis. 

 

CMCSA <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511305&ad=4535204566&h=4moLO-X&sig=DLd96S9kfDF6IdxGRD1sBdyGZVD&v=2&url=https://bernstein-autonomous.bluematrix.com/links2/secure/pdf/b94c4759-2928-483f-88e3-66e7e4cbf5ab>  вҖ“ Laurent YoonвҖҷs prelim SOTP suggests a valuation range of $25-$37 per share (pre-transaction), with the high-end assuming 6x EBITDA for CableCo and 10x EBITDA for MediaCo. The separation creates strategic optionality as both CableCo and MediaCo should emerge as scaled and viable standalone companies with healthy balance sheets. MediaCo has the upside, with durable growth profiles and greater strategic appeal. As a result, it should command a higher multiple and is also likely viewed as attractive by strategic buyers. However, we see no urgency to pursue a sale. Direct valuation comparisons (ie PSKY/WBD) are imperfect as MediaCoвҖҷs EBITDA mix is heavily weighted toward the Parks biz. While MediaCoвҖҷs standalone value unlocks the upside, Cable remains the primary driver of valuation ( ~85% of EBITDA (вҖҳ27E)). Our prelim SOTP analysis suggests a valuation range of $25-$37 per share (pre-transaction), with the high-end assuming 6x EBITDA for CableCo (arguably high in the current environment) and 10x EBITDA for MediaCo. While the separation creates a cleaner path to realizing MediaвҖҷs value, the marketsвҖҷ view on Cable will continue to be the biggest determinant of overall shareholder returns.

 

 

 <https://bernstein.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511305&ad=4535204566&h=KT6XW40&sig=Y2tcx7k0dYOvd3CP1ViKH5oWeoC&v=2&url=https://www.bernsteinresearch.com/brweb/Home.aspx%23> 

 

TUESDAY 30 JUNE 2026


Key Research

________________________________

emails.subject: **JEF AM FIG PICKS & PANZ 6/30/26 --- BX Quickhitter........Clarity Act Update - Moss Latest Thoughts......Biz Services M&A? - Surinder on Persistent/NA9 Laterals
emails.body:
SALES AND TRADING NOTE 
This material is a product of Jefferies Equity Sales and Trading Department. It is not a product of Jefferies Research Department, and it should not be regarded as research or a research report. Refer to additional disclaimers at the end of this email

 

1-LinersвҖҰ..

CBOE/SNEX led exchange weakness in focus all day yesterday

*	no real catalyst that we could identify 
*	(plenty of random excuses for attribution floating aroundвҖҰnone of which felt real enough to explain)

 

IBKR drawing the incoming this AM related to this Susqehanna article

*	and the latterвҖҷs claim of inappropriate trades made on IBKR, along with Futu, Up FinTech (implication being вҖ“ perhaps more of a link between IBKR and those that faced ChinaвҖҷs crackdown)
*	INTO Tomorrow вҖ“ feedback generally inline with yesterdayвҖҷ floated bogeys of 120K account growth and down -LSD margin balances 

 

FDS expectations into tomorrow

*	ASV vs Margin focus? ---- Buyside survey response focused on ASV over margins ------ surprised us a bit in that a little ASV upside feels like a base case vs margins are the delta (the lever or the problem)
*	So does an ASV beat (+4Q reiteration) and in-line margins win the day? --- the buyside bias was a clear yes VS the Desk sees the incremental negative operating leverage as a potential drag on the stock, #s, thesisвҖҰ

 

 

As a segue to the broader discussionвҖҰ..

 

The following chart feels like the best representation of Cap Markets investing over the past 2 months, and more specifically, 2 weeksвҖҰ

вҖҰWhile its not exactly clear what kicked this dispersion off in late MayвҖҰ

вҖҰThe past 2 weeks is undoubtedly technical --- most acutely attributable to the Exchange space as patient 0

Incredible - In the past 9 Trading sessions, TROW has outperformed BLK by 16%....



 

One more for good measure 

вҖҰsince APO announced ADS 2Q redemptions less than 1 week agoвҖҰ.

вҖҰ(results of which we viewed as in-line/to only slightly worse than peers вҖ“ met with modest pushback in ADSвҖҷ self-described relative software immunity)

Incredible вҖ“ in the past 5 Trading sessions, CRBG has outperformed APO by 12% 



 

So вҖ“ what exactly is going on here??

вҖҰAs a starting point вҖ“ I think there is more capital that has been deployed in Cap Markets than ever before 

*	Outsized dispersion = a source of real alpha generation вҖ“ both on an absolute basis and especially relative to other Sub-Sectors in recent years
*	Strong trends = a proxy for market strength, an access point to unthinkable capital issuance needs (AIвҖҰ.) and also the broader proliferation by end user (retail), geography (globalization), and product type (from electronification, to alts, to now perps)
*	Re-Rate potential = a trendline that has gone from cyclical to structural вҖ“ with winners surprisingly capable of busting through presumed multiple caps
*	AI winner potential = from AI enhanced expense leverage to AI related capital formation benefits to other benefits of scale, insulated market/product/distribution moats and less traditional disintermediation risk 

вҖҰAs a second step вҖ“ the вҖҳAI EffectвҖҷ itself

*	A) the crowding effect related to our starting point assumption of AI risk vs AI immunity --- impossible to defend the former in the first inning of the cycle вҖ“ and so positioning understandably gets exaggerated
*	B) then our generic вҖҳunderstandingвҖҷ of AI risk morphs into stuff we hadnвҖҷt really considered; from the 10k foot Citrini report to the monetary risk of tokenmaxxing to the great cash optimization debate, agentic effects, means of distribution, etc etc

вҖҰTo part 3 вҖ“ which is where we are now

*	Where AI is as much real as it is representation of emerging technological change and structural risk ---- that once again, has proven impossible to refute
*	We get the headline catalyst ---- we watch and we debate --- then we defend with a rational perspective on the logic of foreseeable business risk 
*	Until we then grapple with the вҖҳwhat ifвҖҷ, вҖҳtoo hard to disproveвҖҷ phenomenon that plays out wayyyyy beyond the actual question at hand. Ie the realistic efficacy of perps = having such a massive 4th derivative effect on APO, BLK etc etc 

 

 

 

BX FOLLOW-UP

*	BX $3.5B proceeds from DLR sale 

	*	$1.2B in cash вҖ“ fund mix between Tac Ops vs Infra/BREIT is unknown. 

		*	But the Tac Ops piece gain should flow through 2Q realizations (boosting that 500M+ guide a bit!)

	*	~$2.3B in stock вҖ“ turned cash via last nightвҖҷs DLR secondary 

		*	Offering closes tomorrow вҖ“ so assume no 2Q impact on this ~2/3 portion of proceeds

	*	NET 

		*	Tac Ops вҖ“ 1/3 (cash) piece flow-through on whatever % is held in Tax Ops 
		*	BREIT вҖ“ mark-up should flow through 2Q FRPR
		*	Infra вҖ“ no benefit until next yearвҖҷs crystallization (once every 3yrs)

 

*	BX June Subs

	*	BXPE June subs = $1.2B ($500M in May) ----- a record month since launch month --- as previously messaged at MS conference 
	*	BX Infra subs = $360M ($205M in May)

 

BX RETAIL FLOWS ex BCRED



 

BX TOTAL RETAIL FLOWS

 

 

	*	NET ---- if we make generic assumptions for BREIT/BXPE 2Q redemptions; 

		*	1Q26 NET Retail Flow = ~$800M inflow (ex DRIP)
		*	2Q26 NET Retail Flow = ~$700M inflow (ex DRIP)
		*	1H NET Retail Flow = $1.5b (ex Drip)



 

	*	BULL CASE = Continued new $ ex PC demand + enough PC $ recycling = to support ~FY25 level total Alt Net flow levels going forward (FY25 avg $2.1B vs June $1.8B, FY25 avg ex BCRED $1.1B vs June $1.7B)
	*	BEAR CASE = Good (Bull Case) is fine only until the next macro/eco/product problem arises, when we will see the next liability duration mis-match become another flow/growth/mgmt fee/multiple problem
	*	From Here = I think holding near a ~$1.5B monthly sub range ex BCRED would be a win ---- curious if you agree?

 

AM Notes remain tactically positive on BX ---- even into some 2Q #s softness

*	Bullish on June subs --- specifically this bounce in BXPE into a potential narrowing of FA product вҖҳfavoritesвҖҷ 
*	Bullish on 2H Fee rate reacceleration 
*	Bullish on 3Q monetization activity 
*	--- what do you think вҖ“ is this 2Q Street +6.1% mgmt fee growth estimate too high? 

 

 

 

CLARITY ACT --- WHATвҖҷS THE LATEST?

With 20 days left before August recess вҖ“ Andrew sees the likelihood of passage as dropping by the dayвҖҰ

In terms of logistical steps

*	The next big one would be the combination of the Senate Banking version with the Ag Committee proposal
*	The Senate floor would have to vote on that вҖ“ need a supermajority for passage
*	That would then have to be reconciled with the House version 
*	Then the Senate and the House would have to approve this combined version, respectively

The biggest hold-ups remain

*	Ethics provision
*	New concern from law enforcement around illicit finance 

Clarity Implications

*	Expect the following fundamental reaction by TradFi players вҖ“ in the case Clarity gets passed

	*	Banks/Exchanges incentive shift to promote digital asset trading, prime brokerage, token-backed lending, custody, staking and capital formation services
	*	Asset Managers may launch tokenized traditional product offerings and expand spot offerings
	*	Corporates may integrate programmable treasury management to drive capital effiencies

*	With that вҖ“ expect token utilization to accelerate

	*	Focus on revenue-generating blockchain op systems and apps 
	*	Focus on real world use cases with TradFi integrations 
	*	Clarity token winners вҖ“ AAVE, BNB, CC, ETH, HYPE, MORPHO, SOL, TRX, ZRO

Jefferies <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511255&ad=4535194197&h=dCvNVE3&sig=L3R3fN5nioqyg0nDIIICAHMmDRh&v=2&url=https://javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/secure/html/d848db32-bbe5-4169-b742-5251be6f0742> 

 

 

 

BIZ SERVICES M&A?

Surinder writes a follow-up note on the back of PersistentвҖҷs 140% premium takeout of NA9 --- Jefferies Content - Report <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511255&ad=4535194197&h=-UzBlb1&sig=NGBjzS2sKMDcIYEsPRp8ldesomW&v=2&url=https://content.jefferies.com/secure-link/pdf/deb02e69-a063-4fa2-9837-3a6b0d34d386?genericLinkId%3DZ3NjaHdhcnR6QGplZmZlcmllcy5jb206Nw%3D%3D> 

In highlighting 

A.	The premium price tag (140%) вҖ“ 8.2% EV/EBTIDA and 16.7x PE
B.	ACN recently announced plan to double its M&A budget to $9B for FY26вҖҰ.

Surinder suggests

*	Strategic use of M&A is on the rise
*	With support from increasingly attractive valuations
*	Combined with the disconnect between value being ascribed by insiders (NAвҖҷs US peers trade at ~5x EBITDA) vs outsiders (market discount)

That saidвҖҰ.

*	Persistent was not so surprisingly down -11% on deal announcement
*	And Surinder acknowledges some 1-off nature to this --- and does not expect a mini M&A wave to change the broader groupвҖҷs existing valuation framework
