emails.subject: JPM: Chips for Breakfast - GLW Thoughts, Korea AI Mega Plan, Japan Components
emails.sender: Joshua Meyers - Specialist Sales - US TMT
emails.body:
GLW Thoughts, Korea AI Mega Plan, Japan Components 


Joshua Meyers 
+1 617 310 0767 
joshua.meyers@jpmorgan.com <mailto:joshua.meyers@jpmorgan.com>  

  


Some quick thoughts on Korea from tradingвҖҰKOSPI closed +1% to 8,476.48, trading higher for the first time in three days despite another volatile session on the final day of the month and quarter. The index whipsawed between a strong intraday advance and sharp weakness before finishing firmly in positive territory. Foreigners extended their cash selling streak to eight consecutive sessions, while local institutions and retail investors continued to absorb supply. Tech (+2%) outperformed as investors continued to position around Korea's AI investment initiatives, with semiconductor equipment and front-end wafer-fab-exposure names remaining preferred beneficiaries. And early thoughts on the US market from JPM Market Intelligence Head Drew TylerвҖҰFuts are a touch above unchanged following yesterdayвҖҷs rally, with markets behaving as though period-end dynamics have now completed. With the major US holiday coming up, keep an eye on low liquidity moves in the region. Bond yields are flat to down 1bp with the curve flattening. USD is stronger, looking erase all of yesterdayвҖҷs losses. Cmdtys are stronger with crude flat into todayвҖҷs US / Iran discussions, Metals seeing a bid, and Ags outperforming the other cmdty complexes. In Eqys, Semis are mixed, Mag7 are flat, Cyclicals are generally leading Defensives with exceptions being Energy (lower) and Healthcare (higher). TodayвҖҷs macro data focus is on JOLTS 

 


Expectations for the coming few quiet weeks: WarshвҖҷs appears tomorrow at the ECB Sintra conf. We also get US manufacturing ISM @ 10am, and on Thursday at 8:30am the US jobs report. On Monday we get US services ISM at 10am, and on Wed 7/8 the FOMC meeting notes, then US CPI at 8:30am on July 14. Then earningsвҖҰ should be an interesting earnings season after the more conservative вҖңwartime guidesвҖқ of 1QвҖҰ IвҖҷll be marketing in Chicago on July 8 and in Toronto on July 9, and with Harlan in New York on July 13~14; let me know if you want to try and meet! 

 


Just quickly on the +41% 1MO GLW move (the stock is up 24% just this week), a lot of folks yesterday were insinuating that announcement of the GlassWorks AI Platform and in particular of Glass Bridge were principally responsible for the most recent move. I got a lot of TAM questions. The move is puzzling to us from a fundamental standpoint, in part because this product was announced none months ago and demonstrated at ECOC. Joe doesnвҖҷt presently see any reason to view it as incremental to the $10b photonics MAP by 2030. We would love to hear where people disagree. 


вҖӢ 


вҖӢ 

________________________________


Calls & Events 



вҖӢ 

*	Vishay IntвҖҷl IR Peter Henrici is hosting Asia investors for a group update call at 8pm ET on Wed July 1. HeвҖҷs delighted for investors from other geographies to join. The call will not be recorded. Request a spot here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=4LNwgsK&sig=Qap7yg20aLj78ZWUM6pJKkEygqT&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/event_booking/loadEvent?eventToken%3D136991_68b5a90d-8e86-4fe7-bfd0-62876ccbe601> . 
*	Gokul Hariharan is hosting his regular TSMC Pre-earnings call on Tuesday, July 7 at 8am ET.. Let me know if you 
*	If you happen to be in China next month, our Asia team hosts a China PCB Tour across the Greater Bay Area July 8~10. Let me know if you want details. 
*	August 17-28, Gokul, Jay and the team host their 11th Annual Asia Tech Touracross Taiwan, Korea & Japan. Seats for this very popular event are extremely limited. Reach out for full details/invite. Request a spot here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=9PWcnya&sig=MtR0cGeMcnnHYy010wxS28YpqxT&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/event_booking/loadEvent?eventToken%3D136565_a1ea3000-556a-4466-a831-285774fb4b76>  (but also check in with your generalist sales). 


Key Highlights 



Memory Market Update: Korea's AI Mega-investment Plan A Near-term Overhang, but Bullish Long-term. Here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=6hCTWLN&sig=ECsXwJQO0tVBvSnzoY8pRw4dFQV&v=2&isig=8F7OIwO6gSfoia1a0pV9wlWjMur&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-2r0cjdm/GPS-5350909-0.pdf/da2444a2-e834-4dac-ad94-f2ae91621ce3>  
Jay Kwon recaps Korea's national AI investment briefing, where Samsung Group and SK Group jointly announced KRW4,755t (~US$3.1t) in long-term capex through 2040, in service of the governmentвҖҷs tripartite pan for semiconductors, AI datacenters, and robotics. The scale implies doubling current installed DRAM capacity within 5 years, pulling in the Yongyin fab ramp by 7~12 years, developing the Chungcheong region and facilitating a huge longer-term investment roadmap. Jay reads the chairmen's messaging as a decisive signal to debottleneck supply shortages, with memory execs explicitly flagging that supply is only ~50% of demand and worsening вҖ” underpinning a multi-year upcycle thesis that's positive for SPE, power infrastructure, and semi EPC names. On the negative side, the Apple-MU pricing dispute is crystallizing market anxiety around unsustainable profit-sharing dynamics, and a consumer class-action lawsuit has been filed in the US against the three major DRAM makers for alleged price-fixing вҖ” while no government investigation is involved yet, Jay flags this warrants close monitoring given the early-2000s DRAM cartel precedent. Jay expects AI/server-centric demand bipolarization to continue into 2H26-27, with LPDDR pricing remaining favorable, and sees the US$3t investment ultimately generating US$12-20t in cumulative revenue opportunity for Korean memory makers. 

 

 

 

 

 

 


And if youвҖҷre involved in Japanese components, Kanemoto has an interesting note on positioning <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=pfzHsCJ&sig=YB8cYHoB0xswI6wGn801BBcPvA7&v=2&isig=8JTxJAaKJ08v9aPbCrftYGG3DDj&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/1roplmsa/GPS-5352707-0/da2444a2-e834-4dac-ad94-f2ae91621ce3> : 1) investors still sound likely to buy on dips; 2) where expectations are elevated, 3) the вҖңbuy laggardвҖқ (TDK) debate and 4) whatвҖҷs long versus short (folks sound long Murata/short TDK; folks are interested in long Ibiden, and ROHM, short Alps Alpine). 

 


вҖӢ 


Macro : Top of the Agenda 

SPX +0.1%, NDX +0.1%, RTY +0.1%. WTI +14bps at $70.85, NatGas +91bps to $3.21, UK NatGas +240bps to ВЈ1.0502, Gold +10bps to $4,020, Silver +79bps to $58.72, 10Y @ 4.365%, and VIX @ 17.59. 
US:Futs are a touch above unchanged following yesterdayвҖҷs rally, with markets behaving as though period-end dynamics have now completed. With the major US holiday coming up, keep an eye on low liquidity moves in the region. Bond yields are flat to down 1bp with the curve flattening. USD is stronger, looking erase all of yesterdayвҖҷs losses. Cmdtys are stronger with crude flat into todayвҖҷs US / Iran discussions, Metals seeing a bid, and Ags outperforming the other cmdty complexes. In Eqys, Semis are mixed, Mag7 are flat, Cyclicals are generally leading Defensives with exceptions being Energy (lower) and Healthcare (higher). TodayвҖҷs macro data focus is on JOLTS 
EU/UK: Major markets are all higher as the region benefits from both a stronger APAC and US session, Sx5E is leading country-level indices with AI themes and LT Momentum among the top plays. Regional bond yields are lower as curves bull steepen. Thematically, Momentum / Cyclicals area leading with Quality / Value lagging. APAC saw a stronger session with Korea / Taiwan leading. UKX +0.7%, SX5E +1.0%, SXXP +0.9%, DAX +1.0%. CSI +1.1%, HSI -0.6%, NKY +0.9%, ASX -0.5%, KOSPI +1.0%. 


вҖӢ 


News & Reads 



вҖӢ 

 

*	China's Xiaomi, Oppo, Vivo cut 2026 smartphone targets again: sources.Major Chinese smartphone makers including Xiaomi, Oppo and Vivo have told suppliers they will again cut shipment targets for this year вҖ“ some by up to 30% вҖ“ as rising costs and unprecedented component shortages continue to weigh heavily on the market, multiple sources briefed on the matter told Nikkei Asia - Nikkei Asia <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=2UYNsHB&sig=4bMRLcA7BOcbVIciwB0Da8Sw00y&v=2&url=https://asia.nikkei.com/business/technology/china-s-xiaomi-oppo-vivo-cut-2026-smartphone-targets-again-sources>  

 

*	Super Micro Office Raided as Taiwan Expands Chip Smuggling Probe."Super Micro Computer Inc.вҖҷs offices in Taiwan were raided by government authorities on Monday, widening an investigation into the alleged smuggling of Nvidia Corp. chips into China using the companyвҖҷs servers, according to a person familiar with the situation." 6 individual residences and 3 affiliated companies were also raided. This was reported in the Taiwan press earlier in the day yesterday, but didn't impact that share price until Bloomberg picked it up вҖ“ Bloomberg <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=9oZXVfn&sig=5I3JCu1xhPNdCq0qLtZMqjgbDtr&v=2&url=https://blinks.bloomberg.com/news/stories/THF7Y2T9NJLS>  

 

*	Samsung Reportedly Restarts 1.4nm Push, Targets 2029 Mass Production to Close Gap with TSMC, Intel. Samsung is restarting its 1.4nm (SF1.4) foundry push with mass production now targeted for 2029вҖ”later than its original 2027 goal but roughly aligned with IntelвҖҷs 14A timeline, while TSMC remains the frontrunner with 1.4nm mass production planned for 2H28. The delay reflects SamsungвҖҷs near-term focus on stabilizing yields for its 2nm/SF2P processes (even shifting its Exynos 2800 chip to 2nm), though itвҖҷs already installing ASMLвҖҷs High-NA EUV equipment for 1.4nmвҖ”a tool TSMC plans to skip through 2029, potentially giving Samsung and Intel a technical edge there even as they trail on timing - Trendforce <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=KwPDi3K&sig=HLjqB2hQ2UkudHJHUj26Ke8lSBx&v=2&url=https://www.trendforce.com/news/2026/06/30/news-samsung-reportedly-restarts-1-4nm-push-targets-2029-mass-production-to-close-gap-with-tsmc-intel/>  

 

*	Holiday industry prepares for the agentic travel agent. Hotels, tour operators and travel agencies are rushing to launch proprietary online tools and loyalty schemes to fend off future competition from autonomous bots, capable of arranging travel for their users Вӯ-FT <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=O3riG6B&sig=JISX3yExB4VxcKwdaNTllc9UKh5&v=2&url=https://www.ft.com/content/befb7f34-9357-492b-a40b-2132d3256bcc?syn-25a6b1a6%3D1>  

  

*	Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier list, parts and photos exposed in Tata data leak. Sensitive information about AppleвҖҷs upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models, including component lists, supplier details, and photos, was leaked online by the ransomware group World Leaks. The leak, originating from AppleвҖҷs Indian supplier Tata Electronics, exposes AppleвҖҷs supplier arrangements and vulnerabilities. This incident could impact AppleвҖҷs relationship with Tata and its efforts to expand manufacturing in India - Reuters <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=Ds2b-4_&sig=8KLHyNtbzK3DiLWt2QXOF2GOGFC&v=2&url=https://www.reuters.com/business/media-telecom/apple-iphone-18-pro-supplier-list-parts-photos-exposed-tata-data-leak-2026-06-29/>  

 

*	Raw Material Inflation Deepens: FULLTECH Hikes Glass Fiber Cloth Prices Up to 30%; VPEC Adjusts Epi Wafer Prices.Upstream electronics suppliers are raising prices sharply heading into H2 2026: PCB material maker FULLTECH is hiking glass fiber cloth prices up to 30% effective July 1, while GaAs epitaxial wafer producer VPEC is also implementing increases citing sustained raw material cost pressure. Investors should watch for these cost increases вҖ” described as just the вҖңtip of the icebergвҖқ вҖ” to ripple through to downstream device makers and brand owners by Q3, potentially squeezing margins or pushing prices higher across the electronics supply chain - Trendforce <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=MJhhU9i&sig=95WahdnC79BWjjQ2d7MITkILpV3&v=2&url=https://www.trendforce.com/news/2026/06/30/news-raw-material-inflation-deepens-as-fulltech-hikes-glass-fiber-cloth-prices-up-to-30-vpec-adjusts-epi-wafers/>  

 

*	ByteDance Reportedly Eyes Next-Gen In-House CPU for 2H27 Mass Production; May Partner With Qualcomm.ByteDance is finalizing its next-gen in-house CPU design by early 2027 (mass production in 2H27) to reduce reliance on Intel and AMD, whose prices have jumped 10-35% recently, while also exploring a Qualcomm partnership to navigate supply chain bottlenecks. Separately, ByteDance is diversifying its AI chip supply through domestic GPU makers, including a deal for at least 50,000 chips from Iluvatar CoreX this year, signaling a broader shift toward chip self-sufficiency amid global supply constraints  - Trendforce <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511082&ad=4535144709&h=hZ7QZrT&sig=SYiVtFGRKHZPHlIP7UxetWfGM2K&v=2&url=https://www.trendforce.com/news/2026/06/30/news-bytedance-reportedly-eyes-early-2027-in-house-cpu-design-for-2h27-mass-production-may-partner-with-qualcomm/>

emails.subject: Increasing Risk of Tighter Financial Conditions Creates an Opportunity to be Short YTD AI Winners and Long Hyperscalers
emails.body:
DAILY STRATEGY: Main Point вҖ“ High vol and high correlations create an opportunity to hedge the Price Momentum factor via single-name options. YTD AI Winners have witnessed increasingly negative sensitivity to financial conditions. Those AI baskets have significant downside risk if financial conditions tighten. The 2yr yield breaking above 4.2%, which would be associated with core inflation coming in too high (above 0.21% MoM) would be negative for YTD AI thematic basket winners. Mag 7 relative performance is negatively correlated with Semis. Long Mag 7 is a way to hedge against downside risk in Semis, AI thematic basket winners and Price Momentum. The Morgan Stanely (MS) Broad AI index (+73% absolute YTD) has witnessed an increasingly negative beta to financial conditions recently. The negative beta to financial conditions has moved toward extreme levels relative to history for the MS Broad AI basket. The negative sensitivity to financial conditions has become most intense since investors started to shift away from pricing in an easing bias from the Fed ~2 months ago. That is when 2yr yields started to reprice higher. The same dynamic holds true for the MS Global CPU basket (+165% absolute YTD), and the MS Global Memory baskets (358% absolute YTD). Charts below. 
 	
Macro Research 

Portfolio Strategy 

 	

Increasing Risk of Tighter Financial Conditions Creates an Opportunity to be Short YTD AI Winners and Long Hyperscalers



June 30, 2026

DAILY STRATEGY: Main Point вҖ“ High vol and high correlations create an opportunity to hedge the Price Momentum factor via single-name options. YTD AI Winners have witnessed increasingly negative sensitivity to financial conditions. Those AI baskets have significant downside risk if financial conditions tighten. The 2yr yield breaking above 4.2%, which would be associated with core inflation coming in too high (above 0.21% MoM) would be negative for YTD AI thematic basket winners. Mag 7 relative performance is negatively correlated with Semis. Long Mag 7 is a way to hedge against downside risk in Semis, AI thematic basket winners and Price Momentum. 

The Morgan Stanely (MS) Broad AI index (+73% absolute YTD) has witnessed an increasingly negative beta to financial conditions recently. The negative beta to financial conditions has moved toward extreme levels relative to history for the MS Broad AI basket. The negative sensitivity to financial conditions has become most intense since investors started to shift away from pricing in an easing bias from the Fed ~2 months ago. That is when 2yr yields started to reprice higher. 

The same dynamic holds true for the MS Global CPU basket (+165% absolute YTD), and the MS Global Memory baskets (358% absolute YTD). Charts below. 

Bottom Line вҖ“ If core inflation comes in well above the Fed target for 2026 (3.3%), so well above 0.21% MoM between now and year-end 2026 for Core PCE, financial conditions would tighten more aggressively (HERE <https://portalmail.22vresearch.com/ls/click?upn=u001.YDKHS31hO89-2FsaNw5Y3euD2W7dR80VuTCh6Tu4acCX-2BmRKa-2Bio1ghar1ZhQA6ALEC6ee86eLi-2F647z0SwMxrdjoyqWcfle-2FtcEprIzau2Jc-3DA4u1_m62Kq-2BZVlqTCB7tW71uBlxzucvmcl39EI5mlNrCbXb2EsQ9L-2FX-2Fo5QkOPg8S9quKmd5PvGIrNt1lk9wM6fx2f-2FvNtGCcQZKtklwEepVy3G99c-2BK0KFJkmZh-2F-2FirD-2FNmYqDBkYsyNeTnRW-2BcBQ3IrluoNvwGBgm1gSwe-2FBoYNPcuhyh-2F1e0WaebP-2BiQQgPEzXhCYbD6zO4DDdHoY8VGtWRYpKCT0FTJLBfz585Dywl2NBFhKRsbNMTbwUInBQxrkhHW1KD0nk4mAjEG4Cylj8rb1SZGqoE7Mw5kmZnN5aI4hUdBsiUkSQatQf4AkfKEllhXbQ5TbgcP9Dx9qKTLVq8g0eoEI9H8pOEIhP87YnJDCPkuOZ7hpArh2UjXEvpOQp574RN3N0KbaXZ99GrUCrQyLbipuO5bre-2BlOjel6GnN-2BOQnVDtZE0BjFfBqdsgUjsvuUWiMx7UsEVBfNMLE9A62uUJ7Qx-2Bl7z9QJu5TLL6NP41WPjWVISrfZr7Abk3lqynRPFHJwXCaHBQSqXx1Fbgsby1CBPzKVdJSBJKspue8rEdC-2FXtlD-2Bn2d6a5DSBpfioDnBpgCckrwiHcLwVPPcJjSt6p8jXHULSMyvegU3paGUfOSMf0HqPJB5p-2Bi-2B7H-2F2F-2BfpUDU4DUpeWFGcBdlJjIgS0mII6ORg-2BoF-2BbQlXqsbAcEgB-2FfDWne7jA1vw1lRsTJOfKeB1F0Epr1deqcoAHg-3D-3D> ). Given the increasingly negative beta to financial conditions of some AI YTD winners and the Price Momentum factor, downside risk could be significant. 2yr yields moving well above 4.2% would signal investors are discounting hawkish core inflation readings. 

Jeff Jacobson, 22V Derivatives Specialist, sees the vol setup as an opportunity to be long Hyperscalers (short side of Mo). He highlighted costless call spreads HERE <https://portalmail.22vresearch.com/ls/click?upn=u001.YDKHS31hO89-2FsaNw5Y3euMQg8QwKDydCFC9rwUVo9dWx7gyOgnYfLaGSmA0sms8d3FUWz8MaTxZw7YxmM-2Bcas-2FpDky0MrDsto-2F16kw2Yl4cNWxLg7gd2aUmSCG2LlraVUEkpTQ7DUKPZQhsDyV5LgMx-2BQ-2BxDrBdgSKvj1O4Bb22EllxDgUK81BExg8s8TJ4I-2BkydcZXhvpLJsoA6MZXiphEhS-2Fd2DBzo9YSSvHRrx-2BuGooK8xNzLjzbroL2Io4A5zhIkR5f3azQ75jg-2FiQOr5g-3D-3DpPER_m62Kq-2BZVlqTCB7tW71uBlxzucvmcl39EI5mlNrCbXb2EsQ9L-2FX-2Fo5QkOPg8S9quKmd5PvGIrNt1lk9wM6fx2f-2FvNtGCcQZKtklwEepVy3G99c-2BK0KFJkmZh-2F-2FirD-2FNmYqDBkYsyNeTnRW-2BcBQ3IrluoNvwGBgm1gSwe-2FBoYNPcuhyh-2F1e0WaebP-2BiQQgPEzXhCYbD6zO4DDdHoY8VGtWRYpKCT0FTJLBfz585Dywl2NBFhKRsbNMTbwUInBQxrkhHW1KD0nk4mAjEG4Cylj8rb1SZGqoE7Mw5kmZnN5aI4hUdBsiUkSQatQf4AkfKEllhXbQ5TbgcP9Dx9qKTLVq8g0eoEI9H8pOEIhP87YnJDCPkuOZ7hpArh2UjXEvpOQp574RN3N0KbaXZ99GrUCrQyLbipuO5bre-2BlOjel6GnN-2BOQnVDtZE0BjFfBqdsgUjsvuUWiMx7UsEVBfNMLE9A62uUJ7Qx-2Bl7z9QJu5TLL6NP41WPjWVISrfZr7Abk3lqynRPFHJwXCaHBQSqXx1Fbgsby1CBPzKVdJSBJKspue8re5rkJlEGpnvKJu-2FYZSkelmqtnbtdmG2YtKtGbWzp51ZW1uO6VVwfWG7MY1kZbpWBv16c5lC3fYwpdsjcvG1hiH7NiAU8ojxY-2BqeBOU9wyWREChY9O0L0S6Na7Y0PieExLI4cKcQ4iSpRsTw-2BcA1FvCRYAEPWnEKSpMcyOqVU23Q-3D-3D> . An example with MSFT is below. FYI, Jeff can look at different structures across equities.

Long Hyperscalers is a way to hedge against a rotation. Hyperscalers have bounced while some of the best performing AI buildout trades вҖ“ Onsite Power, nuclear, memory, semis вҖ“ have come under pressure. The current rolling correlation between Hyperscalers and Semis, for example, has flipped to negative.

From JeffвҖҰ

Trade:
Buy MSFT Jan 15th 410 calls 1x

Sell MSFT Jan 15th 470 calls 2x

Trades for ~ EVEN (MSFT 373 Fri close ref)

Microsoft (MSFT)

Before the impressive rally on Friday, which also occurred on massive volume, MSFT shares had declined as much as 37% from their October high. The sharp decline brought the stock back to the April 2025 tariff lows. Perhaps even more significant was the move in implied volatility, with 6-month vol moved from 22 to 37 (a fresh five year high). Not only has volatility gotten expensive, but the upside 25-delta calls are now trading at their richest skew to at the money 50-delta calls. Again, when we have a situation like this of a large decline in underlying shares, overall volatility bid up AND upside calls trading вҖңrichвҖқ to the at the money calls, we want to be looking at trades/strategies that offset the vol while capturing this attractive call skew.



ChartsвҖҰ

Price Momentum volatility is at its 94th percentile and top decile Price Momentum has contributed 8.4pp of the S&P 500вҖҷs 8.8% return. Correlation within Momentum baskets is also low, making it difficult to manage risk. 







Hyperscalers have bounced some as other popular and YTD winners in the AI buildout trade have come under some pressure. Jeff Jacobson thinks the vol setup makes this rotation a good one to play with options. 



Mag 7 performance is negatively correlated with Semis. 



The Morgan Stanely (MS) Broad AI index has a deeply negative beta to financial conditions. If inflation comes in well above the Fed target and financial conditions tighten more aggressively, the MS Broad AI basket would come under pressure. 



The MS Global Memory basket negative beta to financial conditions has intensified. 



Same for the MS Global CPU basket.

ItвҖҷs been a while since I last spoke about POET Tech. 

 <https://substack.com/redirect/31921dd5-2f6c-493d-82f6-72faae3a211b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 
	
$POET update <https://substack.com/redirect/31921dd5-2f6c-493d-82f6-72faae3a211b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 

Gaetano <https://substack.com/profile/410947583-gaetano> 
	В·
	Apr 27
<https://substack.com/redirect/31921dd5-2f6c-493d-82f6-72faae3a211b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTBkYXR1In0.wMhVDxPSNfOaCS4gLOy6QmLs4ijRPD8hP3NmO4HbRXc> 
Read full story	

The Marvell blunder made it hard for me to trust management enough to hold any real size. But thereвҖҷs a lot going on, so letвҖҷs talk about it.

The balance sheet is much larger. The customer list is broader. Lumilens gives the 2027 story a clearer revenue target. Management is talking more directly about Malaysia capacity, H2 shipments, Blazar, external light sources, and the design-win funnel.

The bear case is also still very present. Marvell cancelled the Celestial purchase orders, taking out one of my main validation points. I moved that part of the thesis to zero, and it stays there until I see a repaired relationship, reinstated orders, or a new Marvell agreement. They still havenвҖҷt executed in the way that I would really like to see. 

So the story is bigger. The proof still has to show up in shipments.

LetвҖҷs unpack everything on my brain. 

________________________________

The rest of this post is for paid subscribers. Below the paywall: the balance sheet and Lumilens detail, what I think happened with Marvell, the real H2 test IвҖҷm watching, MalaysiaвҖҷs capacity numbers, and where I land on valuation given the dilution.

This report is for informational and educational purposes only and should never serve as the basis for an investment decision. I may hold positions in companies mentioned, including POET, and my views may change at any time without notice. Readers should conduct their own due diligence and consult a qualified financial professional before buying or selling any security.

________________________________

Wow, they have a ton of cash. I feel like I keep saying this and it keeps going higher. POET now has roughly $830 million of pro forma cash and short-term investments after the May financing. ThatвҖҷs practically half the market cap, so thereвҖҷs no imminent financing risk. I thought that two raises ago too, and they keep raising. If the capital goes toward hypergrowth, great. If not, not great. Why are you taking so much of my growth potential? But right now it gives them real room to hire, buy equipment, secure supply, and support customer programs. Management plans to deploy roughly $50 million into capex in H2, which says theyвҖҷre building capacity ahead of the ramp rather than waiting for every order to be fully visible. Right move if demand is real. Higher execution risk if it isnвҖҷt.

Lumilens: an initial $50 million purchase order, products expected to deploy in 2027, with management framing the relationship at more than $500 million over five years. To be precise, the $50 million is commercial visibility, not realized revenue. The products still need development, qualification by Lumilens, qualification by LumilensвҖҷs own customers, manufacturing, shipment, and acceptance. Real positive. Still has to be executed. ThatвҖҷs always what this comes down to me. Execution proof. 

Customer breadth: Lumilens, ADTRAN, LITEON, Lessengers, NTT Innovative Devices, unnamed system integrators, and external-light-source customers. Management says the current funnel could translate into more than $100 million of annualized revenue over the next two years. ThatвҖҷs run-rate language, not reported revenue, and the вҖңif those programs qualify and rampвҖқ is doing a lot of work in that sentence.

Now Marvell. Celestial connected POET to optical scale-up architectures, Marvell acquired Celestial, and that created a great read-through. Then Marvell cancelled every Celestial purchase order for POET. POET disclosed the written cancellation came April 23, announced it publicly four days later, and the stated basis was alleged disclosure of purchase-order and shipping information, not product performance. That suggests this was about communication and legal process rather than the technology. Although, you could push the argument that Marvell wanted out anyway and used this as a reason to get out. But who knows. DoesnвҖҷt matter because thatвҖҷs pure speculation that I canвҖҷt support given what I know. But the cleanest AI-scale-up validation point in the thesis is gone, and it gets zero credit from me until itвҖҷs repaired or replaced. The AGM gave no cleanup on it either (as far as I heard). I expected something and didnвҖҷt get it. Replacement validation now has to come from the existing $5 million-plus order, ADTRAN, Lumilens, LITEON, Lessengers, Blazar, or a new customer entirely.

Which gets to what I actually care about. The $5 million-plus 800G order, disclosed last year, expected to ship in H2, separate from the cancelled Celestial orders. Management again confirmed delivery against existing purchase orders this half. I want to know if POET actually ships. Please, POET. Ship on time. Partnerships and demos build a foundation, but the thesis only really changes once product leaves the factory, gets accepted, revenue gets recognized, and follow-on orders show up. Shipped. Accepted. Qualified. Repeat order. Gross margin. IвҖҷll wait and pay up for that. I am happy taking a bigger position once I get more proof, even if itвҖҷs at a higher price. ThatвҖҷs just me. 

The other number IвҖҷm watching is the 30,000-plus optical engine shipment target for 2026, light-source production in Malaysia first, then 800G engines. I want to know if that target still stands after Marvell. Reaffirmed is a good sign. Walked down, pay attention and definitely a yellow flag. 

Malaysia: the transition from China is complete, the first tranche of capacity is qualified for production, and more space is coming online at Globetronics and NationGate. The capacity gap is the real story though. Management is framing roughly a ten-fold expansion into 2027, from about 1 million optical engines per year today to demand exiting 2027 that could require 1 million per month. ThatвҖҷs the upside and the execution risk in one number, not unlike AAOI for anyone in that name too. Real demand plus clean Malaysia scaling gets POET into a much bigger commercial ramp. Slower scaling or uneven yields push the curve right.

Revenue visibility today: still tiny in reported terms. Not a problem. ItвҖҷs still in a вҖҳpre-commercial, pre-inflectionвҖҷ bucket. Q1 2026 revenue was about $503,000. The future sits in buckets. Near-term from the 800G order and undisclosed ADTRAN orders, development-stage from the $1 million-plus external-light-source NRE, 2027 commercial from the $50 million Lumilens order, pipeline from the $100 million-plus claim, and longer-dated upside from the $500 million-plus Lumilens potential, Blazar, and OCI. Better setup than a few months ago. Still heavily weighted toward future execution of course. 

One thing worth noting is that management said they expect to be selective with new customer announcements over the next 12 months. I wonder how much of that is downstream of Marvell. But read the other way, itвҖҷs a fine signal. The pipeline is already large enough that the job now is development, qualification, and ramping current customers rather than collecting logos. ThatвҖҷs how IвҖҷll judge them too. Less interested in another partner announcement, more interested in whether the current ones convert. Show us the money POET! 

Several products are qualified for production and headed there imminently. Lumilens is a different bucket, still in development, still needing qualification by Lumilens and then by LumilensвҖҷs customers. Some products are close though. 

Blazar is the interesting long-dated option, a high-power multi-channel external laser platform tied to OCI, external light sources, CPO, and future AI scale-up. Could be large. ItвҖҷs a 2028 story: sampling in H2 2026, design wins and qualification in 2027, production in 2028.

PFIC and redomicile, from the short report a couple months back, is still unresolved. Board approved a U.S. redomicile path, but at the AGM Tom Mika said theyвҖҷre still assessing with legal and tax advisors and wonвҖҷt know 2026 PFIC status until year-end. Not core to the thesis, but it deserves clarity given how much of this stockвҖҷs base is U.S. retail. The legal and promotion overhang from the Marvell cancellation is also still hanging around I guess. 

My view is constructive but disciplined. The setup genuinely improved, and Marvell was a genuine hit, both at once. The bull case comes down to whether this story turns into shipped engines, recognized revenue, repeat orders, and a revenue curve that actually supports todayвҖҷs valuation. ThatвҖҷs why H2 carries so much weight. Ship against existing orders, show real product revenue, prove Malaysia can run at volume, and confidence improves a lot. Slip on shipments or stay vague on customers while the valuation holds, and the market starts asking how much of this pipeline is actually real.

So for me, it remains a very small position. Like I said, I will pay up for proof.

Does this mean the stock wonвҖҷt go up? Absolutely not. I would be surprised if it didnвҖҷt go up. Especially if the sector catches a tailwind. But I just want more proof for myself. 

Not a recommendation of course. 

I know I have some really informed POET investors that sub. Please feel free to add any additional notes or some points I might be missing! I havenвҖҷt been tracking this daily like I have in the past. 

________________________________

This report is for informational and educational purposes only and should never serve as the basis for an investment decision. I may hold positions in companies mentioned, including POET, and my views may change at any time without notice. Readers should conduct their own due diligence and consult a qualified financial professional before buying or selling any security.

emails.subject: Jefferies Tech: Morning News, Thoughts / Inbounds Yesterday, Blayne's AVGO Bull Case, Janardan on NOK
emails.body:
1. Movers + Macro

2. Pre-Market News

3. Thoughts on Yesterday / Discussions

4. AVGO, Revisiting The Bull Thesis

5. Charts

6. JanardanвҖҷs NOK Takes

 

The AI Bear Case with Ed Zitron (Today at 11a): Please join Aniket Shah for a call with Ed Zitron to examine Ed's critique of gen AIвҖҷs unit economics, the gap between hype and durable demand, and what investors may be underestimating as on costs and returns. Register here <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=ELo-iYy&sig=5ZZoZYlqJ7JY2vQYZCGesGWE6Nz&v=2&url=https://jefferies-sustainability-transition-strategy.open-exchange.net/session> .

 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Movers + Macro.

 

Equity futures in the green but off the highs of the AM as we wrap up Q2 and QQQs look to put up their best Q in 6 years. While the SOXX is on pace to close up ~87% this Q, 2x its best quarterly return ever.

 

The best single stock from the 1H of this year > $5B in market cap = SNDK up ~765%.

The worst single stock from the 1H of this year > $5B in market cap = INTU down ~60%.

 

Semicap strength continuing in the pre-market, small cap contact center CNXC is down 23% following a big вҖҷ26 guidance cut, & CMCSA is seeing some follow through post the spin announcement yesterday.

 

Pre-market movers:

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Pre-Market News.

 

AI + Jobs: The FT notes that companies investing most heavily in AI are adding workers faster than their peers, according to new research that challenges predictions of broad AI-driven job losses. White-collar worker #вҖҷs increased ~10% overall at companies that used AI most intensely in the first two years after they first adopted the technology, the research found, with gains across occupation types and seniority levels.

 



 

CMCSA: Reuters noted <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=OxHqqSL&sig=EocAa41Dd0Pltf4nJO1xE729vGE&v=2&url=https://www.reuters.com/world/nbcuniversal-may-enter-video-game-business-after-comcast-split-sources-say-2026-06-29/>  last night that NBCUniversal is considering gaming and entertainment franchises for growth after the split. The cable and connectivity business, meanwhile, is ripe for technological investments вҖҢthat could take advantage of the massive surge in data centers and AI.

 

CNXC: A small call center business thatвҖҷs likely off the radar for most, but continues the IT Services and Contact Center pain that weвҖҷve continued to see. Down ~25% in the pre-market following a Q2 revenue, EBITDA, and operating income misses, guides below the Streets for Q3, and cuts across the board.

 

вҖңWe are definitely seeing increased financial pressure on our clients as they try and cope with their own investment needs and their current operating environments. This has created demand for more of our automation solutions, but also increased the urgency of moving work offshore and caused certain clients to prioritize spend across their client base, resulting in reduced spend overall.вҖқ

 

NVDA: SA notes <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=tUHziHY&sig=IzRCn95O9bO3NIrWgIAiFAvLnL0&v=2&url=https://x.com/SemiAnalysis_/status/2071840399627288746>  that their accelerator model estimate has NVDA DC compute revenue ~20% higher compared to consensus expectations for 2H FYвҖҷ27. Rubin will have a big ramp after being pushed out due to earlier HBM4 issues that are now resolved and front end wafer supply is now built up.

 



 

TMUS: The German business outlet Handelsblatt noted <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=HF5Hgqp&sig=EQ3X68SaDTrd9xCQxti4A7i42ZJ&v=2&url=https://www.handelsblatt.com/technik/it-internet/fusion-telekom-forciert-plaene-fuer-mega-holding-elon-musk-als-treiber/100236142.html>  last night according to insiders, Deutsche Telekom CEO is pushing ahead with the merger with its US subsidiary TMUS. Insiders tell Handelsblatt that one plan being considered would see a holding company headquartered in Ireland or Switzerland, while other sources tell Handelsblatt that regardless, Bonn, Germany, will remain the company's operational center. The insiders tell Handelsblatt that the plans are being spurred by the possibility -- seemingly raised by an analyst, that SPCX might target T-Mobile US.

 

xAI: Last night announced <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=5C8RZkF&sig=4n8Vw85mpTQxFqaokCzjyLxRCqq&v=2&url=https://docs.x.com/tools/mcp>  the hosted X MCP server, giving AI agents like Grok and Cursor instant, no-configuration access to real-time data from the X platform.

 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Thoughts on Yesterday / Discussions. 

 

A lot of head scratching moves yesterday to say the least вҖҰ all of which were accompanies by a lack of true answers or what feels like really big stretches.

 

A few notables that received a lot of inbounds in terms of the magnitude vs. the direction вҖҰ PANW having an EPS level move and making continued fresh ATHs | the rip in Semicaps (albeit folks not complaining), following an investment # from SK Hynix and Samsung that felt in-line with FridayвҖҷs chatter | the move in ALGM | the squeeze in RBLX following better platform CCU data from Animal Hospital вҖҰ even with an u/g away from us, still get the sense folks arenвҖҷt convinced we have the all clear | CBRS move all related to GPT5.6 Sol <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=FKg3cgd&sig=1xrusBs8ANPeHP3WsEZvoeeLL5z&v=2&url=https://www.reddit.com/r/codex/comments/1ugp2fe/gpt_56_sol_will_be_on_cerebras_at_750_tokens_per/>  вҖҰ however it has recouped itвҖҷs $185 IPO price.

 

Also, even though earnings were challenging to say the least, itвҖҷs still remarkable the lack of a dead cat bounce weвҖҷve seen in ACN the last week, even yesterday in the face of a better Software tape.

 

And after a fairly consistent +20 years of o/p the S&P 500 вҖҰ thatвҖҷs all but evaporated over the last 2 years.

 



 

And following Telcos having one of the worst relative u/p days vs. the S&P 500 вҖҰ net flows continue to skew better for sale on our desk amongst LOs and HFs within Cable and Telcos.

 



 

Also вҖҰ over the last 5 days, вҖңwhy the strength in GLW?вҖқ has been a consistent top 5 question. GLWвҖҷs now the 2nd best performing single stock in our Tech coverage group over the last 5 days behind just AXON, and over the last month, and a top 5 performer over the course of the last 1 month.

Most cited reasons for the moves weвҖҷve heard in GLWвҖҰ short lean in positioning early last week mostly on a valuation thesis relative to the rest of the group, Fujikura read through from earnings noting stronger demand, and then a note from Needham noting that вҖңNVDA is embarking on a massive project to construct and entirely new very high capacity global long haul network.вҖқ

 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

AVGO, Revisiting The Bull Thesis.

 

With the recent pullback, Blayne notes <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=NG5zt-n&sig=Nqde2iDRCv2VlkyWsKcaW0xLCvf&v=2&url=https://javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/secure/html/444ef7ac-2f28-4cf3-bc52-4540b95078d0>  this has created an interesting opportunity in AVGO. Thoughts and pushback welcomed here.

 

He notes that AVGO has an improved CYвҖҷ28 visibility, CYвҖҷ27 expectations have been reset after last QвҖҷs high expectations (i.e. chips vs racks), TPU roadmap remains on track and head of expectations, overblown MTK concerns, and a broadening out ASIC customer base.

 

Blayne leaves his вҖҷ27 and вҖҷ28 estimates unchanged but believes you can get to $30-40 in CYвҖҷ28.

 

Expectations for CYвҖҷ27 got too high as investors were looking for $150-200B of ASIC revenue. Based on his supply chain checks on XPU volumes and the announced XPV, he thinks that level of revenue should be a floor for CYвҖҷ28. 

 

His scenario analysis points to $30-40 of EPS with the mid-point of $225B set at 20GWs @ $11B/GW, which is at the low-end of the $10-20B/GW range.

 



 

AVGO ASICs by customer:

 



 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Charts.

 

Bloomberg with an interesting chart showing the extreme dispersions within the S&P 500 Information Tech Index over the last several months.

 



 

Below is the MTD performance for a variety of pair trades that are either on or have been on that weвҖҷve tracked over the last year or so. Thoughts welcomed if we missed any glaring pair trades that folks loom at.

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

JanardanвҖҷs NOK Takes.

 

Janardan notes <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=TpDiRXm&sig=5QuYNXxL3pGo8JSY67q4iv7r5uL&v=2&url=https://javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/secure/html/da3d5a69-0432-4dc0-9afb-d70395c807be>  NOKвҖҷs engagement with hyperscalers is rising across its Optical portfolio, with its IP Networks acceleration being driven by Switch wins with one hyperscaler for now. 

 

He models this order momentum leading to higher growth and margin expansion in вҖҳ27 and вҖҳ28 than previously modelled, while also supporting a further re-rating of the stock. He raises his forecasts with вҖҳ27 EPS ~15% ahead of consensus.

 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Surinder on IT Services.

 

Surinder notes <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=EeUzwPo&sig=4p4gC8qwkxV5ow7eMIzTtISh0il&v=2&url=https://javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/secure/html/f16b3c34-e6ce-4b77-9165-356b951a464b>  following yesterdayвҖҷs acquisition of European IT Services firm Nagarro by Indian IT Services firm Persistent Systems, and ACN recently doubling its target M&A spend to $9B for вҖҳ26, suggests strategic use of M&A is increasing. 

 

He expects M&A activity within IT Services to accelerate as incumbents increasingly recognize the need to transform amidst the current tech driven disruption wave, especially with valuations depressed.

 

He believes the space is undergoing significant change and each mgmt team is taking a different view of what the future looks like. For example, HCLT recently acquired Jaspersoft, which provides business intelligence and reporting platforms. ACN has accelerated its M&A strategy to place greater emphasis on a platform offering. And EXLS, recently announced its intent to acquire iMerit, an AI model development and implementation specialist.

 

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Calls + Events to Know.

 

Software Update Call (Tuesday July 7th, 11a): Please join Brent Thill and myself for updated Software takes and discussing current views on the multiple walls of worry Software currently has. Register here <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13511019&ad=4535116764&h=N_S8tj1&sig=ZXSd04ciM2NDaMG519i6Ye7cqe4&v=2&url=https://jefferies.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_c2HUUS9MQaC0eXl81ESEhg%23/registration> .

emails.subject: JPM TECH SKETCH: Why Internet Stocks CanвҖҷt Work, UBER-Waymo PHX Partnership Kaput, CMCSA Spin: Right Move, Surprising Timing
emails.sender: Mark Schilsky - Specialist Sales - US TMT
emails.body:
Internet Stocks CanвҖҷt Work, UBER New Phoenix Partner?, CMCSA Wants to Buy Gaming Assets? (RBLX? TTWO?), QLYS U/G to Neutral 


JPM TECH SKETCH: Why Internet Stocks CanвҖҷt Work, UBER-Waymo PHX Partnership Kaput, CMCSA Spin: Right Move, Surprising Timing 


Mark Schilsky | mark.schilsky@jpmorgan.com | +1 (212) 622-2668 
вҖӢ 


SCHILSKYвҖҷS SNAPSHOT 


- Why Internet Stocks CanвҖҷt Work: Internet investor sentiment is currently as low as IвҖҷve seen it in over the past four years. Consistently, I hear from investors that вҖҳXYZ stock canвҖҷt work becauseвҖҰвҖҷ without any related discussion of what could make the stocks work. As a result, IвҖҷve decided to compile the most commonly cited reasons IвҖҷve heard as to why Internet stocks canвҖҷt work. In theory, if any of these overhangs start to lift, these stocks may begin to work. In theoryвҖҰ I go stock-by-stock below. Please tell me what you think. 

- UBER-Waymo Rides No Longer Available in Phoenix (re: GOOGL): Is this the beginning of the end of the UBER-Waymo partnership <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=se56vvs&sig=UFeBFEnlyyrlF61c7usC2TDktWb&v=2&url=https://waymo.com/blog/2023/10/the-waymo-driver-now-available-on-uber-in-phoenix/> ? A one-off? Something else? UBER IR confirmed the Waymo-Phoenix news in an email to me: вҖңWe can confirm that we have elected to terminate the Phoenix pilot as it has reached the natural end stateвҖҰвҖқ TechCrunch <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=xeE-TS-&sig=NJg75guGdDX66EdcEwlQAwDi6BI&v=2&url=https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/29/waymo-and-uber-quietly-part-ways-in-phoenix/?secureweb%3Dbplus64> verified my reporting вҖ“ and augmented it: вҖңUber said it is readying the launch of a separate autonomous vehicle partnership in the city, but did not name the partner.вҖқ UBER traded down -1% yesterday.

NEWS вҖ“ DESK COLOR вҖ“ RESEARCH HIGHLIGHTS 


Why Internet Stocks CanвҖҷt Work: 


Internet investor sentiment is currently as low as IвҖҷve seen it in over the past four years. Consistently, I hear from investors that вҖҳXYZ stock canвҖҷt work becauseвҖҰвҖҷ without any related discussion of what could make the stocks work. As a result, IвҖҷve decided to compile the most commonly cited reasons IвҖҷve heard as to why Internet stocks canвҖҷt work. In theory, if any of these overhangs start to lift, these stocks may begin to work. In theoryвҖҰ 

*	META: ROIC on ZuckerbergвҖҷs Superintelligence Lab spend is currently highly negative. Without any new products and/or model releases, investors do not yet know if METAвҖҷs AI efforts will lead to a product cycle that looks like Reels вҖ“ or one that looks like the Metaverse. Plus, there remains a significant equity issuance overhang post-GOOGL. 
*	AMZN: Take your pick of possible overhangs: a potentially weakening US consumer, 2026/2027 Capex estimates going much higher (driven materially by higher component pricing), increasing competition amongst hyperscalers and neoclouds, a desire to compete in space via Leo, a potential equity issuance, etc. AMZN remains the most frustrating Internet stock for the most investors. 
*	GOOGL: Post-equity issuance, investors have ratcheted expectations for FY27 Capex estimates meaningfully higher. IвҖҷve heard anywhere from $300B to $400B; the Street remains way too low at ~$245B. Until investors have clarity on what this figure will be, the stock canвҖҷt work without commensurate Revenue acceleration beyond what we already saw in Q1. (Maybe on the Q2 print?) 
*	RDDT: The only two ways that RDDT can dispel the AI Bear Case that products like GOOGL AI Overviews will obviate the need for an increasing number of internet users to peruse RDDT forums are: 1) Materially inflect US DAUs for more than one quarter. [Yes, RDDTвҖҷs stock would pop if they did this on the Q226 print, but this has to be a sustainable trend above +10% Y/Y to get investors to really pay attention.] & 2) Sign a new GOOGL AI content licensing deal <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=B4Wu3s2&sig=MVnTYDxqGABQ22uYlDPJKFe5FQr&v=2&url=https://www.reuters.com/technology/reddit-ai-content-licensing-deal-with-google-sources-say-2024-02-22/> . Not for materially more money, although that would be nice, but for guaranteed traffic. The persistent RDDT overhang is that GOOGL is just one algo change away from crushing referral traffic to RDDT; a new AI deal with some type of traffic guarantees would be welcomed by investors вҖ“ perhaps in early 2027. 
*	APP: While there remain two most-often-cited reasons why APP canвҖҷt work, in my opinion, only one can ever truly go away. The first issue is META. While there have been conflicting boutique research reports about the possibility of META entering the non-IDFA mobile games space since January, the unfortunate reality for APP shareholders is that this boogeyman will never fully go away. As such, only the second reason can truly go away in the next few quarters: eComm. APPвҖҷs stock saw a massive expansion in its multiple in 2025 as investors increasingly got excited about the eComm opportunity, but near-term eComm enthusiasm has been tempered by management вҖ“ both on earnings calls and in meetings. The AppLovin ad platform just opened up to self-serve a week ago and there is a good chance that eComm can inflect in Q4, but I wouldnвҖҷt necessarily assume in Q3. In other words, APP canвҖҷt work until eComm demonstrably inflects. 
*	NFLX: Netflix canвҖҷt work because US engagement growth in the low single-digits remains anemic. Engagement is a harbinger of subscriber growth (which is negative in the US if you believe the alt data for Q2) and NFLX is either going to have to increase cash content spend in order to drive engagement/subscriber growth (on a tough 2H comp) or engage in some type of scaled M&A. Either way, the persistent investor view I hear is that NFLX is slowly morphing into a cable TV network (low subscriber growth, increasingly reliant on advertising) and thus the multiple should head lower. 
*	SPOT: Despite providing investors with nearly everything they wanted, Spotify hasnвҖҷt worked since the May Investor Day because feedback from the labels has been that the AI Product Tier isnвҖҷt yet ready for primetime. It will take all three major labels to sign on, not to mention a majority of artists, in order for the product to launch with any type of product-market fit. Initially, the Bullish view was that this could occur by year end, but increasingly this feels like a 2027 story at best. Until investors obtain clarity on where the new AI products are going, the stock canвҖҷt work. 
*	CVNA: Carvana has been a frustrating stock for Internet investors this year despite the company demonstrating consistent ~40% unit growth. The stock вҖҳcanвҖҷt workвҖҷ because itвҖҷs crowded and thereвҖҷs always a looming US consumer-led slowdown just around the cornerвҖҰ 
*	UBER: The AV overhang, as exemplified by GOOGLвҖҷs Waymo, means that UBER canвҖҷt work. Every Internet investor by now has ridden in a Waymo and sees how well the technology works in dense urban environments like SF. WaymoвҖҷs only real limitation at this point is the companyвҖҷs willingness to expand its fleet вҖ“ but itвҖҷs coming. In particular, 50K Hyundais <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=1vcxvsW&sig=PTmutHVz691YZUR0x8NpKR5x1zl&v=2&url=https://electrek.co/2026/02/11/hyundai-supply-waymo-50000-ioniq-5-robotaxis/>  are coming over the next 2.5 years and that will potentially blunt UBERвҖҷs US Mobility growth. As such, UBER вҖҳcanвҖҷt workвҖҷ until one of their AV partners (Nuro, Wayve, Moove, Zoox, VW-MBLY, etc.) pulls US-based safety drivers from their cars at a scale currently beyond TSLA. Check out DougвҖҷs latest: UBER/GOOGL AV Roadmap Update <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=ABiuUR9&sig=GAKn6sPUtWxMkHs8NvozVJgcUq1&v=2&isig=QlIdIOCKjxOeAQGGdMIG5b0oDS9&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/5v1jq7f6/GPS-5315883-0/00a3f86f-0bfa-452c-9a9c-a6043f79d7c2> . 
*	DASH: DoorDash canвҖҷt work because the stock requires upward Adj. EBITDA revisions to justify its premium multiple вҖ“ and you wonвҖҷt get an earnings revision cycle until DASH has successfully integrated ROO.LN/Seven Rooms and Tony tells us on an earnings call that the reinvestment cycle is over. Plus, US Orders growth decelerated -400 bps Q/Q last quarter, making investors question whether or not this was a blip or the beginning of a volume-led slowdown. 
*	BKNG: Booking canвҖҷt work because their 2H Room Nights growth guidance implies a modest вҖҳhockey stickвҖҷ in the anticipated growth rate. Plus, the guide assumed the Iran War would end by tomorrow and that unfortunately has not yet happened. Plus, BKNG (and EXPE) are squarely in the crosshairs of the вҖҳAgentic AI Internet Bear CaseвҖҷ in which consumer agents will one day book travel on our behalf directly from hotel websites. 

[You may have noticed that I left certain stocks off this list like EBAY, ABNB, etc. and thatвҖҷs because these stocks have actually been working. ThatвҖҷs not to say that they wonвҖҷt be added to this list one day thoughвҖҰ]

UBER IR confirmed the Waymo-Phoenix news in an email to me: вҖңWe can confirm that we have elected to terminate the Phoenix pilot as it has reached the natural end state. Phoenix was our first Waymo deployment and was intentionally designed as a small-scale pilot, reaching just over a dozen dedicated vehicles over a defined period. The learnings from that program helped accelerate our launches in Austin and Atlanta, where the partnership continues to scale significantly. Waymo vehicles remain exclusively available on Uber in both Austin and Atlanta. In those two markets, we operate hundreds of autonomous vehicles, and both fleet size and service areas continue to expand. We view Phoenix as the successful completion of an early pilot that informed our broader collaboration with Waymo. Our focus remains on continuing to execute in Austin and Atlanta, and we're pleased with the progress we're making together.вҖқ 

TechCrunch <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=xeE-TS-&sig=NJg75guGdDX66EdcEwlQAwDi6BI&v=2&url=https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/29/waymo-and-uber-quietly-part-ways-in-phoenix/?secureweb%3Dbplus64> verified my reporting вҖ“ and augmented it: вҖңUber said it is readying the launch of a separate autonomous vehicle partnership in the city, but did not name the partner. Waymo told TechCrunch that the vehicles Uber used for this вҖңpilotвҖқ program have already been integrated into its own Phoenix fleet, available through its app. Waymo users started noticing that the companyвҖҷs vehicles were absent from UberвҖҷs network in recent days. It also appears that Phoenix is no longer listed as an AV city on UBERвҖҷs AV Website <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=wbkNCra&sig=F4JnwrnMDWiEYpBKKiHdiKM98I1&v=2&url=https://www.uber.com/us/en/autonomous/mobility/> .

CMCSA Spin Off: Right Move, Surprising Timing: 


From SebastianoвҖҷs CMCSA Spin Off: Right Move, Surprising Timing <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=00rX1wn&sig=W4a69mS7O8kxoRHS9VLH0Y7L5og&v=2&isig=VZjUEfjpxZ81YPTTjzbB8rNyc3o&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/ltaord5b/GPS-5351318-0/00a3f86f-0bfa-452c-9a9c-a6043f79d7c2> : вҖңAgree with the outcome, somewhat surprised by the timing. While we agree that these businesses are distinct and best pursued independently, the timing surprises us given management's commentary on the 4Q25 call, when they stated: "We don't really see that there's strategic advantage or making NBCUniversal stronger by separating it from the cable sideвҖҰ it doesn't get stronger by being smaller as a standalone entity is our view." On today's call, CEO Mike Cavanagh noted that the media and telecom environments had become increasingly competitive, with those conditions unlikely to change "anytime soon," and that the company had changed its view on the benefits of scale and diversification. Future success for the two businesses, he argued, will instead be predicated on focus, speed, and strategic flexibility. Overall, we believe the separation will allow Comcast and NBCUniversal to compete more aggressively in their respective end-markets, while also creating an opportunity for meaningful cost reduction.вҖқ 

вҖңIncreased odds of cable M&A over the long term. Separating content from connectivity raises the odds of cable M&A longer term, in our view. Given competitive pressures from fiber, fixed wireless access (FWA), and LEO satellite broadband, we expect cable to continue consolidating from here. A larger business would benefit from transaction and operating synergies, and would also support network investment in both fiber upgrades, to fend off market share erosion, and wireless infrastructure, to compete more effectively with scaled convergence players such as the Big 3 incumbents. Charter's acquisition of Cox is still expected to close this summer, but we would not be surprised to see Comcast and Charter attempt to combine over the longer term, a scenario we have explored previously. While the scale of a combined Comcast and Charter may create some noise, we believe the current competitive environment could allow such a deal to be approved, though capital structure likely remains a key consideration.вҖқ 

вҖңSum-of-the-parts valuation of ~$29/share. Our sum-of-the-parts (SOTP) valuation for Comcast implies $29 per share, illustrating the value unlock of a spin-off.вҖқ

QLYS U/G to Neutral + TENB Added to Focus List вҖ“ Chinese AI Models Catching Up: 


From BrianвҖҷsQLYS U/G to Neutral + TENB Added to Focus List вҖ“ Chinese AI Models Catching Up <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13510952&ad=4535072418&h=Y3S9zkN&sig=F0791pjV4pCib7JD0haEYjQM3Zb&v=2&isig=LagsBo3KmbzCiylCYpdnkQfcaS6&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-4rentqn/GPS-5351448-0/00a3f86f-0bfa-452c-9a9c-a6043f79d7c2> : вҖңChinese models are catching up; demand poised to accelerate. In our April note on Project Glasswing, we walked through Anthropic's frontier-AI vulnerability discovery launch and concluded the resilience factors across our preferred names remained intact, with CRWD and PANW best positioned as essential defensive layers rather than disruption targets. The picture has moved since. Open-weight Chinese AI models are closing the gap with frontier models in vulnerability discovery capabilities. Meanwhile, we already see evidence of vulnerability acceleration, illustrated by CVEs tracked for the first quarter of the year (Figure 1). This comes at a time when enterprises are preparing to assess and manage a вҖҳtsunami of vulnerabilitiesвҖҷ expected across their IT estates, and the progress of the Chinese models likely accelerates levels of urgency. Accordingly, we are adding TENB to our Analyst Focus List as we view TENB as the best positioned exposure management vendor to help organizations face this threat. We are also upgrading QLYS from Underweight to Neutral as we think the company will also benefit across its core installed base, and we could see upside to growth expectations in spite of the execution issues that the company has been managing through. Outside of the exposure management vendors, we anticipate that the collapse of mean time to exploit will also accelerate demand across the rest of our coverage universe as the number of vulnerabilities (and likely exploits) accelerate. We continue to view consolidators that have effectively invested in their platforms as best positioned to benefit from incremental demand with PANW and CRWD being best positioned.вҖқ

emails.subject: GS on China: What do local clients think about the economy? Local marketing takeaways, June 2026
emails.body:
Good morning, 

  

GS China Economics team met with onshore clients in Beijing and Shanghai over the past week, with the following overall feedback: 1) local clients appeared more cautious on ChinaвҖҷs near-term growth momentum, 2) Policy easing expectations also remain subdued, 3)  Discussions also focused on fragile consumer confidence, mixed local government incentives to expand investment, implementation risks around the new cross-border investment rules; and the AI capex outlook. 

  

*	* More cautious on near-term growth momentum: absent more visible policy easing, they worry GDP growth could slow to below 4.5% yoy in coming quarters.  

*	* Weaker April <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLefwCWZGyat30xtFRiAnhtUFQmoRCHdJg7-1l7_KqvP8x_MvBJAADAFHS8nLL4uHrXWF0IWqfBK4ZvRUboCp3wLOuf-gL_3A599SPrxWsIRB9RWLo3Mbg-BhDm-tRECl6nembF5UbHohO-F8y4n87cXqLJwkkbpWUE7TBDG5JcgScN6Gt9ET9n4qVlk4-Vyj68j2Sk9V8UoVCTfOmc2H7TfdywploIjPgnXa0elFuyN5uzms0bNkWBt__Yw1DISv-S6qANLUS6IVsmde9ITGz1Vm7-21aX1zvvrhrhdd4UBQkFZw9Ka1w1%26id=d5344981-dd95-4589-97bc-2ed7ba44f145%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/YVoHRK95Q_Jv929qt46lgdGyfieO6feWM_hLZIzbMx8=452> -May <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKVUafMuFa8cOhckv-8aqW9w15WyhD6yn8eLxOqr4NgabWjPaUPAADAQVV36dOqgw4-EoK0px65Ufa1GOB6y7gU4SOFIDeTPNtK1Yb4DXNknjm7Msc_acFz8fn7-poDbhYATXR1QGT1mOQAo_nl6WaHkcpI__ZeSgw-W2z8TiRJyKb0psuQnroaP1oFSm65jmh1jLulPP6JTD637pFSfe8-gFvrhPZhj0Ia9ug2OZhOTY7fZem1-yJZAq-3e0xmx4v2Hlyy5fSErH829GE6PdbYVHcZNwHsCbJXfkHjp2Mpu3yD3pddTR50%26id=ea7a03d8-f960-4bc1-9770-73814b4e5507%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/-24BaIr2p10nIs1VoQLNrGfdOECv9YH3F0EySi8we94=452>  activity data have left onshore investors more cautious on ChinaвҖҷs near-term macro outlook. 
*	* Exports continue to support headline activity, but domestic demand has softened notably (moreover, the boost from exports has not translated into a stronger labor market or meaningful profit improvement, limiting the pass-through from external demand to domestic growth). 
*	* Some investors noted that real export growth slowed to 4.5% yoy in April-May from 15.2% yoy in Q1 <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKIGYif95uLYfnVJBVw5yNxVnwdmhAnPwhYmobslSpA1de2jri7AADAXNnZorwJS5StRo94Nl4dO5VeY03i73DAlEWnS7BNGU6vr3nRsQJDbkXItpk5NMyIx-zNl3L_1xgmffw_p7JzRbXNjDf-DQpPwhb-N74icGuJ7o7J3f6w2sCzu6DeTmPwKnncv1-mhTHi95qDuv6M-Z0KtTqJwtGmortZmnevCaoeJwTqUtZenAWu89hCflInrfnqgTWdS4gjeDIRdZ7Bw7xU3h2J1Yiv4RgmpsddNXzQE1p_B6728P4EzwEoi4qk%26id=414d0c8f-c01f-4d36-9d48-ef7e22f7608a%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/aWK0xLx0v7wbh9hD-HXWngD8FFj3y-O2aLRr-t6frNI=452> ; absent more visible policy easing, they worry GDP growth could slow to below 4.5% yoy in coming quarters.  
*	* That said, lower oil prices could provide some near-term support to exports and industrial production. 

  

*	* Policy easing expectations remain muted despite softer domestic demand: Local clients expect policy support to remain reactive rather than proactive.  

*	* The shift from an вҖңaround 5%вҖқ real GDP growth target to a 4.5вҖ“5.0% range <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJOTx30MJTbgQMAXBzs7qP08-SkJBBR8sHpMq1Cmr1PAeB1F28vAADAHjjJdFrhKkZKcFcLM3qRZNh0Xvb1mCmt9WKx9Z3FiTlzVM3Em_9FAd0Rg4j5iu4WXaf2HLI_SM4UAC9dYNY3d_uX1fJQfSouKjLfonaueIzJChyvpQMHEduoryNYEh98DQR84P7M1rPGEmSB8gPJr-_8IX3vO2nUyo6Dujn0KWum_XZAke4JZihcpCPQVSvwLpOvV8lXowkacByBBaTCMlO2ZfKMIoeQg6iP38E0wA1L9eLLJHNbq577pkFxbuXN%26id=62f7667e-05db-4409-ab76-3db293128fdc%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/gl_xIOwAoo30KjykbkVJRd2QNC0slTvi3EwXsAcn4OI=452>  this year appears to give policymakers more room to respond only if downside risks intensify. 
*	* A key concern among investors is that, with exports still supporting headline growth, policymakers may feel less urgency to deliver stronger domestic demand support.  
*	*  Investors also noted that recent policy messaging has focused <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJg3JUiKWgADm0EgeMLEO4uvh90shC4Iq-47VQhCgXeMzaucoSnAADAdsQZT95Xphr6WO2jPBa2-NXLbXxovJ_-Sx-dSYtzTgR1jvJaclkBjwYQ_frgSFNzYJ_AB840Efru7xd88_9iP2bDKveP0-NrSsx3bbEEh83vB1g9o6FGhjsZPrPHjd1-EDURYbUABilcw8cBL5Jp8lFh3YZEqQg8r5c9nxX-emXmU8pamSBuxu4DeEeUEU4LGv4sH1bOiDH7kvn-Z0bvU_jVtW7orCUSgYoH_kEt4ZLqrysjVCJaiIsN-MLcP_Iu%26id=8ff4e488-acbe-4a2f-a380-65a41d0df88b%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/Jf7dMf2OQneRUZ5MSfWddnUBTbsNjWhDgXFSNCuyCsI=452>  more on medium- to long-term strategic priorities than near-term stimulus, suggesting limited upside from the July Politburo meeting beyond a potential acceleration of existing policy tools. 

  

*	* Consumption recovery concerns rise amid weak labor market 

*	* Nominal retail sales growth slowed to -0.6% yoy in May, the weakest print since December 2022, reinforcing local concerns about the timing of a consumption recovery. 
*	* A recent Qiushi article <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.qstheory.cn%2F20260618%2Fbdbb4aa234a84f6d882631f9e2071afa%2Fc.html/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/CeK2eXeJCp2mQnFwaS_K4KNN9Ro6TDMMItRZbnuOtU4=452>  drew clientsвҖҷ attention by calling for faster household balance-sheet  <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQK4QQa9SoR96H7CDs_3W_XmuV3qnBCvLsYwttQXNY5TGqV8BzgTAADAEPROibrEoDvoB6bP7hZVpw7Brra2oH_n0xeVcIVkbASWauGqB4rpQpLgEEt9GQHCjChZZ8LMXoBdFA_mbbNHkbe-UFizzxmSuqJFmhFUXW8v7bTEcpxUF2FC9DQSZvY1lY-EHunxpSyKs37a0FhvPg03ZCuqO02ipa76AB0Z7oHRWgJFGSP_Cm8nLQ4y5xcEy4OWROgfp7OftaGy9uyHRVlwO8cURfDBXkD65Qx1zx8O9jQ5nhsa9ER2sqDnUMVg%26id=a92481a3-a56d-4e6e-8365-28465eedefe3%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/wWq0MfTFqaxehXto-cHTdiiuH3kIzK_1s_IsZsOLzUY=452> repair and warning of a negative feedback loop between falling asset prices and consumer confidence.  
*	*  Local clients saw this as evidence that policymakers increasingly recognize the wealth-effect <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJLA2a1ali9v0VWQVp_QJlv5NJDSxAgvB-seJL9Wg5FtMYj2r0-AADAcNUVYPgw2o3atC8TKh9L2VC7nruMkgR2AcACpAIibV6NXK3AgVjF_46ECr8mUzhyf5Fl80F2Mlwb196_4F8xboB3tWnlUv3ni36OcOLLEiR3Hi6ImJMFlS4Sb6fV2p9bTvfx4pU146Zl68TrJQrnH2sa71N0xwRAs1u-TjL1JO9YxTfKgySVtGtKW7tkkUKY4O7YvNA6RVgryVTxPC0HJU31DMow6zIB9CAY8ZHZGKO3NCsUeyhQ-aiyuSMvKKOl%26id=ab529f05-fc21-40ae-95cd-beb0a5f5540e%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/VK3o8L4CFFI7EdSjYtkJ8oWSbP7RY7rw94SBoorqkyY=452>  channel of the property market behind cautious household behavior, but not as a signal of imminent broad-based stimulus. 

  

*	* Capital flows: cross-border investment rule changes raise implementation questions: among the most discussed topics in our meetings.  

*	* Clients focused on the timing, policy signal and implementation risk, especially whether conservative local enforcement could slow approvals and create short-term over-tightening in legitimate outbound activity. 
*	* Most clients did not view the measures as broad capital-control tightening, but rather as a sign of closer policy scrutiny on cross-border flows amid persistent overseas allocation demand and corporate globalization plans. 

  

*	* Investment growth: ample funding capacity met with local implementation constraints 

*	* Some clients expect spending to accelerate in H2 given ample funding capacity. 
*	* However, others noted that local officials may have limited incentives to expand investment aggressively amid still-strained local government finances. 
*	* This could keep project approval and execution cautious, leaving investment growth more dependent on local implementation incentives than headline financing capacity. 

  

*	* AI remains the key focus of investors: clients are heavily focused on its operational impact within the AI ecosystem <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIoA5oPXvHWBI0fnpgkHDC893MBqhCj4MV3tu89bBZjNpenZvcqAADAS06wcuwN-iTMmffgFXkHqMXzyzOOJ1oJGnGFW2Zr4g-72VzzD3iddGN2x8ic_INb2gTEl89gjYTHiE6G_cAWDXCAjhslJmhomhrniG5KDdqc0yGsuNVxkKjcGUCjpXNYfOMN_IJSGi8KBMBgLp_Kv0KKiBYDIxs9YSbJLzdJo_RWBrLoWNMVr-ANcIAgV8E8rLijtx_rM2nNe9S9wL2IGp0Pdgqh3JGiOAH2K3OW7_opCvephZeXTz8xaQsmIaMp%26id=857407bc-c9cb-4dea-adcb-ee14af093ed7%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/eBe80IuHEbLADKel3DQ6saeV5uTaRRR9vajxfKuc0zg=452>  and adoption across both traditional and new economy sectors. 

*	* The key metric to watch remains the upcoming 2Q US hyperscaler capex plans <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJFkT0GuQUnYdaSPecw2Sc-BL4zXRAOvDhqTNJmQv5EclQZvMrAAADAJHovv_WrNHMXTWCfV6YCEdKda52pf9PCk5eZZMYpu0LCGMmzlMIXGmLozt1BICrhAEkH6MpeFuAGi1cYpXLzkLBZdcDYo10WwUMB57Pm3SFLLf9Of_8YcjyCGFUZrkPw_jT67vkyEl-FnfvqmhKWdqHUdpM7kdT7zQFuaz1VT504C1kBO2gjt_-JPbYnt_nPjNBNd3Dfb3irHHVX-99QXsU40d5xslq9uVBE0HdE-evYGNcCd9ELwKegaXc-y6H7%26id=a6df8040-3cf2-4b0b-a7d3-a2ea8042108d%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/92PkyDoVtRhajGBlnNpskgLfDNsUYsiKgmLsjzY_zOg=452> , which serve as a pivotal driver of global market sentiment and directly dictate earnings growth <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLeRvoo-M-qqZqv9mU_Ac8LM5fyOhBnac8VygWfDQ0FoamlDAL9AADAyzp7dmOsFdMN_2jzY9QyOEcvoLyvUJxU6r7IdnHO6Rnd-QyaPW1DscIS_1IA1pnaVk1hm2CKsrBlrty7eDVqIKCV4u7rpNamZbSNyfu0DvvOduUt3oBpzx51SGZKQpDt3V_YfvydtAWpaY7ISEjNt3ksprqUQogTuhUMJXQV3sh-QvuMyluT6QN3czOwweysfxiyICrKSv35P2O3lzQ1inJlxt062JrXErGncxY_NSQdggWa_Vainbec-wpmEnOi%26id=000fdcc7-37b6-4eb1-84f3-e19f284ad022%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/zgJHHNDuCkVdD0TNnm4D-P-KKIhBh5WIlmzSvBd3cbA=452>  and cash flow outlooks for Chinese companies. 
*	*  Conversely, there are virtually no expectations for substantial demand-side stimulus by Beijing to drive near-term growth, making a turnaround for the broader H-share market challenging based on our strategy team's views <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ18UVSIr3NlTAWavd_-jQRzLDwJRA4vvNXJYzgfIVitJzBZ-YZAADAx0vrrFM9zeiPlHbSHuWwUXfaOVq_c9X7cKzsmNV5tRL6V9_T6fMq8Jc0rY6Y71V4u_cGwx5ndG75uPwx9Pvsu4R778AjxISO5iqEojmN7F8GFAaj3S7x-7oI5hv0BJqdeUSMfuZPOZd3-UWFxFLj8v2wvqQYu1dg8YY3ux-isauFeixaJgE0G6BGcat_0ZKXvvtUl4Matf3h93kOOaUzP0dD1nEtpuvM0Zk6SyS7TWdRNYH-oP3j7k-XnJsFHt6a%26id=f9bb219e-eeea-4b63-b11c-b7faefd5c9b4%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/lzoCSCEmVHZo1GG_pE8BJAdVT9CtMOEp4WrKq0g9zYE=452> .
