China: What do local clients think about the economy? Local marketing takeaways, June 2026 

  

	
30 June 2026 | 2:01PM HKT | Research | Economics 

	
	

  

	

Over the past week, we met with onshore clients in Beijing and Shanghai, including mutual funds, private equity firms, and asset managers from banks and insurers. Compared with two months ago <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLarzxZmRQuDgm7zakrtqGlC8pzFRDe4imbbEjuvRYjCOu8yprEAADAquShogNW5RRUoAAOf_sLXQzFiDG75GOvdIGgAPpNG08YnhKjGmHbliRWHKZoDeSeyCs3r8eHbqZNteJ6ylJXpWZj4rZ0jn3XMK-aDsz2OlA_1-Z0wEp5nsrcZuU6MGeOw1FXwUrravHfhlHseLr_0sUKj5vARmesMJsnWsu6UMBGJTYEb7Fcu5nDG4WbsfKtx5-HfvyE0HDW3XnAuTOOMEfulTJt7Co4NTaP1HQf5C-eOs3Kiu0CCMHbE85hL4A4%26id=53d1ab2f-8011-4de6-b62b-bcb33fb20cb2%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/Yr4vj7qYctl7lENiK0mDrFl7sUFc-9xS1tzOvXjrZRA=452> , local clients appeared more cautious on ChinaвҖҷs near-term growth momentum, reflecting weaker April-May activity data and a more bifurcated growth mix. Policy easing expectations also remain subdued, with investors generally expecting support to stay reactive rather than proactive unless downside risks intensify. Beyond growth, discussions focused on fragile consumer confidence amid labor-market pressures and the negative wealth effect from the ongoing property downturn; mixed local government incentives to expand investment despite ample fiscal funding; implementation risks around the new cross-border investment rules; and the AI capex outlook. 

  

Key Takeaways: 

  

1. More cautious on near-term growth momentum 

  

Following strong growth in Q1, weaker April <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLefwCWZGyat30xtFRiAnhtUFQmoRCHdJg7-1l7_KqvP8x_MvBJAADAFHS8nLL4uHrXWF0IWqfBK4ZvRUboCp3wLOuf-gL_3A599SPrxWsIRB9RWLo3Mbg-BhDm-tRECl6nembF5UbHohO-F8y4n87cXqLJwkkbpWUE7TBDG5JcgScN6Gt9ET9n4qVlk4-Vyj68j2Sk9V8UoVCTfOmc2H7TfdywploIjPgnXa0elFuyN5uzms0bNkWBt__Yw1DISv-S6qANLUS6IVsmde9ITGz1Vm7-21aX1zvvrhrhdd4UBQkFZw9Ka1w1%26id=d5344981-dd95-4589-97bc-2ed7ba44f145%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/Q7qUrLXfSEdfWFA4e5aZ8CahMIQznVi-_5u-4-ANsFo=452> -May <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKVUafMuFa8cOhckv-8aqW9w15WyhD6yn8eLxOqr4NgabWjPaUPAADAQVV36dOqgw4-EoK0px65Ufa1GOB6y7gU4SOFIDeTPNtK1Yb4DXNknjm7Msc_acFz8fn7-poDbhYATXR1QGT1mOQAo_nl6WaHkcpI__ZeSgw-W2z8TiRJyKb0psuQnroaP1oFSm65jmh1jLulPP6JTD637pFSfe8-gFvrhPZhj0Ia9ug2OZhOTY7fZem1-yJZAq-3e0xmx4v2Hlyy5fSErH829GE6PdbYVHcZNwHsCbJXfkHjp2Mpu3yD3pddTR50%26id=ea7a03d8-f960-4bc1-9770-73814b4e5507%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/PNdYAfleRWpRbmy0sKqIdglIPtJFp9Wdmk7FVuIaK6Y=452>  activity data have left onshore investors more cautious on ChinaвҖҷs near-term macro outlook. Growth has become more uneven: exports continue to support headline activity, but domestic demand has softened notably. Moreover, the boost from exports has not translated into a stronger labor market or meaningful profit improvement, limiting the pass-through from external demand to domestic growth. Some investors noted that real export growth slowed to 4.5% yoy in April-May from 15.2% yoy in Q1 <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQKIGYif95uLYfnVJBVw5yNxVnwdmhAnPwhYmobslSpA1de2jri7AADAXNnZorwJS5StRo94Nl4dO5VeY03i73DAlEWnS7BNGU6vr3nRsQJDbkXItpk5NMyIx-zNl3L_1xgmffw_p7JzRbXNjDf-DQpPwhb-N74icGuJ7o7J3f6w2sCzu6DeTmPwKnncv1-mhTHi95qDuv6M-Z0KtTqJwtGmortZmnevCaoeJwTqUtZenAWu89hCflInrfnqgTWdS4gjeDIRdZ7Bw7xU3h2J1Yiv4RgmpsddNXzQE1p_B6728P4EzwEoi4qk%26id=414d0c8f-c01f-4d36-9d48-ef7e22f7608a%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/q7-9cAkJ0-Z7JWvCwZF_-47wST-cCJEVxPcObCNa19E=452> ; absent more visible policy easing, they worry GDP growth could slow to below 4.5% yoy in coming quarters. That said, lower oil pricesвҖ”helped by rising market hopes for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz amid intensive US-Iran negotiations, notwithstanding tit-for-tat strikes <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.bbc.com%2Fnews%2Farticles%2Fckg590wqxwpo/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/_29s8f8aD3J5cLDtdeZIAZBqXVbRH95qwfyOpSsoBo4=452> вҖ”could provide some near-term support to exports and industrial production. 

  

2. Policy easing expectations remain muted despite softer domestic demand 

  

Onshore investors have relatively low expectations for incremental easing in the near term. The shift from an вҖңaround 5%вҖқ real GDP growth target to a 4.5вҖ“5.0% range <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJOTx30MJTbgQMAXBzs7qP08-SkJBBR8sHpMq1Cmr1PAeB1F28vAADAHjjJdFrhKkZKcFcLM3qRZNh0Xvb1mCmt9WKx9Z3FiTlzVM3Em_9FAd0Rg4j5iu4WXaf2HLI_SM4UAC9dYNY3d_uX1fJQfSouKjLfonaueIzJChyvpQMHEduoryNYEh98DQR84P7M1rPGEmSB8gPJr-_8IX3vO2nUyo6Dujn0KWum_XZAke4JZihcpCPQVSvwLpOvV8lXowkacByBBaTCMlO2ZfKMIoeQg6iP38E0wA1L9eLLJHNbq577pkFxbuXN%26id=62f7667e-05db-4409-ab76-3db293128fdc%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/3yIlKVROroh63IQQBQMdOlxX_0TSzy-mu0mt051QGs4=452>  this year appears to give policymakers more room to respond only if downside risks intensify. Local clients therefore expect policy support to remain reactive rather than proactive. A key concern among investors is that, with exports still supporting headline growth, policymakers may feel less urgency to deliver stronger domestic demand support. Investors also noted that recent policy messaging has focused <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJg3JUiKWgADm0EgeMLEO4uvh90shC4Iq-47VQhCgXeMzaucoSnAADAdsQZT95Xphr6WO2jPBa2-NXLbXxovJ_-Sx-dSYtzTgR1jvJaclkBjwYQ_frgSFNzYJ_AB840Efru7xd88_9iP2bDKveP0-NrSsx3bbEEh83vB1g9o6FGhjsZPrPHjd1-EDURYbUABilcw8cBL5Jp8lFh3YZEqQg8r5c9nxX-emXmU8pamSBuxu4DeEeUEU4LGv4sH1bOiDH7kvn-Z0bvU_jVtW7orCUSgYoH_kEt4ZLqrysjVCJaiIsN-MLcP_Iu%26id=8ff4e488-acbe-4a2f-a380-65a41d0df88b%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/oYrR2jtHrUeynS9NHbExbMa0Uj43NYIcNjnrxRGQyS8=452>  more on medium- to long-term strategic priorities than near-term stimulus, suggesting limited upside from the July Politburo meeting beyond a potential acceleration of existing policy tools. 

  

3. Consumption recovery concerns rise amid weak labor market 

  

Nominal retail sales growth slowed to -0.6% yoy in May, the weakest print since December 2022, reinforcing local concerns about the timing of a consumption recovery. Clients also highlighted rising labor market pressures <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQI3IA3ElNSU4ul2n2m9P4qwscC21xAenTYzDAfPe_c7_I58fvzaAADALIazy3dLR1AwyGgEa3nAJbj1lHRMdrd3hbDcq2U4XTzj_1E7jcbEksAoCzsopxIHHYg9FG_f8Ru4RFFqKhXIG7nxuPr0q-lRfLqNtZmhMkY5ip0lpHluhMrSncJXdFKZUn3cXcu1y_BJ2Njni8sUy8JFgPOOrVUDbtuzSXrPi-pqTLmMRnVe2F-_caTAmQ5O7q1dvqlxa5TF3hTCqx1Bkh9EB1pInwRhurNmmL4B8PkS1rVmMZ_mlf5tLQWLzO27%26id=306b76e9-ba68-47f3-8634-c83643f2d61d%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/gG1YONLSDaIwvDeRu8r3YYFLHLmfG8HkX930wSSvLkc=452> , including AI-related headwinds. They noted that several internet companies have started to reduce headcount, while college graduates face greater pressure as AI adoption weighs on entry-level white-collar jobs <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.cf40.org.cn%2Farticle%2F1%2F289268/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/-WKd_vwnE6VOKQH1arItPFgonuvNHxD_8I51KnaxAYo=452> . Against this backdrop, a recent Qiushi article <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.qstheory.cn%2F20260618%2Fbdbb4aa234a84f6d882631f9e2071afa%2Fc.html/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/eAkaeGPwlclpXU95xLuvTr3TKo2ucImgd4UCTGl_ZF8=452>  drew clientsвҖҷ attention by calling for faster household balance-sheet <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQK4QQa9SoR96H7CDs_3W_XmuV3qnBCvLsYwttQXNY5TGqV8BzgTAADAEPROibrEoDvoB6bP7hZVpw7Brra2oH_n0xeVcIVkbASWauGqB4rpQpLgEEt9GQHCjChZZ8LMXoBdFA_mbbNHkbe-UFizzxmSuqJFmhFUXW8v7bTEcpxUF2FC9DQSZvY1lY-EHunxpSyKs37a0FhvPg03ZCuqO02ipa76AB0Z7oHRWgJFGSP_Cm8nLQ4y5xcEy4OWROgfp7OftaGy9uyHRVlwO8cURfDBXkD65Qx1zx8O9jQ5nhsa9ER2sqDnUMVg%26id=a92481a3-a56d-4e6e-8365-28465eedefe3%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/dVkUedEb1wuWmu2TgD2JpGSGRtApKM_7faVYOKXfwVQ=452> repair and warning of a negative feedback loop between falling asset prices and consumer confidence. Local clients saw this as evidence that policymakers increasingly recognize the wealth-effect <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJLA2a1ali9v0VWQVp_QJlv5NJDSxAgvB-seJL9Wg5FtMYj2r0-AADAcNUVYPgw2o3atC8TKh9L2VC7nruMkgR2AcACpAIibV6NXK3AgVjF_46ECr8mUzhyf5Fl80F2Mlwb196_4F8xboB3tWnlUv3ni36OcOLLEiR3Hi6ImJMFlS4Sb6fV2p9bTvfx4pU146Zl68TrJQrnH2sa71N0xwRAs1u-TjL1JO9YxTfKgySVtGtKW7tkkUKY4O7YvNA6RVgryVTxPC0HJU31DMow6zIB9CAY8ZHZGKO3NCsUeyhQ-aiyuSMvKKOl%26id=ab529f05-fc21-40ae-95cd-beb0a5f5540e%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/Wavzgg50O_unV44O6SLZk4SAW3L-NrLDB8vnpJmvuPg=452>  channel of the property market behind cautious household behavior, but not as a signal of imminent broad-based stimulus. Its signaling value may also be more limited because the article appeared on the Qiushi website rather than in the print journal, implying a lower level of formal endorsement. 

  

4. Investment growth: ample funding capacity met with local implementation constraints 

  

After January-February activity data beat expectations, fiscal spending slowed, with limited evidence so far of a pickup in government expenditure <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIF3leAdYEVxuQ9aZcDv7cHPKzP-hDbk4YyQI5F1mkwWqa-oLTlAADAAiu1y8TCTX3edu9XWH_mucWfQPP03Eq6D4f5BvvySwkPR8IEuNpPgrrbSnqgVbgpy7P1yWlTB1NRGj60KGq7gCUFypCflvYP8Q-NLABI6sTRtgAlS8GZktVFlYm452B0SAhI-QjPUvdG93zQIlXHqnK3M56FV_wROnvIv9AdG-V31Y-KV8gn04hQTjoxhV75qzmtcXXgAwZ6KAyKX38oZaQGnVbsV6FR8wzM9SYadQDEpGw3m8cQS-ovOTFsq35W%26id=08f1b826-32f0-4f7b-b48b-0225336a1ff3%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/XJyFlNQW9LrBh-Bteb2JjNMAN_W1rbMelYy1TnI63Zs=452> . Some clients expect spending to accelerate in H2 given ample funding capacity, including RMB7.7tn of unspent government bond quota as of end-May, the RMB800bn policy bank new financing tool <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIWa5m41xXjK54kN7WDrAf7_bonWhB2_vACcm78HPbTjAZF1RZ3AADAUsAe_2Cp1Q9wQy7ReW6EgFA3WqiZGudZPqHilw-8uCkRYrcVVDFzKUVyw7gsN8iUTIUbAEUu4zwIrOy_WmgITzJvCN7wN1BfmQOndXI-KmwAccg9vUiv57fZpQCY-jn6vsibL83DHinP7I95p5hY25SavCj4LQeCmJFcbCNekr7bqhd78muWZqHN4HZ4tBFCrkDMEMd00q0Ws2vyMOtPfdcNhNaNZHKuYLcfv3_LG3AAVgrZxwSuJc28iEHEEqm9%26id=a8202d73-ae9f-4860-b06f-c9b7a5eb1f4b%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/IrpaoExvnIm4hz9XCVJgYQybCu5Oh0P0oN5AVeliECM=452>  available this year, and a RMB536bn year-on-year increase in outstanding fiscal deposits. However, others noted that local officials may have limited incentives to expand investment aggressively amid still-strained local government finances, the emphasis on the вҖңcorrect view of performanceвҖқ <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.scmp.com%2Fnews%2Fchina%2Fpolitics%2Farticle%2F3348184%2Fwhat-do-chinas-new-correct-view-rules-local-communist-party-officials-mean/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/CkLsvY08I7uweFBtoM8h8750HQj_7bTYSaryZtemRPI=452>  (жӯЈзЎ®ж”ҝз»©и§Ӯ), and the ongoing local government leadership reshuffle cycle <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.spp.gov.cn%2Fdj%2Fdjwj%2F202602%2Ft20260212_719766.shtml/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/KP3RnH5FcLJC8OCWFvKCmbFkdBnHssyhh5-zPMaIJAo=452>  (ең°ж–№ж”ҝеәңжҚўеұҠ). This could keep project approval and execution cautious, leaving investment growth more dependent on local implementation incentives than headline financing capacity. 

  

5. Capital flows: cross-border investment rule changes raise implementation questions 

  

On capital flows, ChinaвҖҷs recent cross-border investment rules <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.gov.cn%2Fzhengce%2Fcontent%2F202606%2Fcontent_7070755.htm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/weh4cO01AARjubztyTlpXS72HVenUTayTXfTRRfCwVg=452>  were among the most discussed topics in our meetings. Clients focused on the timing, policy signal and implementation risk, especially whether conservative local enforcement could slow approvals and create short-term over-tightening in legitimate outbound activity. They are also closely watching <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIFAPksPI5PE3DeCKISvZ-dZFDPwRDWHeT-wkb4rMuuyIo9oA_OAADA7mQ-ouzQtLm1pggtsdF9f4FbjM_SIyJFUOiuRn2VrqH8amRz7qPC4KmOOryOGem30VnFK6BqHAE--hDNz3Ba1tLC-mRk9SCOudkhDqe4_ZCMKt_MMAc49Pz1fBqJ4MnaL_2ajW0ihN_r4QGc4_kKq8vOAupsQ9djGUWTo6ka-9oBS5MxQ9BC9eFOn2vWshKxpzQFMb6Ibmg3dKxlvt_zZDjeUy1lgn6OCyxs3bI9AaERqH3TGMOGi0YQz6Ix_VQT%26id=e3a98fd4-ba0c-46a4-8145-ebc6e226c836%26utm_medium=sm/1/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/21lKLmj1ja99ok4RaUBLGF-CmWT4ho6t3B9ayysATkE=452>  detailed implementation rules for individuals, including how outbound investment channels and overseas asset allocation will be treated. Most clients did not view the measures as broad capital-control tightening, but rather as a sign of closer policy scrutiny on cross-border flows amid persistent overseas allocation demand and corporate globalization plans. 

  

6. AI remains the key focus of investors 

  

Driven by robust enthusiasm and evolving views on AI, clients are heavily focused on its operational impact within the AI ecosystem <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQIoA5oPXvHWBI0fnpgkHDC893MBqhCj4MV3tu89bBZjNpenZvcqAADAS06wcuwN-iTMmffgFXkHqMXzyzOOJ1oJGnGFW2Zr4g-72VzzD3iddGN2x8ic_INb2gTEl89gjYTHiE6G_cAWDXCAjhslJmhomhrniG5KDdqc0yGsuNVxkKjcGUCjpXNYfOMN_IJSGi8KBMBgLp_Kv0KKiBYDIxs9YSbJLzdJo_RWBrLoWNMVr-ANcIAgV8E8rLijtx_rM2nNe9S9wL2IGp0Pdgqh3JGiOAH2K3OW7_opCvephZeXTz8xaQsmIaMp%26id=857407bc-c9cb-4dea-adcb-ee14af093ed7%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/6bHhQoiWGXw6Hil-Ry_rl_7ZMG0UUnumoFUKgKSyIx8=452>  and adoption across both traditional and new economy sectors. Crucially, the key metric to watch remains the upcoming 2Q US hyperscaler capex plans <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJFkT0GuQUnYdaSPecw2Sc-BL4zXRAOvDhqTNJmQv5EclQZvMrAAADAJHovv_WrNHMXTWCfV6YCEdKda52pf9PCk5eZZMYpu0LCGMmzlMIXGmLozt1BICrhAEkH6MpeFuAGi1cYpXLzkLBZdcDYo10WwUMB57Pm3SFLLf9Of_8YcjyCGFUZrkPw_jT67vkyEl-FnfvqmhKWdqHUdpM7kdT7zQFuaz1VT504C1kBO2gjt_-JPbYnt_nPjNBNd3Dfb3irHHVX-99QXsU40d5xslq9uVBE0HdE-evYGNcCd9ELwKegaXc-y6H7%26id=a6df8040-3cf2-4b0b-a7d3-a2ea8042108d%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/PjunAJSs9pZ19JsVctCyBrHOeZ3uEO7MjgcLrX_Hu2U=452> , which serve as a pivotal driver of global market sentiment and directly dictate earnings growth <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQLeRvoo-M-qqZqv9mU_Ac8LM5fyOhBnac8VygWfDQ0FoamlDAL9AADAyzp7dmOsFdMN_2jzY9QyOEcvoLyvUJxU6r7IdnHO6Rnd-QyaPW1DscIS_1IA1pnaVk1hm2CKsrBlrty7eDVqIKCV4u7rpNamZbSNyfu0DvvOduUt3oBpzx51SGZKQpDt3V_YfvydtAWpaY7ISEjNt3ksprqUQogTuhUMJXQV3sh-QvuMyluT6QN3czOwweysfxiyICrKSv35P2O3lzQ1inJlxt062JrXErGncxY_NSQdggWa_Vainbec-wpmEnOi%26id=000fdcc7-37b6-4eb1-84f3-e19f284ad022%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/05xqMXLjAUYKKGF1KOsmvAIKqg4iROETVaQkj-eh-aI=452>  and cash flow outlooks for Chinese companies. Conversely, there are virtually no expectations for substantial demand-side stimulus by Beijing to drive near-term growth, making a turnaround for the broader H-share market challenging based on our strategy team's views <https://publishing.gs.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublishing.gs.com%2FtrustedLink%3Fopentoken=T1RLAQJ18UVSIr3NlTAWavd_-jQRzLDwJRA4vvNXJYzgfIVitJzBZ-YZAADAx0vrrFM9zeiPlHbSHuWwUXfaOVq_c9X7cKzsmNV5tRL6V9_T6fMq8Jc0rY6Y71V4u_cGwx5ndG75uPwx9Pvsu4R778AjxISO5iqEojmN7F8GFAaj3S7x-7oI5hv0BJqdeUSMfuZPOZd3-UWFxFLj8v2wvqQYu1dg8YY3ux-isauFeixaJgE0G6BGcat_0ZKXvvtUl4Matf3h93kOOaUzP0dD1nEtpuvM0Zk6SyS7TWdRNYH-oP3j7k-XnJsFHt6a%26id=f9bb219e-eeea-4b63-b11c-b7faefd5c9b4%26utm_medium=sm/2/0100019f18673a15-dd932575-412a-4ffd-ae68-fe0aa5180c3c-000000/YV4g6fexDPxHFLUdSur550FMJYqxBL7_m2T3YKCE1PE=452> .

<https://open.substack.com/pub/fundaai/p/researchkoreas-ai-investment-plan?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app> 
READ IN APP <https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ET-_!,w_36,c_scale,f_png,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack.com%2Ficon%2FLucideArrowUpRight%3Fv%3D4%26height%3D36%26fill%3Dnone%26stroke%3D%2523808080%26strokeWidth%3D2> 
 	

The South Korean government, along with Samsung and SK Group, co-announced an all-time high domestic industry investment commitment totaling 4,800 trillion KRW (USD 3.1 trillion) on the same day.

The primary incremental takeaway from this event is that Samsung and SK Group have pulled forward their respective mega investment projectsвҖ”originally slated for 2047 and 2045вҖ”to 2040 and 2033. However, even with this acceleration, the earliest completion date still falls 7 years from now. This indicates that in the coming years, we are still highly unlikely to see any unplanned capacity expansion.

Although official sources proclaimed a goal of doubling DRAM capacity within 5 years, a 15% CAGR over the next 5 years is far from sufficient to meet the booming demand driven by AI.

Furthermore, Micron raised its CapEx guidance last week, which, overlaid with this MondayвҖҷs Korea AI Investment Plan, leads us to believe that the former provides a short-term benefit to equipment stocks. At the same time, the latter will boost market sentiment. Among them, Applied Materials, which benefits from HBM capacity expansion, and ASML, which commands the critical EUV technology for advanced nodes, will be the primary beneficiaries.


Details of KoreaвҖҷs AI Investment Plan

Subscribed <https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9mdW5kYWFpLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MjA0MjIxODc4LCJzIjozMjgxMDExLCJmIjpmYWxzZSwidSI6MzAyOTUxNDQyLCJpYXQiOjE3ODI4MjA2NDMsImV4cCI6MjA5ODM5NjY0MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.Qleo43hZX_Zr_X23sFo5Y5JmJ7n63RRTyW4S7nUAZcc?> 

Memory

Investment Amount and Layout:

*	Samsung: Investing 56 trillion KRW in the Chungcheong region (Cheonan, Onyang) to build HBM wafer fabs; additionally building a new semiconductor fab in Gwangju, Honam region (total semiconductor investment in this region amounts to 400 trillion KRW).

*	SK Hynix: Plans to invest a total of approximately 1,100 trillion KRW in semiconductor supply expansion projects. This includes accelerating an investment of approximately 600 trillion KRW to expand next-generation DRAM capacity, investing approximately 100 trillion KRW to expand NAND capacity in Cheongju, and investing approximately 400 trillion KRW in the Southwest region to construct a new production base to address memory shortages.

Timeline:

*	The South Korean government has set a strategic objective to double South KoreaвҖҷs total DRAM production capacity within 5 years.

*	SK Hynix announced that it will significantly accelerate progress on the Yongin clusterвҖ”originally scheduled for completion in 2045вҖ”by 12 years, bringing its completion and production launch forward to 2033.

Semiconductor (Memory & Foundry)

Investment Amount and Layout: Samsung will invest 2,030 trillion KRW in core semiconductor clusters in the Seoul Metropolitan Area, such as the Pyeongtaek Campus and the Yongin National Industrial Park, forming the core of the overall plan.

Timeline: The South Korean government announced it will fully streamline administrative procedures and water supply engineering to accelerate SamsungвҖҷs construction and production at the Yongin National Industrial Park by 7 years, with completion expected by 2040.

AI Data Center

Investment Amount and Layout:

*	SK Group (led by SK Telecom): Plans to invest 1,000 trillion KRW.

*	Samsung Group (led by Samsung SDS): Constructing a large-scale AI data center in Solaseado, Haenam (South Jeolla Province); concurrently constructing a regional AI data center in Gumi, Yeongnam region.

Timeline: To be gradually implemented over the next 10 years. SK plans to deploy a scale of 5GW in the first phase (distributed nationwide in units of 0.5 to 1GW), and further expand to an AI data center network with a total scale of 15GW in the second phase.

Physical AI, Robotics & Components

Investment Amount and Layout: Samsung Group has separately allocated 60 trillion KRW in the Yeongnam region and 140 trillion KRW in the Chungcheong region (including semiconductors and components).

Content:

*	Humanoid Robots: Samsung Electronics will build a global smartphone manufacturing innovation hub and a mass-production line for humanoid robots in Gumi.

*	Advanced Electronic Components: Samsung Electro-Mechanics will build a state-of-the-art AI server packaging substrate production line in Sejong, and expand MLCC capacity in Busan.

*	Batteries: Samsung SDI will establish a global mother factory for next-generation batteries in Cheonan, and expand investments in all-solid-state batteries and energy storage systems (ESS) in Ulsan.

*	Displays: Samsung Display will build a production base for foldable screens and ultra-high-resolution micro-displays in Asan.


Ambitious Investment Plans Still Unable to Alleviate Short-to-Medium Term Memory Supply Shortages


Substantial Time Lags Exist in Physical Fabs Construction 
The high-profile announcements by South Korean companies to pull timelines forward by 12 years / 7 years look more like marketing slogans, as their completion dates still fall in 2033 / 2040 (which are still 7 or 14 years away). The doubling of DRAM capacity within 5 years mentioned in the press release is fundamentally unhelpful for the current supply shortage, as a 5-year doubling is only equivalent to a 15% CAGR.

This is a 10-Year вҖңPhased and Slow ReleaseвҖқ Plan 
SK Group emphasized that its core strategy is to execute more than 100 trillion KRW in domestic investment вҖңannuallyвҖқ over the next 10 years. This implies that capital and capacity will be brought online year-by-year in a phased rhythm over the next decade, which fails to resolve the pressing emergency of the front-loaded explosive demand for global AI servers and high-capacity memory at this very moment.

Stringent Requirements for Infrastructure (Water, Electricity, Land) 
The plan explicitly points out that, whether it is the 15GW giant data center network or the projected 400 trillion KRW new base in the Southwest region, the major prerequisite is that comprehensive conditions, including electricity, land, water resources, and manpower, must be met. Before the infrastructure is fully in place, the massive investment amounts on paper cannot be translated into actual production capacity.

Endogenous Memory Consumption Triggered by the Plan Itself 
While expanding capacity, South Korea is simultaneously constructing 15GW of global-class AI data centers, humanoid robot mass-production lines, and fully automated smart factories. During construction and operation, these physical AI infrastructures and computing networks will consume vast amounts of HBM and DRAM. Before the new capacity can flow to the external market, it will already have been heavily absorbed internally by these domestic expansion projects.

emails.subject: *CANCELED* JEF Access: CEG/VST/DUK Nuclear Update Potential with Alva Energy - July 6th at 1pm ET
emails.body:
WEBINAR

JEF Access: CEG/VST/DUK Nuclear Update Potential with Alva Energy - July 6th at 1pm ET

 

Speaker: 

James Krellenstein, Co-Founder & CEO вҖ“ Alva Energy

 

Host: 

Julien Dumoulin-Smith: US Power, Utilities, Midstream & Clean Energy вҖ“ Lead Analyst

 

Background

We are pleased to host our next nuclear discussion on the uprate potential with James Krellenstein, Co-Founder and CEO at Alva Energy, on Monday, July 6th at 1:00pm ET for an in-depth discussion. Krellenstein brings both industry-leading technical knowledge and direct experience advancing decarbonization and efficiency strategies. At Alva Energy, Krellenstein leads efforts to deliver advanced nuclear engineering solutions and project execution across a range of utility sites with a track record of successful uprate deployments. The conversation will examine current and emerging economics, regulatory approvals, project timelines, and the specific technological modifications involved in unlocking uprate capacity at existing nuclear units. Investors will gain actionable insights into the evolving pipeline of uprate opportunities, permitting hurdles, financing considerations, and timelines for various uprate types. This will be topical for investors in CEG, VST, DUK, ETR, PEG, TLN, CCJ and others evaluating the nuclear uprate potential. 

 

Date: Monday, July 6th, 2026

 

Time: 1:00 PM ET

Ronit Vardi,  Sr. Director of Strategic Marketing, IR and Amol Kalburge, IR. at 12:00pm ET. https://sig.zoom.us/j/91925920339?pwd=Fcfw8MObDAc5ScsagyZq6kuJgChJwv.1 <https://sig.zoom.us/j/91925920339?pwd=Fcfw8MObDAc5ScsagyZq6kuJgChJwv.1> ; Meeting ID: 919 2592 0339, Passcode: 806538

 

Susquehanna Research Summary

вҖў GLW | Mehdi Hosseini: POSCO Takeaways: Confirming GLW Key Role in CPO Supply Chain. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/fce7907d-d583-480f-9c88-cfb67e7874d7> 

вҖў MELI | Jamie Friedman: Call on World Cup Ad Tech Trends 7/1; FIDC Update. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/b776d72c-69a3-46ae-9db2-1f1ae1bda7da> 

вҖў Technology Hardware | Mehdi Hosseini: WFE Trending Toward $300B, Growth Not Fully Unit-Driven; Updating Est/PT. CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/71d4581a-41fa-4451-99cc-00a828b4ddae> 

 

Market Summary

вҖў Markets: Global equities are rebounding strongly into quarter-end, led by a recovery in mega-cap technology after recent underperformance. Technical indicators suggest large-cap technology has stabilized while broader market leadership continues to rotate.

вҖў AI Investment: Investor focus remains firmly on AI, with enthusiasm shifting from cloud-only infrastructure toward on-device AI capabilities. Advances in local AI processing (privacy, routing, summarization) could reduce reliance on hyperscale computing while creating new opportunities across the AI ecosystem.

вҖў Semiconductors: AI-driven demand continues to support memory, advanced packaging, and semiconductor supply chains. Positive pricing trends, tight HBM supply, and increased investment plans across Asia remain supportive for the sector.

вҖў Telecommunications: Satellite-based connectivity is emerging as a competitive threat to traditional wireless operators following new strategic partnerships and spectrum-related acquisitions. Companies with valuable satellite spectrum assets are increasingly viewed as strategic beneficiaries.

вҖў Technology Positioning: Hedge funds continued reducing exposure to mega-cap technology even as software fundamentals remain resilient, suggesting recent weakness has been driven more by positioning than deteriorating fundamentals.

вҖў Market Positioning: U.S. equity inflows remain exceptionally strong despite elevated valuations. Overall positioning has moderated toward neutral, although investor sentiment remains optimistic and leverage in certain areas remains elevated.

вҖў Volatility & Risk: Options markets indicate increased demand for downside protection, with negative market gamma suggesting heightened volatility potential. Record financing costs and concentrated positioning warrant caution despite constructive market momentum.

вҖў Regional Themes: Japan: AI-related investment continues to support growth, while persistent yen weakness reflects limited policy intervention; China: Policy remains focused on manufacturing and AI rather than consumer demand, favoring semiconductors while keeping consumer-linked sectors under pressure.

вҖў Sector Outlook: Software, semiconductors, transportation, financials, and AI infrastructure remain key leadership areas, while telecommunications face competitive disruption and energy has experienced notable fund outflows.

вҖў Near-Term Outlook: As markets enter Q3, attention shifts to earnings season. Seasonal trends, continued AI investment, and resilient economic expectations support a constructive near-term outlook, although elevated expectations, crowded positioning, and market concentration argue for selective risk management.

 

Market Positioning

вҖў U.S. equity fund inflows remain exceptionally strong.

вҖў Hedge funds continued reducing exposure to mega-cap technology while maintaining broader technology exposure.

вҖў Overall equity positioning has moderated toward neutral.

вҖў Investor sentiment remains optimistic despite recent volatility.

 

Flows & Technicals

вҖў Strongest quarterly performance for the S&P 500 in six years.

вҖў Market breadth has improved beneath the surface despite narrow leadership.

вҖў Historically, strong Q2 gains have often been followed by positive second-half performance.

вҖў Hyperscalers have underperformed recently, increasing the probability of catch-up performance if earnings remain supportive.

 

Risk Factors

вҖў Elevated market concentration remains a structural concern.

вҖў Negative market gamma and higher put activity point to increased volatility risk.

вҖў Record financing costs and leveraged positioning increase market fragility.

вҖў AI spending expectations remain elevated, leaving limited room for disappointment during earnings season.

 

Key Themes to Watch

вҖў Q2 earnings and AI spending commentary.

вҖў Hyperscaler capital expenditure outlook.

вҖў Semiconductor pricing and memory supply dynamics.

вҖў Satellite disruption in telecom.

вҖў Japan currency policy.

вҖў China policy support for domestic demand.

вҖў Market positioning and volatility into Q3.

 

Bottom Line

вҖў Constructive near-term market outlook: Strong AI investment, supportive seasonal trends, resilient earnings expectations, and renewed leadership from mega-cap technology continue to underpin equities.

вҖў AI remains the dominant investment theme, but leadership is broadening from hyperscale cloud infrastructure to memory, advanced packaging, on-device AI, and software monetization.

вҖў Preferred sectors: Semiconductors, AI infrastructure, software, select media/information services, and companies leveraged to AI capex remain the strongest areas of fundamental momentum.

вҖў Watch for rotation rather than a broad market reversal: Recent selling in mega-cap technology appears driven more by positioning than deteriorating fundamentals, leaving room for a catch-up rally during earnings season.

вҖў Key risks: Elevated valuations, crowded AI positioning, rising market concentration, satellite disruption in telecom, China consumption weakness, and increasing volatility from options positioning could drive periodic pullbacks.

вҖў Investment takeaway: Maintain an overweight stance on high-quality AI beneficiaries while remaining selective, as the market is likely to reward companies delivering tangible earnings and cash flow rather than AI narratives alone.

 

Sports

вҖў This season, 456 different MLB players have hit a home run. That is the highest total entering July in a season all-time (previous high: 453 in 2021).

вҖў 2018 Ohtani Topps Chrome Superfractor # 150 B9.5 rating: Nov. 2022: $139,200. June 2026: $2,562,229. That is a 1,740.68% ROI in 3 years and 7 months вҖ” both sales at Goldin.

вҖў A.J. DybantsaвҖҷs Draft Combine jersey sold at Sothebys for $20,480.

 

Corporate Access/Expert Calls                                                                     

TMT

This event is relevant for security vendors, particularly those that leverage a two-tier distribution model, including (but not limited to) CHKP, CRWD, CTXS, DDOG, DT, FFIV, FTNT, KNBE, NET, NEWR, NTNX, OKTA, PANW, PING, PSTG, QLYS, RPD, SAIL, S, SYMC, TENB, Trellix, VMW, VRNS and ZS. NVIDIA Corp 
2 documents in order of relevance
	
Former Distinguished Research Scientist at NVIDIA Believes Physical AI Faces Challenges but Sees Progress in Robotics Development <https://research.alpha-sense.com?alertEventId=e3030d56-95ed-41a9-b092-a165f545f22d51443865410&documentId=EC-1000000-222857&source=email_alert&category=main_doc> 	
NVDA  вҖў  Former  вҖў  Expert Insight  вҖў  2 pages  вҖў  30 Jun 2026 	
Call Summary 

1.	The conversation covered trends and challenges in physical AI and robotics, including data and model strategies, hardware and sensor considerations, and the competitive landscape among U.S., Chinese, and other global players. The expert discussed timelines for deployment, key bottlenecks, and the roles of major companies and suppliers in the evolving ecosystem.
2.	Former Distinguished Research Scientist with deep experience in physical AI, robotics, and model/data development, particularly at a leading global technology company.
3.	The expert believes the physical AI space is still in early stages, with significant technical challenges remaining before large-scale deployment is possible.
4.	They are cautiously optimistic about progress in the next 3-5 years for non-humanoid robots, but see humanoid deployment as taking longer due to hardware and generalization challenges.
5.	The expert sees the integrator or platform provider as potentially having the strongest moat in the future, but notes it is too early to be certain.
6.	Physical AI development is advancing along both data (egocentric, UMI, teleop) and model (VLA, world model) axes, with no clear winner yet among approaches.
7.	World models offer more generalization but struggle with complex, dexterous, or long-horizon tasks, while VLA models are more robust for specific applications.
8.	NVIDIA is heavily invested in egocentric data and world models, collaborating with various partners and benefiting from industry-wide adoption of its chips.
9.	Chinese companies are catching up quickly in robotics, especially in hardware and data collection, but U.S. companies currently lead in intelligence and model sophistication.
10.	The main bottlenecks to a 'ChatGPT moment' for robotics are generalization and robustness, with data quality and model co-development being critical.
11.	Deployment of wheel-based robots with grippers is expected within 12-18 months, while humanoid robots will take longer to scale due to hardware and reliability issues.

	
 	 
	
Senior Manager at AMD Sees Dynamic Shifts in AI Server Manufacturing and Supply Chain Trends <https://research.alpha-sense.com?alertEventId=e3030d56-95ed-41a9-b092-a165f545f22d51443865410&documentId=EC-1000000-226362&source=email_alert&category=main_doc> 	
NVDA  вҖў  Competitor  вҖў  Expert Insight  вҖў  2 pages  вҖў  29 Jun 2026 	
Call Summary 

1.	The conversation covered trends and challenges in AI server manufacturing, supply chain dynamics, and the evolving influence of hyperscalers and vendors. It also addressed emerging technologies such as co-packaged optics and liquid cooling, and their potential future impact on the ecosystem.
2.	Senior manager with approximately seven years of experience in server industry, specializing in new product introduction (NPI) and product operations for rack-scale AI systems, interfacing with OEMs, ODMs, and hyperscalers.
3.	The expert sees the current environment as highly dynamic, with rapid acceleration in technology deployment and significant supply chain challenges, but expects constraints to ease and capacity to ramp up significantly in the near term.
4.	They believe that while hyperscalers' bargaining power has diminished somewhat due to new technology demands, vendors like NVIDIA currently hold significant influence, though this may rebalance as competitors mature.
5.	Speed to market is the primary challenge, with compressed timelines putting strain on design, supply chain, and manufacturing processes.
6.	Supply chain shortages persist, especially in memory, storage, power infrastructure, and cables, but are expected to ease as investments and yields improve.
7.	There is no significant shift in manufacturing location or EMS/ODM usage, though some consolidation among ODMs may occur based on capability.
8.	NVIDIA currently dominates the GPU market with strong vendor relationships and early technology investments, but competitors are catching up, potentially leading to more balanced competition in the future.
9.	Technologies like co-packaged optics (CPO) and liquid cooling are progressing but are not yet commercially impactful; CPO is still in early stages and may influence GPU pricing in several years.

emails.subject: JPM US Market Intelligence | Morning Briefing: Futs flat post-rally; NFP Scenario Analysis, Consumer Cash Pile; D1 Positioning
emails.body:
JUNE 30, 2026


 

IDEAS & INSIGHTS вҖ“ IN BRIEF

Market Intelligence will not be publishing July 2 вҖ“ July 6.

*         QUICK THOUGHTS ON THE MKT вҖ“ We give color on bond yields / fx, credit, earnings preview, macro data, and an update on US / Iran

*         NFP SCENARIO ANALYSIS вҖ“ Feroli sees 125k jobs being added, versus the StreetвҖҷs estimate of 110k and 172k jobs in the prior print. For the unemployment rate (U.3) he sees 4.3% vs. 4.3% prior. For Average Hourly Earnings, he sees +0.2% MoM and +3.4% YoY.

*         MKT INTELвҖҷS CONSUMER CASH PILE NOTE вҖ“ our June update is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=RdT9eEp&sig=UUAcasTuKH33RMqvyKNb0eccdQR&v=2&isig=GovXamEVXwVu52grAiabLgqw94y&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b>  and we include snippets below.

*         DELTA-ONE POSITIONING & FLOWS вҖ“ Bram tells us that Asia equity futures bid; ETF investors favor Memory/Semis/Real Estate, cut Commodities/Crypto; HFs sell NDX, buy SPX/RTY futures; ETF rebalance noise persists

*         US MKT INTEL VIEW вҖ“ we maintain our Tactical Bullish call and update the Monetization Menu

*         JPM RESEARCH MID-YEAR OUTLOOK вҖ“ Samantha Azzarello summarizes each outlook into 2-3 bullet points with relevant charts; her note is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=HYabk8R&sig=OqyvQ9BafGekwHzb5HMf0XbgjeM&v=2&isig=XHKRSFVKqiLaKdfl4GHHVTDhQAy&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-s2g3jjf/GPS-5334406-0/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> . Copies of each outlook are linked in her note.

 

JPM MARKET INTEL MORNING UPDATES

*         SPX +0.1%, NDX +0.1%, RTY +0.1%. WTI +14bps at $70.85, NatGas +91bps to $3.21, UK NatGas +240bps to ВЈ1.0502, Gold +10bps to $4,020, Silver +79bps to $58.72, 10Y @ 4.365%, and VIX @ 17.59.

*         US: Futs are a touch above unchanged following yesterdayвҖҷs rally, with markets behaving as though period-end dynamics have now completed. With the major US holiday coming up, keep an eye on low liquidity moves in the region. Bond yields are flat to down 1bp with the curve flattening. USD is stronger, looking erase all of yesterdayвҖҷs losses. Cmdtys are stronger with crude flat into todayвҖҷs US / Iran discussions, Metals seeing a bid, and Ags outperforming the other cmdty complexes. In Eqys, Semis are mixed, Mag7 are flat, Cyclicals are generally leading Defensives with exceptions being Energy (lower) and Healthcare (higher). TodayвҖҷs macro data focus is on JOLTS

*         EU/UK: Major markets are all higher as the region benefits from both a stronger APAC and US session, Sx5E is leading country-level indices with AI themes and LT Momentum among the top plays. Regional bond yields are lower as curves bull steepen. Thematically, Momentum / Cyclicals area leading with Quality / Value lagging. APAC saw a stronger session with Korea / Taiwan leading. UKX +0.7%, SX5E +1.0%, SXXP +0.9%, DAX +1.0%. CSI +1.1%, HSI -0.6%, NKY +0.9%, ASX -0.5%, KOSPI +1.0%.

 

CATALYSTS TODAY (WEEK AHEAD <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=i2RjiTY&sig=1Xntxl5zDbhsMOXHQLaIDvSiMlM&v=2&isig=H0OzQjn6B8Alo8SD2MBt9WEeACp&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-d2hrca1/GPS-5142122-0.pdf/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> )

*         US MACRO DATA: FHFA House Price Index at 9am ET. MNI Chicago PMI at 9:45am ET. Conference Board Consumer Confidence, JOLTS Job Openings at 10am ET. Dallas Fed Services Activity at 10:30am ET.

*	US EARNINGS: NKE, STZ

*         GLOBAL MACRO DATA: (Japan) Housing Starts at 1am ET. (UK) GDP at 2am ET. (France) CPI/PPI at 2:45am ET. (Germany) Unemployment Change at 3:55am ET. (Germany) CPI EU Harmonized at 8am ET. (Canada) GDP at 8:30am ET. (Japan) Tankan Large Mfg Index at 7:50pm ET. (China) RatingDog China PMI Mfg at 9:45pm ET.



JPM MARKET INTEL EQUITY & MACRO NARRATIVE

Market Intelligence will not be publishing July 2 вҖ“ July 6.

Tech sell-off complete? After seeing two intra-month pullbacks, of ~7% and 5%, yesterdayвҖҷs rally feels like it has legs. We may have a Tech sector вҖҳEverything RallyвҖҷ. While Semis and Hardware have stretched positioning, the market jitters may first see a sharp rally followed by a rotation. The Fear / Greed monitor closed in the Extreme Fear region on Friday so there is room for stocks to move on sentiment, and positioning, before we are likely to have another consolidation period. While we have discussed SPX seasonality, NDXвҖҷs best month over the last 10 years has been July, averaging a 4.35% return with a 90% hit rate. August is also positive (+1.68%; 70% hit rate) and then September is typically the worst month of the year for the NDX. 

 



Source: CNN, here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=ubZ_L9l&sig=CsWONBaCM1omEfGMa5jTcjGDnFZ&v=2&url=https://www.cnn.com/markets/fear-and-greed> 

 

*         BOND YIELDS / FX вҖ“ The decline in bond yields may have been driven by period-end rebalancing which may have some more room to decline. Jay Barry sees yields being pressured higher over the medium-term, as the 10Y is 27bps below fair value, and likes 10s / 30s flatteners (full note is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=1wmsCxr&sig=EJtAu7xF54zJCJ6OmL5QFoGHJsa&v=2&isig=T1XhRVweqdbOvOuxRFArUmk6LoG&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/10ro6fgl/GPS-5350096-0/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> ). This is part of the American Exceptionalism theme which is also supportive of the Dollar which may see an ancillary bid from the spike in kinetic actions in the Middle East. Credit spreads have widened primarily due to capital markets issuance.

*         CREDIT вҖ“ IG / HY spreads have widened for 5 consecutive weeks which is the longest such streak since 2022. That said, вҖңwe are wider by not wideвҖқ per Nate Rosenbaum, given the recent widening is driven by issuance, some issuance of the unexpected variety.

*         EARNINGS PREVIEW (FactSet <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=Xc0gtqS&sig=TEwD05MFYziITJ54LTAiTchj9Jt&v=2&url=https://advantage.factset.com/hubfs/Website/Resources%2520Section/Research%2520Desk/Earnings%2520Insight/EarningsInsight_061826.pdf> ) вҖ“ For 26Q2, expectations are for revenue growth of12.1% (vs. 11.9% in 26Q1), earnings growth of 22.0% (vs. 28.8% in 26Q1), with 14.1% margins (vs. 14.8% in 26Q2). Look for MegaCap Tech / AI theme to drive these outcomes with Financials poised to being a material participant.

*         MACRO DATA вҖ“ TodayвҖҷs prints include Consumer Confidence and JOLTS where Feroli remains constructive on the economy, predicting 2.25% real GDP for 26Q2. For Consumer Confidence he forecasts 94.0 (vs. 94.4 survey; 93.1 prior). For JOLTS, he sees 7.400mm (vs. 7.300mm survey; 7.618mm prior). 

*         US / IRAN вҖ“ (i) talks in Qatar are ruled out by Iran, though both US and Iran are still sending delegations; (ii) Iran rejects both France and Oman being involved in de-mining the SoH; 

US MKT INTELвҖҷS NFP SCENARIO ANALYSIS

FeroliвҖҷs full NFP preview is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=GfdmPbf&sig=4Y1suTxoGPH0iDIlcdsWiwwZC45&v=2&isig=PLVBLn7uJK7iLpWm4zaztCaAK9w&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/nahe0bhi/GPS-5321939-0/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> . He sees 125k jobs being added, versus the StreetвҖҷs estimate of 110k and 172k jobs in the prior print. For the unemployment rate (U.3) he sees 4.3% vs. 4.3% prior. For Average Hourly Earnings, he sees +0.2% MoM and +3.4% YoY.

 

The following scenario analysis is NOT A PRODUCT OF JPM RESEARCH, this is a trading desk view from JPM US Market Intelligence. 

 

*         [5%] NFP prints above 160k. SPX loses 50bp to gains 1.5%

*         [25%] NFP prints between 130k вҖ“160k. SPX gains 75bp вҖ“ 1.25%

*         [40%] NFP prints between 100k вҖ“ 130k. SSPX gains 50bp вҖ“ 1%

*         [25%] NFP prints between 70k вҖ“ 100k. SPX loses 50bp вҖ“ 1.25%

*         [5%] NFP prints below 70k. SPX loses 1.25% вҖ“ 2%

*         WHAT ARE OPTIONS PRICING? For options expiring on July 2, 2026, using data from June 29, show a 1.2% implied move.

*         US MKT INTEL ON NFP вҖ“ With period-end rebalancing appearing to be completed, we look for the bullish trend to resume. Dip-buying behavior remains prevalent, which is why the outcomes here skew bullishly, especially when considering neutral aggregate positioning. When considering macro fundamentals, FeroliвҖҷs estimate reflects a resilient economy, and our latest Consumer Cash Pile (here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=RdT9eEp&sig=UUAcasTuKH33RMqvyKNb0eccdQR&v=2&isig=GovXamEVXwVu52grAiabLgqw94y&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> ) reflects ample levels of cash across the income brackets that drive the majority of US consumption with minimal signs of aggregate consumer stress. Combine that with a likely robust 26Q2 earnings season and that should drive stocks higher, ceteris paribus. For 26Q2 earnings season, FactSet expectations are for revenue growth of 12.1% (vs. 11.9% in 26Q1), earnings growth of 22.0% (vs. 28.8% in 26Q1), with 14.1% margins (vs. 14.8% in 26Q1, which was the second consecutive record).

 



Source: JP Morgan Economics

 

MKT INTELвҖҷS CONSUMER CASH PILE вҖ“ the full note is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=RdT9eEp&sig=UUAcasTuKH33RMqvyKNb0eccdQR&v=2&isig=GovXamEVXwVu52grAiabLgqw94y&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> 

*	Cash levels set another record of $22.9T compared to $14.8T pre-COVID, with Checking Accounts representing a significant portion of this, increasing from $1.53T to $6.51T
*	Cash Pile increases are driven by a combination of capital markets, higher interest rates on checking / saving / MMF accounts and lower tax rates.
*	Higher consumer cash is driving consumption. Using Retail Sales a proxy, the growth rate has roughly doubled post-COVID. Adding some numbers, Retail Sales averaged $420bn / month in 2014, $495bn / month in 2019, and now $747bn / month in YTD26 ($726bn / month in FY25). So, the growth rate in the five years preceding COVID was ~3.4% and is not closer to 6.8%.
*	Given the strength of the Consumer and Corporate sectors, this should aid the US in maintaining at / above trend real GDP growth. Since 22Q2, the US has averaged 2.6% real GDP with Headline CPI averaging 3.8% and Core CPI averaging 4.0% (QoQ, saar).
*	CONCLUSION вҖ“ (i) the US Consumer remains resilient; (ii) SPX earnings growth is more tied to nominal GDP growth than real growth; (iii) the economy appears to be accelerating its growth; (iv) the biggest risk is inflation / bond vol.

 

TOTAL CASH PILE BY INCOME PERCENTILE (YE19 = 100, INFLATION-ADJUSTED)



CHECKING ACCOUNTS



HOUSEHOLD DEBT SRVC PAYMENTS AS A %AGE OF DISPOSABLE INCOME



 



Source: FactSet, FRB, JP Morgan Asset Management, BEA

 

DELTA-ONE POSITIONING & FLOWS вҖ“ BramвҖҷs full note is here <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13509753&ad=4534211449&h=i0qYHQx&sig=Zu9eNWzUi2QRuqkAC1L7AhtSe2m&v=2&isig=03PHeAJUsgUaBXp8zzZhQeCMqnR&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/mo7bfv86/GPS-5351534-0/9682cb57-7a91-4940-8b05-b790f48a202b> 

*         Last weekвҖҷs flows leaned risk-on: equities saw above-average inflows and Memory/Semis remained the dominant equity theme, while commodities/energy saw meaningful outflows, suggesting fading geopolitical hedging demand. Asian equities saw large futures buying, including Nikkei 225 (~$7.3Bn, 3z), KOSPI 200 (~$8.5Bn, 4z), and SET 50 (~$1.4Bn, 4.4z). AI demand could be seen in strong inflows to DRAM (memory) and levered semis exposure (both single stock and sector-level), alongside the strong NKY/KOSPI futures buying, though thematic AI ETFs saw outflows.

*         ETF flows remained noisy due to CIBs (custom in-kind basket rebalances). Last week featured both (1) the redemption leg of the prior weekвҖҷs CIB-driven creations, and (2) the creation leg for funds rebalancing around the Russell Reconstitution (e.g. VONG, VONV, VTWO, IWD).

*         ETF Flows: Equities recorded above-average inflows last week ($33.9Bn, 0.6z), Fixed Income was near-average ($11.6Bn, 0.2z), while Commodities (-$3.7Bn, -2z) and Currency/Multi-asset (-$1.6Bn, -1.4z) saw notable outflows. Separately, we saw a >$45Bn switch from VOO into IVV, reversing the shift two weeks earlier.

*         Regionally, the US ($25.3Bn, 0.5z) and International DM ($8.1Bn, 0.5z) drove most equity inflows. Latam outflows continued (-1.1z, 9th straight week) as did China (-1.4z, 6th week), though India recorded ~1z inflows.

*         In equity sectors, Energy saw sharp outflows (-$1.7Bn, -2.6z) while Real Estate logged its largest weekly inflow in 5Y ($1.3Bn, 3.6z). Investors also rotated out of Discretionary/Materials and into Health Care/Tech (led by ~$1Bn into 3x semis fund SOXL)/Financials/Utilities.

*         Equity factor flows were distorted by CIBs, with strong inflows to Momentum (2.8z), Multi-factor and Dividend, and outflows from Value (-1.6z). Single stock ETFs saw large inflows ($4.2Bn, 4.3z) led by ~$1.8Bn into levered NVDA and ~$0.7Bn into SNDK. The memory theme continued to dominate Thematic flows (DRAM/RAM +$3.7Bn), but ex-memory, thematics saw outflows, including from AI, Space and Quantum computing themes. Call writing and defined outcome ETFs each saw ~1z inflows.

*         In Fixed Income, HY Corporates, short-term govвҖҷt bonds, Munis, Inflation, and Agg funds each saw ~1z inflows last week, while Loans, medium-term govвҖҷt bonds, and Mortgages each saw ~1z outflows.

*         Commodities saw broad-based outflows, led by Precious Metals (-$3Bn, -1.5z; mostly gold) and Ags (-1.8z).

*         Crypto ETFs (-$1.9Bn, -1.4z) posted a 7th consecutive week of outflows as Bitcoin fell through $60k.

*         Futures Flows: Beyond the Asia equity buying noted above, last week we saw large net buying (>1.5z) in S&P MidCap 400, UST 10y, SONIA and Copper futures, and large net selling (<-1.5z) in ASX 200, CSI 300, Canada 10y, Wheat and USD/CNH futures.

*         CTAs likely trimmed SPX/NDX longs as the 1M momentum signal was breached last week, but remain significantly long US & global equities. They likely bought US/EMEA rates and sold base metals w/w.

*         CFTC Positioning: Asset Managers sold NDX/RTY and bought UST 2y and Long Bond futures (~2z). Leveraged Funds significantly cut shorts in SPX (2.9z) and RTY (2.5z) but added to NDX shorts (-2.5z). Managed Money sold Oil and Ags w/w. Speculators (non-commercial) sharply cut S&P 500 shorts (+4z).

 

US MKT INTEL VIEW

We maintain our Tactical Bullish call despite the potential for some additional choppiness into month-end from both period-end rebalancing and the US / Iran conflict. We look for markets to stabilize and trade higher into earnings which unofficially kick off on July 14. A few thoughts followed by the Monetization Menu and the balance of the note.

 

*         NEW MONETIZATION MENU вҖ“ We think it makes sense to maintain the Tech / Cyclicals barbell while adding Healthcare exposure as low correlated long. Within Tech, we like AI, Memory, and Semis despite the recent pullback and elevated vol regime, so we would consider adding some hedges from other parts of Tech or factor hedges such as Beta / Momentum. Regarding Cyclicals, a combination of Airlines, Banks, Homebuilders, Retailers, and Transports; we would avoid Autos given USMCA risk. In Healthcare, we like broad-based exposure across the sector including Biotech, MedTech, and Pharma. Internationally, we Korea, parts of Latam, and Taiwan and would consider hedging with Indian Equities. For book hedges, consider SPX puts with July expiry.

*         DELTA-ONE TRADE IDEAS (Manish Sinha, June 26) вҖ“ (i) Short Beta (JPBPURE Index) and Momentum (JPMPURE Index) factors to hedge alpha dispersion risk and to play tightening financial conditions. (ii) Short Residual Vol aka ResVol (JPRVPURE Index) to hedge a hawkish Fed and worsening liquidity; (iii) Short Low Profitability Crowding (JPLPCRW2 Index) as a crowding hedge for AI-heavy exposure; (iv) +Long Dispersion Laggards (JPDISPL Index) vs. -Short Dispersion Winners (JPDISPW Index) to play a dispersion reversal.

*         OLD MONETIZATION MENU (JUNE 23) вҖ“ Unchanged WoW, we would like to return to the Tech and Cyclical barbell but stay nimble on the rate-sensitive or Hormuz reopening trades: stay long in the global AI theme with heavier weight on US vs. Asia. We do think the initial вҖңeverything rallyвҖқ should benefit Airlines, Housing and Retail but historical patterns reflect that the rally could be short-lived and more vulnerable to the shifting headline volatility. We like a tactical long in Financials into the July earnings season and expect it to catch up with the broader Cyclicals, as strong fundamental growth remains intact.

New Research


Ad Agencies - five themes from Cannes <https://specialistsales.cmail19.com/t/d-l-wluhujd-hjyuihouk-r/> 
The Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity took place last week, with 500 speakers sharing their views and visions for the future of the marketing industry. We highlight several themes that emerged from the presentations, with AI taking centre stage in topics that spanned: humans vs machines, the two-audience problem and the merits of effectiveness vs efficiency. Meanwhile, the growing importance of Creators in marketing briefs was another salient theme, as was the evolving role of agencies in the age of AI. We elaborate on these topics in the short email linked below and present our analysis of the Cannes Lions Grand Prix winners by holding group.

Software & Internet


Anthropic - flexing pricing power over Amazon (Neutral)
The model lab has renegotiated a key part of its Amazon partnership, shifting how Amazon pays to use Anthropic's models.  Starting next year, Amazon will be charged based on tokens processed rather than compute hours - a change that could materially raise Amazon's costs. The shift has already prompted some Amazon engineers to "distill" Anthropic models to cut costs ahead of the new token-based pricing taking effect. Amazon relies on Anthropic to power a range of their products, including Alexa for shopping, the Kiro coding tool, and the Quick workspace assistant - they will be evaluating cheaper alternatives, including their in-house Nova models. 

Meta (Buy) - restricting usage of Claude and Code over distillation fears 
Meta is clamping down on how engineers in its applied AI engineering division use Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex, according to internal guidelines reviewed by The Information. The central worry is that outputs from these rival models could inadvertently seep into Meta's own training data вҖ” a form of "distillation" that could violate the tools' usage agreements and trigger, in the words of one internal memo, "serious escalations with partner companies.". The platform has drawn a sharp line since May, prohibiting the use of external model outputs to test Meta's models, using AI to hunt for bugs, or placing any AI-generated material inside infrastructure containers that the in-house model being tested can access. Meta is on track to spend billions of dollars on internal AI use alone this year. Having aggressively pushed AI adoption internally, it's now reining in escalating costs - including by capping employees' token usage and shifting development work onto MetaCode to reduce reliance on expensive external tools. 

AI regulation - Sen. Warner drafts first AI agent regulation bill
Senator Mark Warner will unveil draft legislation regulating the AI agent ecosystem. The bill mandates a strict "duty of loyalty," barring agents from favouring commercial partners over users. Crucially for the big tech/internet platforms, it prohibits networks with over 50m users from blocking registered 3P agents, potentially threatening walled gardens and human-driven ad monetisation. The FTC will oversee registration alongside NIST interoperability standards. While near-term passage is highly unlikely ahead of the midterms, this establishes Washington's first structural regulatory framework for the rapidly expanding agentic AI economy.

Uber (Buy) вҖ“ Robotaxi pilot in Phoenix with Waymo ended
Waymo AVs are no longer available via the Uber app in Phoenix, vehicles will remain in use to make deliveries with DoorDash. Phoenix was UberвҖҷs first pilot market with Waymo and was very limited in deployment (low teen number of AVs that were available on the Uber app versus Waymo having 500 AVs on its own app). As it stands the partnership between the two in both Austin and Atlanta still stand. While the stock u/p on this yday, it shouldnвҖҷt be a major surprise given most were not expecting this particular agreement to last very long given how small it was in scale. Tech Hardware


Nvidia (Buy) вҖ“ reports re Rubin Ultra 2-die transition are not new 
Reports are resurfacing earlier discussions from March this year that indicate NVDA is expected to cancel the previously discussed 4-die Rubin Ultra configuration, reverting to a 2-die design. This is due to manufacturing execution concerns, particularly around CoWos packaging limitations. The proposed transition to a glass-core substrate technology is viewed as a solution offering lower warpage, lower signal loss and larger panel sizing helping NVDA towards its targeted performance roadmap. Importantly, the reports reiterate information that first emerged in March this year, which highlighted a potential reduction from a 4-die to 2-die architecture. This is not a material new development. importantly this is not new news. The market has been aware from earlier reports in March that NVDA was evaluating this configuration move. The key consideration for shareholders remains unchanged, which is whether the Rubin Ultra can maintain a performance advantage versus its competitors. Currently, no published performance statistics exist for Rubin Ultra, nor for any of the rival programs against which it will be ultimately judged. The debate should remain focused on delivered performance, and not specific die count employed.

Qualcomm (n/r) - ByteDance taps Qualcomm for custom AI CPU
ByteDance aims to tape out its next-gen CPU by early 2027 to power its AI models, partnering with Qualcomm to secure heavily constrained TSMC foundry and CoWoS packaging capacity. This helps validate QCOMвҖҷs nascent datacenter custom silicon unit, closely following its Meta design win. It underscores the rising need for high-performance CPUs in complex agentic AI orchestration, while confirming that TSMCвҖҷs advanced packaging (and memory) remains the key bottleneck.

Super Micro (n/r) - Taiwan offices raided in widening Nvidia chip smuggling probe
Taiwanese authorities raided the local offices of SMCI and key distributors as part of an escalating probe into the illegal smuggling of NVDA chips to China. The raids follow March's US DOJ indictment of SMCI affiliates for allegedly orchestrating a $2.5bn smuggling ring via shell companies. In response, Taiwan is moving to explicitly criminalise AI chip exports to align with US restrictions.SMCI shares dropped 8%, reflecting the severe, persistent headline risk dogging the name. While underlying AI server demand remains robust, this compounding compliance overhang acts as a hard ceiling on SMCIвҖҷs multiple. The constant drumbeat of export control violations and regulatory scrutiny makes the stock fairly toxic for investors.


Media


Comcast (Neutral) - Company split sets up new Comcast and NBCU for M&A despite management commentary to the contrary.
Pre market yesterday, Comcast announced its intention to split the company in two - essentially seperating broadband from the broadcast/studio business. New Comcast would be a good partner for Charter, and from our recent bus tour, we know that Charter management believes a deal could receive regulatory approval. Synergies would be very significant вҖ“ the combination would essentially create a national footprint, advertising and network management would be more efficient, there would be major cost savings and move churn would essentially be eliminated.  The Trump administration is constantly lampooned by NBC but that will now be a separate company.  Meanwhile, NBCU could also be an attractive partner, although current regulations prevent the ownership of two broadcast networks, limiting potential buyers to those companies without one, e.g. Netflix, Apple, Amazon or Google.  The spin off is set to take place in about a year and will be tax free while new Comcast will retain a 19.9% stake in NBCU to be monetised within 12 months.  There are additional reports today that NBCU is looking to invest in new franchises and potentially even video games although exact details are scarce. Comcast President and CEO Mike Cavanagh will head up NBC Universal while former Comcast CFO Michael Angelakis will return to head up new Comcast.  The existing share structure will be maintained, through which the Roberts family holds a 1/3 undilutable voting stake. Both companies will have investment grade balance sheets while the dividend policy is yet to be decided upon.

emails.subject: JPM International Market Intelligence | Morning Briefing: 20 Seconds. MKT INTEL VIEWS. From fear to green. Regime Shifts and Record US Consumer Cash Pile. Take on Korean AI Investment. Desk on China Tech. EU Bulls. Cyber for Banks
emails.body:
JUNE 30, 2026

 

20 SECONDS:

*         From extreme fear to green 

*         BurnhamвҖҷs speech was relatively policy lite 

*         The global macro regime shifts back to recovery/expansion as lending picks up and DrewвҖҷs prop US Consumer Cash sets a new record in 1Q26

*         Korean $3.1tril AI investment is moving SPEs and capex plays (see D1 basket JPG1KCPX Index for thematic beta). Jay Kwon estimates 60-70% allocated to front-end wafer spend and 20-30% for infra + cleanroom. 

*         China Tech is doing so well вҖ“ no, not KWEB but KSTR 

*         JPM Analysts upgraded EU Index targets вҖ“ most bullish on Street now. They also tell us that Cyber is bigger than Credit risk for Global Banks and refresh forecasts for EU Oil Services

 

 

MKT INTEL VIEWS: Technicals likely helping the last trading session of the quarter, Most markets in the green with Tech/AI bottlenecks leading. USDJPY continues to march higher вҖ“ but amid broad-based dollar strength - while Gold is breaking below $4000/oz. Beyond emphasis on devolution and mentions of greater public control of utilities, social housing and a вҖңbuy BritishвҖқ procurement agenda (all known in advance), BurnhamвҖҷs speech was relatively policy lite.

 



 

With a) reopening of the SoH; b) normalization in business sentiment from last yearвҖҷs trade war shock; and c) supportive financial conditions, the view is that global macro regime should shift back to recovery/expansion <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=uYL7AQN&sig=U9FtrnJwRfkLI01AGOBPYDlGRpa&v=2&isig=EfYAtImTlLItBa7rpWPTgoUTjmh&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/4d110166/GPS-5339502-0.pdf/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014>  (from slowdown/stagflation). 

 

Incoming releases support this view: A consumer led 1.3pts increase in EC sentiment indicator <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=jq6aiUb&sig=O5kweT550PYYNG6z0uRwKJqE53y&v=2&isig=4s3Y4dkqVFYN62g7qIGOottFvg7&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-i2690rq/GPS-5351237-0/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014> ; the  3rd straight strong gain in JapanвҖҷs May retail sales <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=X_1PCwu&sig=QLEB3V6dwmrq1TNEZ4vVTugU6Wq&v=2&isig=VFEwiNw0zcV5bDCDPnlrVWjhCaG&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/b86j39u1/GPS-5350720-0/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014> ;  positive surprise in China PMIs with internals pointing a easing price pressures and solid trade balance in June; and rebound in DM bank lending to businesses, which likely points to a broadening upturn in investment spending. US bank lending accelerated to a double-digit annual rate in the three months through May, and EA data released yesterday morning showed bank lending to non-financial corporates picked up to 4%oya  <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=PMysjPh&sig=Hiv6n9XHsLClTgrYHw5gTUXiS4O&v=2&isig=UGiIgKYzpmSjSrS51HKbFivSJon&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-q1gnpfq/GPS-5351126-0/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014> last month. 

 

Dulcis in fundo, DrewвҖҷs Consumer Cash Pile <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=RdT9eEp&sig=3YqWopsqEHMYBu22feLl13dH4nq&v=2&isig=9QAGRirvXPL5B7BNd9ndsrnua60&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/t1b49b5u/GPS-5351659-0.pdf/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014>  set a new record <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=NMCKrum&sig=XOPDqugVYBEixDnO9EEncIGPBDw&v=2&url=https://www.jpmm.com/research/content/GPS-5351659-0.pdf>  in 1Q26 of $22.9T, up from $14.8T in 4Q19. The increase in consumer cash is the basis for elevated spending patterns and thus a primary driver for stronger than trend real GDP.  Of note, all income brackets except for the Bottom 20th percentile have more cash than 2019 even when adjusting for inflation, ranging from 8% to 29% more cash, in real terms

 



 



 



 

This am, Korean $3.1tril AI investment is moving SPEs and capex plays (see D1 basket JPG1KCPX Index for thematic beta). Jay Kwon <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=XO0t8Yc&sig=LFUut0mB2FLsGFvwOIlMxVKCMn7&v=2&isig=VQZdZzIi1BTt3hWXNw1H1iQ6pnM&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/lt4mann8/GPS-5350909-0/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014>   estimate 60-70% allocated to front-end wafer spend and 20-30% for infra + cleanroom. Long-term +ve for SPE, Power Infra, and Semi EPC suppliers, and favorable for memory makers in the mid-long run who can eventually enjoy $12-20tril revenue opportunity. We should hear more details on specific timelines for investment (fab and investment plan in multiple stages and timeline) in the upcoming result season and follow-up corporate events

 



 

It is another great week for China tech. No, not KWEB but KSTR because this captures policy-backed multi-year expansion aimed at developing a domestic AI supply chain while leaving aside hyperscalers and names more exposed to consumer headwinds. 

 

The view from the desk, is that this theme will likely remain in focus. Flora (APAC Sales) thinks that over the next 3вҖ“5 years, the companies most likely to emerge as long-term China tech giants will be concentrated in five high-barrier tracks: upstream equipment/materials, the wafer-fabrication вҖңfoundation,вҖқ domestically memory, advanced packaging & testing, and compute (CPU/AI chips). Pure consumer-electronics general-purpose chip design is suffering severe вҖңinvolutionвҖқ (homogenized competition), making it hard for a mega-cap leader to break out.  Lmk if you want the full email. 

 

KSTR vs KWEB



 



 



 



 



 

IDEAS & INSIGHTS вҖ“ IN BRIEF 

*         Equity Strategy <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=15PIaSc&sig=BStI9KkvYGpsWWKaFwSZZQc8oiO&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/sb1r06oj/GPS-5345465-0/cedc0488-9e94-4362-996f-92d2d9e288a5> : Look for further upside in Europe in 2H - updating our targets and positioning. We remain constructive for 2H, are staying with Cyclical trades and look for certain rotations compared to what was an extremely narrow leadership in Q2. We update the EU Index targets in this report, looking for further upside from here, ranging from 5-10%. We believe Eurozone earnings growth is stepping up this year, after 3 flattish years, at index level. It is encouraging that since the Iran conflict started, 2026 EPS projections for Europe have in fact moved higher. The support in 2H is likely to be a continued rebound in activity indicators such as PMIs, along with firmer global growth. We find that global investors donвҖҷt have much risk in Europe. In terms of positioning, we keep positive call on Semis, Industrials and Mining. Banks should also trade well, as they are leveraged to PMIs and to credit growth. If Eurozone PMIs rebound, as we expect in 2H, then Defensives should generally not be favoured, and Energy would have further downside if oil price moves lower. There is no valuation cushion between Energy stocks and Brent. We remain cautious on Business Services, Software and Media. Also, we are still not excited about Defense, having argued since last September to reduce it, post a strong past run.

*         Global Banks <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=Zpu5t_R&sig=2uOX6Wk0mBICnmulmVRRmOyH19A&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/etf1k97b/GPS-5342507-0/cedc0488-9e94-4362-996f-92d2d9e288a5> : Mythos-class era: Cybersecurity a bigger concern than credit risk. Cybersecurity in Banks is currently one of the biggest undiscounted risks not reflected in Bank valuations in our view, partly due to the limited disclosure available which leads to a lack of comparability between banks on their preparedness or lack thereof to deal with emerging cybersecurity risk (pre-event and post-event processes), driven by advanced AI capabilities such as Frontier Models. While there is significant regulatory focus on capital ratios, we believe looking at cybersecurity risk through the lens of the capital framework is not the best approach. In our view, there should be an increased focus on cybersecurity driven liquidity risk and this should be tested through increased infrastructure resilience testing and through a deposit-run liquidity haircut test as we believe liquidity, not credit risk, could be the main trigger in a scenario of a potential banking crisis with social media likely to trigger unprecedented volatility in deposit flows as we witnessed at Credit Suisse. In this changing environment, we are likely to see differentiation between players. We believe a degree of premium for US GSIBs (currently at avg. 12.5x P/E 2028E, BBG consensus) compared to their global counterparts (European Banks at 9x P/E 2028E and Japanese megabanks at 12x P/E 2028E) could be justified due to lower CoE as the market factors-in better cyber risk preparedness through higher absolute scalable tech spending and early access to latest frontier models

*         European Oil Services <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=2NE2vhk&sig=Gqkuo8EfksZMH8u4QQSUlwsUisv&v=2&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/3i0i91qh/GPS-5348481-0/85f68105-5c9b-438d-a220-768b664ded32>  Revisiting Sector Fundamentals and Valuation; Remain OW Saipem and Technip Energies. We refresh our forecasts to incorporate QTD announced orders, reassess the forward trajectory for margins and order intake across our coverage, and update our valuation framework to reflect our revised outlook. The group has delivered a strong start to 2026, up ~50% YTD on average, supported by robust order announcements, net cash balance sheets across our coverage, and solid revenue visibility, with 2026E revenues 80-90% covered and 2027E coverage averaging ~60%, above five-year medians. In our view, the OFS subsector has also benefitted this year given its position as a relative safe haven for energy investors in a turbulent, energy macro environment with volatile commodity price swings. While valuation multiples now screen close to the upper end of historical ranges across our universe, we view the sectorвҖҷs re-rating as broadly defensible given strong year-to-date order momentum and balance sheet strength. 

*         TODAYвҖҷS CATALYSTS: China Mfg & Non-Mfg PMI; EA Regional CPIs; GE Unemployment, UK GDP; US Conference Board Consumer Confidence 

 

IDEAS & INSIGHTS вҖ“ IN DETAILS 

MONETIZATION: We are bullish on Global Equities

-       Macro & thematics still impose the relative regional preference (long US & Asia вҖ“ JA, SK & TW - vs EU). The US is overheating; Europe is in slow recovery from stagflation & Asia has pockets of both. Further, secular themes carry less weight within EU Equities.  That said, we have been tactically bullish on EU Equities <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=SlDmj3a&sig=CxCNimjQv8mx0WNYsF6Cqiq1Bkg&v=2&isig=BfmQqIM9veyRWQrA9mZajEmoe7g&url=https://markets.jpmorgan.com/research/email/scx/-22cvp0m/GPS-5291960-0.pdf/3e061ce0-7d23-4f06-9f93-488eb9357014>  since early May on positioning, underperformance and regime switch on SoH reopening. We continue to play this via options given attractive vol levels and extended outperformance in the near-term. 

-       LONGS

o   Supercycle themes - AI bottlenecks/facilitators, Cyber, Physical AI, Clean Energy (EU Nuclear, EU Renewables, Asia ESS), Modern Warfare and Critical Minerals - are key Longs within a portfolio of International Equities. We continue to think that a multi-week factor rotation is still in the cards, and this could keep the complex choppy/vulnerable to drawdowns. Hence, we stay hedged via options on momentum, but we are minded to buying dips. 

o   Cyclicals but with a secular tailwind. EU & JA Banks, EU Industrials & JA Machinery; GE Infra. EU Miners are looking attractive right now.

o   We would stay selectively long on the Consumer and look in regions anchored by wealth effects вҖ“ notably the Korean Consumer (JPKBWEFT Index). We continue to see a tactical trade in EU Luxury. 

-       SHORTS

o   Span AI Vulnerable (ex-Software), EU Insurance, challenged Industries (Autos, Chemicals, Asian Consumer Electronics, MedTech) & MDAX. 

o    We are high conviction on EU Chemicals and Asia Consumer Electronics currently/

-       Option hedges: Keep 1) long dollar (given cheap vol) either in vanilla or dual digi format (FX & Equity leg) for an H4L air pocket; 2) MCX < UKX until clarity emerges on spending, pragmatism and ideology; 3) Puts on Momentum Factor to hedge mean reversion (seasonality + SoH reopening)

 

POSITIONING INTELLIGENCE

POSITIONING INTELLIGENCE IS NOT A PRODUCT OF RESEARCH

 

Report: Weekly Wrap | Mixed Tech Flows as AxJ, Semis, and Tech HW Positioning Stay Elevated

 

Summary:  

*	Gross leverage easing (as expected): Down WoW from 361% to 338% across All Strategies, but still 98th %-tile vs last 5yrs. Move looks driven by post-expiry roll-offs vs clear active de-grossing 

 

*	Potential for active de-grossing into late July is high: 1) weвҖҷll soon be past the main index rebal events, 2) the starting points of gross flows and leverage are at highs (long and short additions over the past 4wks have been +2z level), and c) July is approaching, during which weвҖҷve seen HF de-grossing each time over the past 5 years (around -1z to -3z events).

 

*	Crowding still working, but could reverse soon: Crowded stocks strong past 5d and MTD (e.g. +14% MTD in N. Am., +5% EMEA, and +10% in APAC). With sharp US crowding rebound MTD, next move could be lowerвҖ”US 20d crowding performance turned negative in each of past 5 Julys; strong 1m/3m performance can raise mean-reversion odds.

 

*	AxJ Tech / Korea:  HFs still bought Korea over the past week. HF AxJ long/short ratio is quite elevated and is up from 1.5x at the end of 1Q26 to 2.2x currently (93rd %-tile since 2020).  HFs mostly sold Tech outside AxJ this past week.

 

*	Positioning extremes within Tech globally: 6m z-scores of positioning + performance getting extreme. Semis ~+3z (moves above +2.5z have historically coincided with a pause/reversal), Tech HW >+4z (prior peaks often ~+2z and marked highs).

 

*	Momentum exposure still stretched: 6m z of HF positioning/performance touched +2z again this week (similar to mid-May). With price z-score > positioning z-score again, forward returns could skew negative. US non-Pure Momentum (JPPQMO) is pulling back per typical seasonality and could remain choppy.

 

*	Overall flows/positioning: HFs were net buyers globally (buying N. America & APAC, neutral EMEA). Financials consistently bought across regions. US Tactical Positioning Monitor: +0.4z WoW, -0.6z over 4wks; level ~neutral vs 10+ yrs. HF net leverage basically unchanged WoW (slight increase All Strats, reduction Eq L/S).

 



 <https://jpmorgan.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508585&ad=4533139655&h=a-Cj5zA&sig=Vn87IYJhVl00JayweChK6Miu4EU&v=2&url=https://www.jpmorgan.com/solutions/cib/markets/market-data-intelligence>
