emails.body:
<https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=daP_SNT&sig=LAiPGPdWmPRkVg9QoFBH4xD7hrV&v=2&url=https://www.jefferies.com>   

Equity Research
June 30, 2026

 

	
ASIA-PACIFIC excl. Japan | Internet

Sea Ltd | BUY

SE | $92.95 | PT: $157.00 | % to PT: +69%

 <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=E7EaLHk&sig=LVJCAN0JNlWoEeHnMD8MywICkAk&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/html/b3a5a408-167c-4745-b508-c656fb76ed53__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw6zawHjLA$> 

	
	
Execution on Track <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=E7EaLHk&sig=LVJCAN0JNlWoEeHnMD8MywICkAk&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/links2/html/b3a5a408-167c-4745-b508-c656fb76ed53__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw6zawHjLA$> 

Per our overseas sub-sector reports in Apr (link <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=5f0wq40&sig=3sU7ySGjsDuY7wK7l1f4ddIWleK&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/docs/html/ced9d106-5bbf-4c0e-8517-e56f8a3dbe95.html__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw7m17G-gg$> ) and May (link <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=x4-388q&sig=JuL6mKHkdFcyJrhx2m778C9m0YC&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/docs/html/f76d8f40-176c-497f-af34-c907afde77f6.html__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw5kEmSxcw$> ), we expect SE to execute on track to meet market expectations in 2Q. By segment, our GMV growth YoY forecast for Shopee is unchanged, and quarterly GMV/segmental EBITDA is expected to be sequentially stable. Our Monee growth assumption for 2Q is unchanged, considering faster growth in Brazil, Thailand, and Malaysia. Digital entertainment seasonality is expected. Full-year outlook is intact. Buy.

Factoring in recent business trends for 2Q. Per our overseas sub-sector reports in Apr (link <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=5f0wq40&sig=3sU7ySGjsDuY7wK7l1f4ddIWleK&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/docs/html/ced9d106-5bbf-4c0e-8517-e56f8a3dbe95.html__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw7m17G-gg$> ) and May (link <https://jefferies.email.streetcontxt.net/platform/al?a=13508311&ad=4532862784&h=x4-388q&sig=JuL6mKHkdFcyJrhx2m778C9m0YC&v=2&url=https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/javatar.bluematrix.com/docs/html/f76d8f40-176c-497f-af34-c907afde77f6.html__;!!PePb2utDNNs!uEdxaDnnP70KWg2ZWPsJ4XKK5KlATBApyPBF_5qsUL4Bd0rirpew5lIQFMgTmqJtqRytNuTpvqmJQGShz3spJXxLPWgZ-FeECe0tagX9Pw5kEmSxcw$> ), we expect SE to execute on track in the quarter to meet market expectations. For Shopee, we expect GMV to be stable sequentially and deliver growth of 25% YoY (unchanged). We estimate GAAP ecommerce revenue to rise by 35% YoY (in line with consensus) as take-rate continues to improve YoY. For digital entertainment, we expect bookings to grow 14% YoY (in line with consensus) and decline QoQ due to seasonality. For Monee, we expect revenue growth of 50% YoY (in line with consensus), considering fast growth in Brazil, Thailand, and Malaysia. We expect overall adjusted EBITDA to reach USD910m (in line with consensus), taking into consideration: (a) Shopee segment adjusted EBITDA is stable QoQ, similar to the GMV trend; (b) segment adjusted EBITDA margin of 0.6% (in line with consensus); (b) Garena segment adjusted EBITDA margin of 56%, lower than the 1Q level due to gaming mix (i.e. Free Fire); and (c) Monee segment EBITDA margin of about 21% (unchanged).

 

Focus areas for upcoming earnings call. These include: (1) updates on investment for growth strategies, and Shopee margin outlook; (2) online shopping competitive landscape in Brazil and Taiwan; (3) monthly GMV growth momentum in recent months and outlook; (4) GMV trends across different categories; (5) online shopping take-rate trends; (6) reinvestment strategies and EBITDA outlook for Southeast Asia and Brazil in 2H; (7) latest progress of Free Fire in recent months and expectations for 2H; (8) new games in the pipeline; and (9) updates on fintech strategies and EBITDA trends.

 

Valuation and risks. We maintain our Buy rating and PT of $157. Risks include: (1) earlier-than-expected maturity of Free Fire; (2) aggressive price wars in online shopping; and (3) monetization of Shopee taking longer than expected.

emails.subject: IDEA: Infineon Technologies AG (IFXGn.DE): Structural Breakout Story Playing Out
emails.body:
Infineon Technologies AG (IFXGn.DE) 	
Structural Breakout Story Playing Out	 
Read Full Report <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/66a7e21a-7154-11f1-b8bb-85ba1f0ac5ae?t=1782792130:2338:7753:527593380b83&m=1&ch=CDF Research ISG#e=ZmVsaXBlLm1vbnRlaXJvQGNhcHN0b25lLmNvbS5icg==> 	 
(PDF: 17 Pages) 	
Europe 	
Stock Rating   Overweight
Price Target   вӮ¬91.00

Lee Simpson, Shawn Kim, Nigel van Putten, Amelia M Scicluna	 
June 30, 2026

	
We continue to view Infineon as the key data centre beneficiary, but think it is unlikely for the upcoming site visit to offer a guidance update. We expect an in-line print, with resilient order book momentum, an industrial recovery profile, and commentary on AI data centre strength. 
Key takeaways	 

*	IFXвҖҷs FY3Q26 print should be broadly in line with guide. 
*	Order book resilience, better pricing, channel refill in autos, and improving industrial demand, are supporting the setup into Q4. 
*	The Dresden site visit is unlikely to trigger an immediate data centre target upgrade, but should improve visibility on capacity expansion plans. 
*	Litigation remains complex and jurisdiction-specific, but does not alter our view that IFX and Innoscience are key competitors in GaN. 
*	We remain OW, PT вӮ¬91 as we continue to like the longer term story and think the stock remains undervalued given data centre earnings potential. 

Preview to earnings	 
Company / Focus KPI	 Focus KPI Surprise	 Likely impact to consensus EPS	 
Infineon Technologies AG (IFXGn.DE) 	
FY26 Revenue and GM% Guide	 	 In-line	 
	 Largely unchanged	 
View our takeaways <https://ny.matrix.ms.com/eqr/article/webapp/66a7e21a-7154-11f1-b8bb-85ba1f0ac5ae/#tkr=IFXGn.DE> 	
* Direction of next 12-month consensus EPS	 
Source: Company data, Morgan Stanley Research	 

Near-term surprise unlikely, but long term DC upside keeps us OW. The structural breakout story is playing out, and while we remain confident that Infineon will be the clear data centre beneficiary, we think the next print is unlikely to offer a guidance surprise. Last quarter, management raised FY revenue and margin guidance to reflect improving cyclical dynamics. We think it is too soon to expect another guidance uptick but remain positive on the long-term story and earnings potential. We expect the site visit to focus on power semi capacity and product offering, in particular strength in GaN, which would reaffirm our view of Infineon's key role in the DC power semi transition. However, we think this visit will be more of a qualitative discussion as opposed to an opportunity for management to update data centre guidance. We continue to like the long-term story and agree that data centre guidance is too conservative, however we expect little surprise this quarter.

Expecting an in-line print. Infineon reports FY3Q26 results on 5 August. We expect the company to deliver in-line numbers with guidance supported by resilient order book momentum, a modestly improving industrial recovery profile, and robust SiC demand, including incremental support from AI data centre applications. Pricing should become a more visible tailwind from Q4 FY26 onward, implying a constructive setup for the next-quarter guide. We also expect distribution channel inventory normalisation to support automotive sales through FY26, while pricing continues to firm, particularly in microcontrollers.

Dresden site visit: a signal of data centre strength but unlikely to offer new numbers. AI data centre demand remains robust, with customers placing orders earlier to secure capacity ahead of strengthening end demand. InfineonвҖҷs Dresden site visit on 1 July is unlikely, in our view, to bring an upward revision to next yearвҖҷs data centre sales target; that is more likely to come with the FY26 print in November. However, we expect the event to provide greater visibility on the operational initiatives underway to expand capacity against demand that continues to exceed current targets. We expect commentary to focus on front-end wafer capacity but to include discussion on the progress with back-end alignment too (in Hungary). We also expect management to address the trajectory of GaN demand, the status and commercial relevance of GaN patent infringement claims, InfineonвҖҷs competitive positioning in vertical power delivery, and any potential FY28+ implications from possible 800V timing delays.

GaN litigation update. Infineon and Innoscience (covered by Daisy Dai) remain engaged in a multi-jurisdictional patent dispute across Germany, the US and China relating to gallium nitride power semiconductors. To date, the Munich Regional Court has issued multiple infringement judgments in InfineonвҖҷs favour, including injunctions restricting the manufacture, marketing and sale of infringing GaN products in Germany, alongside damages awards. In the US, the ITC found infringement of one Infineon patent and issued a limited exclusion order on certain Innoscience products, although some patent claims were invalidated and redesigned products were found non-infringing. By contrast, Innoscience has secured rulings in China, including injunctive relief against certain Infineon GaN products, primarily mid-voltage designs; Infineon continues to argue that proceedings remain ongoing, particularly for high-voltage designs, which represent the more material part of its business. Overall, the litigation remains active across jurisdictions, with rulings to date creating market-specific commercial restrictions rather than a global prohibition. The ultimate impact will depend on product design, patent validity challenges, appeals and related proceedings. Notwithstanding this, we continue to see Infineon and Innoscience as the key competitors in GaN for first-stage 800V server designs.

A reminder of our thesis: OW, PT вӮ¬91. We continue to view Infineon as undervalued given the company's full stack power semi solutions for the data centre and our increasing confidence in Infineon's role in both Nvidia and TPU racks. We value Infineon on a SOTP basis to reflect the divergent growth profiles of the data center and cyclical businesses. We value the data centre business at a 50x P/E multiple on FY28e, a discount to US peer, Monolithic Power Systems, which trades on a 55x P/E multiple. We value the cyclical segment on 24x, in-line with the analog sector's peak cycle multiple and reflecting improving visibility, better end-market dynamics and cyclical recovery.

emails.subject: GLW - POSCO Takeaways: Confirming GLW Key Role in CPO Supply Chain
emails.body:
JUNE 29, 2026	
 
 
 
 
 
	
	
 
 
 
 
 
CLICK HERE TO VIEW FULL PDF REPORT <https://sig.bluematrix.com/links2/pdf/363b4048-a2f0-4616-808c-cfb0b01746bf> 	
Corning	
SIG ALERT 	
POSCO Takeaways: Confirming GLW Key Role in CPO Supply Chain	
Corning

Symbol: GLW

Rating:  Positive

Price:  $255.69

Risk:  Downside Risk

Market cap.:  $219,641mm

Sector: Technology Hardware

HIGHLIGHTS

*	Following Corning's announcements at the AI Data Center Optical Communications & Interconnect Tech Conference at POSCO Tower Yeoksam in Seoul held last week, we are increasingly confident in GLW's progress toward rolling out competitive FAU (Fiber Array Unit) offerings.

*	As we highlighted in our takeaways of CorningвҖҷs Analyst Day (link <https://sig.bluematrix.com/docs/pdf/3340611f-3591-46a0-bf83-08c5a4b95a84.pdf> ), which we reiterated in our 6/24 note (link <https://sig.bluematrix.com/docs/pdf/1a6fbfcf-8a79-43c7-a77e-d8ee8db7aecb.pdf> ), we expect GLW could act as a strategic partner for CPO commercialization through the standardization and manufacturing of the FAU. In this scenario, GLW supplies the FAU for integration into the CPO platform (PIC + EIC + Packaging), an opportunity that also provides customers with alternatives to TSMC's CoWoS-L (COUPE) platform.
*	At the conference, GLW unveiled multiple IP offerings spanning the full FAU stack: Glass Bridge (the optical connector to the PIC), passive optical layer solutions, fiber harnesses, and glass substrates with through-glass vias (TGVs). Glass Bridge directly links photonic integrated circuits (PICs) and optical fibers, bridging a size mismatch of several dozen times between on-chip waveguides (hundreds of nanometers) and fiber cores (1-8 micrometers). It uses wafer-based ion-exchange waveguide technology to create optical pathways within glass, enabling high-density optical I/O at the PIC front end while simplifying fiber-to-chip alignment. The initial product targets core pitches of 30 micrometers or greater and coupling loss below 2 dB and is being co-developed with multiple partners, including GFS. The broader CPO solution layers in fiber harnesses with FAUs integrated into the CPO Optical Engine, CPO-optimized fibers, connectors, fiber management hardware, and a glass-substrate packaging architecture with TGVs and flip-chip-mounted photonic devices.
*	Corning also presented GlassWorks AI, its end-to-end optical connectivity platform for AI data centers, covering infrastructure within data centers, between racks, and across campuses, spanning optical fibers, cables, connectors, FAUs, and alignment components.

Mehdi Hosseini	
415 403 6516

emails.subject: Notes from call with Micron IR. Lots of discussion on SCAs. Also working on super IO NAND.
emails.body:
CONFERENCE CALL WITH MICRON.

 

Our Fubon team hosted a call with head of Micron IR Satya Kumar this morning. 

 

Here are my notes from the call. 

 

*	SCAs announced in the earnings call is a very important development which will accelerate the business model transformation from a company with limited visibility with annual non binding quarterly contracts to SCAs that go 3-5 years with binding take or pay requirements. Includes financial commitments. 

	*	Micron have completed 16 SCAs with total volume closed 20% of DRAM and 1/3rd of NAND volumes. In sales its 25% of total company sales closed so far. Still discussing additional SCAs. Should be up to 50% of company sales. 
	*	SCAs gives more certainty on demand and volume. ItвҖҷs a take or pay contract. Auto customers are 3 and 5 for other customers. The SCAs have financial commitments with $22bn in financial commitments $18bn in cash customer deposits held on the Balance Sheet which will be returned weighted to 2H of the contract.
	*	These are not escrow they are cash deposits $18bn with letter of credits $4bn. The cash goes into financing cash flow it doesnвҖҷt go into operating cash. Its unrestricted available for any purpose it will accumulate interest. In the later part of the contract this cash will be retuned to the customers. 
	*	Pricing 3 mechanisms with a ceiling and a floor price. The ceiling is price to CYQ2 market price. Havent specified the floor but have said the margins are well above the peak margins of any prior cycle. 
	*	Smaller volumes have fixed pricing
	*	2 of the 16 contracts have floating pricing. 

*	SCAs/LTAs have been around before how are they different this time? Micron acknowledges in the past long term agreements havenвҖҷt held. The most fundamental aspects are 

	*	Supply. On supply we have slowing of Moores Law harder to add bits. HBM trade ratio decreases supply. Product complexity is increasing. Its much harder to build supply and build new fabs. 
	*	Demand AI has fundamentally changed demand from mid teens to 20s and we are supply constrained as AI is riding on memory especially as we go to inference the performance of the memory sub system is critical to AI. If we go back into prior downturns there was pull forward not sustainable demand.
	*	Product. The strategic performance of memory has changed for cost per token. Memory is extremely important to customers we are now talking to model companies like Anthropic. We have deep collaboration with customers. The technology roadmap for memory is very different. 

*	The guidance reflects a meaningful slowdown in the rate of price increases. Certain volumes will have known pricing. This is below 25% based on SCA and not all have fixed price 2 donвҖҷt have pricing in them. But expect over time 40% of sales of Micron will have band or fixed pricing. The balance of the business not on SCA can vary with the market price. Micron donвҖҷt comment on market pricing in the future. 
*	Micron said up to 40% of volumes could be on SCAs with known pricing should expect the ceiling price is close to CYQ2 market price. This is the target beyond that cant comment on whether the contracts remaining to be signed will have the same pricing or not. 
*	Micron hasnвҖҷt made a comment on SCA volumes but as invest in capex will have more volumes to sell demand is significantly higher than volumes this will continue in CY27 and CY28. Demand is more than supply even for the SCA portion. The intent of the SCA is to give more certainty for capex planning so its natural to think supply will increase. 
*	Have entered into an agreement with ASML with increased adoption of EUV. The SCA helps to transform the business model of capex planning. 
*	This also allows more predictable financial returns. 
*	FY27 Capex was announced in March and have updated since. In FY27 said capex will be above mid $40bn up from Y27bn in FY26. More than half of this is for facilities. In terms of capacity HBM mix is a key factor and product mix can be different and specific nodes. We normally disclose that later in the year in how CY27 is shaping up. CY 26 DRAM bit shipments are low 20s including HBM and NAND 20% 
*	Demand is definitely above supply and will remain tight in 2027 and into 2028. Agentic AI drives a sharp increase in context windows and KV Cache driving more CPUs. Have to reduce the content in each of these servers to ship more servers. Micron are the pioneers in SOCAMM. Did the 196 and 256 GB have broadest portfolio.
*	Investors do think maybe optimize towards conventional DRAM as HBM margins are lower than DRAM. But its not that simple as clients need matched sets. Micron is working closely with customers on product mix but its not just a simple decision to maximise bits by reducing HBM and increasing conventional like some investors think. 
*	On NAND there are some new applications KV Cache and vector database indexing which requires 50-100M IOPS compared to 5m. This is Storage NEXT from Nvidia Micron is working on this so yes some use cases are looking for SLC. 
*	The other product is KV Cache which cant be kept in DRAM. Keeping for some period of time which is different from normal use of NAND which is there for a long period of time. KV Cache one possibility is fewer bits per cell could improve endurance which could help customer TCO but that has a cost impact. Working on more QLC with better endurance. So maybe some will look at TLC as well. 

 

 

The last questions there on NAND were from me. So Micron is also working on a super IO product like Kioxia. This super IO could be impactful for NAND so Im resending here also why that makes me prefer Kioxia over Sandisk. 

 

 

 3 REASONS WHY I PREFER KIOXIA TO SANDISK. A quarter ago I admitted I was totally wrong on NAND and got onboard. I remain positive. But as I outlined on the call the other night and in meetings for the past few months I prefer Kioxia to Sandisk. 

*	Kioxia is more conservative on capex and the outlook for NAND in general. Even after its analyst day its only assuming a 22% CAGR
*	Kioxia is more respectful of YMTC as a threat. It says its technology is good. Meanwhile investors all around the US told me that Sandisk dismisses YMTC as a threat and says its market share is only low single digit ie 3-4%. That is a factual error as you can check in Counterpoint data. In Q4 YMTC was 11% of global NAND revenues and 13% of bits. Silicon Motion says they will grow bits another 50-60% next year. But demand is so strong at present that wont undermine supply demand. Long term it likely will. 



*	For LLMs and reasoning models KV Cache is super important. Both companies have that. But now with agentic AI booming its Kioxia that has a product on the market with super high I/O and low latency. HBF from Sandisk wont come out until 2028. By then this super IOPS product from Kioxia will have been on the market for 2 years with a 100M IOPS product next year. 

	*	igh-end SSDs for data centers reach only about 6m IOPS which is not sufficient  inference. Tasks like image recognition and text generation are performed using trained models. This requires rapidly accessing a vast number of small pieces of data from large datasets in an unpredictable order.
	*	Next year Kioxia will have a 100m IOPS product.  its emulator display shows the performance of the currently running GPU I/O. While a conventional SSD on the far left has a performance of around 13 MIOPS, the performance of the emulated Super High IOPS SSD (the third graph from the left) exceeds 107 MIOPS. This technology enables developers to have early access to a target device that can help them prepare their software stacks to take full advantage of the unprecedented speed that a Super High IOPS SSD unleashes. 
	*	It plans to begin sample shipments of its first generation products in the KIOXIA GP Series in late 2026, with an expected second generation release sometime in 2027. 
	*	Also note Super IOPS has a trade ratio. Super IOPS is based on SLC. Just as HBM adoption reduces overall bits in DRAM, the adoption of SLC for Super IO will reduce bits at Kioxia and at the industry level.
